#### `public bool PerpendicularFrameAt(double t, out Plane plane)`

Return a 3d frame at a parameter. This is slightly different than FrameAt in that the frame is computed in a way so there is minimal rotation from one frame to the next.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Evaluation parameter.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The frame is returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PerpendicularFrameAt.htm)

#### `public Point3d PointAt(double t)`

Evaluates point at a curve parameter.

**Remarks:** No error handling.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Evaluation parameter.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — Point (location of curve at the parameter t).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PointAt.htm)

#### `public Point3d PointAtLength(double length)`

Gets a point at a certain length along the curve. The length must be non-negative and less than or equal to the length of the curve. Lengths will not be wrapped when the curve is closed or periodic.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Double) — Length along the curve between the start point and the returned point.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — Point on the curve at the specified length from the start point or Poin3d.Unset on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PointAtLength.htm)

#### `public Point3d PointAtNormalizedLength(double length)`

Gets a point at a certain normalized length along the curve. The length must be between or including 0.0 and 1.0, where 0.0 equals the start of the curve and 1.0 equals the end of the curve.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Double) — Normalized length along the curve between the start point and the returned point.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — Point on the curve at the specified normalized length from the start point or Poin3d.Unset on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PointAtNormalizedLength.htm)

#### `public Curve[] PullToBrepFace(BrepFace face, double tolerance)`

Pulls this curve to a brep face and returns the result of that operation.

**Parameters:**
- `face` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — A brep face.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value.

**Returns:** `Curve[]` — An array containing the resulting curves after pulling. This array could be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PullToBrepFace.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve PullToMesh(Mesh mesh, double tolerance)`

Makes a polyline approximation of the curve and gets the closest point on the mesh for each point on the curve. Then it "connects the points" so that you have a polyline on the mesh.

**Parameters:**
- `mesh` (Rhino.Geometry.Mesh) — Mesh to project onto.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Input tolerance (RhinoDoc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance is a good default)

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — A polyline curve on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PullToMesh.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve Rebuild(int pointCount, int degree, bool preserveTangents)`

Rebuild a curve with a specific point count.

**Parameters:**
- `pointCount` (System.Int32) — Number of control points in the rebuild curve.
- `degree` (System.Int32) — Degree of curve. Valid values are between and including 1 and 11.
- `preserveTangents` (System.Boolean) — If true, the end tangents of the input curve will be preserved.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A NURBS curve on success or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Rebuild.htm)

Looks for segments that are shorter than tolerance that can be removed. Does not change the domain, but it will change the relative parameterization.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if removable short segments were found. false if no removable short segments were found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_RemoveShortSegments.htm)

#### `public bool Reverse()`

Reverses the direction of the curve.

**Remarks:** If reversed, the domain changes from [a,b] to [-b,-a]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Reverse.htm)

#### `public Curve RibbonOffset(double distance, double blendRadius, Point3d directionPoint, Vector3d normal, double tolerance)`

Offsets a closed curve in the following way: pProject the curve to a plane with given normal. Then, loose Offset the projection by distance + blend_radius and trim off self-intersection. THen, Offset the remaining curve back in the opposite direction by blend_radius, filling gaps with blends. Finally, use the elevations of the input curve to get the correct elevations of the result.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — The positive distance to offset the curve.
- `blendRadius` (System.Double) — Positive, typically the same as distance. When the offset results in a self-intersection that gets trimmed off at a kink, the kink will be blended out using this radius.
- `directionPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point that indicates the direction of the offset. If the offset is inward, the point's projection to the plane should be well within the curve. It will be used to decide which part of the offset to keep if there are self-intersections.
- `normal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector that indicates the normal of the plane in which the offset will occur.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Used to determine self-intersections, not offset error.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The offset curve if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_RibbonOffset.htm)

#### `public Curve RibbonOffset(double distance, double blendRadius, Point3d directionPoint, Vector3d normal, double tolerance, out Curve[] crossSections, out Surface[] ruledSurfaces)`

Offsets a closed curve in the following way: pProject the curve to a plane with given normal. Then, loose Offset the projection by distance + blend_radius and trim off self-intersection. THen, Offset the remaining curve back in the opposite direction by blend_radius, filling gaps with blends. Finally, use the elevations of the input curve to get the correct elevations of the result.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — The positive distance to offset the curve.
- `blendRadius` (System.Double) — Positive, typically the same as distance. When the offset results in a self-intersection that gets trimmed off at a kink, the kink will be blended out using this radius.
- `directionPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point that indicates the direction of the offset. If the offset is inward, the point's projection to the plane should be well within the curve. It will be used to decide which part of the offset to keep if there are self-intersections.
- `normal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector that indicates the normal of the plane in which the offset will occur.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Used to determine self-intersections, not offset error.
- `crossSections` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve[]) — Contains lines between input and the offset that might be useful as input to Brep.CreateFromSweep or some other surface creation tool.
- `ruledSurfaces` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface[]) — Contain ruled surfaces between the input and the parts of the offset that correspond exactly. Note, there will be gaps between these at blends.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The offset curve if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_RibbonOffset_1.htm)

#### `public Curve RibbonOffset(double distance, double blendRadius, Point3d directionPoint, Vector3d normal, double tolerance, out double[] outputParameters, out double[] curveParameters)`

Offsets a closed curve in the following way: pProject the curve to a plane with given normal. Then, loose Offset the projection by distance + blend_radius and trim off self-intersection. THen, Offset the remaining curve back in the opposite direction by blend_radius, filling gaps with blends. Finally, use the elevations of the input curve to get the correct elevations of the result.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — The positive distance to offset the curve.
- `blendRadius` (System.Double) — Positive, typically the same as distance. When the offset results in a self-intersection that gets trimmed off at a kink, the kink will be blended out using this radius.
- `directionPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point that indicates the direction of the offset. If the offset is inward, the point's projection to the plane should be well within the curve. It will be used to decide which part of the offset to keep if there are self-intersections.
- `normal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector that indicates the normal of the plane in which the offset will occur.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Used to determine self-intersections, not offset error.
- `outputParameters` (System.Double[]) — A list of parameter, paired with curveParameters, from the output curve for creating cross sections.
- `curveParameters` (System.Double[]) — A list of parameter, paired with outputParameters, from the input curve for creating cross sections.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The offset curve if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_RibbonOffset_2.htm)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Scale.htm)

#### `public Curve Simplify(CurveSimplifyOptions options, double distanceTolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Returns a geometrically equivalent PolyCurve. The PolyCurve has the following properties 1. All the PolyCurve segments are LineCurve, PolylineCurve, ArcCurve, or NurbsCurve. 2. The NURBS Curves segments do not have fully multiple interior knots. 3. Rational NURBS curves do not have constant weights. 4. Any segment for which IsLinear() or IsArc() is true is a Line, Polyline segment, or an Arc. 5. Adjacent co-linear or co-circular segments are combined. 6. Segments that meet with G1-continuity have there ends tuned up so that they meet with G1-continuity to within machine precision.

**Parameters:**
- `options` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveSimplifyOptions) — Simplification options.
- `distanceTolerance` (System.Double) — A distance tolerance for the simplification.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — An angle tolerance for the simplification.

**Returns:** `Curve` — New simplified curve on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Simplify.htm)

#### `public Curve SimplifyEnd(CurveEnd end, CurveSimplifyOptions options, double distanceTolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Same as SimplifyCurve, but simplifies only the last two segments at "side" end.

**Parameters:**
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveEnd) — If CurveEnd.Start the function simplifies the last two start side segments, otherwise if CurveEnd.End the last two end side segments are simplified.
- `options` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveSimplifyOptions) — Simplification options.
- `distanceTolerance` (System.Double) — A distance tolerance for the simplification.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — An angle tolerance for the simplification.

**Returns:** `Curve` — New simplified curve on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_SimplifyEnd.htm)

#### `public Curve Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem)`

Smooths a curve by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the curve ends don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The smoothed curve if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Smooth.htm)

#### `public Curve Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem, Plane plane)`

Smooths a curve by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the curve ends don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — If SmoothingCoordinateSystem.CPlane specified, then the construction plane.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The smoothed curve if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Smooth_1.htm)

#### `public PolyCurve ToArcsAndLines(double tolerance, double angleTolerance, double minimumLength, double maximumLength)`

Converts a curve into polycurve consisting of arc segments. Sections of the input curves that are nearly straight are converted to straight-line segments.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance. This is the maximum deviation from arc midpoints to the curve. When in doubt, use the document's model space absolute tolerance.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — The angle tolerance in radians. This is the maximum deviation of the arc end directions from the curve direction. When in doubt, use the document's model space angle tolerance.
- `minimumLength` (System.Double) — The minimum segment length.
- `maximumLength` (System.Double) — The maximum segment length.

**Returns:** `PolyCurve` — PolyCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToArcsAndLines.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(double tolerance, double angleTolerance, double minimumLength, double maximumLength)`

Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance. This is the maximum deviation from line midpoints to the curve. When in doubt, use the document's model space absolute tolerance.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — The angle tolerance in radians. This is the maximum deviation of the line directions. When in doubt, use the document's model space angle tolerance.
- `minimumLength` (System.Double) — The minimum segment length.
- `maximumLength` (System.Double) — The maximum segment length.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — PolyCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToPolyline.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(int mainSegmentCount, int subSegmentCount, double maxAngleRadians, double maxChordLengthRatio, double maxAspectRatio, double tolerance, double minEdgeLength, double maxEdgeLength, bool keepStartPoint)`

Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `mainSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — If mainSegmentCount <= 0, then both subSegmentCount and mainSegmentCount are ignored. If mainSegmentCount > 0, then subSegmentCount must be >= 1. In this case the NURBS will be broken into mainSegmentCount equally spaced chords. If needed, each of these chords can be split into as many subSegmentCount sub-parts if the subdivision is necessary for the mesh to meet the other meshing constraints. In particular, if subSegmentCount = 0, then the curve is broken into mainSegmentCount pieces and no further testing is performed.
- `subSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — An amount of subsegments.
- `maxAngleRadians` (System.Double) — ( 0 to pi ) Maximum angle (in radians) between unit tangents at adjacent vertices.
- `maxChordLengthRatio` (System.Double) — Maximum permitted value of (distance chord midpoint to curve) / (length of chord).
- `maxAspectRatio` (System.Double) — If maxAspectRatio < 1.0, the parameter is ignored. If 1 <= maxAspectRatio < sqrt(2), it is treated as if maxAspectRatio = sqrt(2). This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of (length of longest chord) / (length of shortest chord).
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — If tolerance = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of the distance from the curve to the polyline.
- `minEdgeLength` (System.Double) — The minimum permitted edge length.
- `maxEdgeLength` (System.Double) — If maxEdgeLength = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted edge length.
- `keepStartPoint` (System.Boolean) — If true the starting point of the curve is added to the polyline. If false the starting point of the curve is not added to the polyline.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — PolylineCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToPolyline_1.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(int mainSegmentCount, int subSegmentCount, double maxAngleRadians, double maxChordLengthRatio, double maxAspectRatio, double tolerance, double minEdgeLength, double maxEdgeLength, bool keepStartPoint, Interval curveDomain)`

Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `mainSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — If mainSegmentCount <= 0, then both subSegmentCount and mainSegmentCount are ignored. If mainSegmentCount > 0, then subSegmentCount must be >= 1. In this case the NURBS will be broken into mainSegmentCount equally spaced chords. If needed, each of these chords can be split into as many subSegmentCount sub-parts if the subdivision is necessary for the mesh to meet the other meshing constraints. In particular, if subSegmentCount = 0, then the curve is broken into mainSegmentCount pieces and no further testing is performed.
- `subSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — An amount of subsegments.
- `maxAngleRadians` (System.Double) — ( 0 to pi ) Maximum angle (in radians) between unit tangents at adjacent vertices.
- `maxChordLengthRatio` (System.Double) — Maximum permitted value of (distance chord midpoint to curve) / (length of chord).
- `maxAspectRatio` (System.Double) — If maxAspectRatio < 1.0, the parameter is ignored. If 1 <= maxAspectRatio < sqrt(2), it is treated as if maxAspectRatio = sqrt(2). This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of (length of longest chord) / (length of shortest chord).
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — If tolerance = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of the distance from the curve to the polyline.
- `minEdgeLength` (System.Double) — The minimum permitted edge length.
- `maxEdgeLength` (System.Double) — If maxEdgeLength = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted edge length.
- `keepStartPoint` (System.Boolean) — If true the starting point of the curve is added to the polyline. If false the starting point of the curve is not added to the polyline.
- `curveDomain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — This sub-domain of the NURBS curve is approximated.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — PolylineCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToPolyline_2.htm)

#### `public double TorsionAt(double t)`

Evaluate the torsion of a curve at a parmeter. Sometimes also called the "second curvature", torsion is the rate of change of a curve's osculating plane.

**Remarks:** See Barrett O'Neill, Elementary Differential Geometry, page 69.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — The evaluation parameter.

**Returns:** `Double` — The torsion if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TorsionAt.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

#### `public bool Translate(double x, double y, double z)`

Translates the object along the specified vector.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — The X component.
- `y` (System.Double) — The Y component.
- `z` (System.Double) — The Z component.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully translated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Translate_1.htm)

#### `public bool Translate(Vector3d translationVector)`

Translates the object along the specified vector.

**Parameters:**
- `translationVector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A moving vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully translated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Translate.htm)

#### `public Curve Trim(CurveEnd side, double length)`

Shortens a curve by a given length

**Parameters:**
- `side` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveEnd) — [Missing <param name="side"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Curve.Trim(Rhino.Geometry.CurveEnd,System.Double)"]
- `length` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="length"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Curve.Trim(Rhino.Geometry.CurveEnd,System.Double)"]

#### `public Curve Trim(double t0, double t1)`

Removes portions of the curve outside the specified interval.

**Parameters:**
- `t0` (System.Double) — Start of the trimming interval. Portions of the curve before curve(t0) are removed.
- `t1` (System.Double) — End of the trimming interval. Portions of the curve after curve(t1) are removed.

**Returns:** `Curve` — Trimmed portion of this curve is successful, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Trim_2.htm)

#### `public Curve Trim(Interval domain)`

Removes portions of the curve outside the specified interval.

**Parameters:**
- `domain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — Trimming interval. Portions of the curve before curve(domain[0]) and after curve(domain[1]) are removed.

**Returns:** `Curve` — Trimmed curve if successful, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Trim_1.htm)

Test a curve for planarity and return the plane.

#### `public bool TryGetPolyline(out Polyline polyline)`

Several types of Curve can have the form of a polyline including a degree 1 NurbsCurve, a PolylineCurve, and a PolyCurve all of whose segments are some form of polyline. IsPolyline tests a curve to see if it can be represented as a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline) — If true is returned, then the polyline form is returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this curve can be represented as a polyline; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TryGetPolyline.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetPolyline(out Polyline polyline, out double[] parameters)`

Several types of Curve can have the form of a polyline including a degree 1 NurbsCurve, a PolylineCurve, and a PolyCurve all of whose segments are some form of polyline. IsPolyline tests a curve to see if it can be represented as a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline) — If true is returned, then the polyline form is returned here.
- `parameters` (System.Double[]) — if true is returned, then the parameters of the polyline points are returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this curve can be represented as a polyline; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TryGetPolyline_1.htm)

### Properties
- `AngleDegrees` (Double, get) — Gets the angles of this arc in degrees.
- `AngleRadians` (Double, get) — Gets the angles of this arc in radians.
- `Arc` (Arc, get) — Gets the arc that is contained within this ArcCurve.
- `Degree` (Int32, get) — Gets the maximum algebraic degree of any span or a good estimate if curve spans are not algebraic.
- `Dimension` (Int32, get) — Gets the dimension of the object. The dimension is typically three. For parameter space trimming curves the dimension is two. In rare cases the dimension can be one or greater than three.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `Domain` (Interval, get/set) — Gets or sets the domain of the curve.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsClosed` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is a closed curve.
- `IsCompleteCircle` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve can be represented by a complete circle.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsPeriodic` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is considered to be Periodic.
- `IsSubDFriendly` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the curve is a cubic, non-rational, uniform NURBS curve that is either periodic or has natural end conditions. Othewise, false is returned.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `PointAtEnd` (Point3d, get) — Evaluates point at the end of the curve.
- `PointAtStart` (Point3d, get) — Evaluates point at the start of the curve.
- `Radius` (Double, get) — Gets the radius of this ArcCurve.
- `SpanCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of non-empty smooth (c-infinity) spans in the curve.
- `TangentAtEnd` (Vector3d, get) — Evaluate unit tangent vector at the end of the curve.
- `TangentAtStart` (Vector3d, get) — Evaluates the unit tangent vector at the start of the curve.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## AreaMassProperties (class)

Contains static initialization methods and allows access to the computed metrics of area, area centroid and area moments in closed planar curves, in meshes, in surfaces, in hatches and in boundary representations.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties.htm)

### Methods
#### `public bool CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMoments(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the moments matrix with repect to centroid coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMoments.htm)

#### `public bool CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the principal moments of inertia and principal axes with repect to centroid coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia.htm)

#### `public static AreaMassProperties Compute(Brep brep)`

Computes an AreaMassProperties for a brep.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — Brep to measure.

**Returns:** `AreaMassProperties` — The AreaMassProperties for the given Brep or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_Compute.htm)

#### `public static AreaMassProperties Compute(Brep brep, bool area, bool firstMoments, bool secondMoments, bool productMoments)`

Compute the AreaMassProperties for a single Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — Brep to measure.
- `area` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate area.
- `firstMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate area first moments, area, and area centroid.
- `secondMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate area second moments.
- `productMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate area product moments.

**Returns:** `AreaMassProperties` — The AreaMassProperties for the given Brep or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_Compute_1.htm)

#### `public static AreaMassProperties Compute(Brep brep, bool area, bool firstMoments, bool secondMoments, bool productMoments, double relativeTolerance, double absoluteTolerance)`

Compute the AreaMassProperties for a single Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — Brep to measure.
- `area` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate area.
- `firstMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate area first moments, area, and area centroid.
- `secondMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate area second moments.
- `productMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate area product moments.
- `relativeTolerance` (System.Double) — The relative tolerance used for the calculation. In overloads of this function where tolerances are not specified, 1.0e-6 is used.
- `absoluteTolerance` (System.Double) — The absolute tolerancwe used for the calculation. In overloads of this function where tolerances are not specified, 1.0e-6 is used.

**Returns:** `AreaMassProperties` — The AreaMassProperties for the given Brep or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_Compute_2.htm)

#### `public static AreaMassProperties Compute(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry)`

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_Dispose_1.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_Finalize.htm)

#### `public bool WorldCoordinatesPrincipalMoments(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of moments matrix in world coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_WorldCoordinatesPrincipalMoments.htm)

#### `public bool WorldCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the principal moments of inertia and principal axes with repect to world coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AreaMassProperties_WorldCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia.htm)

### Properties
- `Area` (Double, get) — Gets the area solution.
- `AreaError` (Double, get) — Gets the uncertainty in the area calculation.
- `Centroid` (Point3d, get) — Gets the area centroid in the world coordinate system.
- `CentroidCoordinatesMomentsOfInertia` (Vector3d, get) — Moments of inertia with respect to centroid coordinate system. X = integral of ((y-y0)^2 + (z-z0)^2) dm Y = integral of ((z-z0)^2 + (x-x0)^2) dm Z = integral of ((z-z0)^2 + (y-y0)^2) dm where (x0,y0,z0) = centroid.
- `CentroidCoordinatesMomentsOfInertiaError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in centroid coordinates moments of inertia calculation.
- `CentroidCoordinatesProductMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Product moments with respect to centroid coordinate system.
- `CentroidCoordinatesProductMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in product moments with respect to centroid coordinate system.
- `CentroidCoordinatesRadiiOfGyration` (Vector3d, get) — Radii of gyration with respect to centroid coordinate system. X = sqrt(integral of ((y-y0)^2 + (z-z0)^2) dm/M) Y = sqrt(integral of ((z-z0)^2 + (x-x0)^2) dm/M) Z = sqrt(integral of ((z-z0)^2 + (y-y0)^2) dm/M) where (x0,y0,z0) = centroid.
- `CentroidCoordinatesSecondMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Second moments with respect to centroid coordinate system. X = integral of (x-x0)^2 dm Y = integral of (y-y0)^2 dm Z = integral of (z-z0)^2 dm where (x0,y0,z0) = centroid.
- `CentroidCoordinatesSecondMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in centroid coordinates second moments calculation.
- `CentroidError` (Vector3d, get) — Gets the uncertainty in the centroid calculation.
- `WorldCoordinatesFirstMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Returns the world coordinate first moments if they were able to be calculated. X is integral of "x dm" over the area Y is integral of "y dm" over the area Z is integral of "z dm" over the area.
- `WorldCoordinatesFirstMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in world coordinates first moments calculation.
- `WorldCoordinatesMomentsOfInertia` (Vector3d, get) — The moments of inertia about the world coordinate axes. X = integral of (y^2 + z^2) dm Y = integral of (z^2 + x^2) dm Z = integral of (z^2 + y^2) dm.
- `WorldCoordinatesMomentsOfInertiaError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in world coordinates moments of inertia calculation.
- `WorldCoordinatesProductMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Returns the world coordinate product moments if they were able to be calculated. X is integral of "xy dm" over the area Y is integral of "yz dm" over the area Z is integral of "zx dm" over the area.
- `WorldCoordinatesProductMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in world coordinates second moments calculation.
- `WorldCoordinatesRadiiOfGyration` (Vector3d, get) — Radii of gyration with respect to world coordinate system. X = sqrt(integral of (y^2 + z^2) dm/M) Y = sqrt(integral of (z^2 + x^2) dm/M) Z = sqrt(integral of (z^2 + y^2) dm/M)
- `WorldCoordinatesSecondMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Returns the world coordinate second moments if they were able to be calculated. X is integral of "xx dm" over the area Y is integral of "yy dm" over the area Z is integral of "zz dm" over the area.
- `WorldCoordinatesSecondMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in world coordinates second moments calculation.

## BezierCurve (class)

Represents a Bezier curve. Note: as an exception, the bezier curve is not derived from Curve.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve.htm)

### Constructors
- `public BezierCurve(IEnumerable<Point2d> controlPoints)` — Create bezier curve with controls defined by a list of 2d points
- `public BezierCurve(IEnumerable<Point3d> controlPoints)` — Create bezier curve with controls defined by a list of 3d points
- `public BezierCurve(IEnumerable<Point4d> controlPoints)` — Create bezier curve with controls defined by a list of 4d points
- `protected BezierCurve(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Protected constructor for internal use.

### Methods
#### `public bool ChangeDimension(int desiredDimension)`

Change dimension of bezier.

**Parameters:**
- `desiredDimension` (System.Int32)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful. false if desired_dimension < 1

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_ChangeDimension.htm)

#### `public static BezierCurve[] CreateBeziers(Curve sourceCurve)`

Create an array of Bezier curves that fit to an existing curve. Please note, these Beziers can be of any order and may be rational.

**Parameters:**
- `sourceCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The curve to fit Beziers to

**Returns:** `BezierCurve[]` — A new array of Bezier curves

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_CreateBeziers.htm)

#### `public static BezierCurve[] CreateCubicBeziers(Curve sourceCurve, double distanceTolerance, double kinkTolerance)`

Constructs an array of cubic, non-rational Beziers that fit a curve to a tolerance.

**Parameters:**
- `sourceCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — A curve to approximate.
- `distanceTolerance` (System.Double) — The max fitting error. Use RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon as a minimum.
- `kinkTolerance` (System.Double) — If the input curve has a g1-discontinuity with angle radian measure greater than kinkTolerance at some point P, the list of beziers will also have a kink at P.

**Returns:** `BezierCurve[]` — A new array of bezier curves. The array can be empty and might contain null items.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_CreateCubicBeziers.htm)

#### `public static BezierCurve CreateLoftedBezier(IEnumerable<Point2d> points)`

Loft a bezier through a list of points

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — 2 or more points to interpolate

**Returns:** `BezierCurve` — new bezier curve if successful

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_CreateLoftedBezier.htm)

#### `public static BezierCurve CreateLoftedBezier(IEnumerable<Point3d> points)`

Loft a bezier through a list of points

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — 2 or more points to interpolate

**Returns:** `BezierCurve` — new bezier curve if successful

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_CreateLoftedBezier_1.htm)

Evaluate the curvature vector at a curve parameter.

**Remarks:** No error handling.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Evaluation parameter.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — Curvature vector of the curve at the parameter t.

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_Dispose_1.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_Finalize.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the curve.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_GetBoundingBox.htm)

- `index` (System.Int32) — Control vertex index (0 <= index < ControlVertexCount)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_GetControlVertex3d.htm)

#### `public Point4d GetControlVertex4d(int index)`

Get location of a control vertex.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — Control vertex index (0 <= index < ControlVertexCount)

**Returns:** `Point4d` — Homogeneous value of control vertex. If the bezier is not rational, the weight is 1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BezierCurve_GetControlVertex4d.htm)

Constructs a NURBS curve representation of this curve.

### Properties
- `ControlVertexCount` (Int32, get) — Number of control vertices in this curve
- `Dimension` (Int32, get) — Dimension of Bezier
- `IsRational` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the curve is rational. Rational curves have control-points with custom weights.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.

## BlendContinuity (enum)

Used in curve and surface blending and matching functions.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BlendContinuity.htm)

### Values
- `Position` = `0` — G0: The curves or surfaces touch at the join point (position).
- `Tangency` = `1` — G1: The curves or surfaces also share a common tangent direction at the join point (tangent).
- `Curvature` = `2` — G2: The curves or surfaces also share a common center of curvature at the join point (curvature).

## BlendType (enum)

Blend types used for creating filleted Brep edges

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BlendType.htm)

### Values
- `Chamfer` = `0` — Creates a ruled surface between brep edges with varying chamfer distances.
- `Fillet` = `1` — Creates a tangent surface between brep edges with varying radius values.
- `Blend` = `2` — Creates a curvature-continuous blend surface between brep edges with varying radius values.

## BoundingBox (struct)

Represents the value of two points in a bounding box defined by the two extreme corner points. This box is therefore aligned to the world X, Y and Z axes.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox.htm)

### Constructors
- `public BoundingBox(IEnumerable<Point3d> points)` — Constructs a bounding box from a collection of points.
- `public BoundingBox(Point3d min, Point3d max)` — Constructs a new bounding box from two corner points.
- `public BoundingBox(IEnumerable<Point3d> points, Transform xform)` — Constructs a bounding box from a collection of points.
- `public BoundingBox(double minX, double minY, double minZ, double maxX, double maxY, double maxZ)` — Constructs a bounding box from numeric extremes.

### Methods
#### `public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d point)`

Finds the closest point on or in the bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Sample point.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on or in the box that is closest to the sample point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d point, bool includeInterior)`

Finds the closest point on or in the bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Sample point.
- `includeInterior` (System.Boolean) — If false, the point is projected onto the boundary faces only, otherwise the interior of the box is also taken into consideration.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on or in the box that is closest to the sample point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_ClosestPoint_1.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(BoundingBox box)`

Determines whether this bounding box contains another bounding box. This is the same as calling Contains(box,false).

**Parameters:**
- `box` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — Box to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the box is on the inside of this bounding box, or is coincident with the surface of it.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Contains.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(BoundingBox box, bool strict)`

Determines whether this bounding box contains another bounding box. The user can choose how to treat bounding boxes with coincident surfaces.

**Parameters:**
- `box` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — Box to test.
- `strict` (System.Boolean) — If true, the box needs to be fully on the inside of the bounding box. I.e. coincident boxes will be considered 'outside'.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the box is (strictly) on the inside of this BoundingBox.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Contains_1.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(Point3d point)`

Tests a point for bounding box inclusion. This is the same as calling Contains(point, false)

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the point is on the inside of or coincident with this bounding box; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Contains_2.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(Point3d point, bool strict)`

Tests a point for BoundingBox inclusion.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.
- `strict` (System.Boolean) — If true, the point needs to be fully on the inside of the BoundingBox. I.e. coincident points will be considered 'outside'.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — If 'strict' is affirmative, true if the point is inside this bounding box; false if it is on the surface or outside.If 'strict' is negative, true if the point is on the surface or on the inside of the bounding box; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Contains_3.htm)

#### `public Point3d Corner(bool minX, bool minY, bool minZ)`

Gets one of the eight corners of the box.

**Parameters:**
- `minX` (System.Boolean) — true for the minimum on the X axis; false for the maximum.
- `minY` (System.Boolean) — true for the minimum on the Y axis; false for the maximum.
- `minZ` (System.Boolean) — true for the minimum on the Z axis; false for the maximum.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The requested point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Corner.htm)

#### `public Point3d FurthestPoint(Point3d point)`

Finds the furthest point on the Box.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Sample point.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on the box that is furthest from the sample point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_FurthestPoint.htm)

#### `public Point3d[] GetCorners()`

Gets an array filled with the 8 corner points of this box. See remarks for the return order.

**Remarks:** [0] Min.X, Min.Y, Min.Z[1] Max.X, Min.Y, Min.Z[2] Max.X, Max.Y, Min.Z[3] Min.X, Max.Y, Min.Z[4] Min.X, Min.Y, Max.Z[5] Max.X, Min.Y, Max.Z[6] Max.X, Max.Y, Max.Z[7] Min.X, Max.Y, Max.Z

**Returns:** `Point3d[]` — An array of 8 corners.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_GetCorners.htm)

#### `public Line[] GetEdges()`

Gets an array of the 12 edges of this box.

**Returns:** `Line[]` — If the bounding box IsValid, the 12 edges; otherwise, null.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_GetEdges.htm)

#### `public void Inflate(double amount)`

Inflates the box with equal amounts in all directions. Inflating with negative amounts may result in decreasing boxes. Invalid boxes can not be inflated.

**Parameters:**
- `amount` (System.Double) — Amount (in model units) to inflate this box in all directions.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Inflate.htm)

#### `public void Inflate(double xAmount, double yAmount, double zAmount)`

Inflate the box with custom amounts in all directions. Inflating with negative amounts may result in decreasing boxes. InValid boxes can not be inflated.

**Parameters:**
- `xAmount` (System.Double) — Amount (in model units) to inflate this box in the x direction.
- `yAmount` (System.Double) — Amount (in model units) to inflate this box in the y direction.
- `zAmount` (System.Double) — Amount (in model units) to inflate this box in the z direction.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Inflate_1.htm)

#### `public static BoundingBox Intersection(BoundingBox a, BoundingBox b)`

Computes the intersection of two bounding boxes.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — A first bounding box.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — A second bounding box.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The intersection bounding box.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Intersection.htm)

#### `public int IsDegenerate(double tolerance)`

Determines whether a bounding box is degenerate (flat) in one or more directions.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Distances <= tolerance will be considered to be zero. If tolerance is negative (default), then a scale invariant tolerance is used.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 = box is not degenerate 1 = box is a rectangle (degenerate in one direction). 2 = box is a line (degenerate in two directions). 3 = box is a point (degenerate in three directions) 4 = box is not valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_IsDegenerate.htm)

#### `public bool MakeValid()`

Ensures that the box is defined in an increasing fashion along X, Y and Z axes. If the Min or Max points are unset, this function will not change the box.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the box was made valid, false if the box could not be made valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_MakeValid.htm)

#### `public Point3d PointAt(double tx, double ty, double tz)`

Evaluates the bounding box with normalized parameters. The box has idealized side length of 1x1x1.

**Parameters:**
- `tx` (System.Double) — Normalized (between 0 and 1 is inside the box) parameter along the X direction.
- `ty` (System.Double) — Normalized (between 0 and 1 is inside the box) parameter along the Y direction.
- `tz` (System.Double) — Normalized (between 0 and 1 is inside the box) parameter along the Z direction.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point at the {tx, ty, tz} parameters.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_PointAt.htm)

#### `public Brep ToBrep()`

Constructs a Brep representation of this bounding box.

**Returns:** `Brep` — If this operation is successful, a Brep representation of this box; otherwise null.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_ToBrep.htm)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Constructs the string representation of this aligned bounding box.

**Returns:** `String` — Text.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_ToString.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Updates this bounding box to be the smallest axis aligned bounding box that contains the transformed result of its 8 original corner points.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — A transform.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this operation is successful; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Transform.htm)

#### `public void Union(BoundingBox other)`

Updates this BoundingBox to represent the union of itself and another box.

**Remarks:** If either this BoundingBox or the other BoundingBox is InValid, the Valid BoundingBox will be the only one included in the union.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — Box to include in this union.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Union.htm)

#### `public static BoundingBox Union(BoundingBox a, BoundingBox b)`

Returns a new BoundingBox that represents the union of boxes a and b.

**Remarks:** Invalid boxes are ignored and will not affect the union.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — First box to include in union.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — Second box to include in union.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The BoundingBox that contains both a and b.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Union_1.htm)

#### `public static BoundingBox Union(BoundingBox box, Point3d point)`

Returns a new BoundingBox that represents the union of a bounding box and a point.

**Remarks:** Invalid boxes and points are ignored and will not affect the union.

**Parameters:**
- `box` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — Box to include in the union.
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to include in the union.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The BoundingBox that contains both the box and the point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Union_2.htm)

#### `public void Union(Point3d point)`

Updates this BoundingBox to represent the union of itself and a point.

**Remarks:** If this bounding box is InValid then the union will be the BoundingBox containing only the point. If the point is InValid, this BoundingBox will remain unchanged.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to include in the union.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BoundingBox_Union_3.htm)

### Properties
- `Area` (Double, get) — Gets the area of this BoundingBox.
- `Center` (Point3d, get) — Gets the point in the center of the bounding box.
- `Diagonal` (Vector3d, get) — Gets the diagonal vector of this BoundingBox. The diagonal connects the Min and Max points.
- `Empty` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets an [Empty] bounding box. An Empty box is an invalid structure that has negative width.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value that indicates whether or not this bounding box is valid. Empty boxes are not valid, and neither are boxes with unset points.
- `Max` (Point3d, get/set) — Gets or sets the point in the maximal corner.
- `Min` (Point3d, get/set) — Gets or sets the point in the minimal corner.
- `Unset` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets a bounding box that has Unset coordinates for Min and Max.
- `Volume` (Double, get) — Gets the volume of this BoundingBox.

## Box (struct)

Represents the value of a plane and three intervals in an orthogonal, oriented box that is not necessarily parallel to the world Y, X, Z axes.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Box.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Box(BoundingBox bbox)` — Initializes a new Box that mimics a BoundingBox struct. The orientation plane of the Box is coincident with the World XY plane.
- `public Box(Box other)` — Copy constructor.
- `public Box(Plane basePlane, BoundingBox boundingbox)` — Initializes a world aligned box from a base plane and a bounding box.
- `public Box(Plane basePlane, GeometryBase geometry)` — Initializes a box that contains a generic piece of geometry. This box will be aligned with an arbitrary plane.
- `public Box(Plane basePlane, IEnumerable<Point3d> points)` — Initializes the smallest box that contains a set of points.
- `public Box(Plane basePlane, Interval xSize, Interval ySize, Interval zSize)` — Initializes a new box from a base Plane and three Intervals.

### Methods
#### `public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d point)`

Finds the closest point on or in the Box. The box should be Valid for this to work.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Sample point.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on or in the box that is closest to the sample point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(BoundingBox box)`

Test a bounding box for Box inclusion. This is the same as calling Contains(box,false)

**Parameters:**
- `box` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — Box to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the box is on the inside of or coincident with this Box.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Contains.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(BoundingBox box, bool strict)`

Test a bounding box for Box inclusion.

**Parameters:**
- `box` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — Box to test.
- `strict` (System.Boolean) — If true, the bounding box needs to be fully on the inside of this Box. I.e. coincident boxes will be considered 'outside'.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the box is (strictly) on the inside of this Box.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Contains_1.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(Box box)`

Test a box for Box inclusion. This is the same as calling Contains(box,false)

**Parameters:**
- `box` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Box to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the box is on the inside of or coincident with this Box.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Contains_2.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(Box box, bool strict)`

Test a box for Box inclusion.

**Parameters:**
- `box` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Box to test.
- `strict` (System.Boolean) — If true, the box needs to be fully on the inside of this Box. I.e. coincident boxes will be considered 'outside'.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the box is (strictly) on the inside of this Box.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Contains_3.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(Point3d point)`

Determines whether a point is included in this box. This is the same as calling Contains(point,false)

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the point is on the inside of or coincident with this Box.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Contains_4.htm)

#### `public bool Contains(Point3d point, bool strict)`

Determines whether a point is included in this box.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.
- `strict` (System.Boolean) — If true, the point needs to be fully on the inside of the Box. I.e. coincident points will be considered 'outside'.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the point is (strictly) on the inside of this Box.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Contains_5.htm)

#### `public bool EpsilonEquals(Box other, double epsilon)`

Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — [Missing <param name="other"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Box.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Box,System.Double)"]
- `epsilon` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="epsilon"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Box.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Box,System.Double)"]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Box.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Box,System.Double)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_EpsilonEquals.htm)

#### `public Point3d FurthestPoint(Point3d point)`

Finds the furthest point on the Box. The Box should be Valid for this to work properly.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Sample point.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on the box that is furthest from the sample point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_FurthestPoint.htm)

#### `public Point3d[] GetCorners()`

Gets an array of the 8 corner points of this box.

**Returns:** `Point3d[]` — An array of 8 corners.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_GetCorners.htm)

#### `public void Inflate(double amount)`

Inflates the box by a given offset in each direction. Inflating with negative amounts may result in decreasing boxes. InValid boxes cannot be inflated.

**Parameters:**
- `amount` (System.Double) — Amount (in model units) to inflate this box in all directions.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Inflate.htm)

#### `public void Inflate(double xAmount, double yAmount, double zAmount)`

Inflates the box by a given offset in each direction. Inflating with negative amounts may result in decreasing boxes. InValid boxes cannot be inflated.

**Parameters:**
- `xAmount` (System.Double) — Amount (in model units) to inflate this box in the x direction.
- `yAmount` (System.Double) — Amount (in model units) to inflate this box in the y direction.
- `zAmount` (System.Double) — Amount (in model units) to inflate this box in the z direction.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Inflate_1.htm)

#### `public bool MakeValid()`

Attempts to make the Box valid. This is not always possible.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the box was made valid, or if it was valid to begin with. false if the box remains unchanged.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_MakeValid.htm)

#### `public Point3d PointAt(double x, double y, double z)`

Evaluates the box volume at the given unitized parameters. The box has idealized side length of 1x1x1.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Unitized parameter (between 0 and 1 is inside the box) along box X direction.
- `y` (System.Double) — Unitized parameter (between 0 and 1 is inside the box) along box Y direction.
- `z` (System.Double) — Unitized parameter (between 0 and 1 is inside the box) along box Z direction.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point at (x,y,z).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_PointAt.htm)

#### `public void RepositionBasePlane(Point3d origin)`

Repositions the origin of the Base plane for this box without affecting the physical dimensions.

**Parameters:**
- `origin` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The new base plane origin.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_RepositionBasePlane.htm)

#### `public Brep ToBrep()`

Constructs a brep representation of this box.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A Brep representation of this box or null.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_ToBrep.htm)

#### `public Extrusion ToExtrusion()`

Constructs an extrusion representation of this box.

**Returns:** `Extrusion` — An Extrusion representation of this box or null.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_ToExtrusion.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms this Box using a Transformation matrix. If the Transform does not preserve Similarity, the dimensions of the resulting box cannot be trusted.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation matrix to apply to this Box.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the Box was successfully transformed, false if otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Transform.htm)

#### `public void Union(Point3d point)`

Constructs a union between this Box and the given point. This grows the box in directions so it contains the point.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to include.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Box_Union.htm)

### Properties
- `Area` (Double, get) — Gets the total surface area of this box.
- `BoundingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the world axis aligned Bounding box for this oriented box.
- `Center` (Point3d, get) — Gets the point that is in the center of the box.
- `Empty` (Box, get) — Empty Box. Empty boxes are considered to be invalid.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets the validity of this Box. Boxes are invalid when the base plane or any of the dimension intervals are invalid or decreasing.
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — Gets or sets the orientation plane for this Box.
- `Unset` (Box, get) — Gets a Box whose base plane and axis dimensions are all Unset.
- `Volume` (Double, get) — Gets the total volume of this box.
- `X` (Interval, get/set) — Gets or sets the Interval that describes the dimension of the Box along the orientation plane X-Axis. Otherwise known as the Width of the Box.
- `Y` (Interval, get/set) — Gets or sets the Interval that describes the dimension of the Box along the orientation plane Y-Axis. Otherwise known as the Depth of the Box.
- `Z` (Interval, get/set) — Gets or sets the Interval that describes the dimension of the Box along the orientation plane Z-Axis. Otherwise known as the Height of the Box.

## Brep (class)

/// Boundary Representation. A surface or polysurface along with trim curve information.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Brep.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Brep()` — Initializes a new empty brep
- `protected Brep(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Protected constructor used in serialization.

### Methods
#### `public int AddEdgeCurve(Curve curve)`

Add a 3d curve used by the brep edges

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — [Missing <param name="curve"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Brep.AddEdgeCurve(Rhino.Geometry.Curve)"]

**Returns:** `Int32` — Index used to reference this geometry in the edge curve list

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_AddEdgeCurve.htm)

#### `public int AddSurface(Surface surface)`

Adds a 3D surface used by BrepFace.

**Parameters:**
- `surface` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — A copy of the surface is added to this brep.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Index that should be used to reference the geometry. -1 is returned if the input is not acceptable.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_AddSurface.htm)

#### `public int AddTrimCurve(Curve curve)`

Add a 2d curve used by the brep trims

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — [Missing <param name="curve"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Brep.AddTrimCurve(Rhino.Geometry.Curve)"]

**Returns:** `Int32` — Index used to reference this geometry in the trimming curve list

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_AddTrimCurve.htm)

#### `public void Append(Brep other)`

Appends a copy of another brep to this and updates indices of appended brep parts. Duplicates are not removed

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — [Missing <param name="other"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Brep.Append(Rhino.Geometry.Brep)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Append.htm)

#### `public Brep CapPlanarHoles(double tolerance)`

Returns a new Brep that is equivalent to this Brep with all planar holes capped.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance to use for capping.

**Returns:** `Brep` — New brep on success. null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CapPlanarHoles.htm)

#### `public static Brep ChangeSeam(BrepFace face, int direction, double parameter, double tolerance)`

Change the seam of a closed trimmed surface.

**Parameters:**
- `face` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — A Brep face with a closed underlying surface.
- `direction` (System.Int32) — The parameter direction (0 = U, 1 = V). The face's underlying surface must be closed in this direction.
- `parameter` (System.Double) — The parameter at which to place the seam.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used to cut up surface.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A new Brep that has the same geometry as the face with a relocated seam if successful, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_ChangeSeam.htm)

#### `public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint)`

Finds a point on the brep that is closest to testPoint.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Base point to project to brep.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on the Brep closest to testPoint or Point3d.Unset if the operation failed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out Point3d closestPoint, out ComponentIndex ci, out double s, out double t, double maximumDistance, out Vector3d normal)`

Finds a point on the Brep that is closest to testPoint. The method searches all Brep faces looking for the one closest to testPoint. When found, if the closest point falls on the inactive region of the face, then the method finds the face's edge that is closest to testPoint.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Base point to project to surface.
- `closestPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Location of the closest Brep point.
- `ci` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentIndex) — Component index of the Brep component that contains the closest point. Possible component index types are ComponentIndexType.BrepEdge and ComponentIndexType.BrepFace.
- `s` (System.Double) — If ci.ComponentIndexType == ComponentIndexType.BrepEdge, then s is the parameter of the closest edge point.
- `t` (System.Double) — If ci.ComponentIndexType == ComponentIndexType.BrepFace, then (s,t) is the parameter of the closest face point.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — If maximumDistance > 0, then only points whose distance is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.
- `normal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — If ci.ComponentIndexType == ComponentIndexType.BrepEdge, then this is the tangent to the edge at s. If ci.ComponentIndexType == ComponentIndexType.BrepFace, then this is the normal to the face at (s,t).

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the operation succeeded, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_ClosestPoint_1.htm)

#### `public void Compact()`

Deletes any unreferenced objects from arrays, re-indexes as needed, and shrinks arrays to minimum required size. Uses CUllUnused* members to delete any unreferenced objects from arrays.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Compact.htm)

#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

Copy all trims from a Brep face onto a surface.

- `surfaceSource` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — The surface to trim.

#### `public static Brep CreateBaseballSphere(Point3d center, double radius, double tolerance)`

Creates a brep representation of the sphere with two similar trimmed NURBS surfaces, and no singularities.

**Parameters:**
- `center` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The center of the sphere.
- `radius` (System.Double) — The radius of the sphere.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Used in computing 2d trimming curves. If >= 0.0, then the max of ON_0.0001 * radius and RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance will be used.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A new brep, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateBaseballSphere.htm)

#### `public static Curve CreateBlendShape(BrepFace face0, BrepEdge edge0, double t0, bool rev0, BlendContinuity continuity0, BrepFace face1, BrepEdge edge1, double t1, bool rev1, BlendContinuity continuity1)`

Makes a curve blend between points on two surface edges. The blend will be tangent to the surfaces and perpendicular to the edges.

**Parameters:**
- `face0` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — First face to blend from.
- `edge0` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepEdge) — First edge to blend from.
- `t0` (System.Double) — Location on first edge for first end of blend curve.
- `rev0` (System.Boolean) — If false, edge0 will be used in its natural direction. If true, edge0 will be used in the reversed direction.
- `continuity0` (Rhino.Geometry.BlendContinuity) — Continuity for the blend at the start.
- `face1` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — Second face to blend from.
- `edge1` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepEdge) — Second edge to blend from.
- `t1` (System.Double) — Location on second edge for second end of blend curve.
- `rev1` (System.Boolean) — If false, edge1 will be used in its natural direction. If true, edge1 will be used in the reversed direction.
- `continuity1` (Rhino.Geometry.BlendContinuity) — >Continuity for the blend at the end.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The blend curve on success. null on failure

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateBlendShape.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateBlendSurface(BrepFace face0, BrepEdge edge0, Interval domain0, bool rev0, BlendContinuity continuity0, BrepFace face1, BrepEdge edge1, Interval domain1, bool rev1, BlendContinuity continuity1)`

**Parameters:**

- `edge0` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepEdge) — First edge to blend from.
- `domain0` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The domain of edge0 to use.

- `continuity0` (Rhino.Geometry.BlendContinuity) — Continuity for the blend at the start.
- `face1` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — Second face to blend from.
- `edge1` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepEdge) — Second edge to blend from.
- `domain1` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The domain of edge1 to use.
- `rev1` (System.Boolean) — If false, edge1 will be used in its natural direction. If true, edge1 will be used in the reversed direction.
- `continuity1` (Rhino.Geometry.BlendContinuity) — Continuity for the blend at the end.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateBlendSurface.htm)
