#### `public static Brep[] CreateChamferSurface(BrepFace face0, Point2d uv0, double radius0, BrepFace face1, Point2d uv1, double radius1, bool trim, bool extend, double tolerance, out Brep[] outBreps0, out Brep[] outBreps1)`

Creates a ruled surface as a bevel between two input surface edges.

**Parameters:**
- `face0` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — First face to chamfer from.
- `uv0` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — A parameter face0 at the side you want to keep after chamfering.
- `radius0` (System.Double) — The distance from the intersection of face0 to the edge of the chamfer.
- `face1` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — Second face to chamfer from.
- `uv1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — A parameter face1 at the side you want to keep after chamfering.
- `radius1` (System.Double) — The distance from the intersection of face1 to the edge of the chamfer.
- `trim` (System.Boolean) — If true, the input faces will be trimmed, if false, the input faces will be split.
- `extend` (System.Boolean) — If true, then when one input surface is longer than the other, the chamfer surface is extended to the input surface edges.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.
- `outBreps0` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep[]) — The trim or split results of the Brep owned by face0.
- `outBreps1` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep[]) — The trim or split results of the Brep owned by face1.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateChamferSurface.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateChamferSurface(BrepFace face0, Point2d uv0, double radius0, BrepFace face1, Point2d uv1, double radius1, bool extend, double tolerance)`

Creates a ruled surface as a bevel between two input surface edges.

**Parameters:**
- `face0` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — First face to chamfer from.
- `uv0` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — A parameter face0 at the side you want to keep after chamfering.
- `radius0` (System.Double) — The distance from the intersection of face0 to the edge of the chamfer.
- `face1` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — Second face to chamfer from.
- `uv1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — A parameter face1 at the side you want to keep after chamfering.
- `radius1` (System.Double) — The distance from the intersection of face1 to the edge of the chamfer.
- `extend` (System.Boolean) — If true, then when one input surface is longer than the other, the chamfer surface is extended to the input surface edges.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateChamferSurface_1.htm)

Fillets, chamfers, or blends the edges of a brep.

- `startRadii` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Double>) — An array of starting fillet, chamfer, or blend radaii, one for each edge index.

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFilletSurface(BrepFace face0, Point2d uv0, BrepFace face1, Point2d uv1, double radius, bool trim, bool extend, double tolerance, out Brep[] outBreps0, out Brep[] outBreps1)`

Creates a constant-radius round surface between two surfaces.

**Parameters:**
- `face0` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — First face to fillet from.
- `uv0` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — A parameter face0 at the side you want to keep after filleting.
- `face1` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — Second face to fillet from.
- `uv1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — A parameter face1 at the side you want to keep after filleting.
- `radius` (System.Double) — The fillet radius.
- `trim` (System.Boolean) — If true, the input faces will be trimmed, if false, the input faces will be split.
- `extend` (System.Boolean) — If true, then when one input surface is longer than the other, the fillet surface is extended to the input surface edges.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.
- `outBreps0` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep[]) — The trim or split results of the Brep owned by face0.
- `outBreps1` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep[]) — The trim or split results of the Brep owned by face1.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFilletSurface.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFilletSurface(BrepFace face0, Point2d uv0, BrepFace face1, Point2d uv1, double radius, bool extend, double tolerance)`

Creates a constant-radius round surface between two surfaces.

**Parameters:**
- `face0` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — First face to fillet from.
- `uv0` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — A parameter face0 at the side you want to keep after filleting.
- `face1` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — Second face to fillet from.
- `uv1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — A parameter face1 at the side you want to keep after filleting.
- `radius` (System.Double) — The fillet radius.
- `extend` (System.Boolean) — If true, then when one input surface is longer than the other, the fillet surface is extended to the input surface edges.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFilletSurface_1.htm)

Constructs new brep that matches a bounding box.

Constructs new brep that matches an aligned box.

#### `public static Brep CreateFromBox(IEnumerable<Point3d> corners)`

Constructs new brep from 8 corner points.

**Parameters:**
- `corners` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — 8 points defining the box corners arranged as the vN labels indicate. v7_______e6____v6|\ |\| e7 | e5| \ ______e4_____\e11 v4 | v5| | e10 || | | |v3--|---e2----v2 e9 \ e8 \ | e3 | e1| \| \| v0_____e0______v1

#### `public static Brep CreateFromCone(Cone cone, bool capBottom)`

Constructs a Brep representation of the cone with a single face for the cone, an edge along the cone seam, and vertices at the base and apex ends of this seam edge. The optional cap is a single face with one circular edge starting and ending at the base vertex.

**Parameters:**
- `cone` (Rhino.Geometry.Cone) — A cone value.
- `capBottom` (System.Boolean) — if true the base of the cone should be capped.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A Brep if successful, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromCone.htm)

#### `public static Brep CreateFromCornerPoints(Point3d corner1, Point3d corner2, Point3d corner3, double tolerance)`

Makes a Brep with one face from three corner points.

**Parameters:**
- `corner1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A first corner.
- `corner2` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A second corner.
- `corner3` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A third corner.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Minimum edge length allowed before collapsing the side into a singularity.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A boundary representation, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromCornerPoints_1.htm)

Makes a Brep with one face from four corner points.

- `corner1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A first corner.
- `corner2` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A second corner.
- `corner3` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A third corner.
- `corner4` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A fourth corner.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Minimum edge length allowed before collapsing the side into a singularity.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A boundary representation, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromCornerPoints.htm)

#### `public static Brep CreateFromCylinder(Cylinder cylinder, bool capBottom, bool capTop)`

Constructs a Brep definition of a cylinder.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — cylinder.IsFinite() must be true.
- `capBottom` (System.Boolean) — if true end at cylinder.m_height[0] should be capped.
- `capTop` (System.Boolean) — if true end at cylinder.m_height[1] should be capped.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A Brep representation of the cylinder with a single face for the cylinder, an edge along the cylinder seam, and vertices at the bottom and top ends of this seam edge. The optional bottom/top caps are single faces with one circular edge starting and ending at the bottom/top vertex.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromCylinder.htm)

#### `public static Brep CreateFromJoinedEdges(Brep brep0, int edgeIndex0, Brep brep1, int edgeIndex1, double joinTolerance)`

Joins two naked edges, or edges that are coincident or close together, from two Breps.

**Parameters:**
- `brep0` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The first Brep.
- `edgeIndex0` (System.Int32) — The edge index on the first Brep.
- `brep1` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The second Brep.
- `edgeIndex1` (System.Int32) — The edge index on the second Brep.
- `joinTolerance` (System.Double) — The join tolerance.

**Returns:** `Brep` — The resulting Brep if successful, null on failure.

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromLoft(IEnumerable<Curve> curves, Point3d start, Point3d end, bool StartTangent, bool EndTangent, BrepTrim StartTrim, BrepTrim EndTrim, LoftType loftType, bool closed)`

Constructs one or more Breps by lofting through a set of curves, optionally matching start and end tangents of surfaces when first and/or last loft curves are surface edges

**Parameters:**
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — The curves to loft through. This function will not perform any curve sorting. You must pass in curves in the order you want them lofted. This function will not adjust the directions of open curves. Use Curve.DoDirectionsMatch and Curve.Reverse to adjust the directions of open curves. This function will not adjust the seams of closed curves. Use Curve.ChangeClosedCurveSeam to adjust the seam of closed curves.
- `start` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional starting point of loft. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include a start point. "start" and "StartTangent" cannot both be true.
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional ending point of loft. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include an end point. "end and "EndTangent" cannot both be true.
- `StartTangent` (System.Boolean) — If StartTangent is true and the first loft curve is a surface edge, the loft will match the tangent of the surface behind that edge.
- `EndTangent` (System.Boolean) — If EndTangent is true and the first loft curve is a surface edge, the loft will match the tangent of the surface behind that edge.
- `StartTrim` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepTrim) — BrepTrim from the surface edge where start tangent is to be matched
- `EndTrim` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepTrim) — BrepTrim from the surface edge where end tangent is to be matched
- `loftType` (Rhino.Geometry.LoftType) — type of loft to perform.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — true if the last curve in this loft should be connected back to the first one.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Constructs a closed surface, continuing the surface past the last curve around to the first curve. Available when you have selected three shape curves.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromLoft_1.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromLoft(IEnumerable<Curve> curves, Point3d start, Point3d end, LoftType loftType, bool closed)`

Constructs one or more Breps by lofting through a set of curves.

**Parameters:**
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — The curves to loft through. This function will not perform any curve sorting. You must pass in curves in the order you want them lofted. This function will not adjust the directions of open curves. Use Curve.DoDirectionsMatch and Curve.Reverse to adjust the directions of open curves. This function will not adjust the seams of closed curves. Use Curve.ChangeClosedCurveSeam to adjust the seam of closed curves.
- `start` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional starting point of loft. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include a start point.
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional ending point of loft. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include an end point.
- `loftType` (Rhino.Geometry.LoftType) — type of loft to perform.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — true if the last curve in this loft should be connected back to the first one.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Constructs a closed surface, continuing the surface past the last curve around to the first curve. Available when you have selected three shape curves.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromLoft.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromLoftRebuild(IEnumerable<Curve> curves, Point3d start, Point3d end, LoftType loftType, bool closed, int rebuildPointCount)`

Constructs one or more Breps by lofting through a set of curves. Input for the loft is simplified by rebuilding to a specified number of control points.

**Parameters:**
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — The curves to loft through. This function will not perform any curve sorting. You must pass in curves in the order you want them lofted. This function will not adjust the directions of open curves. Use Curve.DoDirectionsMatch and Curve.Reverse to adjust the directions of open curves. This function will not adjust the seams of closed curves. Use Curve.ChangeClosedCurveSeam to adjust the seam of closed curves.
- `start` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional starting point of loft. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include a start point.
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional ending point of lost. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include an end point.
- `loftType` (Rhino.Geometry.LoftType) — type of loft to perform.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — true if the last curve in this loft should be connected back to the first one.
- `rebuildPointCount` (System.Int32) — A number of points to use while rebuilding the curves. 0 leaves turns this parameter off.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Constructs a closed surface, continuing the surface past the last curve around to the first curve. Available when you have selected three shape curves.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromLoftRebuild.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromLoftRefit(IEnumerable<Curve> curves, Point3d start, Point3d end, LoftType loftType, bool closed, double refitTolerance)`

Constructs one or more Breps by lofting through a set of curves. Input for the loft is simplified by refitting to a specified tolerance.

**Parameters:**
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — The curves to loft through. This function will not perform any curve sorting. You must pass in curves in the order you want them lofted. This function will not adjust the directions of open curves. Use Curve.DoDirectionsMatch and Curve.Reverse to adjust the directions of open curves. This function will not adjust the seams of closed curves. Use Curve.ChangeClosedCurveSeam to adjust the seam of closed curves.
- `start` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional starting point of loft. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include a start point.
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional ending point of lost. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include an end point.
- `loftType` (Rhino.Geometry.LoftType) — type of loft to perform.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — true if the last curve in this loft should be connected back to the first one.
- `refitTolerance` (System.Double) — A distance to use in refitting, or 0 if you want to turn this parameter off.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Constructs a closed surface, continuing the surface past the last curve around to the first curve. Available when you have selected three shape curves.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromLoftRefit.htm)

Create a brep representation of a mesh

#### `public static Brep CreateFromOffsetFace(BrepFace face, double offsetDistance, double offsetTolerance, bool bothSides, bool createSolid)`

Offsets a face including trim information to create a new brep.

**Parameters:**
- `face` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — the face to offset.
- `offsetDistance` (System.Double) — An offset distance.
- `offsetTolerance` (System.Double) — Use 0.0 to make a loose offset. Otherwise, the document's absolute tolerance is usually sufficient.
- `bothSides` (System.Boolean) — When true, offset to both sides of the input face.
- `createSolid` (System.Boolean) — When true, make a solid object.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A new brep if successful. The brep can be disjoint if bothSides is true and createSolid is false, or if createSolid is true and connecting the offsets with side surfaces fails. null if unsuccessful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromOffsetFace.htm)

#### `public static Brep CreateFromRevSurface(RevSurface surface, bool capStart, bool capEnd)`

Constructs a brep form of a surface of revolution.

**Parameters:**
- `surface` (Rhino.Geometry.RevSurface) — The surface of revolution.
- `capStart` (System.Boolean) — if true, the start of the revolute is not on the axis of revolution, and the surface of revolution is closed, then a circular cap will be added to close of the hole at the start of the revolute.
- `capEnd` (System.Boolean) — if true, the end of the revolute is not on the axis of revolution, and the surface of revolution is closed, then a circular cap will be added to close of the hole at the end of the revolute.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A Brep if successful, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromRevSurface.htm)

Constructs a Brep definition of a sphere.

- `sphere` (Rhino.Geometry.Sphere) — The input sphere provides the orienting plane and radius.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A Brep if successful, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromSphere.htm)

#### `public static Brep CreateFromSurface(Surface surface)`

Constructs a Brep from a surface. The resulting Brep has an outer boundary made from four trims. The trims are ordered so that they run along the south, east, north, and then west side of the surface's parameter space.

**Parameters:**
- `surface` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — A surface to convert.

**Returns:** `Brep` — Resulting brep or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromSurface.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromSweep(Curve rail1, Curve rail2, IEnumerable<Curve> shapes, Point3d start, Point3d end, bool closed, double tolerance, SweepRebuild rebuild, int rebuildPointCount, double refitTolerance, bool preserveHeight)`

Sweep2 function that fits a surface through profile curves that define the surface cross-sections and two curves that defines the surface edges.

**Parameters:**
- `rail1` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Rail to sweep shapes along
- `rail2` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Rail to sweep shapes along
- `shapes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — Shape curves
- `start` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional starting point of sweep. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include a start point.
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional ending point of sweep. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include an end point.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — Only matters if shapes are closed.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for fitting surface and rails.
- `rebuild` (Rhino.Geometry.SweepRebuild) — The rebuild style.
- `rebuildPointCount` (System.Int32) — If rebuild == SweepRebuild.Rebuild, the number of points. Otherwise specify 0.
- `refitTolerance` (System.Double) — If rebuild == SweepRebuild.Refit, the refit tolerance. Otherwise, specify 0.0
- `preserveHeight` (System.Boolean) — Removes the association between the height scaling from the width scaling

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Brep sweep results

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromSweep_2.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromSweep(Curve rail1, Curve rail2, IEnumerable<Curve> shapes, Point3d start, Point3d end, bool closed, double tolerance, SweepRebuild rebuild, int rebuildPointCount, double refitTolerance, bool preserveHeight, bool autoAdjust)`

Sweep2 function that fits a surface through profile curves that define the surface cross-sections and two curves that defines the surface edges.

**Parameters:**
- `rail1` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Rail to sweep shapes along
- `rail2` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Rail to sweep shapes along
- `shapes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — Shape curves
- `start` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional starting point of sweep. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include a start point.
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional ending point of sweep. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include an end point.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — Only matters if shapes are closed.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for fitting surface and rails.
- `rebuild` (Rhino.Geometry.SweepRebuild) — The rebuild style.
- `rebuildPointCount` (System.Int32) — If rebuild == SweepRebuild.Rebuild, the number of points. Otherwise specify 0.
- `refitTolerance` (System.Double) — If rebuild == SweepRebuild.Refit, the refit tolerance. Otherwise, specify 0.0
- `preserveHeight` (System.Boolean) — Removes the association between the height scaling from the width scaling
- `autoAdjust` (System.Boolean) — Set to true to have shape curves adjusted, sorted, and matched automatically. This will produce results comparable to Rhino's Sweep2 command. Set to false to not have shape curves adjusted, sorted, and matched automatically.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Brep sweep results

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromSweep_3.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromSweepInParts(Curve rail1, Curve rail2, IEnumerable<Curve> shapes, IEnumerable<Point2d> rail_params, bool closed, double tolerance)`

Makes a 2 rail sweep. Like CreateFromSweep but the result is split where parameterization along a rail changes abruptly.

**Parameters:**
- `rail1` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Rail to sweep shapes along
- `rail2` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Rail to sweep shapes along
- `shapes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — Shape curves
- `rail_params` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — Shape parameters
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — Only matters if shapes are closed
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for fitting surface and rails

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Brep sweep results

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromSweepInParts.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromSweepSegmented(Curve rail, Curve shape, bool closed, double tolerance)`

Sweep1 function that fits a surface through a profile curve that define the surface cross-sections and one curve that defines a surface edge. The Segmented version breaks the rail at curvature kinks and sweeps each piece separately, then put the results together into a Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `rail` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Rail to sweep shapes along
- `shape` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Shape curve
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — Only matters if shape is closed
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for fitting surface and rails

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Brep sweep results

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromSweepSegmented.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromSweepSegmented(Curve rail, IEnumerable<Curve> shapes, bool closed, double tolerance)`

Sweep1 function that fits a surface through a series of profile curves that define the surface cross-sections and one curve that defines a surface edge. The Segmented version breaks the rail at curvature kinks and sweeps each piece separately, then put the results together into a Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `rail` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Rail to sweep shapes along.
- `shapes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — Shape curves.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — Only matters if shapes are closed.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for fitting surface and rails.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Brep sweep results.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromSweepSegmented_2.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromSweepSegmented(Curve rail, IEnumerable<Curve> shapes, Point3d startPoint, Point3d endPoint, SweepFrame frameType, Vector3d roadlikeNormal, bool closed, SweepBlend blendType, SweepMiter miterType, double tolerance, SweepRebuild rebuildType, int rebuildPointCount, double refitTolerance)`

Sweep1 function that fits a surface through a series of profile curves that define the surface cross-sections and one curve that defines a surface edge. The Segmented version breaks the rail at curvature kinks and sweeps each piece separately, then put the results together into a Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `rail` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Rail to sweep shapes along.
- `shapes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — Shape curves.
- `startPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional starting point of sweep. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include a start point.
- `endPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Optional ending point of sweep. Use Point3d.Unset if you do not want to include an end point.
- `frameType` (Rhino.Geometry.SweepFrame) — The frame type.
- `roadlikeNormal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The roadlike normal directoion. Use Vector3d.Unset if the frame type is not set to roadlike.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — Only matters if shapes are closed.
- `blendType` (Rhino.Geometry.SweepBlend) — The shape blending type.
- `miterType` (Rhino.Geometry.SweepMiter) — The mitering type.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="tolerance"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Brep.CreateFromSweepSegmented(Rhino.Geometry.Curve,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Rhino.Geometry.Curve},Rhino.Geometry.Point3d,Rhino.Geometry.Point3d,Rhino.Geometry.SweepFrame,Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d,System.Boolean,Rhino.Geometry.SweepBlend,Rhino.Geometry.SweepMiter,System.Double,Rhino.Geometry.SweepRebuild,System.Int32,System.Double)"]
- `rebuildType` (Rhino.Geometry.SweepRebuild) — The rebuild style.
- `rebuildPointCount` (System.Int32) — If rebuild == SweepRebuild.Rebuild, the number of points. Otherwise specify 0.
- `refitTolerance` (System.Double) — If rebuild == SweepRebuild.Refit, the refit tolerance. Otherwise, specify 0.0

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Brep sweep results.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromSweepSegmented_1.htm)

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — array of breps on success

- `draftAngleRadians` (System.Double) — angle of the extrusion
- `cornerType` (Rhino.Geometry.ExtrudeCornerType) — [Missing <param name="cornerType"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Brep.CreateFromTaperedExtrude(Rhino.Geometry.Curve,System.Double,Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d,Rhino.Geometry.Point3d,System.Double,Rhino.Geometry.ExtrudeCornerType,System.Double,System.Double)"]

#### `public static Brep[] CreateFromTaperedExtrudeWithRef(Curve curve, Vector3d direction, double distance, double draftAngle, Plane plane, double tolerance)`

Creates one or more Breps by extruding a curve a distance along an axis with draft angle.

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The curve to extrude.
- `direction` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The extrusion direction.
- `distance` (System.Double) — The extrusion distance.
- `draftAngle` (System.Double) — The extrusion draft angle in radians.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The end of the extrusion will be parallel to this plane, and "distance" from the plane's origin. The plane's origin is generally be a point on the curve. For planar curves, a natural choice for the plane's normal direction will be the normal direction of the curve's plane. In any case, plane.Normal = direction may make sense.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The intersecting and trimming tolerance.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — An array of Breps if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateFromTaperedExtrudeWithRef.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateOffsetBrep(Brep brep, double distance, bool solid, bool extend, bool shrink, double tolerance, out Brep[] outBlends, out Brep[] outWalls)`

Offsets a Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The Brep to offset.
- `distance` (System.Double) — The distance to offset. This is a signed distance value with respect to face normals and flipped faces.
- `solid` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the function makes a closed solid from the input and offset surfaces by lofting a ruled surface between all of the matching edges.
- `extend` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the function maintains the sharp corners when the original surfaces have sharps corner. If False, then the function creates fillets at sharp corners in the original surfaces.
- `shrink` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the function shrinks the underlying surfaces to their face's outer boundary loop.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The offset tolerance.
- `outBlends` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep[]) — The results of the calculation.
- `outWalls` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep[]) — The results of the calculation.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps if successful. If the function succeeds in offsetting, a single Brep will be returned. Otherwise, the array will contain the offset surfaces, outBlends will contain the set of blends used to fill in gaps (if extend is false), and outWalls will contain the set of wall surfaces that was supposed to join the offset to the original (if solid is true).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateOffsetBrep.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateOffsetBrep(Brep brep, double distance, bool solid, bool extend, double tolerance, out Brep[] outBlends, out Brep[] outWalls)`

Offsets a Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The Brep to offset.
- `distance` (System.Double) — The distance to offset. This is a signed distance value with respect to face normals and flipped faces.
- `solid` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the function makes a closed solid from the input and offset surfaces by lofting a ruled surface between all of the matching edges.
- `extend` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the function maintains the sharp corners when the original surfaces have sharps corner. If False, then the function creates fillets at sharp corners in the original surfaces.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The offset tolerance.
- `outBlends` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep[]) — The results of the calculation.
- `outWalls` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep[]) — The results of the calculation.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps if successful. If the function succeeds in offsetting, a single Brep will be returned. Otherwise, the array will contain the offset surfaces, outBlends will contain the set of blends used to fill in gaps (if extend is false), and outWalls will contain the set of wall surfaces that was supposed to join the offset to the original (if solid is true).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateOffsetBrep_1.htm)

#### `public static Brep CreatePatch(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, int uSpans, int vSpans, double tolerance)`

Constructs a brep patch. This is the simple version of fit that uses a plane with u x v spans. It makes a plane by fitting to the points from the input geometry to use as the starting surface. The surface has the specified u and v span count.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — A combination of curves, brep trims, points, point clouds or meshes. Curves and trims are sampled to get points. Trims are sampled for points and normals.
- `uSpans` (System.Int32) — The number of spans in the U direction.
- `vSpans` (System.Int32) — The number of spans in the V direction.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used by input analysis functions for loop finding, trimming, etc.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A brep fit through input on success, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreatePatch_2.htm)

#### `public static Brep CreatePatch(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, Surface startingSurface, double tolerance)`

Constructs a brep patch. This is the simple version of fit that uses a specified starting surface.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — Combination of Curves, BrepTrims, Points, PointClouds or Meshes. Curves and trims are sampled to get points. Trims are sampled for points and normals.
- `startingSurface` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — A starting surface (can be null).
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used by input analysis functions for loop finding, trimming, etc.

**Returns:** `Brep` — Brep fit through input on success, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreatePatch.htm)

#### `public static Brep CreatePatch(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, Surface startingSurface, int uSpans, int vSpans, bool trim, bool tangency, double pointSpacing, double flexibility, double surfacePull, bool[] fixEdges, double tolerance)`

Constructs a brep patch using all controls

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — A combination of curves, brep trims, points, point clouds or meshes. Curves and trims are sampled to get points. Trims are sampled for points and normals.
- `startingSurface` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — A starting surface (can be null).
- `uSpans` (System.Int32) — Number of surface spans used when a plane is fit through points to start in the U direction.
- `vSpans` (System.Int32) — Number of surface spans used when a plane is fit through points to start in the U direction.
- `trim` (System.Boolean) — If true, try to find an outer loop from among the input curves and trim the result to that loop
- `tangency` (System.Boolean) — If true, try to find brep trims in the outer loop of curves and try to fit to the normal direction of the trim's surface at those locations.
- `pointSpacing` (System.Double) — Basic distance between points sampled from input curves.
- `flexibility` (System.Double) — Determines the behavior of the surface in areas where its not otherwise controlled by the input. Lower numbers make the surface behave more like a stiff material; higher, less like a stiff material. That is, each span is made to more closely match the spans adjacent to it if there is no input geometry mapping to that area of the surface when the flexibility value is low. The scale is logarithmic. Numbers around 0.001 or 0.1 make the patch pretty stiff and numbers around 10 or 100 make the surface flexible.
- `surfacePull` (System.Double) — Tends to keep the result surface where it was before the fit in areas where there is on influence from the input geometry
- `fixEdges` (System.Boolean[]) — Array of four elements. Flags to keep the edges of a starting (untrimmed) surface in place while fitting the interior of the surface. Order of flags is left, bottom, right, top
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used by input analysis functions for loop finding, trimming, etc.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A brep fit through input on success, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreatePatch_1.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreatePipe(Curve rail, double radius, bool localBlending, PipeCapMode cap, bool fitRail, double absoluteTolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Creates a single walled pipe.

**Parameters:**
- `rail` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The rail, or path, curve.
- `radius` (System.Double) — The radius of the pipe.
- `localBlending` (System.Boolean) — The shape blending. If True, Local (pipe radius stays constant at the ends and changes more rapidly in the middle) is applied. If False, Global (radius is linearly blended from one end to the other, creating pipes that taper from one radius to the other) is applied.
- `cap` (Rhino.Geometry.PipeCapMode) — The end cap mode.
- `fitRail` (System.Boolean) — If the curve is a polycurve of lines and arcs, the curve is fit and a single surface is created; otherwise the result is a Brep with joined surfaces created from the polycurve segments.
- `absoluteTolerance` (System.Double) — The sweeping and fitting tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's absolute tolerance.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — The angle tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's angle tolerance in radians.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreatePipe_1.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreatePipe(Curve rail, IEnumerable<double> railRadiiParameters, IEnumerable<double> radii, bool localBlending, PipeCapMode cap, bool fitRail, double absoluteTolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Creates a single walled pipe.

**Parameters:**
- `rail` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The rail, or path, curve.
- `railRadiiParameters` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Double>) — One or more normalized curve parameters where changes in radius occur. Important: curve parameters must be normalized - ranging between 0.0 and 1.0. Use Interval.NormalizedParameterAt to calculate these.
- `radii` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Double>) — One or more radii - one at each normalized curve parameter in railRadiiParameters.
- `localBlending` (System.Boolean) — The shape blending. If True, Local (pipe radius stays constant at the ends and changes more rapidly in the middle) is applied. If False, Global (radius is linearly blended from one end to the other, creating pipes that taper from one radius to the other) is applied.
- `cap` (Rhino.Geometry.PipeCapMode) — The end cap mode.
- `fitRail` (System.Boolean) — If the curve is a polycurve of lines and arcs, the curve is fit and a single surface is created; otherwise the result is a Brep with joined surfaces created from the polycurve segments.
- `absoluteTolerance` (System.Double) — The sweeping and fitting tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's absolute tolerance.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — The angle tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's angle tolerance in radians.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreatePipe.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateShell(Brep brep, IEnumerable<int> facesToRemove, double distance, double tolerance)`

Creates a hollowed out shell from a solid Brep. Function only operates on simple, solid, manifold Breps.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The solid Brep to shell.
- `facesToRemove` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Int32>) — The indices of the Brep faces to remove. These surfaces are removed and the remainder is offset inward, using the outer parts of the removed surfaces to join the inner and outer parts.
- `distance` (System.Double) — The distance, or thickness, for the shell. This is a signed distance value with respect to face normals and flipped faces.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The offset tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's absolute tolerance.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — An array of Brep results or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateShell.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateSolid(IEnumerable<Brep> breps, double tolerance)`

Constructs closed polysurfaces from surfaces and polysurfaces that bound a region in space.

**Parameters:**
- `breps` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Brep>) — The intersecting surfaces and polysurfaces to automatically trim and join into closed polysurfaces.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The trim and join tolerance. If set to RhinoMath.UnsetValue, Rhino's global absolute tolerance is used.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — The resulting polysurfaces on success or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateSolid.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateThickPipe(Curve rail, double radius0, double radius1, bool localBlending, PipeCapMode cap, bool fitRail, double absoluteTolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Creates a double-walled pipe.

**Parameters:**
- `rail` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The rail, or path, curve.
- `radius0` (System.Double) — The first radius of the pipe.
- `radius1` (System.Double) — The second radius of the pipe.
- `localBlending` (System.Boolean) — The shape blending. If True, Local (pipe radius stays constant at the ends and changes more rapidly in the middle) is applied. If False, Global (radius is linearly blended from one end to the other, creating pipes that taper from one radius to the other) is applied.
- `cap` (Rhino.Geometry.PipeCapMode) — The end cap mode.
- `fitRail` (System.Boolean) — If the curve is a polycurve of lines and arcs, the curve is fit and a single surface is created; otherwise the result is a Brep with joined surfaces created from the polycurve segments.
- `absoluteTolerance` (System.Double) — The sweeping and fitting tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's absolute tolerance.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — The angle tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's angle tolerance in radians.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateThickPipe_1.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] CreateThickPipe(Curve rail, IEnumerable<double> railRadiiParameters, IEnumerable<double> radii0, IEnumerable<double> radii1, bool localBlending, PipeCapMode cap, bool fitRail, double absoluteTolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Creates a double-walled pipe.

**Parameters:**
- `rail` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The rail, or path, curve.
- `railRadiiParameters` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Double>) — One or more normalized curve parameters where changes in radius occur. Important: curve parameters must be normalized - ranging between 0.0 and 1.0. Use Interval.NormalizedParameterAt to calculate these.
- `radii0` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Double>) — One or more radii for the first wall - one at each normalized curve parameter in railRadiiParameters.
- `radii1` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Double>) — One or more radii for the second wall - one at each normalized curve parameter in railRadiiParameters.
- `localBlending` (System.Boolean) — The shape blending. If True, Local (pipe radius stays constant at the ends and changes more rapidly in the middle) is applied. If False, Global (radius is linearly blended from one end to the other, creating pipes that taper from one radius to the other) is applied.
- `cap` (Rhino.Geometry.PipeCapMode) — The end cap mode.
- `fitRail` (System.Boolean) — If the curve is a polycurve of lines and arcs, the curve is fit and a single surface is created; otherwise the result is a Brep with joined surfaces created from the polycurve segments.
- `absoluteTolerance` (System.Double) — The sweeping and fitting tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's absolute tolerance.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — The angle tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's angle tolerance in radians.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — Array of Breps success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_CreateThickPipe.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

**Returns:** `Brep` — A brep, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_DuplicateSubBrep.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

#### `public bool GetPointInside(double tolerance, out Point3d point)`

Finds a point inside of a solid Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Used for intersecting rays and is not necessarily related to the distance from the brep to the found point. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point inside the solid Brep.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Returns false if the input is not solid and manifold, if the Brep's bounding box is less than 2.0 * tolerance wide, or if no point could be found due to ray shooting or other errors. Otherwise, true is returned.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_GetPointInside.htm)

#### `public bool IsPointInside(Point3d point, double tolerance, bool strictlyIn)`

Determines if point is inside a Brep. This question only makes sense when the brep is a closed and manifold. This function does not check for closed or manifold, so result is not valid in those cases. Intersects a line through point with brep, finds the intersection point Q closest to point, and looks at face normal at Q. If the point Q is on an edge or the intersection is not transverse at Q, then another line is used.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — 3d point to test.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — 3d distance tolerance used for intersection and determining strict inclusion. A good default is RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon.
- `strictlyIn` (System.Boolean) — if true, point is in if inside brep by at least tolerance. if false, point is in if truly in or within tolerance of boundary.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if point is in, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_IsPointInside.htm)

#### `public bool IsValidGeometry(out string log)`

Expert user function that tests the brep to see if its geometry information is valid. The value of brep.IsValidTopology() must be true before brep.IsValidGeometry() can be safely called.

**Parameters:**
- `log` (System.String) — If the brep geometry is not valid, then a brief description of the problem in English is assigned to this out parameter. The information is suitable for low-level debugging purposes by programmers and is not intended to be useful as a high level user interface tool. Otherwise, Empty.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — A value that indicates whether the geometry is valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_IsValidGeometry.htm)

#### `public bool IsValidTolerancesAndFlags(out string log)`

Expert user function that tests the brep to see if its tolerances and flags are valid. The values of brep.IsValidTopology() and brep.IsValidGeometry() must be true before brep.IsValidTolerancesAndFlags() can be safely called.

**Parameters:**
- `log` (System.String) — If the brep tolerance or flags are not valid, then a brief description of the problem in English is assigned to this out parameter. The information is suitable for low-level debugging purposes by programmers and is not intended to be useful as a high level user interface tool. Otherwise, Empty.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — A value that indicates

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_IsValidTolerancesAndFlags.htm)

- `log` (System.String) — If the brep topology is not valid, then a brief English description of the problem is appended to the log. The information appended to log is suitable for low-level debugging purposes by programmers and is not intended to be useful as a high level user interface tool.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the topology is valid; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_IsValidTopology.htm)

#### `public bool Join(Brep otherBrep, double tolerance, bool compact)`

If any edges of this brep overlap edges of otherBrep, merge a copy of otherBrep into this brep joining all edges that overlap within tolerance.

**Remarks:** if no edges overlap, this brep is unchanged. otherBrep is copied if it is merged with this, and otherBrep is always unchanged Use this to join a list of breps in a series. When joining multiple breps in series, compact should be set to false. Call compact on the last Join.

**Parameters:**
- `otherBrep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — Brep to be added to this brep.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — 3d distance tolerance for detecting overlapping edges.
- `compact` (System.Boolean) — if true, set brep flags and tolerances, remove unused faces and edges.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if any edges were joined.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Join.htm)

#### `public static Brep[] JoinBreps(IEnumerable<Brep> brepsToJoin, double tolerance)`

Joins the breps in the input array at any overlapping edges to form as few as possible resulting breps. There may be more than one brep in the result array.

**Parameters:**
- `brepsToJoin` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Brep>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of breps to join.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — 3d distance tolerance for detecting overlapping edges.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — new joined breps on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_JoinBreps.htm)

#### `public bool JoinEdges(int edgeIndex0, int edgeIndex1, double joinTolerance, bool compact)`

Joins two naked edges, or edges that are coincident or close together.

**Parameters:**
- `edgeIndex0` (System.Int32) — The first edge index.
- `edgeIndex1` (System.Int32) — The second edge index.
- `joinTolerance` (System.Double) — The join tolerance.
- `compact` (System.Boolean) — If joining more than one edge pair and want the edge indices of subsequent pairs to remain valid, set to false. But then call Brep.Compact() on the final result.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_JoinEdges.htm)

#### `public int JoinNakedEdges(double tolerance)`

Joins naked edge pairs within the same brep that overlap within tolerance.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance value.

**Returns:** `Int32` — number of joins made.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_JoinNakedEdges.htm)

#### `public bool MakeDeformable()`

If possible, converts the object into a form that can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, an non-uniform scaling.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — false if object cannot be converted to a deformable object. true if object was already deformable or was converted into a deformable object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_MakeDeformable.htm)

No support is available for this function. Expert user function that converts all geometry in Brep to NURB form.

**Remarks:** Don't call this function unless you know exactly what you are doing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Brep.MakeValidForV2"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_MakeValidForV2.htm)

#### `public uint MemoryEstimate()`

Computes an estimate of the number of bytes that this object is using in memory.

**Returns:** `UInt32` — An estimated memory footprint.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_MemoryEstimate.htm)

#### `public static Brep MergeBreps(IEnumerable<Brep> brepsToMerge, double tolerance)`

Combines two or more breps into one. A merge is like a boolean union that keeps the inside pieces. This function creates non-manifold Breps which in general are unusual in Rhino. You may want to consider using JoinBreps or CreateBooleanUnion functions instead.

**Parameters:**
- `brepsToMerge` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Brep>) — must contain more than one Brep.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — the tolerance to use when merging.

**Returns:** `Brep` — Single merged Brep on success. Null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_MergeBreps.htm)

#### `public bool MergeCoplanarFaces(double tolerance)`

Merges adjacent coplanar faces into single faces.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for determining when edges are adjacent. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAbsoluteTolerance property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if faces were merged, false if no faces were merged.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_MergeCoplanarFaces.htm)

#### `public bool MergeCoplanarFaces(double tolerance, double angleTolerance)`

Merges adjacent coplanar faces into single faces.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for determining when edges are adjacent. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAbsoluteTolerance property.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — Angle tolerance, in radians, for determining when faces are parallel. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAngleToleranceRadians property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if faces were merged, false if no faces were merged.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_MergeCoplanarFaces_1.htm)

#### `public bool MergeCoplanarFaces(int faceIndex, double tolerance, double angleTolerance)`

Merges coplanar faces adjacent to Brep face into a single face.

**Parameters:**
- `faceIndex` (System.Int32) — The index of the Brep face to search.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for determining when edges are adjacent. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAbsoluteTolerance property.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — Angle tolerance, in radians, for determining when faces are parallel. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAngleToleranceRadians property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if faces were merged, false if no faces were merged.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_MergeCoplanarFaces_2.htm)

#### `public bool MergeCoplanarFaces(int faceIndex0, int faceIndex1, double tolerance, double angleTolerance)`

Merges two coplanar, adjacent Brep faces into a single face.

**Parameters:**
- `faceIndex0` (System.Int32) — The index of the first Brep face.
- `faceIndex1` (System.Int32) — The index of the second Brep face.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for determining when edges are adjacent. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAbsoluteTolerance property.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — Angle tolerance, in radians, for determining when faces are parallel. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAngleToleranceRadians property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if faces were merged, false if no faces were merged.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_MergeCoplanarFaces_3.htm)

#### `public static Brep MergeSurfaces(Brep brep0, Brep brep1, double tolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Merges two surfaces into one surface at untrimmed edges. Both surfaces must be untrimmed and share an edge.

**Parameters:**
- `brep0` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The first single-face Brep to merge.
- `brep1` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The second single-face Brep to merge.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Surface edges must be within this tolerance for the two surfaces to merge.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Edge must be within this angle tolerance in order for contiguous edges to be combined into a single edge.

**Returns:** `Brep` — The merged Brep if successful, null if not successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_MergeSurfaces.htm)

#### `public static Brep MergeSurfaces(Brep brep0, Brep brep1, double tolerance, double angleToleranceRadians, Point2d point0, Point2d point1, double roundness, bool smooth)`

Merges two surfaces into one surface at untrimmed edges. Both surfaces must be untrimmed and share an edge.

**Parameters:**
- `brep0` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The first single-face Brep to merge.
- `brep1` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The second single-face Brep to merge.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Surface edges must be within this tolerance for the two surfaces to merge.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Edge must be within this angle tolerance in order for contiguous edges to be combined into a single edge.
- `point0` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — 2D pick point on the first single-face Brep. The value can be unset.
- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — 2D pick point on the second single-face Brep. The value can be unset.
- `roundness` (System.Double) — Defines the roundness of the merge. Acceptable values are between 0.0 (sharp) and 1.0 (smooth).
- `smooth` (System.Boolean) — The surface will be smooth. This makes the surface behave better for control point editing, but may alter the shape of both surfaces.

**Returns:** `Brep` — The merged Brep if successful, null if not successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_MergeSurfaces_1.htm)

#### `public static Brep MergeSurfaces(Surface surface0, Surface surface1, double tolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Merges two surfaces into one surface at untrimmed edges.

**Parameters:**
- `surface0` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — The first surface to merge.
- `surface1` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — The second surface to merge.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Surface edges must be within this tolerance for the two surfaces to merge.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Edge must be within this angle tolerance in order for contiguous edges to be combined into a single edge.

**Returns:** `Brep` — The merged surfaces as a Brep if successful, null if not successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_MergeSurfaces_2.htm)

#### `public void RebuildTrimsForV2(BrepFace face, NurbsSurface nurbsSurface)`

No support is available for this function. Expert user function used by MakeValidForV2 to convert trim curves from one surface to its NURBS form. After calling this function, you need to change the surface of the face to a NurbsSurface.

**Remarks:** Don't call this function unless you know exactly what you are doing.

**Parameters:**
- `face` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — Face whose underlying surface has a parameterization that is different from its NURBS form.

Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of the axis of rotation.
- `rotationCenter` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully rotated.

#### `public void SetTolerancesBoxesAndFlags(bool bLazy, bool bSetVertexTolerances, bool bSetEdgeTolerances, bool bSetTrimTolerances, bool bSetTrimIsoFlags, bool bSetTrimTypeFlags, bool bSetLoopTypeFlags, bool bSetTrimBoxes)`

Set tolerances and flags in a Brep.

- `bSetTrimBoxes` (System.Boolean) — Set true to compute BrepTrim bounding boxes.

Splits a Brep into pieces using a Brep as a cutter.

- `intersectionTolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance with which to compute intersections.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Split.htm)

#### `public Brep[] Split(Brep cutter, double intersectionTolerance, out bool toleranceWasRaised)`

Splits a Brep into pieces using a Brep as a cutter.

**Parameters:**
- `cutter` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — The Brep to use as a cutter.
- `intersectionTolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance with which to compute intersections.
- `toleranceWasRaised` (System.Boolean) — Set to true if the split failed at intersectionTolerance but succeeded when the tolerance was increased to twice intersectionTolerance.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — A new array of Breps. This array can be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Split_1.htm)

#### `public Brep[] Split(IEnumerable<Brep> cutters, double intersectionTolerance)`

Splits a Brep into pieces using Breps as cutters.

**Parameters:**
- `cutters` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Brep>) — One or more Breps to use as cutters.
- `intersectionTolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance with which to compute intersections.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — A new array of Breps. This array can be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Split_2.htm)

#### `public Brep[] Split(IEnumerable<Curve> cutters, double intersectionTolerance)`

Splits a Brep into pieces using curves, at least partially on the Brep, as cutters.

**Parameters:**
- `cutters` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — The splitting curves. Only the portion of the curve on the Brep surface will be used for cutting.
- `intersectionTolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance with which to compute intersections.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — A new array of Breps. This array can be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Split_3.htm)

#### `public Brep[] Split(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> cutters, Vector3d normal, bool planView, double intersectionTolerance)`

Splits a Brep into pieces using a combination of curves, to be extruded, and Breps as cutters.

**Remarks:** A Curve in cutters is extruded to produce a surface to use as a cutter. The extrusion direction is chosen, as in the Rhino Split command, based on the properties of the active view. In particular the construction plane Normal and whether the active view is a plan view, a parallel projection with construction plane normal as the view direction. If planView is false and the curve is planar then the curve is extruded perpendicular to the curve, otherwise the curve is extruded in the normal direction.

**Parameters:**
- `cutters` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — The curves, surfaces, faces and Breps to be used as cutters. Any other geometry is ignored.
- `normal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A construction plane normal, used in deciding how to extrude a curve into a cutter.
- `planView` (System.Boolean) — Set true if the assume view is a plan, or parallel projection, view.
- `intersectionTolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance with which to compute intersections.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — A new array of Breps. This array can be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Split_4.htm)

Standardizes all trims, edges, and faces in the brep. After standardizing, there may be unused curves and surfaces in the brep. Call Brep.Compact to remove these unused curves and surfaces.

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

#### `public bool TransformComponent(IEnumerable<ComponentIndex> components, Transform xform, double tolerance, double timeLimit, bool useMultipleThreads)`

Transform an array of Brep components, bend neighbors to match, and leave the rest fixed.

**Parameters:**
- `components` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<ComponentIndex>) — The Brep components to transform.
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — The transformation to apply.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The desired fitting tolerance to use when bending faces that share edges with both fixed and transformed components.
- `timeLimit` (System.Double) — If the deformation is extreme, it can take a long time to calculate the result. If time_limit > 0, then the value specifies the maximum amount of time in seconds you want to spend before giving up.
- `useMultipleThreads` (System.Boolean) — True if multiple threads can be used.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_TransformComponent.htm)

- `z` (System.Double) — The Z component.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully translated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Translate_1.htm)

#### `public Brep[] Trim(Brep cutter, double intersectionTolerance)`

Trims a brep with an oriented cutter. The parts of the brep that lie inside (opposite the normal) of the cutter are retained while the parts to the outside (in the direction of the normal) are discarded. If the Cutter is closed, then a connected component of the Brep that does not intersect the cutter is kept if and only if it is contained in the inside of cutter. That is the region bounded by cutter opposite from the normal of cutter, If cutter is not closed all these components are kept.

**Parameters:**
- `cutter` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — A cutting brep.
- `intersectionTolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value with which to compute intersections.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — This Brep is not modified, the trim results are returned in an array.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Trim.htm)

#### `public Brep[] Trim(Plane cutter, double intersectionTolerance)`

Trims a Brep with an oriented cutter. The parts of Brep that lie inside (opposite the normal) of the cutter are retained while the parts to the outside ( in the direction of the normal ) are discarded. A connected component of Brep that does not intersect the cutter is kept if and only if it is contained in the inside of Cutter. That is the region bounded by cutter opposite from the normal of cutter, or in the case of a Plane cutter the half space opposite from the plane normal.

**Parameters:**
- `cutter` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — A cutting plane.
- `intersectionTolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value with which to compute intersections.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — This Brep is not modified, the trim results are returned in an array.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_Trim_1.htm)

#### `public static Brep TryConvertBrep(GeometryBase geometry)`

Attempts to convert a generic Geometry object into a Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase) — Geometry to convert, not all types of GeometryBase can be represented by BReps.

**Returns:** `Brep` — Brep if a brep form could be created or null if this is not possible. If geometry was of type Brep to begin with, the same object is returned, i.e. it is not duplicated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_TryConvertBrep.htm)

#### `public Brep[] UnjoinEdges(IEnumerable<int> edgesToUnjoin)`

Un-joins, or separates, edges within the Brep. Note, seams in closed surfaces will not separate.

**Parameters:**
- `edgesToUnjoin` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Int32>) — The indices of the Brep edges to un-join.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — This Brep is not modified, the trim results are returned in an array.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Brep_UnjoinEdges.htm)

### Properties
- `Curves2D` (BrepCurveList, get) — Parameter space trimming curves (used by trims)
- `Curves3D` (BrepCurveList, get) — Pointers to 3d curves (used by edges)
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `Edges` (BrepEdgeList, get) — Gets the brep edges list accessor.
- `Faces` (BrepFaceList, get) — Gets the brep faces list accessor.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasRegionTopology` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep has region topology information, false otherwise.
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsManifold` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the Brep is manifold. Non-Manifold breps have at least one edge that is shared among three or more faces.
- `IsSolid` (Boolean, get) — Determines whether this brep is a solid, or a closed oriented manifold.
- `IsSurface` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep has a single face and that face is geometrically the same as the underlying surface. I.e., the face has trivial trimming. In this case, the surface is the first face surface. The flag Brep.Faces[0].OrientationIsReversed records the correspondence between the surface's natural parametric orientation and the orientation of the Brep.trivial trimming here means that there is only one loop curve in the brep and that loop curve is the same as the underlying surface boundary.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `Loops` (BrepLoopList, get) — Gets the brep loop list accessor.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `SolidOrientation` (BrepSolidOrientation, get) — Gets the solid orientation state of this Brep.
- `Surfaces` (BrepSurfaceList, get) — Parametric surfaces used by faces
- `Trims` (BrepTrimList, get) — Gets the brep trims list accessor.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.
- `Vertices` (BrepVertexList, get) — Gets the brep vertices list accessor.

## BrepEdge (class)

Represents a single edge curve in a Brep object.
