Test a curve for planarity and return the plane.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — On success, the plane parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if there is a plane such that the maximum distance from the curve to the plane is <= tolerance.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TryGetPlane_1.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetPolyline(out Polyline polyline)`

Several types of Curve can have the form of a polyline including a degree 1 NurbsCurve, a PolylineCurve, and a PolyCurve all of whose segments are some form of polyline. IsPolyline tests a curve to see if it can be represented as a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline) — If true is returned, then the polyline form is returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this curve can be represented as a polyline; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TryGetPolyline.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetPolyline(out Polyline polyline, out double[] parameters)`

Several types of Curve can have the form of a polyline including a degree 1 NurbsCurve, a PolylineCurve, and a PolyCurve all of whose segments are some form of polyline. IsPolyline tests a curve to see if it can be represented as a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline) — If true is returned, then the polyline form is returned here.
- `parameters` (System.Double[]) — if true is returned, then the parameters of the polyline points are returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this curve can be represented as a polyline; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TryGetPolyline_1.htm)

### Properties
- `Brep` (Brep, get) — Gets the Brep that owns this edge.
- `Degree` (Int32, get) — Gets the maximum algebraic degree of any span or a good estimate if curve spans are not algebraic.
- `Dimension` (Int32, get) — Gets the dimension of the object. The dimension is typically three. For parameter space trimming curves the dimension is two. In rare cases the dimension can be one or greater than three.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `Domain` (Interval, get/set) — Gets or sets the domain of the curve.
- `EdgeCurve` (Curve, get) — Get the brep.Curves3D[] 3d curve geometry used by this edge or null.
- `EdgeCurveIndex` (Int32, get) — Get the brep.Curves3D[] index of the 3d curve geometry used by this edge or -1.
- `EdgeIndex` (Int32, get) — Gets the index of this edge in the Brep.Edges collection.
- `EndVertex` (BrepVertex, get) — BrepVertex at end of edge
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsClosed` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is a closed curve.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsPeriodic` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is considered to be Periodic.
- `IsSubDFriendly` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the curve is a cubic, non-rational, uniform NURBS curve that is either periodic or has natural end conditions. Othewise, false is returned.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `PointAtEnd` (Point3d, get) — Evaluates point at the end of the curve.
- `PointAtStart` (Point3d, get) — Evaluates point at the start of the curve.
- `ProxyBrepSubDEdgeId` (UInt32, get) — If this edge is part of a brep created by SubD.ProxyBrep(), then the subd component id of the corresponding SubD edge is returned. Otherwise 0 is returned.
- `ProxyCurveIsReversed` (Boolean, get) — True if "this" is a curve is reversed from the "real" curve geometry
- `SpanCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of non-empty smooth (c-infinity) spans in the curve.
- `StartVertex` (BrepVertex, get) — BrepVertex at start of edge
- `TangentAtEnd` (Vector3d, get) — Evaluate unit tangent vector at the end of the curve.
- `TangentAtStart` (Vector3d, get) — Evaluates the unit tangent vector at the start of the curve.
- `Tolerance` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the accuracy of the edge curve (>=0.0 or RhinoMath.UnsetValue) A value of UnsetValue indicates that the tolerance should be computed. The maximum distance from the edge's 3d curve to any surface of a face that has this edge as a portion of its boundary must be <= this tolerance.
- `TrimCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of trim-curves that use this edge.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.
- `Valence` (EdgeAdjacency, get) — Gets the topological valency of this edge. The topological valency is defined by how many adjacent faces share this edge.

## BrepFace (class)

Provides strongly-typed access to brep faces. A Brep face is composed of one surface and trimming curves.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace.htm)

### Methods
#### `public int[] AdjacentEdges()`

Gets the indices of all the BrepEdges that delineate this Face.

**Returns:** `Int32[]` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace.AdjacentEdges"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_AdjacentEdges.htm)

#### `public int[] AdjacentFaces()`

Gets the indices of all the BrepFaces that surround (are adjacent to) this face.

**Returns:** `Int32[]` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace.AdjacentFaces"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_AdjacentFaces.htm)

#### `public bool ChangeSurface(int surfaceIndex)`

Expert user tool that replaces the 3d surface geometry use by the face.

**Remarks:** If the face had a surface and new surface has a different shape, then you probably want to call something like RebuildEdges() to move the 3d edge curves so they will lie on the new surface. This doesn't delete the old surface; call Brep.CullUnusedSurfaces() or Brep.Compact() to remove unused surfaces.

**Parameters:**
- `surfaceIndex` (System.Int32) — brep surface index of new surface.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_ChangeSurface.htm)

#### `public void ClearMaterialChannelIndex()`

Clear this face's rendering material channel index.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_ClearMaterialChannelIndex.htm)

Sets BrepFace.PackId to 0.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_ClearPackId.htm)

- `v` (System.Double) — V parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

Extrude a face in a Brep.

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public Brep DuplicateFace(bool duplicateMeshes)`

Duplicate a face from the brep to create new single face brep.

**Parameters:**
- `duplicateMeshes` (System.Boolean) — If true, shading meshes will be copied as well.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A new single-face brep synonymous with the current Face.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_DuplicateFace.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public Surface DuplicateSurface()`

Gets a copy to the untrimmed surface that this face is based on.

**Returns:** `Surface` — A copy of this face's underlying surface.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_DuplicateSurface.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `public bool Evaluate(double u, double v, int numberDerivatives, out Point3d point, out Vector3d[] derivatives)`

Evaluates a surface mathematically.

**Remarks:** The partial derivatives will be in the order {Su, Sv} for numberDerivatives = 1, {Su, Sv, Suu, Suv, Svv} for 2, {Su, Sv, Suu, Suv, Svv, Suuu, Suuv, Suvv, Svvv} for 3, and similar for higher counts.

**Parameters:**
- `u` (System.Double) — A U parameter.
- `v` (System.Double) — A V parameter.
- `numberDerivatives` (System.Int32) — The number of derivatives.
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point. This out parameter will be assigned during this call.
- `derivatives` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d[]) — A vector array. This out parameter will be assigned during this call. This can be null.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the operation succeeded; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Evaluate.htm)

#### `public bool Extend(int direction, Interval interval)`

Analytically extends the surface to include the interval.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — If 0, Surface.Domain(0) will include the interval. (the first surface parameter). If 1, Surface.Domain(1) will include the interval. (the second surface parameter).
- `interval` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — If the interval is not included in surface domain, the surface will be extended so that its domain includes the interval. Note, this method will fail if the surface is closed in the specified direction.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Extend_1.htm)

#### `public Surface Extend(IsoStatus edge, double extensionLength, bool smooth)`

Extends an untrimmed surface along one edge.

**Parameters:**
- `edge` (Rhino.Geometry.IsoStatus) — Edge to extend. Must be North, South, East, or West.
- `extensionLength` (System.Double) — distance to extend.
- `smooth` (System.Boolean) — true for smooth (C-infinity) extension. false for a C1- ruled extension.

**Returns:** `Surface` — New extended surface on success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Extend.htm)

Fits a new surface through an existing surface.

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

#### `public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(int direction, Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, out double t)`

Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — If 0, then "u" parameter is checked. If 1, then the "v" parameter is checked.
- `continuityType` (Rhino.Geometry.Continuity) — The desired continuity.
- `t0` (System.Double) — Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity and step through the discontinuities.
- `t1` (System.Double) — (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 is will be ignored unless c is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.
- `t` (System.Double) — if a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): TRUE if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): TRUE if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetNextDiscontinuity_1.htm)

#### `public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(int direction, Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, double cosAngleTolerance, double curvatureTolerance, out double t)`

Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — If 0, then "u" parameter is checked. If 1, then the "v" parameter is checked.
- `continuityType` (Rhino.Geometry.Continuity) — The desired continuity.
- `t0` (System.Double) — Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity and step through the discontinuities.
- `t1` (System.Double) — (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 is will be ignored unless c is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.
- `cosAngleTolerance` (System.Double) — default = cos(1 degree) Used only when continuityType is G1_continuous or G2_continuous. If the cosine of the angle between two tangent vectors is <= cos_angle_tolerance, then a G1 discontinuity is reported.
- `curvatureTolerance` (System.Double) — (default = ON_SQRT_EPSILON) Used only when continuityType is G2_continuous. If K0 and K1 are curvatures evaluated from above and below and |K0 - K1| > curvature_tolerance, then a curvature discontinuity is reported.
- `t` (System.Double) — if a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): TRUE if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): TRUE if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetNextDiscontinuity.htm)

- `nurbsT` (System.Double) — T on the NURBS form.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetNurbsFormParameterFromSurfaceParameter.htm)

#### `public int HasNurbsForm()`

Is there a NURBS surface representation of this surface.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 unable to create NURBS representation with desired accuracy. 1 success - NURBS parameterization matches the surface's 2 success - NURBS point locus matches the surface's and the domain of the NURBS surface is correct. However, This surface's parameterization and the NURBS surface parameterization may not match. This situation happens when getting NURBS representations of surfaces that have a transcendental parameterization like spheres, cylinders, and cones.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_HasNurbsForm.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurface(IEnumerable<Point3d> points, double tolerance)`

Constructs an interpolated curve on a surface, using 3D points.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of points.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A new NURBS curve, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_InterpolatedCurveOnSurface.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV(IEnumerable<Point2d> points, double tolerance)`

Returns a curve that interpolates points on a surface. The interpolant lies on the surface.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — List of at least two UV parameter locations on the surface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used for the fit of the push-up curve. Generally, the resulting interpolating curve will be within tolerance of the surface.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A new NURBS curve if successful, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV(IEnumerable<Point2d> points, double tolerance, bool closed, int closedSurfaceHandling)`

Returns a curve that interpolates points on a surface. The interpolant lies on the surface.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — List of at least two UV parameter locations on the surface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used for the fit of the push-up curve. Generally, the resulting interpolating curve will be within tolerance of the surface.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — If false, the interpolating curve is not closed. If true, the interpolating curve is closed, and the last point and first point should generally not be equal.
- `closedSurfaceHandling` (System.Int32) — If 0, all points must be in the rectangular domain of the surface. If the surface is closed in some direction, then this routine will interpret each point and place it at an appropriate location in the covering space. This is the simplest option and should give good results. If 1, then more options for more control of handling curves going across seams are available. If the surface is closed in some direction, then the points are taken as points in the covering space. Example, if srf.IsClosed(0)=true and srf.IsClosed(1)=false and srf.Domain(0)=srf.Domain(1)=Interval(0,1) then if closedSurfaceHandling=1 a point(u, v) in points can have any value for the u coordinate, but must have 0<=v<=1. In particular, if points = { (0.0,0.5), (2.0,0.5) } then the interpolating curve will wrap around the surface two times in the closed direction before ending at start of the curve. If closed=true the last point should equal the first point plus an integer multiple of the period on a closed direction.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A new NURBS curve if successful, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV_1.htm)

#### `public int IsAtSeam(double u, double v)`

Tests if a surface parameter value is at a seam.

**Parameters:**
- `u` (System.Double) — Surface u parameter to test.
- `v` (System.Double) — Surface v parameter to test.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if not a seam, 1 if u == Domain(0)[i] and srf(u, v) == srf(Domain(0)[1-i], v) 2 if v == Domain(1)[i] and srf(u, v) == srf(u, Domain(1)[1-i]) 3 if 1 and 2 are true.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_IsAtSeam.htm)

Tests if a surface parameter value is at a singularity.

- `u` (System.Double) — Surface u parameter to test.
- `v` (System.Double) — Surface v parameter to test.
- `exact` (System.Boolean) — If true, test if (u,v) is exactly at a singularity. If false, test if close enough to cause numerical problems.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if surface is singular at (s,t)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_IsAtSingularity.htm)

#### `public Curve IsoCurve(int direction, double constantParameter)`

Gets isoparametric curve.

**Remarks:** In this function "direction" indicates which direction the resulting curve runs. 0: horizontal, 1: vertical In the other Surface functions that take a "direction" argument, "direction" indicates if "constantParameter" is a "u" or "v" parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 first parameter varies and second parameter is constant e.g., point on IsoCurve(0,c) at t is srf(t,c) This is a horizontal line from left to right 1 first parameter is constant and second parameter varies e.g., point on IsoCurve(1,c) at t is srf(c,t This is a vertical line from bottom to top.
- `constantParameter` (System.Double) — The parameter that was constant on the original surface.

**Returns:** `Curve` — An isoparametric curve or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_IsoCurve.htm)

#### `public bool LocalClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, double seedU, double seedV, out double u, out double v)`

Find parameters of the point on a surface that is locally closest to the testPoint. The search for a local close point starts at seed parameters.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point to test against.
- `seedU` (System.Double) — The seed parameter in the U direction.
- `seedV` (System.Double) — The seed parameter in the V direction.
- `u` (System.Double) — U parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.
- `v` (System.Double) — V parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the search is successful, false if the search fails.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_LocalClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public bool MakeDeformable()`

If possible, converts the object into a form that can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, an non-uniform scaling.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — false if object cannot be converted to a deformable object. true if object was already deformable or was converted into a deformable object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_MakeDeformable.htm)

Pulls a 3d curve back to the surface's parameter space.

- `curve3d` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The curve to pull.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — the maximum acceptable 3d distance between from surface(curve_2d(t)) to the locus of points on the surface that are closest to curve_3d.

Pulls a 3d curve back to the surface's parameter space.

- `curve3d` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — A curve.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — the maximum acceptable 3d distance between from surface(curve_2d(t)) to the locus of points on the surface that are closest to curve_3d.

#### `public Curve Pushup(Curve curve2d, double tolerance)`

Computes a 3d curve that is the composite of a 2d curve and the surface map.

**Parameters:**
- `curve2d` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — a 2d curve whose image is in the surface's domain.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — the maximum acceptable distance from the returned 3d curve to the image of curve_2d on the surface.

**Returns:** `Curve` — 3d curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Pushup.htm)

#### `public Curve Pushup(Curve curve2d, double tolerance, Interval curve2dSubdomain)`

Computes a 3d curve that is the composite of a 2d curve and the surface map.

**Parameters:**
- `curve2d` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — a 2d curve whose image is in the surface's domain.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — the maximum acceptable distance from the returned 3d curve to the image of curve_2d on the surface.
- `curve2dSubdomain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The curve interval (a sub-domain of the original curve) to use.

**Returns:** `Curve` — 3d curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Pushup_1.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface Rebuild(int uDegree, int vDegree, int uPointCount, int vPointCount)`

Rebuilds an existing surface to a given degree and point count.

**Parameters:**
- `uDegree` (System.Int32) — the output surface u degree.
- `vDegree` (System.Int32) — the output surface u degree.
- `uPointCount` (System.Int32) — The number of points in the output surface u direction. Must be bigger than uDegree (maximum value is 1000)
- `vPointCount` (System.Int32) — The number of points in the output surface v direction. Must be bigger than vDegree (maximum value is 1000)

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — new rebuilt surface on success. null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Rebuild.htm)

#### `public bool RebuildEdges(double tolerance, bool rebuildSharedEdges, bool rebuildVertices)`

Rebuild the edges used by a face so they lie on the surface.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance for fitting 3d edge curves.
- `rebuildSharedEdges` (System.Boolean) — if false and edge is used by this face and a neighbor, then the edge will be skipped.
- `rebuildVertices` (System.Boolean) — if true, vertex locations are updated to lie on the surface.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_RebuildEdges.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface RebuildOneDirection(int direction, int pointCount, LoftType loftType, double refitTolerance)`

Rebuilds an existing surface with a new surface to a given point count in either the u or v directions independently.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — The direction (0 = U, 1 = V).
- `pointCount` (System.Int32) — The number of points in the output surface in the "direction" direction.
- `loftType` (Rhino.Geometry.LoftType) — The loft type
- `refitTolerance` (System.Double) — The refit tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — new rebuilt surface on success. null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_RebuildOneDirection.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)`

#### `public Curve ShortPath(Point2d start, Point2d end, double tolerance)`

Constructs a geodesic between 2 points, used by ShortPath command in Rhino.

**Parameters:**
- `start` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — start point of curve in parameter space. Points must be distinct in the domain of the surface.
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — end point of curve in parameter space. Points must be distinct in the domain of the surface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance used in fitting discrete solution.

**Returns:** `Curve` — a geodesic curve on the surface on success. null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ShortPath.htm)

#### `public bool ShrinkFace(BrepFace.ShrinkDisableSide disableSide)`

Shrinks the underlying untrimmed surface of this Brep face close to trimming boundaries. Shrinking a surface is like extending smoothly, only backwards. knot of full multiplicity are added where you want the surface to be cut off. Then the remaining control points are thrown away.

**Parameters:**
- `disableSide` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace.ShrinkDisableSide) — The side(s) of the surface to not shrink.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_ShrinkFace.htm)

#### `public bool ShrinkSurfaceToEdge()`

Shrinks the underlying untrimmed surface of this Brep face right to the trimming boundaries. Note, shrinking the trimmed surface can sometimes cause problems later since having the edges so close to the trimming boundaries can cause commands that use the surface edges as input to fail.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_ShrinkSurfaceToEdge.htm)

#### `public Surface Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem)`

Smooths a surface by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the surface edges don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The smoothed surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Smooth.htm)

#### `public Surface Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem, Plane plane)`

Smooths a surface by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the surface edges don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — If SmoothingCoordinateSystem.CPlane specified, then the construction plane.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The smoothed surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Smooth_1.htm)

#### `public Brep Split(IEnumerable<Curve> curves, double tolerance)`

Split this face using 3D trimming curves.

**Parameters:**

- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for splitting, when in doubt use the Document Absolute Tolerance.

#### `public Surface[] Split(int direction, double parameter)`

Splits (divides) the surface into two parts at the specified parameter

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 = The surface is split vertically. The "west" side is returned as the first surface in the array and the "east" side is returned as the second surface in the array. 1 = The surface is split horizontally. The "south" side is returned as the first surface in the array and the "north" side is returned as the second surface in the array
- `parameter` (System.Double) — value of constant parameter in interval returned by Domain(direction)

**Returns:** `Surface[]` — Array of two surfaces on success

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Split.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface ToNurbsSurface()`

Gets a NURBS surface representation of this surface. Default tolerance of 0.0 is used.

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — NurbsSurface on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ToNurbsSurface.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface ToNurbsSurface(double tolerance, out int accuracy)`

Gets a NURBS surface representation of this surface.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when creating NURBS representation.
- `accuracy` (System.Int32) — 0 = unable to create NURBS representation with desired accuracy. 1 = success - returned NURBS parameterization matches the surface's to the desired accuracy. 2 = success - returned NURBS point locus matches the surface's to the desired accuracy and the domain of the NURBS surface is correct. However, this surface's parameterization and the NURBS surface parameterization may not match to the desired accuracy. This situation happens when getting NURBS representations of surfaces that have a transcendental parameterization like spheres, cylinders, and cones.

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — NurbsSurface on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ToNurbsSurface_1.htm)

#### `public Curve[] TrimAwareIsoCurve(int direction, double constantParameter)`

Similar to IsoCurve function, except this function pays attention to trims on faces and may return multiple curves.

**Remarks:** In this function "direction" indicates which direction the resulting curve runs. 0: horizontal, 1: vertical In the other Surface functions that take a "direction" argument, "direction" indicates if "constantParameter" is a "u" or "v" parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — Direction of isocurve. 0 = Isocurve connects all points with a constant U value.1 = Isocurve connects all points with a constant V value.
- `constantParameter` (System.Double) — Surface parameter that remains identical along the isocurves.

**Returns:** `Curve[]` — Isoparametric curves connecting all points with the constantParameter value.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_TrimAwareIsoCurve.htm)

#### `public Interval[] TrimAwareIsoIntervals(int direction, double constantParameter)`

Gets intervals where the iso curve exists on a BrepFace (trimmed surface)

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — Direction of isocurve. 0 = Isocurve connects all points with a constant U value.1 = Isocurve connects all points with a constant V value.
- `constantParameter` (System.Double) — Surface parameter that remains identical along the isocurves.

**Returns:** `Interval[]` — If direction = 0, the parameter space iso interval connects the 2d points (intervals[i][0],iso_constant) and (intervals[i][1],iso_constant). If direction = 1, the parameter space iso interval connects the 2d points (iso_constant,intervals[i][0]) and (iso_constant,intervals[i][1]).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_TrimAwareIsoIntervals.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCone(out Cone cone)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cone within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance and return the cone.

**Parameters:**
- `cone` (Rhino.Geometry.Cone) — On success, the cone parameters are filled in.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCone.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCone(out Cone cone, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cone and returns the cone.

**Parameters:**
- `cone` (Rhino.Geometry.Cone) — On success, the cone parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCone_1.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cylinder within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance and return the cylinder.

**Remarks:** If successful, an infinite cylinder is returned. When a surface has a cylindrical shape, even if its ends are not circles on the cylinder, it returns the axis and radius. You can detect infinite cylinders using Cylinder.IsFinite.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCylinder.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cylinder and return the infinite cylinder.

**Remarks:** If successful, an infinite cylinder is returned. When a surface has a cylindrical shape, even if its ends are not circles on the cylinder, it returns the axis and radius. You can detect infinite cylinders using Cylinder.IsFinite.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCylinder_1.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetFiniteCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface with the assumption that it might be a right circular cylinder and returns this geometry.

**Remarks:** If successful, a finite cylinder is returned.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetFiniteCylinder.htm)

- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — On success, the plane parameters are filled in.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetPlane_1.htm)

Gets the untrimmed surface that is the base of this face.

#### `public Surface VariableOffset(double uMinvMin, double uMinvMax, double uMaxvMin, double uMaxvMax, double tolerance)`

Copies a surface so that all locations at the corners of the copied surface are specified distances from the original surface.

**Parameters:**
- `uMinvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMinvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Max.
- `uMaxvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMaxvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Max.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The offset tolerance.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The offset surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_VariableOffset_1.htm)

#### `public Surface VariableOffset(double uMinvMin, double uMinvMax, double uMaxvMin, double uMaxvMax, IEnumerable<Point2d> interiorParameters, IEnumerable<double> interiorDistances, double tolerance)`

Copies a surface so that all locations at the corners, and from specified interior locations, of the copied surface are specified distances from the original surface.

**Parameters:**
- `uMinvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMinvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Max.
- `uMaxvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMaxvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Max.
- `interiorParameters` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — An array of interior UV parameters to offset from.
- `interiorDistances` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Double>) — >An array of offset distances at the interior UV parameters.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The offset tolerance.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The offset surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_VariableOffset.htm)

### Properties
- `Brep` (Brep, get) — Gets the Brep that owns this face.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `FaceIndex` (Int32, get) — Index of face in Brep.Faces array.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `Id` (Guid, get/set) — Gets or sets the persistent id of this Brep face. By default, the id is Guid.Zero. Note, Rhino does not set this id. Thus, the property is available for use as a way of tracking Brep faces.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsSolid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a values indicating whether a surface is solid.
- `IsSubDFriendly` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the surface is a non-rational, uniform, natural or periodic, cubic NURBS surface. Otherwise, false is returend.
- `IsSurface` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the face is synonymous with the underlying surface. If a Face has no trimming curves then it is considered a Surface.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `Loops` (BrepLoopList, get) — Loops in this face.
- `MaterialChannelIndex` (Int32, get/set) — This face's rendering material channel index.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `OrientationIsReversed` (Boolean, get/set) — true if face orientation is opposite of natural surface orientation.
- `OuterLoop` (BrepLoop, get) — Every face has a single outer loop.
- `PackId` (UInt32, get) — 0 if the pack it is unset, or >0 if the set pack id.
- `PerFaceColor` (Color, get/set) — If per-face color is "Empty", then this face does not have a custom color
- `ProxyBrepSubDFaceId` (UInt32, get) — If this face is part of a brep created by SubD.ProxyBrep(), then the subd component id of the corresponding SubD face is returned. Otherwise 0 is returned.
- `SurfaceIndex` (Int32, get) — Surface index of the 3d surface geometry used by this face or -1
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## BrepFace.ShrinkDisableSide (enum)

Defines bitwise mask flags indicating what side of a surface to not shrink.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BrepFace_ShrinkDisableSide.htm)

### Values
- `ShrinkAllSides` = `0` — Shrink on all four sides.
- `DoNotShrinkWestSide` = `1` — Do not shrink on the west side of domain.
- `DoNotShrinkSouthSide` = `2` — Do not shrink on the south side of domain.
- `DoNotShrinkEastSide` = `4` — Do not shrink on the east side of domain.
- `DoNotShrinkNorthSide` = `8` — Do not shrink on the north side of domain.

## BrepLoop (class)

Represent a single loop in a Brep object. A loop is composed of a list of trim curves.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BrepLoop.htm)

### Methods
#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)`

Overridden in order to destroy local display cache information

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Dispose.htm)

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)`

Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

**Parameters:**

- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of the axis of rotation.
- `rotationCenter` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully rotated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Rotate.htm)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Scale.htm)

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

### Properties
- `Brep` (Brep, get) — Gets the Brep that owns this loop.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `Face` (BrepFace, get) — BrepFace this loop belongs to.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `LoopIndex` (Int32, get) — Gets the index of this loop in the Brep.Loops collection.
- `LoopType` (BrepLoopType, get) — type of loop.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `Trims` (BrepTrimList, get) — List of trims for this loop
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## BrepLoopType (enum)

Each brep loop has a defined type, e.g. outer, inner or point on surface.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BrepLoopType.htm)

### Values
- `Unknown` = `0` — Unknown loop type.
- `Outer` = `1` — 2d loop curves form a simple closed curve with a counterclockwise orientation.
- `Inner` = `2` — 2d loop curves form a simple closed curve with a clockwise orientation.
- `Slit` = `3` — Always closed - used internally during splitting operations.
- `CurveOnSurface` = `4` — "loop" is a curve-on-surface made from a single (open or closed) trim that has type TrimType.CurveOnSurface.
- `PointOnSurface` = `5` — "loop" is a PointOnSurface made from a single trim that has type TrimType.PointOnSurface.

## BrepRegion (class)

Represents a brep topological region that has sides.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BrepRegion.htm)

### Methods
#### `public Brep BoundaryBrep()`

Gets the boundary of a region as a brep object. If the region is finite, the boundary will be a closed manifold brep. The boundary may have more than one connected component.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A brep or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_BrepRegion_BoundaryBrep.htm)

#### `protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose_1.htm)

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

## BrepSolidOrientation (enum)

Enumerates all possible Solid Orientations for a Brep.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BrepSolidOrientation.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — Brep is not a Solid.
- `Inward` = `-1` — Brep is a Solid with inward facing normals.
- `Outward` = `1` — Brep is a Solid with outward facing normals.
- `Unknown` = `2` — Breps is a Solid but no orientation could be computed.

## BrepTrim (class)

Brep trim information is stored in BrepTrim classes. Brep.Trims is an array of all the trims in the brep. A BrepTrim is derived from CurveProxy so the trim can supply easy to use evaluation tools via the Curve virtual member functions. Note well that the domains and orientations of the curve m_C2[trim.m_c2i] and the trim as a curve may not agree.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_BrepTrim.htm)

### Methods
#### `public bool ChangeClosedCurveSeam(double t)`

If this curve is closed, then modify it so that the start/end point is at curve parameter t.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Curve parameter of new start/end point. The returned curves domain will start at t.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ChangeClosedCurveSeam.htm)

#### `public bool ChangeDimension(int desiredDimension)`

Changes the dimension of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `desiredDimension` (System.Int32) — The desired dimension.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the curve's dimension was already desiredDimension or if the curve's dimension was successfully changed to desiredDimension; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ChangeDimension.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation()`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve in the world XY plane. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve with respect to world XY plane.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation(Plane plane)`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve in a given plane. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The plane in which to solve the orientation.

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve in the given plane.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation_1.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation(Transform xform)`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to map the curve to the world XY plane.

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve in the world XY-plane.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation_2.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation(Vector3d upDirection)`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve in a given plane. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Parameters:**
- `upDirection` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector that is considered "up".

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve with respect to a defined up direction.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation_3.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double t)`

Finds parameter of the point on a curve that is closest to testPoint. If the maximumDistance parameter is > 0, then only points whose distance to the given point is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to search from.
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter of local closest point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double t, double maximumDistance)`

Finds the parameter of the point on a curve that is closest to testPoint. If the maximumDistance parameter is > 0, then only points whose distance to the given point is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to project.
- `t` (System.Double) — parameter of local closest point returned here.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — The maximum allowed distance. Past this distance, the search is given up and false is returned.Use 0 to turn off this parameter.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoint_1.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoints(Curve otherCurve, out Point3d pointOnThisCurve, out Point3d pointOnOtherCurve)`

Gets closest points between this and another curves.

**Parameters:**
- `otherCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The other curve.
- `pointOnThisCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on this curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `pointOnOtherCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on other curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoints.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoints(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, out Point3d pointOnCurve, out Point3d pointOnObject, out int whichGeometry)`

Finds the object (and the closest point in that object) that is closest to this curve. Breps, surfaces, curves and point clouds are examples of objects that can be passed to this function.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of geometry to search.
- `pointOnCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `pointOnObject` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `whichGeometry` (System.Int32) — The index of the geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false if no object was found or selected.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoints_1.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoints(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, out Point3d pointOnCurve, out Point3d pointOnObject, out int whichGeometry, double maximumDistance)`

Finds the object (and the closest point in that object) that is closest to this curve. Breps, surfaces, curves and point clouds are examples of objects that can be passed to this function.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of geometry to search.
- `pointOnCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `pointOnObject` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `whichGeometry` (System.Int32) — The index of the geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — Maximum allowable distance. Past this distance, the research is given up and false is returned.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false if no object was found or selected.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoints_2.htm)

#### `public bool CombineShortSegments(double tolerance)`

Looks for segments that are shorter than tolerance that can be combined. For NURBS of degree greater than 1, spans are combined by removing knots. Similarly for NURBS segments of polycurves. Otherwise, RemoveShortSegments() is called. Does not change the domain, but it will change the relative parameterization.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="tolerance"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Curve.CombineShortSegments(System.Double)"]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if short segments were combined or removed. False otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CombineShortSegments.htm)

#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

#### `protected void ConstructConstObject(Object parentObject, int subobjectIndex)`

Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

**Parameters:**
- `parentObject` (System.Object) — The parent object.
- `subobjectIndex` (System.Int32) — The sub-object index.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_ConstructConstObject.htm)

#### `[ObsoleteAttribute("Use version that takes a tolerance")] public PointContainment Contains(Point3d testPoint)`

Computes the relationship between a point and a closed curve region. This curve must be closed or the return value will be Unset. Both curve and point are projected to the World XY plane.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Relationship between point and curve region.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Contains.htm)

#### `[ObsoleteAttribute("Use version that takes a tolerance")] public PointContainment Contains(Point3d testPoint, Plane plane)`

Computes the relationship between a point and a closed curve region. This curve must be closed or the return value will be Unset.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Plane in which to compare point and region.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Relationship between point and curve region.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Contains_1.htm)

#### `public PointContainment Contains(Point3d testPoint, Plane plane, double tolerance)`

Computes the relationship between a point and a closed curve region. This curve must be closed or the return value will be Unset.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Plane in which to compare point and region.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance to use during comparison.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Relationship between point and curve region.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Contains_2.htm)

#### `public Vector3d CurvatureAt(double t)`

Evaluate the curvature vector at a curve parameter.

**Remarks:** No error handling.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Evaluation parameter.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — Curvature vector of the curve at the parameter t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CurvatureAt.htm)

#### `public Vector3d[] DerivativeAt(double t, int derivativeCount)`

Evaluate the derivatives at the specified curve parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Curve parameter to evaluate.
- `derivativeCount` (System.Int32) — Number of derivatives to evaluate, must be at least 0.

**Returns:** `Vector3d[]` — An array of vectors that represents all the derivatives starting at zero.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DerivativeAt.htm)

#### `public Vector3d[] DerivativeAt(double t, int derivativeCount, CurveEvaluationSide side)`

Evaluate the derivatives at the specified curve parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Curve parameter to evaluate.
- `derivativeCount` (System.Int32) — Number of derivatives to evaluate, must be at least 0.
- `side` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveEvaluationSide) — Side of parameter to evaluate. If the parameter is at a kink, it makes a big difference whether the evaluation is from below or above.

**Returns:** `Vector3d[]` — An array of vectors that represents all the derivatives starting at zero.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DerivativeAt_1.htm)

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

#### `protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Dispose.htm)

#### `public Point3d[] DivideAsContour(Point3d contourStart, Point3d contourEnd, double interval)`

Divides this curve at fixed steps along a defined contour line.

**Parameters:**
- `contourStart` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The start of the contouring line.
- `contourEnd` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The end of the contouring line.
- `interval` (System.Double) — A distance to measure on the contouring axis.

**Returns:** `Point3d[]` — An array of points; or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DivideAsContour.htm)

#### `public double[] DivideByCount(int segmentCount, bool includeEnds)`

Divide the curve into a number of equal-length segments.

**Parameters:**
- `segmentCount` (System.Int32) — Segment count. Note that the number of division points may differ from the segment count.
- `includeEnds` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the point at the start of the first division segment is returned.

**Returns:** `Double[]` — List of curve parameters at the division points on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DivideByCount.htm)

#### `public double[] DivideByCount(int segmentCount, bool includeEnds, out Point3d[] points)`

Divide the curve into a number of equal-length segments.

**Parameters:**
- `segmentCount` (System.Int32) — Segment count. Note that the number of division points may differ from the segment count.
- `includeEnds` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the point at the start of the first division segment is returned.
- `points` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d[]) — A list of division points. If the function returns successfully, this point-array will be filled in.

**Returns:** `Double[]` — Array containing division curve parameters on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DivideByCount_1.htm)

Divide the curve into specific length segments.

- `includeEnds` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the point at the start of the first division segment is returned.

Divide the curve into specific length segments.

**Parameters:**
- `segmentLength` (System.Double) — The length of each and every segment (except potentially the last one).
- `includeEnds` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the point at the start of the first division segment is returned.
- `reverse` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the divisions start from the end of the curve.

**Returns:** `Double[]` — Array containing division curve parameters if successful, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DivideByLength_2.htm)

Divide the curve into specific length segments.

- `includeEnds` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the point at the start of the first division segment is returned.
- `reverse` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the divisions start from the end of the curve.
- `points` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d[]) — If function is successful, points at each parameter value are returned in points.

**Returns:** `Double[]` — Array containing division curve parameters if successful, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DivideByLength_3.htm)

- `includeEnds` (System.Boolean) — If true, then the point at the start of the first division segment is returned.

#### `public override GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs an exact duplicate of this Curve.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Curve.Duplicate"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public Curve DuplicateCurve()`

Constructs an exact duplicate of this curve.

**Returns:** `Curve` — An exact copy of this curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DuplicateCurve.htm)

#### `public Curve[] DuplicateSegments()`

Duplicates curve segments. Explodes polylines, polycurves and G1 discontinuous NURBS curves. Single segment curves, such as lines, arcs, unkinked NURBS curves, are duplicated.

**Returns:** `Curve[]` — An array of all the segments that make up this curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DuplicateSegments.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `public Curve Extend(CurveEnd side, CurveExtensionStyle style, IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry)`

Extends a curve until it intersects a collection of objects.
