## Circle (struct)

Represents a circle in 3D. The values used are a radius and an orthonormal frame of the plane containing the circle, with origin at the center.The circle is parameterized by radians from 0 to 2 Pi given byt -> center + cos(t)*radius*xaxis + sin(t)*radius*yaxiswhere center, xaxis and yaxis define the orthonormal frame of the circle plane.

**Remarks:** >An IsValid circle has positive radius and an IsValid plane defining the frame.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Circle.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Circle(double radius)` — Initializes a circle with center (0,0,0) in the world XY plane.
- `public Circle(Arc arc)` — Initializes a circle from an arc.
- `public Circle(Plane plane, double radius)` — Initializes a circle on a plane with a given radius.
- `public Circle(Point3d center, double radius)` — Initializes a circle parallel to the world XY plane with given center and radius.
- `public Circle(Plane plane, Point3d center, double radius)` — Initializes a circle parallel to a given plane with given center and radius.
- `public Circle(Point3d point1, Point3d point2, Point3d point3)` — Initializes a circle through three 3d points.
- `public Circle(Point3d startPoint, Vector3d tangentAtP, Point3d pointOnCircle)` — Initializes a circle from two 3d points and a tangent at the first point. The start/end of the circle is at point "startPoint".

### Methods
#### `public bool ClosestParameter(Point3d testPoint, out double t)`

Gets the parameter on the circle which is closest to a test point.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to project onto the circle.
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter on circle closes to testPoint.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Circle_ClosestParameter.htm)

#### `public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint)`

Gets the point on the circle which is closest to a test point.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to project onto the circle.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on the circle that is closest to testPoint or Point3d.Unset on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Circle_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public Vector3d DerivativeAt(int derivative, double t)`

Determines the value of the Nth derivative at a parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `derivative` (System.Int32) — Which order of derivative is wanted.
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter to evaluate derivative. Valid values are 0, 1, 2 and 3.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — The derivative of the circle at the given parameter.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Circle_DerivativeAt.htm)

#### `public bool EpsilonEquals(Circle other, double epsilon)`

Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

**Parameters:**

### Properties
- `BoundingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the circle's 3d axis aligned bounding box.
- `Center` (Point3d, get/set) — Gets or sets the center point of this circle.
- `Circumference` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the circumference of this circle.
- `Diameter` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the diameter (radius * 2.0) of this circle. Diameters should be positive values.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — A valid circle has radius larger than 0.0 and a base plane which is must also be valid.
- `Normal` (Vector3d, get) — Gets the normal vector for this circle.
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — Gets or sets the plane of the circle.
- `Radius` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the radius of this circle. Radii should be positive values.
- `Unset` (Circle, get) — Gets a circle with Unset components.

## ClippingPlaneSurface (class)

Represents a planar surface that is used as clipping plane in viewports. A clipping plane object maintains a list of viewports that it clips against.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_ClippingPlaneSurface.htm)

### Constructors
- `protected ClippingPlaneSurface(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Protected constructor for internal use.

### Methods
#### `public bool AddClipViewportId(Guid viewportId)`

Adds a viewport id to the list of viewports that this clipping plane clips.

**Parameters:**
- `viewportId` (System.Guid) — The id of the RhinoViewport to add.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the viewport was added, false if the viewport is already in the list.

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `public bool Evaluate(double u, double v, int numberDerivatives, out Point3d point, out Vector3d[] derivatives)`

Evaluates a surface mathematically.

**Remarks:** The partial derivatives will be in the order {Su, Sv} for numberDerivatives = 1, {Su, Sv, Suu, Suv, Svv} for 2, {Su, Sv, Suu, Suv, Svv, Suuu, Suuv, Suvv, Svvv} for 3, and similar for higher counts.

**Parameters:**
- `u` (System.Double) — A U parameter.
- `v` (System.Double) — A V parameter.
- `numberDerivatives` (System.Int32) — The number of derivatives.
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point. This out parameter will be assigned during this call.
- `derivatives` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d[]) — A vector array. This out parameter will be assigned during this call. This can be null.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the operation succeeded; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Evaluate.htm)

#### `public bool Extend(int direction, Interval interval)`

Analytically extends the surface to include the interval.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — If 0, Surface.Domain(0) will include the interval. (the first surface parameter). If 1, Surface.Domain(1) will include the interval. (the second surface parameter).
- `interval` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — If the interval is not included in surface domain, the surface will be extended so that its domain includes the interval. Note, this method will fail if the surface is closed in the specified direction.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Extend_1.htm)

#### `public Surface Extend(IsoStatus edge, double extensionLength, bool smooth)`

Extends an untrimmed surface along one edge.

**Parameters:**
- `edge` (Rhino.Geometry.IsoStatus) — Edge to extend. Must be North, South, East, or West.
- `extensionLength` (System.Double) — distance to extend.
- `smooth` (System.Boolean) — true for smooth (C-infinity) extension. false for a C1- ruled extension.

**Returns:** `Surface` — New extended surface on success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Extend.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

#### `public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(int direction, Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, out double t)`

Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — If 0, then "u" parameter is checked. If 1, then the "v" parameter is checked.
- `continuityType` (Rhino.Geometry.Continuity) — The desired continuity.
- `t0` (System.Double) — Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity and step through the discontinuities.
- `t1` (System.Double) — (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 is will be ignored unless c is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.
- `t` (System.Double) — if a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): TRUE if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): TRUE if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetNextDiscontinuity_1.htm)

#### `public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(int direction, Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, double cosAngleTolerance, double curvatureTolerance, out double t)`

Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — If 0, then "u" parameter is checked. If 1, then the "v" parameter is checked.
- `continuityType` (Rhino.Geometry.Continuity) — The desired continuity.
- `t0` (System.Double) — Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity and step through the discontinuities.
- `t1` (System.Double) — (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 is will be ignored unless c is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.
- `cosAngleTolerance` (System.Double) — default = cos(1 degree) Used only when continuityType is G1_continuous or G2_continuous. If the cosine of the angle between two tangent vectors is <= cos_angle_tolerance, then a G1 discontinuity is reported.
- `curvatureTolerance` (System.Double) — (default = ON_SQRT_EPSILON) Used only when continuityType is G2_continuous. If K0 and K1 are curvatures evaluated from above and below and |K0 - K1| > curvature_tolerance, then a curvature discontinuity is reported.
- `t` (System.Double) — if a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): TRUE if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): TRUE if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetNextDiscontinuity.htm)

#### `public bool GetNurbsFormParameterFromSurfaceParameter(double surfaceS, double surfaceT, out double nurbsS, out double nurbsT)`

Translates a parameter from the current surface to the parameter space of the surface returned by ToNurbsSurface().

**Parameters:**
- `surfaceS` (System.Double) — The parameter in the S, or sometimes U, direction, of this surface.
- `surfaceT` (System.Double) — The parameter in the T, or sometimes V, direction of this surface.
- `nurbsS` (System.Double) — S on the NURBS form.
- `nurbsT` (System.Double) — T on the NURBS form.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetNurbsFormParameterFromSurfaceParameter.htm)

#### `public double[] GetSpanVector(int direction)`

Gets array of span "knots".

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 gets first parameter's domain, 1 gets second parameter's domain.

**Returns:** `Double[]` — An array with span vectors; or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetSpanVector.htm)

#### `public int HasNurbsForm()`

Is there a NURBS surface representation of this surface.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 unable to create NURBS representation with desired accuracy. 1 success - NURBS parameterization matches the surface's 2 success - NURBS point locus matches the surface's and the domain of the NURBS surface is correct. However, This surface's parameterization and the NURBS surface parameterization may not match. This situation happens when getting NURBS representations of surfaces that have a transcendental parameterization like spheres, cylinders, and cones.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_HasNurbsForm.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurface(IEnumerable<Point3d> points, double tolerance)`

Constructs an interpolated curve on a surface, using 3D points.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of points.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A new NURBS curve, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_InterpolatedCurveOnSurface.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV(IEnumerable<Point2d> points, double tolerance)`

Returns a curve that interpolates points on a surface. The interpolant lies on the surface.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — List of at least two UV parameter locations on the surface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used for the fit of the push-up curve. Generally, the resulting interpolating curve will be within tolerance of the surface.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A new NURBS curve if successful, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV(IEnumerable<Point2d> points, double tolerance, bool closed, int closedSurfaceHandling)`

Returns a curve that interpolates points on a surface. The interpolant lies on the surface.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — List of at least two UV parameter locations on the surface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used for the fit of the push-up curve. Generally, the resulting interpolating curve will be within tolerance of the surface.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — If false, the interpolating curve is not closed. If true, the interpolating curve is closed, and the last point and first point should generally not be equal.
- `closedSurfaceHandling` (System.Int32) — If 0, all points must be in the rectangular domain of the surface. If the surface is closed in some direction, then this routine will interpret each point and place it at an appropriate location in the covering space. This is the simplest option and should give good results. If 1, then more options for more control of handling curves going across seams are available. If the surface is closed in some direction, then the points are taken as points in the covering space. Example, if srf.IsClosed(0)=true and srf.IsClosed(1)=false and srf.Domain(0)=srf.Domain(1)=Interval(0,1) then if closedSurfaceHandling=1 a point(u, v) in points can have any value for the u coordinate, but must have 0<=v<=1. In particular, if points = { (0.0,0.5), (2.0,0.5) } then the interpolating curve will wrap around the surface two times in the closed direction before ending at start of the curve. If closed=true the last point should equal the first point plus an integer multiple of the period on a closed direction.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A new NURBS curve if successful, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV_1.htm)

#### `public int IsAtSeam(double u, double v)`

Tests if a surface parameter value is at a seam.

**Parameters:**
- `u` (System.Double) — Surface u parameter to test.
- `v` (System.Double) — Surface v parameter to test.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if not a seam, 1 if u == Domain(0)[i] and srf(u, v) == srf(Domain(0)[1-i], v) 2 if v == Domain(1)[i] and srf(u, v) == srf(u, Domain(1)[1-i]) 3 if 1 and 2 are true.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_IsAtSeam.htm)

#### `public bool IsAtSingularity(double u, double v, bool exact)`

Tests if a surface parameter value is at a singularity.

**Parameters:**
- `u` (System.Double) — Surface u parameter to test.
- `v` (System.Double) — Surface v parameter to test.
- `exact` (System.Boolean) — If true, test if (u,v) is exactly at a singularity. If false, test if close enough to cause numerical problems.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if surface is singular at (s,t)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_IsAtSingularity.htm)

#### `public Curve IsoCurve(int direction, double constantParameter)`

Gets isoparametric curve.

**Remarks:** In this function "direction" indicates which direction the resulting curve runs. 0: horizontal, 1: vertical In the other Surface functions that take a "direction" argument, "direction" indicates if "constantParameter" is a "u" or "v" parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 first parameter varies and second parameter is constant e.g., point on IsoCurve(0,c) at t is srf(t,c) This is a horizontal line from left to right 1 first parameter is constant and second parameter varies e.g., point on IsoCurve(1,c) at t is srf(c,t This is a vertical line from bottom to top.
- `constantParameter` (System.Double) — The parameter that was constant on the original surface.

**Returns:** `Curve` — An isoparametric curve or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_IsoCurve.htm)

#### `public bool LocalClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, double seedU, double seedV, out double u, out double v)`

Find parameters of the point on a surface that is locally closest to the testPoint. The search for a local close point starts at seed parameters.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point to test against.
- `seedU` (System.Double) — The seed parameter in the U direction.
- `seedV` (System.Double) — The seed parameter in the V direction.
- `u` (System.Double) — U parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.
- `v` (System.Double) — V parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the search is successful, false if the search fails.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_LocalClosestPoint.htm)

- `vPointCount` (System.Int32) — The number of points in the output surface v direction. Must be bigger than vDegree (maximum value is 1000)

#### `public Curve ShortPath(Point2d start, Point2d end, double tolerance)`

Constructs a geodesic between 2 points, used by ShortPath command in Rhino.

**Parameters:**
- `start` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — start point of curve in parameter space. Points must be distinct in the domain of the surface.
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — end point of curve in parameter space. Points must be distinct in the domain of the surface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance used in fitting discrete solution.

**Returns:** `Curve` — a geodesic curve on the surface on success. null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ShortPath.htm)

#### `public Surface Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem)`

Smooths a surface by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the surface edges don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The smoothed surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Smooth.htm)

#### `public Surface Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem, Plane plane)`

Smooths a surface by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the surface edges don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — If SmoothingCoordinateSystem.CPlane specified, then the construction plane.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The smoothed surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Smooth_1.htm)

#### `public int SpanCount(int direction)`

Gets number of smooth nonempty spans in the parameter direction.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 gets first parameter's domain, 1 gets second parameter's domain.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The span count.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_SpanCount.htm)

#### `public Surface[] Split(int direction, double parameter)`

Splits (divides) the surface into two parts at the specified parameter

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 = The surface is split vertically. The "west" side is returned as the first surface in the array and the "east" side is returned as the second surface in the array. 1 = The surface is split horizontally. The "south" side is returned as the first surface in the array and the "north" side is returned as the second surface in the array
- `parameter` (System.Double) — value of constant parameter in interval returned by Domain(direction)

**Returns:** `Surface[]` — Array of two surfaces on success

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Split.htm)

Converts the surface into a Brep.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ToBrep.htm)

Create a JSON string representation of this object

#### `public NurbsSurface ToNurbsSurface()`

Gets a NURBS surface representation of this surface. Default tolerance of 0.0 is used.

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — NurbsSurface on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ToNurbsSurface.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface ToNurbsSurface(double tolerance, out int accuracy)`

Gets a NURBS surface representation of this surface.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when creating NURBS representation.
- `accuracy` (System.Int32) — 0 = unable to create NURBS representation with desired accuracy. 1 = success - returned NURBS parameterization matches the surface's to the desired accuracy. 2 = success - returned NURBS point locus matches the surface's to the desired accuracy and the domain of the NURBS surface is correct. However, this surface's parameterization and the NURBS surface parameterization may not match to the desired accuracy. This situation happens when getting NURBS representations of surfaces that have a transcendental parameterization like spheres, cylinders, and cones.

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — NurbsSurface on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ToNurbsSurface_1.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCone(out Cone cone)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cone within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance and return the cone.

- `cone` (Rhino.Geometry.Cone) — On success, the cone parameters are filled in.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCone.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCone(out Cone cone, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cone and returns the cone.

**Parameters:**
- `cone` (Rhino.Geometry.Cone) — On success, the cone parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCone_1.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cylinder within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance and return the cylinder.

**Remarks:** If successful, an infinite cylinder is returned. When a surface has a cylindrical shape, even if its ends are not circles on the cylinder, it returns the axis and radius. You can detect infinite cylinders using Cylinder.IsFinite.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCylinder.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cylinder and return the infinite cylinder.

**Remarks:** If successful, an infinite cylinder is returned. When a surface has a cylindrical shape, even if its ends are not circles on the cylinder, it returns the axis and radius. You can detect infinite cylinders using Cylinder.IsFinite.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCylinder_1.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetFiniteCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface with the assumption that it might be a right circular cylinder and returns this geometry.

**Remarks:** If successful, a finite cylinder is returned.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetFiniteCylinder.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetPlane(out Plane plane)`

Tests a surface for planarity and return the plane.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — On success, the plane parameters are filled in.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if there is a plane such that the maximum distance from the surface to the plane is <= RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetPlane.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetPlane(out Plane plane, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface for planarity and return the plane.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — On success, the plane parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if there is a plane such that the maximum distance from the surface to the plane is <= tolerance.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetPlane_1.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetSphere(out Sphere sphere)`

- `sphere` (Rhino.Geometry.Sphere) — On success, the sphere parameters are filled in.

#### `public Surface VariableOffset(double uMinvMin, double uMinvMax, double uMaxvMin, double uMaxvMax, double tolerance)`

Copies a surface so that all locations at the corners of the copied surface are specified distances from the original surface.

**Parameters:**
- `uMinvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMinvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Max.
- `uMaxvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMaxvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Max.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The offset tolerance.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The offset surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_VariableOffset_1.htm)

#### `public Surface VariableOffset(double uMinvMin, double uMinvMax, double uMaxvMin, double uMaxvMax, IEnumerable<Point2d> interiorParameters, IEnumerable<double> interiorDistances, double tolerance)`

Copies a surface so that all locations at the corners, and from specified interior locations, of the copied surface are specified distances from the original surface.

**Parameters:**
- `uMinvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMinvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Max.
- `uMaxvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMaxvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Max.
- `interiorParameters` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — An array of interior UV parameters to offset from.
- `interiorDistances` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Double>) — >An array of offset distances at the interior UV parameters.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The offset tolerance.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The offset surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_VariableOffset.htm)

#### `public Guid[] ViewportIds()`

Returns the ids of RhinoViewport objects that are clipped by this clipping plane.

**Returns:** `Guid[]` — The ids of RhinoViewport objects.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ClippingPlaneSurface_ViewportIds.htm)

### Properties
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsSolid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a values indicating whether a surface is solid.
- `IsSubDFriendly` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the surface is a non-rational, uniform, natural or periodic, cubic NURBS surface. Otherwise, false is returend.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — Gets or sets the clipping plane.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## ComponentIndex (struct)

Represents an index of an element contained in another object.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentIndex.htm)

### Constructors
- `public ComponentIndex(ComponentIndexType type, int index)` — Construct component index with a specific type/index combination

### Methods
#### `public bool IsUnset()`

Return true is this component index is the same as the Unset component index

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.ComponentIndex.IsUnset"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentIndex_IsUnset.htm)

### Properties
- `ComponentIndexType` (ComponentIndexType, get) — The interpretation of Index depends on the Type value. Type m_index interpretation (0 based indices) no_type used when context makes it clear what array is being index brep_vertex Brep.m_V[] array index brep_edge Brep.m_E[] array index brep_face Brep.m_F[] array index brep_trim Brep.m_T[] array index brep_loop Brep.m_L[] array index mesh_vertex Mesh.m_V[] array index meshtop_vertex MeshTopology.m_topv[] array index meshtop_edge MeshTopology.m_tope[] array index mesh_face Mesh.m_F[] array index idef_part InstanceDefinition.m_object_uuid[] array index polycurve_segment PolyCurve::m_segment[] array index dim_linear_point LinearDimension2::POINT_INDEX dim_radial_point RadialDimension2::POINT_INDEX dim_angular_point AngularDimension2::POINT_INDEX dim_ordinate_point OrdinateDimension2::POINT_INDEX dim_text_point TextEntity2 origin point.
- `Index` (Int32, get) — The interpretation of m_index depends on the m_type value. m_type m_index interpretation (0 based indices) no_type used when context makes it clear what array is being index brep_vertex Brep.m_V[] array index brep_edge Brep.m_E[] array index brep_face Brep.m_F[] array index brep_trim Brep.m_T[] array index brep_loop Brep.m_L[] array index mesh_vertex Mesh.m_V[] array index meshtop_vertex MeshTopology.m_topv[] array index meshtop_edge MeshTopology.m_tope[] array index mesh_face Mesh.m_F[] array index idef_part InstanceDefinition.m_object_uuid[] array index polycurve_segment PolyCurve::m_segment[] array index dim_linear_point LinearDimension2::POINT_INDEX dim_radial_point RadialDimension2::POINT_INDEX dim_angular_point AngularDimension2::POINT_INDEX dim_ordinate_point OrdinateDimension2::POINT_INDEX dim_text_point TextEntity2 origin point.
- `Unset` (ComponentIndex, get) — The unset value of component index.

## ComponentIndexType (enum)

Defines enumerated values to represent component index types.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentIndexType.htm)

### Values
- `InvalidType` = `0` — Not used. This is the default value of the enumeration type.
- `BrepVertex` = `1` — Targets a brep vertex index.
- `BrepEdge` = `2` — Targets a brep edge index.
- `BrepFace` = `3` — Targets a brep face index.
- `BrepTrim` = `4` — Targets a brep trim index.
- `BrepLoop` = `5` — Targets a brep loop index.
- `MeshVertex` = `11` — Targets a mesh vertex index.
- `MeshTopologyVertex` = `12` — Targets a mesh topology vertex index.
- `MeshTopologyEdge` = `13` — Targets a mesh topology edge index.
- `MeshFace` = `14` — Targets a mesh face index.
- `MeshNgon` = `15` — Targets a mesh n-gon index.
- `InstanceDefinitionPart` = `21` — Targets an instance definition part index.
- `PolycurveSegment` = `31` — Targets a polycurve segment index.
- `PointCloudPoint` = `41` — Targets a point cloud point index.
- `GroupMember` = `51` — Targets a group member index.
- `ExtrusionBottomProfile` = `61` — 3d bottom profile curves. Index identifies profile component
- `ExtrusionTopProfile` = `62` — 3d top profile curves. Index identifies profile component
- `ExtrusionWallEdge` = `63` — 3d wall edge curve. Index/2: identifies profile component Index%2: 0=start, 1=end
- `ExtrusionWallSurface` = `64` — Side wall surfaces. Index identifies profile component
- `ExtrusionCapSurface` = `65` — Bottom and top cap surfaces. Index 0=bottom, 1=top
- `ExtrusionPath` = `66` — Extrusion path (axis line). Index -1=entire path, 0=start point, 1=endpoint
- `SubdVertex` = `71` — Targets a SubD vertex pointer Id. Ids are not guaranteed to be sequential.
- `SubdEdge` = `72` — Targets a SubD edge pointer Id. Ids are not guaranteed to be sequential.
- `SubdFace` = `73` — Targets a SubD face pointer Id. Ids are not guaranteed to be sequential.
- `DimLinearPoint` = `100` — Targets a linear dimension point index.
- `DimRadialPoint` = `101` — Targets a radial dimension point index.
- `DimAngularPoint` = `102` — Targets an angular dimension point index.
- `DimOrdinatePoint` = `103` — Targets an ordinate dimension point index.
- `DimTextPoint` = `104` — Targets a text point index.
- `NoType` = `268435455` — Targets no specific type.

## ComponentStatus (struct)

Provides information about selection, highlighting, visibility, editability and integrity states of a component. This structure is immutable.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus.htm)

### Methods
#### `public static ComponentStatus operator +(ComponentStatus a, ComponentStatus b)`

Sets flags from both component states and returns a new ComponentStatus.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — The first ComponentStatus.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — The second ComponentStatus.

**Returns:** `ComponentStatus` — A new ComponentStatus.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_op_Addition.htm)

Determines if two ComponentStatus objects are equal.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — The first ComponentStatus.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — The second ComponentStatus.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if they are exactly equal. False otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_op_Equality.htm)

Determines if another ComponentStatus and this are equal.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_Equals.htm)

Determines if an object and this are equal.

- `obj` (System.Object) — An object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if equal in value. false otherwise

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_Equals_1.htm)

Serves as a special hash function. The inner value is used for the purpose.

**Returns:** `Int32` — An integer deriving from a bit mask.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_GetHashCode.htm)

#### `public bool HasAllEqualStates(ComponentStatus statesFilter, ComponentStatus comparand)`

For the purposes of this test, Selected and SelectedPersistent are considered equal.

**Parameters:**
- `statesFilter` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — If no states are specified, then false is returned.
- `comparand` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — If a state is set in states_filter, the corresponding state in "this" and comparand will be tested.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at all tested states in "this" and comparand are identical.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_HasAllEqualStates.htm)

#### `public bool HasNoEqualStates(ComponentStatus statesFilter, ComponentStatus comparand)`

For the purposes of this test, Selected and SelectedPersistent are considered equal.

**Parameters:**
- `statesFilter` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — If no states are specified, then false is returned.
- `comparand` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — If a state is set in states_filter, the corresponding state in "this" and comparand will be tested.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at all tested states in "this" and comparand are identical.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_HasNoEqualStates.htm)

#### `public bool HasSomeEqualStates(ComponentStatus statesFilter, ComponentStatus comparand)`

For the purposes of this test, Selected and SelectedPersistent are considered equal.

**Parameters:**
- `statesFilter` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — If no states are specified, then false is returned.
- `comparand` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — If a state is set in states_filter, the corresponding state in "this" and comparand will be tested.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one tested state in "this" and comparand are identical.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_HasSomeEqualStates.htm)

Provides a string representation of this ComponentStatus.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_ToString.htm)

#### `public ComponentStatus WithStates(ComponentStatus additionalStatus)`

Activates any information flag described in any of the two input component statuses and returns a new ComponentStatus with those flags checked.

**Parameters:**
- `additionalStatus` (Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus) — To be used for adding status values.

**Returns:** `ComponentStatus` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus.WithStates(Rhino.Geometry.ComponentStatus)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ComponentStatus_WithStates.htm)

### Properties
- `AllSet` (ComponentStatus, get) — All flags are checked.
- `Clear` (ComponentStatus, get) — This is the default value and equal to undefined.
- `Damaged` (ComponentStatus, get) — The damaged flag is checked.
- `Hidden` (ComponentStatus, get) — The hidden flag is checked.
- `Highlighted` (ComponentStatus, get) — The highlight selection flag is checked.
- `IsClear` (Boolean, get) — True if every setting is 0 or false.
- `IsDamaged` (Boolean, get) — Returns false if component is not damaged. True otherwise.
- `IsHidden` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if hidden. False otherwise.
- `IsHighlighted` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if highlighted. False otherwise.
- `IsLocked` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if locked. False otherwise.
- `IsSelected` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if selected or selected persistent. False otherwise.
- `IsSelectedPersistent` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if selected persistent. False otherwise.
- `Locked` (ComponentStatus, get) — The locked flag is checked.
- `Selected` (ComponentStatus, get) — The selection flag is checked.
- `SelectedPersistent` (ComponentStatus, get) — The persistent selection flag is checked.

## Concavity (enum)

Enumerates the possible types of edge concavity types.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Concavity.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `-1` — Concavity is not relevant, for example naked and non-manifold edges cannot have a concavity measure.
- `Tangent` = `0` — Faces on either side of the edge are tangent within tolerance.
- `Convex` = `1` — Faces on either side of the edge are convex.
- `Concave` = `2` — Faces on either side of the edge are concave.

## Cone (struct)

Represents the center plane, radius and height values in a right circular cone.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Cone.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Cone(Plane plane, double height, double radius)` — Initializes a new cone with a specified base plane, height and radius.

### Methods
#### `public double AngleInDegrees()`

Computes the angle (in degrees) between the axis and the side of the cone. The angle and the height have the same sign.

**Returns:** `Double` — An angle in degrees.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Cone_AngleInDegrees.htm)

#### `public double AngleInRadians()`

Computes the angle (in radians) between the axis and the side of the cone. The angle and the height have the same sign.

**Returns:** `Double` — Math.Atan(Radius / Height) if the height is not 0; 0 if the radius is 0; Math.PI otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Cone_AngleInRadians.htm)

#### `public bool EpsilonEquals(Cone other, double epsilon)`

Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Cone) — [Missing <param name="other"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Cone.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Cone,System.Double)"]
- `epsilon` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="epsilon"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Cone.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Cone,System.Double)"]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Cone.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Cone,System.Double)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Cone_EpsilonEquals.htm)

#### `public Brep ToBrep(bool capBottom)`

Gets a Brep representation of the cone with a single face for the cone, an edge along the cone seam, and vertices at the base and apex ends of this seam edge. The optional cap is a single face with one circular edge starting and ending at the base vertex.

**Parameters:**
- `capBottom` (System.Boolean) — true if the bottom should be filled with a surface. false otherwise.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A brep (polysurface) representation of this cone values.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Cone_ToBrep.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface ToNurbsSurface()`

Constructs a Nurbs surface representation of this Cone. This is synonymous with calling NurbsSurface.CreateFromCone().

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — A Nurbs surface representation of the cone or null.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Cone_ToNurbsSurface.htm)

#### `public RevSurface ToRevSurface()`

Constructs a RevSurface representation of this Cone. This is synonymous with calling RevSurface.CreateFromCone().

**Returns:** `RevSurface` — A RevSurface representation of the cone or null.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Cone_ToRevSurface.htm)

### Properties
- `ApexPoint` (Point3d, get) — Point at tip of the cone.
- `Axis` (Vector3d, get) — Unit vector axis of cone.
- `BasePoint` (Point3d, get) — Center of base circle.
- `Height` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the height of the circular right cone.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — true if plane is valid, height is not zero and radius is not zero.
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — Gets or sets the base plane of the cone. The apex of the cone is at the plane's origin.
- `Radius` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the radius of the cone.
- `Unset` (Cone, get) — Gets an invalid Cone.

## ConicSectionType (enum)

Defines enumerated values for types of conic sections.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_ConicSectionType.htm)

### Values
- `Unknown` = `0` — The curve shape is unknown.
- `Circle` = `1` — The curve has the shape of a circle.
- `Ellipse` = `2` — The curve has the shape of an ellipse.
- `Hyperbola` = `3` — The curve has the shape of a hyperbola.
- `Parabola` = `4` — The curve has the shape of a parabola.

## Continuity (enum)

Provides enumerated values for continuity along geometry, such as continuous first derivative or continuous unit tangent and curvature.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Continuity.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — There is no continuity.
- `C0_continuous` = `1` — Continuous Function : Test for parametric continuity. In particular, all types of curves are considered infinitely continuous at the start/end of the evaluation domain.
- `C1_continuous` = `2` — Continuous first derivative : Test for parametric continuity. In particular, all types of curves are considered infinitely continuous at the start/end of the evaluation domain.
- `C2_continuous` = `3` — Continuous first derivative and second derivative : Test for parametric continuity. In particular, all types of curves are considered infinitely continuous at the start/end of the evaluation domain.
- `G1_continuous` = `4` — Continuous unit tangent : Test for parametric continuity. In particular, all types of curves are considered infinitely continuous at the start/end of the evaluation domain.
- `G2_continuous` = `5` — Continuous unit tangent and curvature : Test for parametric continuity. In particular, all types of curves are considered infinitely continuous at the start/end of the evaluation domain.
- `C0_locus_continuous` = `6` — Locus continuous function : Continuity tests using the following enum values are identical to tests using the preceding enum values on the INTERIOR of a curve's domain. At the END of a curve a "locus" test is performed in place of a parametric test. In particular, at the END of a domain, all open curves are locus discontinuous. At the END of a domain, all closed curves are at least C0_locus_continuous. By convention all Curves are considered locus continuous at the START of the evaluation domain. This convention is not strictly correct, but it was adopted to make iterative kink finding tools easier to use and so that locus discontinuities are reported once at the end parameter of a curve rather than twice.
- `C1_locus_continuous` = `7` — Locus continuous first derivative : Continuity tests using the following enum values are identical to tests using the preceding enum values on the INTERIOR of a curve's domain. At the END of a curve a "locus" test is performed in place of a parametric test. In particular, at the END of a domain, all open curves are locus discontinuous. At the END of a domain, all closed curves are at least C0_locus_continuous. By convention all Curves are considered locus continuous at the START of the evaluation domain. This convention is not strictly correct, but it was adopted to make iterative kink finding tools easier to use and so that locus discontinuities are reported once at the end parameter of a curve rather than twice.
- `C2_locus_continuous` = `8` — Locus continuous first and second derivative : Continuity tests using the following enum values are identical to tests using the preceding enum values on the INTERIOR of a curve's domain. At the END of a curve a "locus" test is performed in place of a parametric test. In particular, at the END of a domain, all open curves are locus discontinuous. At the END of a domain, all closed curves are at least C0_locus_continuous. By convention all Curves are considered locus continuous at the START of the evaluation domain. This convention is not strictly correct, but it was adopted to make iterative kink finding tools easier to use and so that locus discontinuities are reported once at the end parameter of a curve rather than twice.
- `G1_locus_continuous` = `9` — Locus continuous unit tangent : Continuity tests using the following enum values are identical to tests using the preceding enum values on the INTERIOR of a curve's domain. At the END of a curve a "locus" test is performed in place of a parametric test. In particular, at the END of a domain, all open curves are locus discontinuous. At the END of a domain, all closed curves are at least C0_locus_continuous. By convention all Curves are considered locus continuous at the START of the evaluation domain. This convention is not strictly correct, but it was adopted to make iterative kink finding tools easier to use and so that locus discontinuities are reported once at the end parameter of a curve rather than twice.
- `G2_locus_continuous` = `10` — Locus continuous unit tangent and curvature : Continuity tests using the following enum values are identical to tests using the preceding enum values on the INTERIOR of a curve's domain. At the END of a curve a "locus" test is performed in place of a parametric test. In particular, at the END of a domain, all open curves are locus discontinuous. At the END of a domain, all closed curves are at least C0_locus_continuous. By convention all Curves are considered locus continuous at the START of the evaluation domain. This convention is not strictly correct, but it was adopted to make iterative kink finding tools easier to use and so that locus discontinuities are reported once at the end parameter of a curve rather than twice.
- `Cinfinity_continuous` = `11` — Analytic discontinuity. Cinfinity_continuous is a euphemism for "at a knot".
- `Gsmooth_continuous` = `12` — Aesthetic discontinuity

## ControlPoint (struct)

Represents control point geometry with three-dimensional position and weight.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_ControlPoint.htm)

### Constructors
- `public ControlPoint(Point3d pt)` — Constructs a new world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point. The 4-D representation of this is (x, y, z, 1.0).
- `public ControlPoint(Point4d pt)` — Constructs a new homogeneous control point, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
- `public ControlPoint(Point3d euclideanPt, double weight)` — Constructs a control point from a world 3-D, or Euclidean, location and a weight. The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
- `public ControlPoint(double x, double y, double z)` — Constructs a new world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point. The 4-D representation is (x, y, z, 1.0).
- `public ControlPoint(double x, double y, double z, double weight)` — Constructs a new homogeneous control point, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).

### Methods
#### `public bool EpsilonEquals(ControlPoint other, double epsilon)`

Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.ControlPoint) — [Missing <param name="other"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.ControlPoint.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.ControlPoint,System.Double)"]
- `epsilon` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="epsilon"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.ControlPoint.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.ControlPoint,System.Double)"]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.ControlPoint.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.ControlPoint,System.Double)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ControlPoint_EpsilonEquals.htm)

#### `public bool Equals(ControlPoint other)`

Determines if two points exactly match.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.ControlPoint) — The other point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the other control point exactly matches this one.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_ControlPoint_Equals.htm)

### Properties
- `Location` (Point3d, get/set) — Gets or sets world 3-D, or Euclidean location of the control point.
- `Unset` (ControlPoint, get) — Gets the predefined, unset control point.
- `Weight` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the weight of this control point.
- `X` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X coordinate of the control point.
- `Y` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y coordinate of the control point.
- `Z` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Z coordinate of the control point.

## Curve (class)

Represents a base class that is common to most RhinoCommon curve types. A curve represents an entity that can be all visited by providing a single parameter, usually called t.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Curve.htm)

### Constructors
- `protected Curve(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Protected serialization constructor for internal use.

### Methods
#### `public bool ChangeClosedCurveSeam(double t)`

If this curve is closed, then modify it so that the start/end point is at curve parameter t.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Curve parameter of new start/end point. The returned curves domain will start at t.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ChangeClosedCurveSeam.htm)

#### `public bool ChangeDimension(int desiredDimension)`

Changes the dimension of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `desiredDimension` (System.Int32) — The desired dimension.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the curve's dimension was already desiredDimension or if the curve's dimension was successfully changed to desiredDimension; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ChangeDimension.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation()`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve in the world XY plane. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve with respect to world XY plane.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation(Plane plane)`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve in a given plane. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The plane in which to solve the orientation.

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve in the given plane.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation_1.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation(Transform xform)`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to map the curve to the world XY plane.

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve in the world XY-plane.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation_2.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation(Vector3d upDirection)`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve in a given plane. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Parameters:**
- `upDirection` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector that is considered "up".

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve with respect to a defined up direction.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation_3.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double t)`

Finds parameter of the point on a curve that is closest to testPoint. If the maximumDistance parameter is > 0, then only points whose distance to the given point is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to search from.
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter of local closest point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double t, double maximumDistance)`

Finds the parameter of the point on a curve that is closest to testPoint. If the maximumDistance parameter is > 0, then only points whose distance to the given point is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to project.
- `t` (System.Double) — parameter of local closest point returned here.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — The maximum allowed distance. Past this distance, the search is given up and false is returned.Use 0 to turn off this parameter.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoint_1.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoints(Curve otherCurve, out Point3d pointOnThisCurve, out Point3d pointOnOtherCurve)`

Gets closest points between this and another curves.

**Parameters:**
- `otherCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The other curve.
- `pointOnThisCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on this curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `pointOnOtherCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on other curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoints.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoints(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, out Point3d pointOnCurve, out Point3d pointOnObject, out int whichGeometry)`

Finds the object (and the closest point in that object) that is closest to this curve. Breps, surfaces, curves and point clouds are examples of objects that can be passed to this function.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of geometry to search.
- `pointOnCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `pointOnObject` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `whichGeometry` (System.Int32) — The index of the geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false if no object was found or selected.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoints_1.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoints(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, out Point3d pointOnCurve, out Point3d pointOnObject, out int whichGeometry, double maximumDistance)`

Finds the object (and the closest point in that object) that is closest to this curve. Breps, surfaces, curves and point clouds are examples of objects that can be passed to this function.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of geometry to search.
- `pointOnCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `pointOnObject` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `whichGeometry` (System.Int32) — The index of the geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — Maximum allowable distance. Past this distance, the research is given up and false is returned.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false if no object was found or selected.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoints_2.htm)

#### `public bool CombineShortSegments(double tolerance)`

Looks for segments that are shorter than tolerance that can be combined. For NURBS of degree greater than 1, spans are combined by removing knots. Similarly for NURBS segments of polycurves. Otherwise, RemoveShortSegments() is called. Does not change the domain, but it will change the relative parameterization.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="tolerance"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Curve.CombineShortSegments(System.Double)"]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if short segments were combined or removed. False otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CombineShortSegments.htm)

#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

#### `protected void ConstructConstObject(Object parentObject, int subobjectIndex)`

Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

**Parameters:**
- `parentObject` (System.Object) — The parent object.
- `subobjectIndex` (System.Int32) — The sub-object index.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_ConstructConstObject.htm)

#### `[ObsoleteAttribute("Use version that takes a tolerance")] public PointContainment Contains(Point3d testPoint)`

Computes the relationship between a point and a closed curve region. This curve must be closed or the return value will be Unset. Both curve and point are projected to the World XY plane.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Relationship between point and curve region.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Contains.htm)

#### `[ObsoleteAttribute("Use version that takes a tolerance")] public PointContainment Contains(Point3d testPoint, Plane plane)`

Computes the relationship between a point and a closed curve region. This curve must be closed or the return value will be Unset.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Plane in which to compare point and region.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Relationship between point and curve region.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Contains_1.htm)

#### `public PointContainment Contains(Point3d testPoint, Plane plane, double tolerance)`

Computes the relationship between a point and a closed curve region. This curve must be closed or the return value will be Unset.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Plane in which to compare point and region.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance to use during comparison.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Relationship between point and curve region.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Contains_2.htm)

#### `public static Curve CreateArcBlend(Point3d startPt, Vector3d startDir, Point3d endPt, Vector3d endDir, double controlPointLengthRatio)`

Creates a polycurve consisting of two tangent arc segments that connect two points and two directions.

**Parameters:**
- `startPt` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Start of the arc blend curve.
- `startDir` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Start direction of the arc blend curve.
- `endPt` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — End of the arc blend curve.
- `endDir` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — End direction of the arc blend curve.
- `controlPointLengthRatio` (System.Double) — The ratio of the control polygon lengths of the two arcs. Note, a value of 1.0 means the control polygon lengths for both arcs will be the same.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The arc blend curve, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CreateArcBlend.htm)

#### `public static Curve CreateArcLineArcBlend(Point3d startPt, Vector3d startDir, Point3d endPt, Vector3d endDir, double radius)`

Creates an arc-line-arc blend curve between two curves. The output is generally a PolyCurve with three segments: arc, line, arc. In some cases, one or more of those segments will be absent because they would have 0 length. If there is only a single segment, the result will either be an ArcCurve or a LineCurve.

**Remarks:** The first arc segment will start at startPt, with starting tangent startDir. The second arc segment will end at endPt with end tangent endDir. The line segment will start from the end of the first arc segment and end at start of the second arc segment, and it will be tangent to both arcs at those points.

**Parameters:**
- `startPt` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Start of the blend curve.
- `startDir` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Start direction of the blend curve.
- `endPt` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — End of the blend curve.
- `endDir` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — End direction of the arc blend curve.
- `radius` (System.Double) — The radius of the arc segments.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The blend curve if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CreateArcLineArcBlend.htm)

#### `public static Curve CreateBlendCurve(Curve curveA, Curve curveB, BlendContinuity continuity)`

Create a Blend curve between two existing curves.

**Parameters:**
- `curveA` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Curve to blend from (blending will occur at curve end point).
- `curveB` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Curve to blend to (blending will occur at curve start point).
- `continuity` (Rhino.Geometry.BlendContinuity) — Continuity of blend.

**Returns:** `Curve` — A curve representing the blend between A and B or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CreateBlendCurve.htm)

#### `public static Curve CreateBlendCurve(Curve curveA, Curve curveB, BlendContinuity continuity, double bulgeA, double bulgeB)`

Create a Blend curve between two existing curves.

**Parameters:**
- `curveA` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Curve to blend from (blending will occur at curve end point).
- `curveB` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Curve to blend to (blending will occur at curve start point).
- `continuity` (Rhino.Geometry.BlendContinuity) — Continuity of blend.
- `bulgeA` (System.Double) — Bulge factor at curveA end of blend. Values near 1.0 work best.
- `bulgeB` (System.Double) — Bulge factor at curveB end of blend. Values near 1.0 work best.

**Returns:** `Curve` — A curve representing the blend between A and B or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CreateBlendCurve_1.htm)

#### `public static Curve CreateBlendCurve(Curve curve0, double t0, bool reverse0, BlendContinuity continuity0, Curve curve1, double t1, bool reverse1, BlendContinuity continuity1)`

Makes a curve blend between 2 curves at the parameters specified with the directions and continuities specified

**Parameters:**
- `curve0` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — First curve to blend from
- `t0` (System.Double) — Parameter on first curve for blend endpoint
- `reverse0` (System.Boolean) — If false, the blend will go in the natural direction of the curve. If true, the blend will go in the opposite direction to the curve
- `continuity0` (Rhino.Geometry.BlendContinuity) — Continuity for the blend at the start
- `curve1` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Second curve to blend from
- `t1` (System.Double) — Parameter on second curve for blend endpoint
- `reverse1` (System.Boolean) — If false, the blend will go in the natural direction of the curve. If true, the blend will go in the opposite direction to the curve
- `continuity1` (Rhino.Geometry.BlendContinuity) — Continuity for the blend at the end

**Returns:** `Curve` — The blend curve on success. null on failure

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CreateBlendCurve_2.htm)

#### `public static CurveBooleanRegions CreateBooleanRegions(IEnumerable<Curve> curves, Plane plane, bool combineRegions, double tolerance)`

Calculates curve Boolean regions, which trims and splits curves based on their overlapping regions.

**Parameters:**
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — The input curves.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Regions will be found in the projection of the curves to this plane.
- `combineRegions` (System.Boolean) — If true, then adjacent regions will be combined.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Function tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.

**Returns:** `CurveBooleanRegions` — The curve Boolean regions if successful, null of no successful.

#### `public static CurveBooleanRegions CreateBooleanRegions(IEnumerable<Curve> curves, Plane plane, IEnumerable<Point3d> points, bool combineRegions, double tolerance)`

- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — The input curves.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Regions will be found in the projection of the curves to this plane.
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — These points will be projected to plane. All regions that contain at least one of these points will be found.
- `combineRegions` (System.Boolean) — If true, then adjacent regions will be combined.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Function tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.

**Returns:** `CurveBooleanRegions` — The curve Boolean regions if successful, null of no successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CreateBooleanRegions_1.htm)
