Add geometry that should be included in the calculation

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase) — Currently only points, point clouds, curves, meshes, breps, surfaces, and extrusions are supported
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — [Missing <param name="xform"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.HiddenLineDrawingParameters.AddGeometry(Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase,Rhino.Geometry.Transform,System.Object)"]
- `tag` (System.Object) — arbitrary data to be associated with this geometry

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the type of geometry can be added for calculations. Currently only points, point clouds, curves, meshes, breps, surfaces and extrusions are supported

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_HiddenLineDrawingParameters_AddGeometry.htm)

#### `public void SetViewport(RhinoViewport viewport)`

Set the viewport for the hidden line drawing (HLD). The viewport supplies the projection that determines the visibility of curves, and the HLD coordinate system in which the resulting curves are represented. The HLD-coordinate system is a right handed system for 3-D model space, in which the 3rd coordinate direction is the projection direction. In particular the z-coordinate direction points from the scene toward the camera.

**Parameters:**
- `viewport` (Rhino.Display.RhinoViewport) — A copy of rhinoViewport is made inside of HiddenLineDrawing.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_HiddenLineDrawingParameters_SetViewport.htm)

#### `public void SetViewport(ViewportInfo viewport)`

Set the viewport for the hidden line drawing (HLD). The viewport supplies the projection that determines the visibility of curves, and the HLD coordinate system in which the resulting curves are represented. The HLD-coordinate system is a right handed system for 3-D model space, in which the 3rd coordinate direction is the projection direction. In particular the z-coordinate direction points from the scene toward the camera.

**Parameters:**
- `viewport` (Rhino.DocObjects.ViewportInfo) — A copy of viewportInfo is made inside of HiddenLineDrawing.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_HiddenLineDrawingParameters_SetViewport_1.htm)

### Properties
- `AbsoluteTolerance` (Double, get/set) — Absolute tolerance is used to decide if curves overlap or intersect. A suggested value is to use RhinoDoc.AbsoluteTolerance
- `Flatten` (Boolean, get/set) — Flatten the computed geometry
- `IncludeHiddenCurves` (Boolean, get/set) — Include hidden curves in hidden line drawing (default is true)
- `IncludeTangentEdges` (Boolean, get/set) — Include tangent edges in hidden line drawing (default is true)
- `IncludeTangentSeams` (Boolean, get/set) — Include tangent seams in hidden line drawing (default is true)

## HiddenLineDrawingPoint (class)

Points generated from source objects which correspond to point and point cloud source objects.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_HiddenLineDrawingPoint.htm)

### Properties
- `ClippingPlaneIndex` (Int32, get) — Index into HiddenLineDrawing.ClippingPlanes when SilhouetteType == SilhouetteType.SectionCut.
- `Index` (Int32, get) — Index of this object in HiddenLineDrawing.Points.
- `Location` (Point3d, get) — Returns the location of this object in HiddenLineDrawing coordinates.
- `PointVisibility` (HiddenLineDrawingPoint.Visibility, get) — Returns the point's visibility
- `SourceObject` (HiddenLineDrawingObject, get) — Return the source object that this point came from.
- `SourceObjectComponentIndex` (ComponentIndex, get) — Component of source object part that generated this curve.

## HiddenLineDrawingPoint.Visibility (enum)

The different types of HiddenLineObjectPoint visibility

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_HiddenLineDrawingPoint_Visibility.htm)

### Values
- `Unset` = `0` — Unset value
- `Visible` = `1` — Visible
- `Hidden` = `2` — Hidden
- `Duplicate` = `3` — Duplicate

## HiddenLineDrawingSegment (class)

The results of HiddenLineDrawing calculation are a collection of segments. A segment is a sub-curve of a HiddenLineDrawingObjectCurve.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_HiddenLineDrawingSegment.htm)

### Properties
- `CurveGeometry` (Curve, get) — The actual curve geometry
- `CurveSideFills` (HiddenLineDrawingSegment.SideFill[], get) — The SideFill fields are only valid for visible curves. With respect to the HiddenLineDrawing, the region to the left (or right respectively) of this curve is described by CurveSideFills[0] or CurveSideFills[1], respectively. If exactly one of these regions is empty this is a scene silhouette. If this region contains a surface it is either a surface which in 3-D is adjacent to this edge or it an surface that is further away from the camera, we call this a shadow surface. unknown is used for unset values and for projecting curves
- `Index` (Int32, get) — Index of this curve in HiddenLineDrawing.Curves.
- `IsSceneSilhouette` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if this curve is a scene silhouette.
- `ParentCurve` (HiddenLineDrawingObjectCurve, get) — This curve is a sub-curve of the returned HiddenLineDrawingFullCurve object.
- `SegmentVisibility` (HiddenLineDrawingSegment.Visibility, get) — Returns the segment's visibility

## HiddenLineDrawingSegment.SideFill (enum)

When a silhouette is projected on the image plane (determined by the camera location or direction), and projects to a curve (not a point), the image area to the left or right of a projected silhouette curve is filled with either the surface or a void.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_HiddenLineDrawingSegment_SideFill.htm)

### Values
- `SideUnset` = `0` — Unset
- `SideSurface` = `1` — Surface
- `SideVoid` = `2` — Void
- `OtherSurface` = `3` — Other Surface

## HiddenLineDrawingSegment.Visibility (enum)

The different types of HiddenLineDrawingSegment visibility

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_HiddenLineDrawingSegment_Visibility.htm)

### Values
- `Unset` = `0` — Unset value
- `Visible` = `1` — Visible
- `Hidden` = `2` — Hidden
- `Duplicate` = `3` — Duplicate
- `Projecting` = `4` — Projects to a point (smaller than tolerance)
- `Clipped` = `5` — Clipped by clipping planes

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose_1.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

### Properties
- `ComponentStatus` (ComponentStatus, get/set) — Gets or sets the component status of the model component.
- `ComponentType` (ModelComponentType, get) — Returns InstanceDefinition.
- `DeletedName` (String, get) — Gets the name of a component that is deleted.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the definition.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `HasId` (Boolean, get) — Returns a value indicating whether the component has an ID.
- `HasIndex` (Boolean, get) — Returns a value indicating whether the component has an Index.
- `HasName` (Boolean, get) — Returns a value indicating whether the component has a Name.
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `Id` (Guid, get/set) — Gets or sets the ID of the current instance.
- `IdIsLocked` (Boolean, get) — Returns a value indicating whether the component ID is already locked.
- `Index` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the model component index attribute.
- `IndexIsLocked` (Boolean, get) — Returns a value indicating whether the component Index is already locked.
- `InstanceDefinitionModelSerialNumber` (UInt32, get) — When a component is in a model as part of the information required for a linked instance definition, this value identifies the linked instance definition reference model.
- `IsComponentStatusLocked` (Boolean, get) — The component status itself can be locked. This returns an indication.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsSystemComponent` (Boolean, get) — True if this model component is a system constant. An incomplete list of system constant model components is below:ON_ModelComponent::Unset ON_InstanceDefinition::Empty ON_Linetype::UnsetON_Linetype::ContinuousON_Linetype::ByLayerON_Linetype::ByParent ON_Layer::UnsetON_Layer::Default ON_TextStyle::UnsetON_TextStyle::DefaultON_TextStyle::ByLayerON_TextStyle::ByParent ON_DimStyle::UnsetON_DimStyle::DefaultON_DimStyle::DefaultInchDecimalON_DimStyle::DefaultInchFractionalON_DimStyle::DefaultFootInchArchitectureON_DimStyle::DefaultMillimeterSmallON_DimStyle::DefaultMillimeterLargeON_DimStyle::DefaultMillimeterArchitecture
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `ModelSerialNumber` (UInt32, get) — A value identifying the model that manages this component.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name
- `NameIsLocked` (Boolean, get) — Returns a value indicating whether the component Name is already locked.
- `ReferenceModelSerialNumber` (UInt32, get) — When a component is in a model for reference, this value identifies the reference model.
- `Url` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the URL or hyperlink of the definition.
- `UrlDescription` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the URL or hyperlink of the definition.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.

## InstanceReferenceGeometry (class)

Represents a reference to the geometry in a block definition.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_InstanceReferenceGeometry.htm)

### Constructors
- `public InstanceReferenceGeometry(Guid instanceDefinitionId, Transform transform)` — Constructor used when creating nested instance references.

### Methods
#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

**Parameters:**
- `parentObject` (System.Object) — The parent object.
- `subobjectIndex` (System.Int32) — The sub-object index.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_ConstructConstObject.htm)

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)`

Overridden in order to destroy local display cache information

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Dispose.htm)

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)`

Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Angle of rotation in radians.
- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of the axis of rotation.
- `rotationCenter` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully rotated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Rotate.htm)

#### `public bool Scale(double scaleFactor)`

Scales the object by the specified factor. The scale is centered at the origin.

**Parameters:**
- `scaleFactor` (System.Double) — The uniform scaling factor.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully scaled.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Scale.htm)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_SetUserString.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

### Properties
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `ParentIdefId` (Guid, get) — The unique id for the parent instance definition of this instance reference.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.
- `Xform` (Transform, get) — Transformation for this reference.

## Interpolator (class)

Exposes a set of standard numeric interpolation algorithms.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Interpolator.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Interpolator()` — Constructs a new, empty Interpolator.
- `public Interpolator(IEnumerable<double> collection)` — Constructs an Interpolator from a collection of numbers.
- `public Interpolator(int initialCapacity)` — Constructs an empty Interpolator with a certain capacity.
- `public Interpolator(RhinoList<double> list)` — Copy all the numbers from an existing RhinoList.
- `public Interpolator(int amount, double defaultValue)` — Constructs a new Interpolator with a specified amount of numbers.

### Methods
#### `public void Add(T item)`

Adds an object to the end of the List.

**Parameters:**
- `item` (T) — Item to append.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_Add.htm)

#### `public void AddRange(IEnumerable collection)`

Adds the elements of the specified collection to the end of the List.

**Parameters:**
- `collection` (System.Collections.IEnumerable) — The collection whose elements should be added to the end of the List. The collection itself cannot be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), but it can contain elements that are a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic). Objects in collection which cannot be represented as T will throw exceptions.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_AddRange_1.htm)

Adds the elements of the specified collection to the end of the List.

**Parameters:**
- `collection` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<T>) — The collection whose elements should be added to the end of the List. The collection itself cannot be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), but it can contain elements that are a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), if type T is a reference type.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_AddRange.htm)

Constructs a read-only wrapper of this class.

**Returns:** `ReadOnlyCollection<T>` — A wrapper.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_AsReadOnly.htm)

#### `public int BinarySearch(int index, int count, T item, IComparer<T> comparer)`

Searches the entire sorted List for an element using the specified comparer and returns the zero-based index of the element.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based starting index of the range to search.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The length of the range to search.
- `item` (T) — The object to locate. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.
- `comparer` (System.Collections.Generic.IComparer<T>) — The IComparer(T) implementation to use when comparing elements. Or a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) to use the default comparer Comparer(T)::Default.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The zero-based index of item in the sorted List, if item is found; otherwise, a negative number that is the bitwise complement of the index of the next element that is larger than item or, if there is no larger element, the bitwise complement of Count.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_BinarySearch.htm)

#### `public int BinarySearch(T item)`

Searches the entire sorted List for an element using the default comparer and returns the zero-based index of the element.

**Parameters:**
- `item` (T) — The object to locate. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The zero-based index of item in the sorted List, if item is found; otherwise, a negative number that is the bitwise complement of the index of the next element that is larger than item or, if there is no larger element, the bitwise complement of Count.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_BinarySearch_1.htm)

#### `public int BinarySearch(T item, IComparer<T> comparer)`

Searches the entire sorted List for an element using the specified comparer and returns the zero-based index of the element.

**Parameters:**
- `item` (T) — The object to locate. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.
- `comparer` (System.Collections.Generic.IComparer<T>) — The IComparer(T) implementation to use when comparing elements. Or a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) to use the default comparer Comparer(T)::Default.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The zero-based index of item in the sorted List, if item is found; otherwise, a negative number that is the bitwise complement of the index of the next element that is larger than item or, if there is no larger element, the bitwise complement of Count.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_BinarySearch_2.htm)

Aggregates all results of a conversion function over this table into a new list.

#### `public void CopyTo(int index, T[] array, int arrayIndex, int count)`

Copies a range of elements from the List to a compatible one-dimensional array, starting at the specified index of the target array.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based index in the source List at which copying begins.
- `array` (T[]) — The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from List. The Array must have zero-based indexing.
- `arrayIndex` (System.Int32) — The zero-based index in array at which copying begins.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of elements to copy.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_CopyTo.htm)

#### `public void CopyTo(T[] array)`

Copies the entire List to a compatible one-dimensional array, starting at the beginning of the target array.

- `array` (T[]) — The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from List. The Array must have zero-based indexing.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_CopyTo_1.htm)

**Parameters:**
- `array` (T[]) — The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from List. The Array must have zero-based indexing.
- `arrayIndex` (System.Int32) — The zero-based index in array at which copying begins.

Returns a shallow copy of this instance. If the generic type is comprised of only value types (struct, enum, ptr), then the result will be a deep copy.

**Returns:** `RhinoList<T>` — The duplicated list.

#### `public T Find(Predicate<T> match)`

Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the first occurrence within the entire List.

**Parameters:**
- `match` (System.Predicate<T>) — The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

**Returns:** `T` — The first element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, if found; otherwise, the default value for type T.

#### `public RhinoList<T> FindAll(Predicate<T> match)`

Retrieves all the elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate.

**Parameters:**
- `match` (System.Predicate<T>) — The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the elements to search for.

**Returns:** `RhinoList<T>` — A ON_List(T) containing all the elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate, if found; otherwise, an empty ON_List(T).

Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence within the range of elements in the List that extends from the specified index to the last element.

**Parameters:**
- `startIndex` (System.Int32) — The zero-based starting index of the search.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of elements in the section to search.
- `match` (System.Predicate<T>) — The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The zero-based index of the first occurrence of an element that matches the conditions defined by match, if found; otherwise, -1.

Searches for the specified object and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence within the range of elements in the List that extends from the specified index to the last element.

**Parameters:**
- `item` (T) — The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based starting index of the search.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The zero-based index of the first occurrence of item within the entire List, if found; otherwise, -1.

Searches for the specified object and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence within the range of elements in the List that starts at the specified index and contains the specified number of elements.

**Parameters:**
- `item` (T) — The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based starting index of the search.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of elements in the section to search.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The zero-based index of the first occurrence of item within the entire List, if found; otherwise, -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_IndexOf_2.htm)

Inserts an element into the List at the specified index.

- `item` (T) — The object to insert. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

#### `public void InsertRange(int index, IEnumerable<T> collection)`

Inserts the elements of a collection into the List at the specified index.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based index at which the new elements should be inserted.
- `collection` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<T>) — The collection whose elements should be inserted into the List. The collection itself cannot be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), but it can contain elements that are a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), if type T is a reference type.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_InsertRange.htm)

#### `public double InterpolateCatmullRom(double t)`

Sample the list of numbers with Catmull-Rom interpolation.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter to sample at. The integer portion of the parameter indicates the index of the left-hand value. If this Interpolator is cyclical, parameters will be wrapped.

**Returns:** `Double` — The sampled value at t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interpolator_InterpolateCatmullRom.htm)

#### `public double InterpolateCosine(double t)`

Sample the list of numbers with cosine interpolation.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter to sample at. The integer portion of the parameter indicates the index of the left-hand value. If this Interpolator is cyclical, parameters will be wrapped.

**Returns:** `Double` — The sampled value at t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interpolator_InterpolateCosine.htm)

Sample the list of numbers with cubic interpolation.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter to sample at. The integer portion of the parameter indicates the index of the left-hand value. If this Interpolator is cyclical, parameters will be wrapped.

**Returns:** `Double` — The sampled value at t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interpolator_InterpolateCubic.htm)

Sample the list of numbers with linear interpolation.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter to sample at. The integer portion of the parameter indicates the index of the left-hand value. If this Interpolator is cyclical, parameters will be wrapped.

**Returns:** `Double` — The sampled value at t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interpolator_InterpolateLinear.htm)

#### `public double InterpolateNearestNeighbour(double t)`

Sample the list of numbers with Nearest Neighbor interpolation.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter to sample at. The integer portion of the parameter indicates the index of the left-hand value. If this Interpolator is cyclical, parameters will be wrapped.

**Parameters:**
- `item` (T) — The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based starting index of the backward search.

**Parameters:**
- `item` (T) — The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based starting index of the backward search.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of elements in the section to search.

#### `public void Sort(Comparison<T> comparison)`

- `comparison` (System.Comparison<T>) — The System.Comparison(T) to use when comparing elements.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_Sort_2.htm)

#### `public void Sort(double[] keys)`

Sort this list based on a list of numeric keys of equal length. The keys array will not be altered.

**Remarks:** This function does not exist on the DotNET List class. David thought it was a good idea.

**Parameters:**
- `keys` (System.Double[]) — Numeric keys to sort with.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_Sort_3.htm)

#### `public void Sort(IComparer<T> comparer)`

Sorts the elements in the entire list using the specified System.Comparison(T)

**Parameters:**
- `comparer` (System.Collections.Generic.IComparer<T>) — The IComparer(T) implementation to use when comparing elements, or a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) to use the default comparer Comparer(T).Default.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_Sort_1.htm)

#### `public void Sort(int index, int count, IComparer<T> comparer)`

Sorts the elements in a range of elements in list using the specified comparer.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based starting index of the range to sort.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The length of the range to sort.
- `comparer` (System.Collections.Generic.IComparer<T>) — The IComparer(T) implementation to use when comparing elements, or a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) to use the default comparer Comparer(T).Default.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_Sort_4.htm)

#### `public void Sort(int[] keys)`

Sort this list based on a list of numeric keys of equal length. The keys array will not be altered.

**Remarks:** This function does not exist on the DotNET List class. David thought it was a good idea.

**Parameters:**
- `keys` (System.Int32[]) — Numeric keys to sort with.

#### `public T[] ToArray()`

Constructs an array that contains all items in this list.

**Returns:** `T[]` — An array containing all items in this list.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_ToArray.htm)

#### `public void TrimExcess()`

Sets the capacity to the actual number of elements in the List, if that number is less than a threshold value.

**Remarks:** This function differs from the DotNET implementation of List<T> since that one only trims the excess if the excess exceeds 10% of the list length.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_TrimExcess.htm)

#### `public bool TrueForAll(Predicate<T> match)`

Determines whether every element in the List matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate.

**Parameters:**
- `match` (System.Predicate<T>) — The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions to check against the elements.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if every element in the List matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate; otherwise, false. If the list has no elements, the return value is true.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Collections_RhinoList_1_TrueForAll.htm)

### Properties
- `Capacity` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the total number of elements the internal data structure can hold without resizing.
- `Count` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of elements actually contained in the List.
- `Cyclical` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the values inside this Interpolator are to be treated as cyclical (i.e. circular).
- `First` (T, get/set) — Gets or sets the first item in the list. This is synonymous to calling List[0].
- `Item` (T, get/set) — Gets or sets the element at the specified index.
- `Last` (T, get/set) — Gets or sets the last item in the list. This is synonymous to calling List[Count-1].
- `NullCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of null references (Nothing in Visual Basic) in this list. If T is a ValueType, this property always return zero.

## Interval (struct)

Represents an interval in one-dimensional space, that is defined as two extrema or bounds.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Interval.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Interval(Interval other)` — Initializes a new instance copying the other instance values.
- `public Interval(double t0, double t1)` — Initializes a new instance of the Rhino.Geometry.Interval class.

### Methods
#### `public static Interval operator +(double number, Interval interval)`

Shifts an interval by a specific amount (addition).

**Parameters:**
- `number` (System.Double) — The shifting value.
- `interval` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The interval to be used as a base.

**Returns:** `Interval` — A new interval where T0 and T1 are summed with number.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interval_op_Addition_1.htm)

#### `public static Interval operator +(Interval interval, double number)`

Shifts a Interval by a specific amount (addition).

**Parameters:**
- `interval` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The interval to be used as a base.
- `number` (System.Double) — The shifting value.

**Returns:** `Interval` — A new interval where T0 and T1 are summed with number.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interval_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(Interval other)`

Compares this Interval with another interval. The lower bound has first evaluation priority.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The other Interval to compare with.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this[0] < other[0]+1: if this[0] > other[0]-1: if this[0] == other[0] and this[1] < other[1]+1: if this[0] == other[0] and this[1] > other[1].

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interval_CompareTo.htm)

#### `public static Interval FromIntersection(Interval a, Interval b)`

Returns a new Interval that is the Intersection of the two input Intervals.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The first input interval.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The second input interval.

**Returns:** `Interval` — If the intersection is not empty, then intersection = [max(a.Min(),b.Min()), min(a.Max(),b.Max())] The interval [ON.UnsetValue,ON.UnsetValue] is considered to be the empty set interval. The result of any intersection involving an empty set interval or disjoint intervals is the empty set interval.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interval_FromIntersection.htm)

#### `public static Interval FromUnion(Interval a, Interval b)`

Returns a new Interval which contains both inputs.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The first input interval.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The second input interval.

**Returns:** `Interval` — The union of an empty set and an increasing interval is the increasing interval. The union of two empty sets is empty.The union of an empty set an a non-empty interval is the non-empty interval.The union of two non-empty intervals is [min(a.Min(),b.Min()), max(a.Max(),b.Max())]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interval_FromUnion.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >(Interval a, Interval b)`

Determines whether the first specified Interval comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second Interval. The lower bound has first evaluation priority.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — First interval.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — Second interval.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a[0] is larger than b[0], or a[0] == b[0] and a[1] is larger than b[1]; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interval_op_GreaterThan.htm)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Returns a string representation of this Interval.

**Returns:** `String` — A string with T0,T1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Interval_ToString.htm)

### Properties
- `IsDecreasing` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if T[0] > T[1].
- `IsIncreasing` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if T0 < T1.
- `IsSingleton` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if T0 == T1 != ON.UnsetValue.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this Interval is valid. Valid intervals must contain valid numbers.
- `Item` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the indexed bound of this Interval.
- `Length` (Double, get) — Gets the signed length of the numeric range. If the interval is decreasing, a negative length will be returned.
- `Max` (Double, get) — Gets the larger of T0 and T1.
- `Mid` (Double, get) — Gets the average of T0 and T1.
- `Min` (Double, get) — Gets the smaller of T0 and T1.
- `T0` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the lower bound of the Interval.
- `T1` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the upper bound of the Interval.
- `Unset` (Interval, get) — Gets an Interval whose limits are RhinoMath.UnsetValue.

## InvalidDimensionStyleIdException (class)

General exception that can be thrown by annotations

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_InvalidDimensionStyleIdException.htm)

### Constructors
- `public InvalidDimensionStyleIdException(string msg)` — Initializes a new instance of the InvalidDimensionStyleIdException class

## IsoStatus (enum)

Defines enumerated values for isoparametric curve direction on a surface, such as X or Y, and curve sides, such as North or West boundary. Note: odd values are all x-constant; even values > 0 are all y-constant.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_IsoStatus.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — curve is not an isoparametric curve.
- `X` = `1` — curve is a "x" = constant (vertical) isoparametric curve in the interior of the surface's domain.
- `Y` = `2` — curve is a "y" = constant (horizontal) isoparametric curve in the interior of the surface's domain.
- `West` = `3` — curve is a "x" = constant isoparametric curve along the west side of the surface's domain.
- `South` = `4` — curve is a "y" = constant isoparametric curve along the south side of the surface's domain.
- `East` = `5` — curve is a "x" = constant isoparametric curve along the east side of the surface's domain.
- `North` = `6` — curve is a "y" = constant isoparametric curve along the north side of the surface's domain.

## KnotStyle (enum)

Knot styles for NURBS curves and surfaces. If a knot vector meets the conditions of two styles, then the style with the lowest value is used.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_KnotStyle.htm)

### Values
- `Unknown` = `0` — Unknown knot style
- `Uniform` = `1` — Uniform knots (ends not clamped).
- `QuasiUniform` = `2` — Uniform knots (clamped ends, degree >= 2).
- `PiecewiseBezier` = `3` — All internal knots have full multiplicity.
- `ClampedEnd` = `4` — Clamped end knots (with at least one interior non-uniform knot).
- `NonUniform` = `5` — Known to be none of the other styles

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)`

Overridden in order to destroy local display cache information

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Dispose.htm)

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public override BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to bounding box after calculation. The geometry is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AnnotationBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)`

- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of the axis of rotation.
- `rotationCenter` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the axis of rotation.

#### `public bool SetOverrideDimStyle(DimensionStyle OverrideStyle)`

Set a style including overrides for this annotation object. The DimensionStyle OverrideStyle must have the override fields marked as overridden and must have it's Id set to nil. Use DimensinoStyle.SetFieldOverride(Field field) and related functions to manage override settings. To override a field, the field value must be set and the field must be marked as an override. The DimensionStyle passed in here must not be in the dimstyle table

**Parameters:**
- `OverrideStyle` (Rhino.DocObjects.DimensionStyle) — [Missing <param name="OverrideStyle"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.AnnotationBase.SetOverrideDimStyle(Rhino.DocObjects.DimensionStyle)"]

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

### Properties
- `AlternateDimensionLengthDisplay` (DimensionStyle.LengthDisplay, get/set) — Alternate length display units and format
- `AnnotationType` (AnnotationType, get) — Type of annotation
- `Curve` (NurbsCurve, get) — Gets the curve used by this leader
- `DecimalSeparator` (Char, get/set) — Set or get the decimal separator c
- `DimensionLengthDisplay` (DimensionStyle.LengthDisplay, get/set) — Length display units and format
- `DimensionScale` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the dimension scale
- `DimensionStyle` (DimensionStyle, get) — If there are no overrides then the parent style is returned otherwise the dimension style saved with the annotation is returned.
- `DimensionStyleId` (Guid, get/set) — Id of this annotation's parent dimstyle If this annotation has overrides to dimstyle properties, those overrides will be represented in the DimensionStyle returned by DimensionStyle(ParentStyle)
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `DrawForward` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the text is oriented towards the reader when viewed from behind
- `DrawTextFrame` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether to draw a frame around a text mask
- `FirstCharFont` (Font, get) — Returns the font used by the first run of text in an annotation
- `Font` (Font, get/set) — The base Font for the text of the annotation. The text string is rich text and therefore a different font that the base font can be associated with sub strings of the text
- `FormatWidth` (Double, get/set) — Text format width (Wrapping rectangle)
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasPropertyOverrides` (Boolean, get) — (Inherited from AnnotationBase.)
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `LeaderArrowBlockId` (Guid, get/set) — Id of the block used as the arrow for the leader when the arrow type is 'User arrow'
- `LeaderArrowSize` (Double, get/set) — The size of the leader arrow
- `LeaderArrowType` (DimensionStyle.ArrowType, get/set) — The arrowhead type for the leader
- `LeaderContentAngleStyle` (DimensionStyle.LeaderContentAngleStyle, get/set) — Angle for text of leader text
- `LeaderCurveStyle` (DimensionStyle.LeaderCurveStyle, get/set) — The style of the leader curve: polyline or spline
- `LeaderHasLanding` (Boolean, get/set) — Returns true if the leader has a landing line
- `LeaderLandingLength` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the length of the landing line
- `LeaderTextHorizontalAlignment` (TextHorizontalAlignment, get/set) — Gets or sets the horizontal alignment of the leader's text
- `LeaderTextVerticalAlignment` (TextVerticalAlignment, get/set) — Gets or sets the vertical alignment of the leader's text
- `MaskColor` (Color, get/set) — Color to use for drawing a text mask when it is enabled. If the mask is enabled and MaskColor is System.Drawing.Color.Transparent, then the viewport's color will be used for the MaskColor
- `MaskColorSource` (DimensionStyle.MaskType, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the mask background color is from the background or from a color
- `MaskEnabled` (Boolean, get/set) — Determines whether or not to draw a Text Mask
- `MaskFrame` (DimensionStyle.MaskFrame, get/set) — Gets or sets whether to draw a frame around a text mask
- `MaskOffset` (Double, get/set) — Offset for the border around text of the rectangle used to draw the mask. This value multiplied by TextHeight is the offset on each side of the tight rectangle around the text characters to the mask rectangle. The default value is 0.1.
- `MaskUsesViewportColor` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, the viewport's color is used for the mask color. If false, the color defined by MaskColor is used
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `ParentDimensionStyle` (DimensionStyle, get/set) — The parent dimension style associated with this annotation
- `PlainText` (String, get/set) — Text stripped of RTF formatting information
- `PlainTextWithFields` (String, get) — Text stripped of RTF formatting information and with field expressions intact
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — Plane that this annotation lies on
- `Points2D` (Point2d[], get/set) — Get or set the 2d points defining the curve used by this leader
- `Points3D` (Point3d[], get/set) — Get or set the 3d points defining the curve used by this leader
- `RichText` (String, get/set) — Text including additional RTF formatting information
- `Text` (String, get/set) — Text including additional RTF formatting information
- `TextFormula` (String, get/set) — See RichText
- `TextHasRtfFormatting` (Boolean, get) — (Inherited from AnnotationBase.)
- `TextHeight` (Double, get/set) — AnnotationBase.TextHeight Gets the parent dimstyle for the annotation and gets or sets the text height in the dimstyle
- `TextIsWrapped` (Boolean, get/set) — Is text wrapping on
- `TextModelWidth` (Double, get) — Width of text in the model
- `TextRotationDegrees` (Double, get/set) — Rotation of text in degrees
- `TextRotationRadians` (Double, get/set) — Rotation of text in radians
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## LengthMassProperties (class)

Contains static initialization methods and allows access to the computed metrics of length, length centroid, and length moments in curves.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties.htm)

### Methods
#### `public bool CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMoments(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the moments matrix with repect to centroid coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties_CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMoments.htm)

#### `public bool CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the principal moments of inertia and principal axes with repect to centroid coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties_CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia.htm)

#### `public static LengthMassProperties Compute(Curve curve)`

Calculates the length mass properties of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The curve to measure.

**Returns:** `LengthMassProperties` — The LengthMassProperties for the given curve, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties_Compute.htm)

Calculates the length mass properties of a curve.

- `length` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate length.
- `firstMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate length first moments, length, and length centroid.
- `secondMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate length second moments.
- `productMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate length product moments.

**Returns:** `LengthMassProperties` — The LengthMassProperties for the given curve, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties_Compute_1.htm)

- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — [Missing <param name="curves"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.LengthMassProperties.Compute(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Rhino.Geometry.Curve})"]

**Returns:** `LengthMassProperties` — The LengthMassProperties for the given enumeration of curves, or null on failure.

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties_Dispose_1.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties_Finalize.htm)

#### `public bool WorldCoordinatesPrincipalMoments(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of moments matrix in world coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties_WorldCoordinatesPrincipalMoments.htm)

#### `public bool WorldCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the principal moments of inertia and principal axes with repect to world coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LengthMassProperties_WorldCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia.htm)

### Properties
- `Centroid` (Point3d, get) — Gets the length centroid in the world coordinate system.
- `CentroidCoordinatesMomentsOfInertia` (Vector3d, get) — Moments of inertia with respect to centroid coordinate system. X = integral of ((y-y0)^2 + (z-z0)^2) dm Y = integral of ((z-z0)^2 + (x-x0)^2) dm Z = integral of ((z-z0)^2 + (y-y0)^2) dm where (x0,y0,z0) = centroid.
- `CentroidCoordinatesMomentsOfInertiaError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in centroid coordinates moments of inertia calculation.
- `CentroidCoordinatesProductMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Product moments with respect to centroid coordinate system.
- `CentroidCoordinatesProductMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in product moments with respect to centroid coordinate system.
- `CentroidCoordinatesRadiiOfGyration` (Vector3d, get) — Radii of gyration with respect to centroid coordinate system. X = sqrt(integral of ((y-y0)^2 + (z-z0)^2) dm/M) Y = sqrt(integral of ((z-z0)^2 + (x-x0)^2) dm/M) Z = sqrt(integral of ((z-z0)^2 + (y-y0)^2) dm/M) where (x0,y0,z0) = centroid.
- `CentroidCoordinatesSecondMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Second moments with respect to centroid coordinate system. X = integral of (x-x0)^2 dm Y = integral of (y-y0)^2 dm Z = integral of (z-z0)^2 dm where (x0,y0,z0) = centroid.
- `CentroidCoordinatesSecondMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in centroid coordinates second moments calculation.
- `CentroidError` (Vector3d, get) — Gets the uncertainty in the centroid calculation.
- `Length` (Double, get) — Gets the length solution.
- `LengthError` (Double, get) — Gets the uncertainty in the length calculation.
- `WorldCoordinatesFirstMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Returns the world coordinate first moments if they were able to be calculated. X is integral of "x dm" over the length Y is integral of "y dm" over the length Z is integral of "z dm" over the length.
- `WorldCoordinatesFirstMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in world coordinates first moments calculation.
- `WorldCoordinatesMomentsOfInertia` (Vector3d, get) — The moments of inertia about the world coordinate axes. X = integral of (y^2 + z^2) dm Y = integral of (z^2 + x^2) dm Z = integral of (z^2 + y^2) dm.
- `WorldCoordinatesMomentsOfInertiaError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in world coordinates moments of inertia calculation.
- `WorldCoordinatesProductMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Returns the world coordinate product moments if they were able to be calculated. X is integral of "xy dm" over the length Y is integral of "yz dm" over the length Z is integral of "zx dm" over the length.
- `WorldCoordinatesProductMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in world coordinates second moments calculation.
- `WorldCoordinatesRadiiOfGyration` (Vector3d, get) — Radii of gyration with respect to world coordinate system. X = sqrt(integral of (y^2 + z^2) dm/M) Y = sqrt(integral of (z^2 + x^2) dm/M) Z = sqrt(integral of (z^2 + y^2) dm/M)
- `WorldCoordinatesSecondMoments` (Vector3d, get) — Returns the world coordinate second moments if they were able to be calculated. X is integral of "xx dm" over the length Y is integral of "yy dm" over the length Z is integral of "zz dm" over the length.
- `WorldCoordinatesSecondMomentsError` (Vector3d, get) — Uncertainty in world coordinates second moments calculation.

## Light (class)

Represents a light that shines in the modeling space.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Light.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Light()` — Initializes a new light.
- `protected Light(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Protected constructor used in serialization.

### Methods
#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

#### `protected void ConstructConstObject(Object parentObject, int subobjectIndex)`

Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

**Parameters:**
- `parentObject` (System.Object) — The parent object.
- `subobjectIndex` (System.Int32) — The sub-object index.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_ConstructConstObject.htm)

#### `public static Light CreateSunLight(double northAngleDegrees, DateTime when, double latitudeDegrees, double longitudeDegrees)`

Constructs a light which simulates the Sun based on a given time and location on Earth.

**Parameters:**
- `northAngleDegrees` (System.Double) — The angle of North in degrees. North is the angle between positive World Y axis and model North, as measured on World XY plane.
- `when` (System.DateTime) — The time of the measurement. The Kind property of DateTime specifies whether this is in local or universal time. Local and Undefined daytime kinds in this argument are considered local.
- `latitudeDegrees` (System.Double) — The latitude, in degrees, of the location on Earth.
- `longitudeDegrees` (System.Double) — The longitude, in degrees, of the location on Earth.

**Returns:** `Light` — A newly constructed light object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Light_CreateSunLight_1.htm)

#### `public static Light CreateSunLight(double northAngleDegrees, double azimuthDegrees, double altitudeDegrees)`

Constructs a light that represents the Sun.

**Parameters:**
- `northAngleDegrees` (System.Double) — The angle of North in degrees. North is the angle between positive World Y axis and model North, as measured on World XY plane.
- `azimuthDegrees` (System.Double) — The Azimuth angle value in degrees. Azimuth is the compass angle from North.
- `altitudeDegrees` (System.Double) — The Altitude angle in degrees. Altitude is the angle above the ground plane.

**Returns:** `Light` — A new sun light.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Light_CreateSunLight_2.htm)

#### `public static Light CreateSunLight(Sun sun)`

Constructs a light which simulates a Sun.

**Parameters:**
- `sun` (Rhino.Render.Sun) — A Sun object from the Rhino.Render namespace.

**Returns:** `Light` — A light.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Light_CreateSunLight.htm)

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)`

Overridden in order to destroy local display cache information

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Dispose.htm)

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public double GetAttenuation(double d)`

Gets the attenuation settings (ignored for "directional" and "ambient" lights). attenuation = 1/(a0 + d*a1 + d^2*a2) where d = distance to light.

**Parameters:**
- `d` (System.Double) — The distance to evaluate.

**Returns:** `Double` — 0 if a0 + d*a1 + d^2*a2 <= 0.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Light_GetAttenuation.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)`

Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Angle of rotation in radians.
- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of the axis of rotation.
- `rotationCenter` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully rotated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Rotate.htm)

Scales the object by the specified factor. The scale is centered at the origin.

**Parameters:**
- `scaleFactor` (System.Double) — The uniform scaling factor.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully scaled.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Scale.htm)

#### `public void SetAttenuation(double a0, double a1, double a2)`

Sets the attenuation settings (ignored for "directional" and "ambient" lights). attenuation = 1/(a0 + d*a1 + d^2*a2) where d = distance to light.

**Parameters:**
- `a0` (System.Double) — The new constant attenuation divisor term.
- `a1` (System.Double) — The new reverse linear attenuation divisor term.
- `a2` (System.Double) — The new reverse quadratic attenuation divisor term.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Light_SetAttenuation.htm)

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.
