#### `public bool PerpendicularFrameAt(double t, out Plane plane)`

Return a 3d frame at a parameter. This is slightly different than FrameAt in that the frame is computed in a way so there is minimal rotation from one frame to the next.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Evaluation parameter.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The frame is returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PerpendicularFrameAt.htm)

#### `public Point3d PointAt(double t)`

Evaluates point at a curve parameter.

**Remarks:** No error handling.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Evaluation parameter.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — Point (location of curve at the parameter t).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PointAt.htm)

#### `public Point3d PointAtLength(double length)`

Gets a point at a certain length along the curve. The length must be non-negative and less than or equal to the length of the curve. Lengths will not be wrapped when the curve is closed or periodic.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Double) — Length along the curve between the start point and the returned point.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — Point on the curve at the specified length from the start point or Poin3d.Unset on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PointAtLength.htm)

#### `public Point3d PointAtNormalizedLength(double length)`

Gets a point at a certain normalized length along the curve. The length must be between or including 0.0 and 1.0, where 0.0 equals the start of the curve and 1.0 equals the end of the curve.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Double) — Normalized length along the curve between the start point and the returned point.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — Point on the curve at the specified normalized length from the start point or Poin3d.Unset on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PointAtNormalizedLength.htm)

#### `public Curve[] PullToBrepFace(BrepFace face, double tolerance)`

Pulls this curve to a brep face and returns the result of that operation.

**Parameters:**
- `face` (Rhino.Geometry.BrepFace) — A brep face.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value.

**Returns:** `Curve[]` — An array containing the resulting curves after pulling. This array could be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PullToBrepFace.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve PullToMesh(Mesh mesh, double tolerance)`

Makes a polyline approximation of the curve and gets the closest point on the mesh for each point on the curve. Then it "connects the points" so that you have a polyline on the mesh.

**Parameters:**
- `mesh` (Rhino.Geometry.Mesh) — Mesh to project onto.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Input tolerance (RhinoDoc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance is a good default)

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — A polyline curve on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_PullToMesh.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve Rebuild(int pointCount, int degree, bool preserveTangents)`

Rebuild a curve with a specific point count.

**Parameters:**
- `pointCount` (System.Int32) — Number of control points in the rebuild curve.
- `degree` (System.Int32) — Degree of curve. Valid values are between and including 1 and 11.
- `preserveTangents` (System.Boolean) — If true, the end tangents of the input curve will be preserved.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A NURBS curve on success or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Rebuild.htm)

#### `public bool RemoveShortSegments(double tolerance)`

Looks for segments that are shorter than tolerance that can be removed. Does not change the domain, but it will change the relative parameterization.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance which defines "short" segments.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if removable short segments were found. false if no removable short segments were found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_RemoveShortSegments.htm)

#### `public bool Reverse()`

Reverses the direction of the curve.

**Remarks:** If reversed, the domain changes from [a,b] to [-b,-a]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Reverse.htm)

#### `public Curve RibbonOffset(double distance, double blendRadius, Point3d directionPoint, Vector3d normal, double tolerance)`

Offsets a closed curve in the following way: pProject the curve to a plane with given normal. Then, loose Offset the projection by distance + blend_radius and trim off self-intersection. THen, Offset the remaining curve back in the opposite direction by blend_radius, filling gaps with blends. Finally, use the elevations of the input curve to get the correct elevations of the result.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — The positive distance to offset the curve.
- `blendRadius` (System.Double) — Positive, typically the same as distance. When the offset results in a self-intersection that gets trimmed off at a kink, the kink will be blended out using this radius.
- `directionPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point that indicates the direction of the offset. If the offset is inward, the point's projection to the plane should be well within the curve. It will be used to decide which part of the offset to keep if there are self-intersections.
- `normal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector that indicates the normal of the plane in which the offset will occur.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Used to determine self-intersections, not offset error.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The offset curve if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_RibbonOffset.htm)

#### `public Curve RibbonOffset(double distance, double blendRadius, Point3d directionPoint, Vector3d normal, double tolerance, out Curve[] crossSections, out Surface[] ruledSurfaces)`

Offsets a closed curve in the following way: pProject the curve to a plane with given normal. Then, loose Offset the projection by distance + blend_radius and trim off self-intersection. THen, Offset the remaining curve back in the opposite direction by blend_radius, filling gaps with blends. Finally, use the elevations of the input curve to get the correct elevations of the result.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — The positive distance to offset the curve.
- `blendRadius` (System.Double) — Positive, typically the same as distance. When the offset results in a self-intersection that gets trimmed off at a kink, the kink will be blended out using this radius.
- `directionPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point that indicates the direction of the offset. If the offset is inward, the point's projection to the plane should be well within the curve. It will be used to decide which part of the offset to keep if there are self-intersections.
- `normal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector that indicates the normal of the plane in which the offset will occur.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Used to determine self-intersections, not offset error.
- `crossSections` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve[]) — Contains lines between input and the offset that might be useful as input to Brep.CreateFromSweep or some other surface creation tool.
- `ruledSurfaces` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface[]) — Contain ruled surfaces between the input and the parts of the offset that correspond exactly. Note, there will be gaps between these at blends.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The offset curve if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_RibbonOffset_1.htm)

#### `public Curve RibbonOffset(double distance, double blendRadius, Point3d directionPoint, Vector3d normal, double tolerance, out double[] outputParameters, out double[] curveParameters)`

Offsets a closed curve in the following way: pProject the curve to a plane with given normal. Then, loose Offset the projection by distance + blend_radius and trim off self-intersection. THen, Offset the remaining curve back in the opposite direction by blend_radius, filling gaps with blends. Finally, use the elevations of the input curve to get the correct elevations of the result.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — The positive distance to offset the curve.
- `blendRadius` (System.Double) — Positive, typically the same as distance. When the offset results in a self-intersection that gets trimmed off at a kink, the kink will be blended out using this radius.
- `directionPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point that indicates the direction of the offset. If the offset is inward, the point's projection to the plane should be well within the curve. It will be used to decide which part of the offset to keep if there are self-intersections.
- `normal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector that indicates the normal of the plane in which the offset will occur.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Used to determine self-intersections, not offset error.
- `outputParameters` (System.Double[]) — A list of parameter, paired with curveParameters, from the output curve for creating cross sections.
- `curveParameters` (System.Double[]) — A list of parameter, paired with outputParameters, from the input curve for creating cross sections.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The offset curve if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_RibbonOffset_2.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)`

Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Angle of rotation in radians.
- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of the axis of rotation.
- `rotationCenter` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully rotated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Rotate.htm)

#### `public bool Scale(double scaleFactor)`

Scales the object by the specified factor. The scale is centered at the origin.

**Parameters:**
- `scaleFactor` (System.Double) — The uniform scaling factor.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully scaled.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Scale.htm)

#### `public bool SetEndPoint(Point3d point)`

Forces the curve to end at a specified point. Not all curve types support this operation.

**Remarks:** Some end points cannot be moved. Be sure to check return code

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — New end point of curve.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_SetEndPoint.htm)

#### `public bool SetStartPoint(Point3d point)`

Forces the curve to start at a specified point. Not all curve types support this operation.

**Remarks:** Some start points cannot be moved. Be sure to check return code.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — New start point of curve.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_SetStartPoint.htm)

#### `public bool SetUserString(string key, string value)`

Attach a user string (key,value combination) to this geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `key` (System.String) — id used to retrieve this string.
- `value` (System.String) — string associated with key.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_SetUserString.htm)

#### `public Curve Simplify(CurveSimplifyOptions options, double distanceTolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Returns a geometrically equivalent PolyCurve. The PolyCurve has the following properties 1. All the PolyCurve segments are LineCurve, PolylineCurve, ArcCurve, or NurbsCurve. 2. The NURBS Curves segments do not have fully multiple interior knots. 3. Rational NURBS curves do not have constant weights. 4. Any segment for which IsLinear() or IsArc() is true is a Line, Polyline segment, or an Arc. 5. Adjacent co-linear or co-circular segments are combined. 6. Segments that meet with G1-continuity have there ends tuned up so that they meet with G1-continuity to within machine precision.

**Parameters:**
- `options` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveSimplifyOptions) — Simplification options.
- `distanceTolerance` (System.Double) — A distance tolerance for the simplification.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — An angle tolerance for the simplification.

**Returns:** `Curve` — New simplified curve on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Simplify.htm)

#### `public Curve SimplifyEnd(CurveEnd end, CurveSimplifyOptions options, double distanceTolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Same as SimplifyCurve, but simplifies only the last two segments at "side" end.

**Parameters:**
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveEnd) — If CurveEnd.Start the function simplifies the last two start side segments, otherwise if CurveEnd.End the last two end side segments are simplified.
- `options` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveSimplifyOptions) — Simplification options.
- `distanceTolerance` (System.Double) — A distance tolerance for the simplification.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — An angle tolerance for the simplification.

**Returns:** `Curve` — New simplified curve on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_SimplifyEnd.htm)

#### `public Curve Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem)`

Smooths a curve by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the curve ends don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The smoothed curve if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Smooth.htm)

#### `public Curve Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem, Plane plane)`

Smooths a curve by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the curve ends don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — If SmoothingCoordinateSystem.CPlane specified, then the construction plane.

**Returns:** `Curve` — The smoothed curve if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Smooth_1.htm)

Get the domain of the curve span with the given index. Use the SpanCount property to test how many spans there are.

**Parameters:**
- `spanIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of span.

**Returns:** `Interval` — Interval of the span with the given index.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_SpanDomain.htm)

#### `[ObsoleteAttribute("Use version that takes an angle tolerance")] public Curve[] Split(Brep cutter, double tolerance)`

Splits a curve into pieces using a polysurface.

**Parameters:**
- `cutter` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — A cutting surface or polysurface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance for computing intersections.

**Returns:** `Curve[]` — An array of curves. This array can be empty.

#### `public PolyCurve ToArcsAndLines(double tolerance, double angleTolerance, double minimumLength, double maximumLength)`

Converts a curve into polycurve consisting of arc segments. Sections of the input curves that are nearly straight are converted to straight-line segments.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance. This is the maximum deviation from arc midpoints to the curve. When in doubt, use the document's model space absolute tolerance.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — The angle tolerance in radians. This is the maximum deviation of the arc end directions from the curve direction. When in doubt, use the document's model space angle tolerance.
- `minimumLength` (System.Double) — The minimum segment length.
- `maximumLength` (System.Double) — The maximum segment length.

**Returns:** `PolyCurve` — PolyCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToArcsAndLines.htm)

Constructs a NURBS curve representation of this curve.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — NURBS representation of the curve on success, null on failure.

#### `public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(double tolerance, double angleTolerance, double minimumLength, double maximumLength)`

Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance. This is the maximum deviation from line midpoints to the curve. When in doubt, use the document's model space absolute tolerance.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — The angle tolerance in radians. This is the maximum deviation of the line directions. When in doubt, use the document's model space angle tolerance.
- `minimumLength` (System.Double) — The minimum segment length.
- `maximumLength` (System.Double) — The maximum segment length.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — PolyCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToPolyline.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(int mainSegmentCount, int subSegmentCount, double maxAngleRadians, double maxChordLengthRatio, double maxAspectRatio, double tolerance, double minEdgeLength, double maxEdgeLength, bool keepStartPoint)`

Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `mainSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — If mainSegmentCount <= 0, then both subSegmentCount and mainSegmentCount are ignored. If mainSegmentCount > 0, then subSegmentCount must be >= 1. In this case the NURBS will be broken into mainSegmentCount equally spaced chords. If needed, each of these chords can be split into as many subSegmentCount sub-parts if the subdivision is necessary for the mesh to meet the other meshing constraints. In particular, if subSegmentCount = 0, then the curve is broken into mainSegmentCount pieces and no further testing is performed.
- `subSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — An amount of subsegments.
- `maxAngleRadians` (System.Double) — ( 0 to pi ) Maximum angle (in radians) between unit tangents at adjacent vertices.
- `maxChordLengthRatio` (System.Double) — Maximum permitted value of (distance chord midpoint to curve) / (length of chord).
- `maxAspectRatio` (System.Double) — If maxAspectRatio < 1.0, the parameter is ignored. If 1 <= maxAspectRatio < sqrt(2), it is treated as if maxAspectRatio = sqrt(2). This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of (length of longest chord) / (length of shortest chord).
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — If tolerance = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of the distance from the curve to the polyline.
- `minEdgeLength` (System.Double) — The minimum permitted edge length.
- `maxEdgeLength` (System.Double) — If maxEdgeLength = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted edge length.
- `keepStartPoint` (System.Boolean) — If true the starting point of the curve is added to the polyline. If false the starting point of the curve is not added to the polyline.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — PolylineCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToPolyline_1.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(int mainSegmentCount, int subSegmentCount, double maxAngleRadians, double maxChordLengthRatio, double maxAspectRatio, double tolerance, double minEdgeLength, double maxEdgeLength, bool keepStartPoint, Interval curveDomain)`

Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `mainSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — If mainSegmentCount <= 0, then both subSegmentCount and mainSegmentCount are ignored. If mainSegmentCount > 0, then subSegmentCount must be >= 1. In this case the NURBS will be broken into mainSegmentCount equally spaced chords. If needed, each of these chords can be split into as many subSegmentCount sub-parts if the subdivision is necessary for the mesh to meet the other meshing constraints. In particular, if subSegmentCount = 0, then the curve is broken into mainSegmentCount pieces and no further testing is performed.
- `subSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — An amount of subsegments.
- `maxAngleRadians` (System.Double) — ( 0 to pi ) Maximum angle (in radians) between unit tangents at adjacent vertices.
- `maxChordLengthRatio` (System.Double) — Maximum permitted value of (distance chord midpoint to curve) / (length of chord).
- `maxAspectRatio` (System.Double) — If maxAspectRatio < 1.0, the parameter is ignored. If 1 <= maxAspectRatio < sqrt(2), it is treated as if maxAspectRatio = sqrt(2). This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of (length of longest chord) / (length of shortest chord).
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — If tolerance = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of the distance from the curve to the polyline.
- `minEdgeLength` (System.Double) — The minimum permitted edge length.
- `maxEdgeLength` (System.Double) — If maxEdgeLength = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted edge length.
- `keepStartPoint` (System.Boolean) — If true the starting point of the curve is added to the polyline. If false the starting point of the curve is not added to the polyline.
- `curveDomain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — This sub-domain of the NURBS curve is approximated.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — PolylineCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToPolyline_2.htm)

#### `public double TorsionAt(double t)`

Evaluate the torsion of a curve at a parmeter. Sometimes also called the "second curvature", torsion is the rate of change of a curve's osculating plane.

**Remarks:** See Barrett O'Neill, Elementary Differential Geometry, page 69.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — The evaluation parameter.

**Returns:** `Double` — The torsion if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TorsionAt.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

- `domain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — Trimming interval. Portions of the curve before curve(domain[0]) and after curve(domain[1]) are removed.

#### `public bool TryGetPolyline(out Polyline polyline)`

Several types of Curve can have the form of a polyline including a degree 1 NurbsCurve, a PolylineCurve, and a PolyCurve all of whose segments are some form of polyline. IsPolyline tests a curve to see if it can be represented as a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline) — If true is returned, then the polyline form is returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this curve can be represented as a polyline; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TryGetPolyline.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetPolyline(out Polyline polyline, out double[] parameters)`

Several types of Curve can have the form of a polyline including a degree 1 NurbsCurve, a PolylineCurve, and a PolyCurve all of whose segments are some form of polyline. IsPolyline tests a curve to see if it can be represented as a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline) — If true is returned, then the polyline form is returned here.
- `parameters` (System.Double[]) — if true is returned, then the parameters of the polyline points are returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this curve can be represented as a polyline; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TryGetPolyline_1.htm)

### Properties
- `Degree` (Int32, get) — Gets the maximum algebraic degree of any span or a good estimate if curve spans are not algebraic.
- `Dimension` (Int32, get) — Gets the dimension of the object. The dimension is typically three. For parameter space trimming curves the dimension is two. In rare cases the dimension can be one or greater than three.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `Domain` (Interval, get/set) — Gets or sets the domain of the curve.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsClosed` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is a closed curve.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsPeriodic` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is considered to be Periodic.
- `IsSubDFriendly` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the curve is a cubic, non-rational, uniform NURBS curve that is either periodic or has natural end conditions. Othewise, false is returned.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `Line` (Line, get/set) — Gets or sets the Line value inside this curve.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `PointAtEnd` (Point3d, get) — Evaluates point at the end of the curve.
- `PointAtStart` (Point3d, get) — Evaluates point at the start of the curve.
- `SpanCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of non-empty smooth (c-infinity) spans in the curve.
- `TangentAtEnd` (Vector3d, get) — Evaluate unit tangent vector at the end of the curve.
- `TangentAtStart` (Vector3d, get) — Evaluates the unit tangent vector at the start of the curve.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## LinearDimension (class)

Represents a linear dimension

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_LinearDimension.htm)

- `public LinearDimension()` — Initializes a new instance of the LinearDimension class

Clears all overridden properties for this annotation

#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

#### `public static LinearDimension Create(AnnotationType dimtype, DimensionStyle dimStyle, Plane plane, Vector3d horizontal, Point3d defpoint1, Point3d defpoint2, Point3d dimlinepoint, double rotationInPlane)`

Initialize Dimension parameters

**Parameters:**
- `dimtype` (Rhino.Geometry.AnnotationType) — AnnotationType.Rotated or AnnotationType.Aligned
- `dimStyle` (Rhino.DocObjects.DimensionStyle) — Dimension's DimensionStyle
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Dimension's plane
- `horizontal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Horizontal reference direction
- `defpoint1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — First definition point
- `defpoint2` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Second definition point
- `dimlinepoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on dimension line
- `rotationInPlane` (System.Double) — For Rotated style

**Returns:** `LinearDimension` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.LinearDimension.Create(Rhino.Geometry.AnnotationType,Rhino.DocObjects.DimensionStyle,Rhino.Geometry.Plane,Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d,Rhino.Geometry.Point3d,Rhino.Geometry.Point3d,Rhino.Geometry.Point3d,System.Double)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_LinearDimension_Create.htm)

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)`

Overridden in order to destroy local display cache information

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Dispose.htm)

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public override BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to bounding box after calculation. The geometry is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AnnotationBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public bool SetOverrideDimStyle(DimensionStyle OverrideStyle)`

Set a style including overrides for this annotation object. The DimensionStyle OverrideStyle must have the override fields marked as overridden and must have it's Id set to nil. Use DimensinoStyle.SetFieldOverride(Field field) and related functions to manage override settings. To override a field, the field value must be set and the field must be marked as an override. The DimensionStyle passed in here must not be in the dimstyle table

- `OverrideStyle` (Rhino.DocObjects.DimensionStyle) — [Missing <param name="OverrideStyle"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.AnnotationBase.SetOverrideDimStyle(Rhino.DocObjects.DimensionStyle)"]

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

#### `public bool Translate(double x, double y, double z)`

Translates the object along the specified vector.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — The X component.
- `y` (System.Double) — The Y component.
- `z` (System.Double) — The Z component.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully translated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Translate_1.htm)

#### `public bool Translate(Vector3d translationVector)`

Translates the object along the specified vector.

**Parameters:**
- `translationVector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A moving vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully translated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Translate.htm)

- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.

- `MaskColorSource` (DimensionStyle.MaskType, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the mask background color is from the background or from a color
- `MaskEnabled` (Boolean, get/set) — Determines whether or not to draw a Text Mask
- `MaskFrame` (DimensionStyle.MaskFrame, get/set) — Gets or sets whether to draw a frame around a text mask
- `MaskOffset` (Double, get/set) — Offset for the border around text of the rectangle used to draw the mask. This value multiplied by TextHeight is the offset on each side of the tight rectangle around the text characters to the mask rectangle. The default value is 0.1.
- `MaskUsesViewportColor` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, the viewport's color is used for the mask color. If false, the color defined by MaskColor is used
- `NumericValue` (Double, get) — Gets the numeric value (or measurement), depending on geometry type. LinearDimension: distance between arrow tipsRadialDimension: radius or diameter depending on typeAngularDimension: angle in radians
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `ParentDimensionStyle` (DimensionStyle, get/set) — The parent dimension style associated with this annotation

- `PlainTextWithFields` (String, get) — Text stripped of RTF formatting information and with field expressions intact

- `Prefix` (String, get/set) — (Inherited from Dimension.)
- `RichText` (String, get/set) — Text including additional RTF formatting information
- `Suffix` (String, get/set) — (Inherited from Dimension.)
- `SuppressExtension1` (Boolean, get/set) — (Inherited from Dimension.)
- `SuppressExtension2` (Boolean, get/set) — (Inherited from Dimension.)

- `TextIsWrapped` (Boolean, get/set) — Is text wrapping on

## LoftType (enum)

Specifies enumerated constants for all supported loft types.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_LoftType.htm)

### Values
- `Normal` = `0` — Uses chord-length parameterization in the loft direction.
- `Loose` = `1` — The surface is allowed to move away from the original curves to make a smoother surface. The surface control points are created at the same locations as the control points of the loft input curves.
- `Tight` = `2` — The surface sticks closely to the original curves. Uses square root of chord-length parameterization in the loft direction.
- `Straight` = `3` — The sections between the curves are straight. This is also known as a ruled surface.
- `Developable` = `4` — Obsolete. Obsolete, do not use.
- `Uniform` = `5` — Constructs a uniform loft. The object knot vectors will be uniform.

## Matrix (class)

Represents an arbitrarily sized matrix of double-precision floating point numbers. If you are working with a 4x4 matrix, then you may want to use the Transform class instead.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Matrix(Transform xform)` — Initializes a new instance of the matrix based on a 4x4 matrix Transform.
- `public Matrix(int rowCount, int columnCount)` — Initializes a new instance of the matrix.

### Methods
#### `public static Matrix operator +(Matrix a, Matrix b)`

Adds two matrices and returns a new sum matrix.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Matrix) — A first matrix to use in calculation.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Matrix) — Another matrix to use in calculation.

**Returns:** `Matrix` — The sum matrix.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public double[] BackSolve(double zeroTolerance, double[] b)`

Solves M*x=b where M is upper triangular with a unit diagonal and b is a column of values.

**Parameters:**
- `zeroTolerance` (System.Double) — (>=0.0) used to test for "zero" values in b in under determined systems of equations.
- `b` (System.Double[]) — The values in B[RowCount],...,B[B.Length-1] are tested to make sure they are within "zeroTolerance".

**Returns:** `Double[]` — Array of length ColumnCount on success. null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_BackSolve.htm)

#### `public Point3d[] BackSolvePoints(double zeroTolerance, Point3d[] b)`

Solves M*x=b where M is upper triangular with a unit diagonal and b is a column of 3d points.

**Parameters:**
- `zeroTolerance` (System.Double) — (>=0.0) used to test for "zero" values in b in under determined systems of equations.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d[]) — The values in B[RowCount],...,B[B.Length-1] are tested to make sure they are "zero".

**Returns:** `Point3d[]` — Array of length ColumnCount on success. null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_BackSolvePoints.htm)

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_Dispose_1.htm)

#### `public Matrix Duplicate()`

Create a duplicate of this matrix.

**Returns:** `Matrix` — An exact duplicate of this matrix.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_Duplicate.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_Finalize.htm)

#### `public override int GetHashCode()`

Gets the hash code for this matrix. The hash code will change when the matrix changes so you cannot change matrices while they are stored in hash tables.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Hash code.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_GetHashCode.htm)

#### `public bool Invert(double zeroTolerance)`

Modifies this matrix to become its own inverse. Matrix might be non-invertible (singular) and the return value will be false.

**Parameters:**
- `zeroTolerance` (System.Double) — The admitted tolerance for 0.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_Invert.htm)

#### `public static Matrix operator *(Matrix a, Matrix b)`

Multiplies two matrices and returns a new product matrix.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Matrix) — A first matrix to use in calculation.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Matrix) — Another matrix to use in calculation.

**Returns:** `Matrix` — The product matrix.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_op_Multiply.htm)

#### `public int RowReduce(double zeroTolerance, out double determinant, out double pivot)`

Row reduces a matrix to calculate rank and determinant.

**Remarks:** The matrix itself is row reduced so that the result is an upper triangular matrix with 1's on the diagonal.

**Parameters:**
- `zeroTolerance` (System.Double) — (>=0.0) zero tolerance for pivot test. If a the absolute value of a pivot is <= zeroTolerance, then the pivot is assumed to be zero.
- `determinant` (System.Double) — value of determinant is returned here.
- `pivot` (System.Double) — value of the smallest pivot is returned here.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Rank of the matrix.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_RowReduce_1.htm)

#### `public int RowReduce(double zeroTolerance, double[] b, out double pivot)`

Row reduces a matrix as the first step in solving M*X=b where b is a column of values.

**Remarks:** The matrix itself is row reduced so that the result is an upper triangular matrix with 1's on the diagonal.

**Parameters:**
- `zeroTolerance` (System.Double) — (>=0.0) zero tolerance for pivot test. If the absolute value of a pivot is <= zero_tolerance, then the pivot is assumed to be zero.
- `b` (System.Double[]) — an array of RowCount values that is row reduced with the matrix.
- `pivot` (System.Double) — the value of the smallest pivot is returned here.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Rank of the matrix.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_RowReduce_2.htm)

#### `public int RowReduce(double zeroTolerance, Point3d[] b, out double pivot)`

Row reduces a matrix as the first step in solving M*X=b where b is a column of 3d points.

**Remarks:** The matrix itself is row reduced so that the result is an upper triangular matrix with 1's on the diagonal.

**Parameters:**
- `zeroTolerance` (System.Double) — (>=0.0) zero tolerance for pivot test. If the absolute value of a pivot is <= zero_tolerance, then the pivot is assumed to be zero.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d[]) — An array of RowCount 3d points that is row reduced with the matrix.
- `pivot` (System.Double) — The value of the smallest pivot is returned here.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Rank of the matrix.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_RowReduce.htm)

#### `public void Scale(double s)`

Modifies the current matrix by multiplying its values by a number.

**Parameters:**
- `s` (System.Double) — A scale factor.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_Scale.htm)

#### `public void SetDiagonal(double d)`

Sets diagonal value and zeros off all non-diagonal values.

**Parameters:**
- `d` (System.Double) — The new diagonal value.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_SetDiagonal.htm)

#### `public bool SwapColumns(int columnA, int columnB)`

Exchanges two columns.

**Parameters:**
- `columnA` (System.Int32) — A first column.
- `columnB` (System.Int32) — Another column.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_SwapColumns.htm)

#### `public bool SwapRows(int rowA, int rowB)`

Exchanges two rows.

**Parameters:**
- `rowA` (System.Int32) — A first row.
- `rowB` (System.Int32) — Another row.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_SwapRows.htm)

#### `public bool Transpose()`

Modifies this matrix to be its transpose. This is like swapping rows with columns.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transpose

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_Transpose.htm)

#### `public void Zero()`

Sets all values inside the matrix to zero.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Matrix_Zero.htm)

### Properties
- `ColumnCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of columns.
- `IsColumnOrthogonal` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the matrix is column orthogonal.
- `IsColumnOrthoNormal` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the matrix is column orthonormal.
- `IsRowOrthogonal` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the matrix is row orthogonal.
- `IsRowOrthoNormal` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the matrix is row orthonormal.
- `IsSquare` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether this matrix has the same number of rows and columns. 0x0 matrices are not considered square.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether this matrix is valid.
- `Item` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the matrix value at the given row and column indices.
- `RowCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of rows.

## Mesh (class)

Represents a geometry type that is defined by vertices and faces. This is often called a face-vertex mesh.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Mesh()` — Initializes a new empty mesh.
- `protected Mesh(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Protected constructor for internal use.

### Methods
#### `public void Append(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes)`

Append a list of meshes. This function is much more efficient than making repeated calls to Mesh.Append(Mesh) when lots of meshes are being joined into a single large mesh.

**Parameters:**
- `meshes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Mesh>) — Meshes to append to this one.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Append_1.htm)

#### `public void Append(Mesh other)`

Appends a copy of another mesh to this one and updates indices of appended mesh parts.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Mesh) — Mesh to append to this one.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Append.htm)

#### `public bool Check(TextLog textLog, ref MeshCheckParameters parameters)`

Examines the mesh and logs a description of what it finds right or wrong. The various properties the function checks for are described in MeshCheckParameters.

**Parameters:**
- `textLog` (Rhino.FileIO.TextLog) — The text log
- `parameters` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshCheckParameters) — The mesh checking parameter and results.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Returns true if the mesh is valid, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Check.htm)

#### `public void ClearSurfaceData()`

Removes surface parameters, curvature parameters and surface statistics from the mesh.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ClearSurfaceData.htm)

#### `public void ClearTextureData()`

Removes all texture coordinate information from this mesh.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ClearTextureData.htm)

#### `public MeshPoint ClosestMeshPoint(Point3d testPoint, double maximumDistance)`

Gets the point on the mesh that is closest to a given test point. Similar to the ClosestPoint function except this returns a MeshPoint class which includes extra information beyond just the location of the closest point.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The source of the search.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — Optional upper bound on the distance from test point to the mesh. If you are only interested in finding a point Q on the mesh when testPoint.DistanceTo(Q) < maximumDistance, then set maximumDistance to that value. This parameter is ignored if you pass 0.0 for a maximumDistance.

**Returns:** `MeshPoint` — closest point information on success. null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ClosestMeshPoint.htm)

#### `public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint)`

Gets the point on the mesh that is closest to a given test point.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to search for.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on the mesh closest to testPoint, or Point3d.Unset on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public int ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out Point3d pointOnMesh, double maximumDistance)`

Gets the point on the mesh that is closest to a given test point.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to search for.
- `pointOnMesh` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the mesh closest to testPoint.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — Optional upper bound on the distance from test point to the mesh. If you are only interested in finding a point Q on the mesh when testPoint.DistanceTo(Q) < maximumDistance, then set maximumDistance to that value. This parameter is ignored if you pass 0.0 for a maximumDistance.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Index of face that the closest point lies on if successful. -1 if not successful; the value of pointOnMesh is undefined.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ClosestPoint_2.htm)

#### `public int ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out Point3d pointOnMesh, out Vector3d normalAtPoint, double maximumDistance)`

Gets the point on the mesh that is closest to a given test point.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to search for.
- `pointOnMesh` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the mesh closest to testPoint.
- `normalAtPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The normal vector of the mesh at the closest point.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — Optional upper bound on the distance from test point to the mesh. If you are only interested in finding a point Q on the mesh when testPoint.DistanceTo(Q) < maximumDistance, then set maximumDistance to that value. This parameter is ignored if you pass 0.0 for a maximumDistance.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Index of face that the closest point lies on if successful. -1 if not successful; the value of pointOnMesh is undefined.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ClosestPoint_1.htm)

#### `public int CollapseFacesByArea(double lessThanArea, double greaterThanArea)`

Collapses multiple mesh faces, with areas less than LessThanArea and greater than GreaterThanArea, based on the principles found in Stan Melax's mesh reduction PDF, see http://pomax.nihongoresources.com/downloads/PolygonReduction.pdf

**Remarks:** This number may differ from the initial number of faces that meet the input criteria because the areas of some initial faces may be altered as other faces are collapsed. The face area must be both less than LessThanArea AND greater than GreaterThanArea in order to be considered. Use large numbers for lessThanArea or zero for greaterThanArea to simulate an OR.

**Parameters:**
- `lessThanArea` (System.Double) — Area in which faces are selected if their area is less than or equal to.
- `greaterThanArea` (System.Double) — Area in which faces are selected if their area is greater than or equal to.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of faces that were collapsed in the process.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CollapseFacesByArea.htm)

#### `public int CollapseFacesByByAspectRatio(double aspectRatio)`

Collapses a multiple mesh faces, determined by face aspect ratio, based on criteria found in Stan Melax's polygon reduction, see http://pomax.nihongoresources.com/downloads/PolygonReduction.pdf

**Remarks:** This number may differ from the initial number of faces that meet the input criteria because the aspect ratios of some initial faces may be altered as other faces are collapsed.

**Parameters:**
- `aspectRatio` (System.Double) — Faces with an aspect ratio less than aspectRatio are considered as candidates.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of faces that were collapsed in the process.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CollapseFacesByByAspectRatio.htm)

#### `public int CollapseFacesByEdgeLength(bool bGreaterThan, double edgeLength)`

Collapses multiple mesh faces, with greater/less than edge length, based on the principles found in Stan Melax's mesh reduction PDF, see http://pomax.nihongoresources.com/downloads/PolygonReduction.pdf

**Remarks:** This number may differ from the initial number of edges that meet the input criteria because the lengths of some initial edges may be altered as other edges are collapsed.

**Parameters:**
- `bGreaterThan` (System.Boolean) — Determines whether edge with lengths greater than or less than edgeLength are collapsed.
- `edgeLength` (System.Double) — Length with which to compare to edge lengths.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of edges (faces) that were collapsed, -1 for general failure (like bad topology or index out of range) or -2 if all of the edges would be collapsed and the resulting mesh would be invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CollapseFacesByEdgeLength.htm)

#### `public Color ColorAt(int faceIndex, double t0, double t1, double t2, double t3)`

Evaluate a mesh normal at a set of barycentric coordinates. Barycentric coordinates must be assigned in accordance with the rules as defined by MeshPoint.T.

**Remarks:** Coordinate 0,0,0,0 is not a valid set of barycentric coordinates. The sum of t0 to t3 should be 1.

**Parameters:**
- `faceIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of triangle or quad to evaluate.
- `t0` (System.Double) — First barycentric coordinate.
- `t1` (System.Double) — Second barycentric coordinate.
- `t2` (System.Double) — Third barycentric coordinate.
- `t3` (System.Double) — Fourth barycentric coordinate. If the face is a triangle, this coordinate will be ignored.

**Returns:** `Color` — The interpolated vertex color on the mesh or Color.Transparent if the faceIndex is not valid, if the barycentric coordinates could not be evaluated, or if there are no colors defined on the mesh.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ColorAt_1.htm)

#### `public Color ColorAt(MeshPoint meshPoint)`

Evaluate a mesh color at a set of barycentric coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `meshPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshPoint) — MeshPoint instance containing a valid Face Index and Barycentric coordinates.

**Returns:** `Color` — The interpolated vertex color on the mesh or Color.Transparent if the faceIndex is not valid, if the barycentric coordinates could not be evaluated, or if there are no colors defined on the mesh.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ColorAt.htm)

#### `public bool Compact()`

Removes any unreferenced objects from arrays, re-indexes as needed and shrinks arrays to minimum required size.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Compact.htm)

#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

- `maximumThickness` (System.Double) — Maximum thickness to consider. Use as small a thickness as possible to speed up the solver.

**Returns:** `MeshThicknessMeasurement[]` — Array of thickness measurements.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ComputeThickness_2.htm)

#### `public static Curve[] CreateContourCurves(Mesh meshToContour, Plane sectionPlane, double tolerance)`

Constructs contour curves for a mesh, sectioned at a plane.

**Parameters:**
- `meshToContour` (Rhino.Geometry.Mesh) — A mesh to contour.
- `sectionPlane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — A cutting plane.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value. If negative, the positive value will be used. WARNING! Good tolerance values are in the magnitude of 10^-7, or RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon*10. See comments at MeshIntersectionsTolerancesCoefficient

**Returns:** `Curve[]` — An array of curves. This array can be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreateContourCurves_1.htm)

- `interval` (System.Double) — Avoid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreateContourCurves_2.htm)

- `contourEnd` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — An end point of the contouring axis.
- `interval` (System.Double) — An interval distance.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value. If negative, the positive value will be used. WARNING! Good tolerance values are in the magnitude of 10^-7, or RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon*10. See comments at MeshIntersectionsTolerancesCoefficient

**Returns:** `Curve[]` — An array of curves. This array can be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreateContourCurves_3.htm)

#### `public static Mesh CreateFromCurveExtrusion(Curve curve, Vector3d direction, MeshingParameters parameters, BoundingBox boundingBox)`

Constructs a mesh from an extruded curve.

**Remarks:** This method is designed to be used when projecting curves onto a mesh. In general, a better solution is to use extrude the curve into a surface using CreateExtrusion(Curve, Vector3d), and then create a mesh from the surface using CreateFromSurface(Surface). The Extrusion and SumSurface classes can also be used to create extrusions.

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — A curve to extrude.
- `direction` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The direction of extrusion.
- `parameters` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshingParameters) — The parameters of meshing.
- `boundingBox` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — The bounding box controls the length of the extrusion.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreateFromCurveExtrusion.htm)

Constructs a new mesh pipe from a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — A curve to pipe.
- `radius` (System.Double) — The radius of the pipe.
- `segments` (System.Int32) — The number of segments in the pipe.
- `accuracy` (System.Int32) — The accuracy of the pipe.
- `capType` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshPipeCapStyle) — The type of cap to be created at the end of the pipe.
- `faceted` (System.Boolean) — Specifies whether the pipe is faceted, or not.
- `intervals` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Interval>) — A series of intervals to pipe. This value can be null.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreateFromCurvePipe.htm)

#### `public static Mesh CreateFromFilteredFaceList(Mesh original, IEnumerable<bool> inclusion)`

Constructs a sub-mesh, that contains a filtered list of faces.

**Parameters:**
- `original` (Rhino.Geometry.Mesh) — The mesh to copy. This item can be null, and in this case an empty mesh is returned.
- `inclusion` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Boolean>) — A series of true and false values, that determine if each face is used in the new mesh. If the amount does not match the length of the face list, the pattern is repeated. If it exceeds the amount of faces in the mesh face list, the pattern is truncated. This items can be null or empty, and the mesh will simply be duplicated.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Mesh.CreateFromFilteredFaceList(Rhino.Geometry.Mesh,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Boolean})"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreateFromFilteredFaceList.htm)

- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A minimum distance for clean vertices.

#### `public static Mesh CreateFromTessellation(IEnumerable<Point3d> points, IEnumerable<IEnumerable<Point3d>> edges, Plane plane, bool allowNewVertices)`

Attempts to create a mesh that is a triangulation of a list of points, projected on a plane, including its holes and fixed edges.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — A list, an array or any enumerable of points.
- `edges` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<IEnumerable<Point3d>>) — A list of polylines, or other lists of points representing edges. This can be null. If nested enumerable items are null, they will be discarded.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — A plane.
- `allowNewVertices` (System.Boolean) — If true, the mesh might have more vertices than the list of input points, if doing so will improve long thin triangles.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh, or null if not successful.

- `vertical` (System.Int32) — Number of faces in the top-to-bottom direction.
- `around` (System.Int32) — Number of faces around the torus.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — Returns a mesh torus if successful, null otherwise.

#### `public static Mesh CreateIcoSphere(Sphere sphere, int subdivisions)`

Constructs a icospherical mesh. A mesh icosphere differs from a standard UV mesh sphere in that it's vertices are evenly distributed. A mesh icosphere starts from an icosahedron (a regular polyhedron with 20 equilateral triangles). It is then refined by splitting each triangle into 4 smaller triangles. This splitting can be done several times.

**Parameters:**
- `sphere` (Rhino.Geometry.Sphere) — The input sphere provides the orienting plane and radius.
- `subdivisions` (System.Int32) — The number of times you want the faces split, where 0 <= subdivisions <= 7. Note, the total number of mesh faces produces is: 20 * (4 ^ subdivisions)

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A welded mesh icosphere if successful, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreateIcoSphere.htm)

#### `public bool CreatePartitions(int maximumVertexCount, int maximumTriangleCount)`

In ancient times (or modern smart phone times), some rendering engines were only able to process small batches of triangles and the CreatePartitions() function was provided to partition the mesh into subsets of vertices and faces that those rendering engines could handle.

**Parameters:**
- `maximumVertexCount` (System.Int32) — [Missing <param name="maximumVertexCount"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Mesh.CreatePartitions(System.Int32,System.Int32)"]
- `maximumTriangleCount` (System.Int32) — [Missing <param name="maximumTriangleCount"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Mesh.CreatePartitions(System.Int32,System.Int32)"]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreatePartitions.htm)

#### `public static Mesh CreatePatch(Polyline outerBoundary, double angleToleranceRadians, Surface pullbackSurface, IEnumerable<Curve> innerBoundaryCurves, IEnumerable<Curve> innerBothSideCurves, IEnumerable<Point3d> innerPoints, bool trimback, int divisions)`

Construct a mesh patch from a variety of input geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `outerBoundary` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline) — (optional: can be null) Outer boundary polyline, if provided this will become the outer boundary of the resulting mesh. Any of the input that is completely outside the outer boundary will be ignored and have no impact on the result. If any of the input intersects the outer boundary the result will be unpredictable and is likely to not include the entire outer boundary.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Maximum angle between unit tangents and adjacent vertices. Used to divide curve inputs that cannot otherwise be represented as a polyline.
- `pullbackSurface` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — (optional: can be null) Initial surface where 3d input will be pulled to make a 2d representation used by the function that generates the mesh. Providing a pullbackSurface can be helpful when it is similar in shape to the pattern of the input, the pulled 2d points will be a better representation of the 3d points. If all of the input is more or less coplanar to start with, providing pullbackSurface has no real benefit.
- `innerBoundaryCurves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — (optional: can be null) Polylines to create holes in the output mesh. If innerBoundaryCurves are the only input then the result may be null if trimback is set to false (see comments for trimback) because the resulting mesh could be invalid (all faces created contained vertexes from the perimeter boundary).
- `innerBothSideCurves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — (optional: can be null) These polylines will create faces on both sides of the edge. If there are only input points(innerPoints) there is no way to guarantee a triangulation that will create an edge between two particular points. Adding a line, or polyline, to innerBothsideCurves that includes points from innerPoints will help guide the triangulation.
- `innerPoints` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — (optional: can be null) Points to be used to generate the mesh. If outerBoundary is not null, points outside of that boundary after it has been pulled to pullbackSurface (or the best plane through the input if pullbackSurface is null) will be ignored.
- `trimback` (System.Boolean) — Only used when a outerBoundary has not been provided. When that is the case, the function uses the perimeter of the surface as the outer boundary instead. If true, any face of the resulting triangulated mesh that contains a vertex of the perimeter boundary will be removed.
- `divisions` (System.Int32) — Only used when a outerBoundary has not been provided. When that is the case, division becomes the number of divisions each side of the surface's perimeter will be divided into to create an outer boundary to work with.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — mesh on success; null on failure

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreatePatch.htm)

#### `public static Mesh CreateQuadSphere(Sphere sphere, int subdivisions)`

Constructs a quad mesh sphere. A quad mesh sphere differs from a standard UV mesh sphere in that it's vertices are evenly distributed. A quad mesh sphere starts from a cube (a regular polyhedron with 6 square sides). It is then refined by splitting each quad into 4 smaller quads. This splitting can be done several times.

**Parameters:**
- `sphere` (Rhino.Geometry.Sphere) — The input sphere provides the orienting plane and radius.
- `subdivisions` (System.Int32) — The number of times you want the faces split, where 0 <= subdivisions <= 8. Note, the total number of mesh faces produces is: 6 * (4 ^ subdivisions)

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A welded quad mesh sphere if successful, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_CreateQuadSphere.htm)

- `mapping` (Rhino.Render.TextureMapping) — The texture mapping to be used on the mesh. Surface parameter mapping is assumed if null - but surface parameters must be available on the mesh.
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Local mapping transform for the mesh mapping. Use identity for surface parameter mapping.
- `bitmap` (System.Drawing.Bitmap) — The bitmap to use for the colors.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Mesh.CreateVertexColorsFromBitmap(Rhino.RhinoDoc,Rhino.Render.TextureMapping,Rhino.Geometry.Transform,System.Drawing.Bitmap)"]

#### `public Mesh DuplicateMesh()`

Constructs a copy of this mesh. This is the same as Duplicate().

**Returns:** `Mesh` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Mesh.DuplicateMesh"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_DuplicateMesh.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `public Mesh[] ExplodeAtUnweldedEdges()`

Explode the mesh into sub-meshes where a sub-mesh is a collection of faces that are contained within a closed loop of "unwelded" edges. Unwelded edges are edges where the faces that share the edge have unique mesh vertexes (not mesh topology vertexes) at both ends of the edge.

**Returns:** `Mesh[]` — Array of sub-meshes on success; null on error. If the count in the returned array is 1, then nothing happened and the output is essentially a copy of the input.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_ExplodeAtUnweldedEdges.htm)

#### `public bool FileHole(int topologyEdgeIndex)`

Given a starting "naked" edge index, this function attempts to determine a "hole" by chaining additional naked edges together until if returns to the start index. Then it triangulates the closed polygon and either adds the faces to the mesh.

**Parameters:**
- `topologyEdgeIndex` (System.Int32) — Starting naked edge index.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_FileHole.htm)

#### `public bool FillHoles()`

Attempts to determine "holes" in the mesh by chaining naked edges together. Then it triangulates the closed polygons adds the faces to the mesh.

**Remarks:** This function does not differentiate between inner and outer naked edges. If you need that, it would be better to use Mesh.FillHole.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_FillHoles.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

Returns all edges of a mesh that are considered "naked" in the sense that the edge only has one face.

Retrieves the count of items that GetNgonAndFacesEnumerable() will provide.
