#### `public IEnumerable<MeshNgon> GetNgonAndFacesEnumerable()`

Retrieves a complete enumerable, i.e., one that provides an iterator over every face that is present, no matter if defined as a triangle, a quad, or a strictly over-four-sided ngon.

**Returns:** `IEnumerable<MeshNgon>` — The enumerator capable of enumerating through Mesh.Ngons> Mesh.Ngons and Faces

Constructs the outlines of a mesh projected against a plane.

- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — A plane to project against.

**Returns:** `Polyline[]` — An array of polylines, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_GetOutlines_2.htm)

#### `public Polyline[] GetOutlines(RhinoViewport viewport)`

Constructs the outlines of a mesh. The projection information in the viewport is used to determine how the outlines are projected.

**Parameters:**
- `viewport` (Rhino.Display.RhinoViewport) — A viewport to determine projection direction.

**Returns:** `Polyline[]` — An array of polylines, or null on error.

#### `public Polyline[] GetOutlines(ViewportInfo viewportInfo, Plane plane)`

Constructs the outlines of a mesh.

**Parameters:**
- `viewportInfo` (Rhino.DocObjects.ViewportInfo) — The viewport info that provides the outline direction.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Usually the view's construction plane. If a parallel projection and view plane is parallel to this, then project the results to the plane.

**Returns:** `Polyline[]` — An array of polylines, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_GetOutlines_1.htm)

#### `public bool GetSelfIntersections(double tolerance, out Polyline[] perforations, bool overlapsPolylines, out Polyline[] overlapsPolylinesResult, bool overlapsMesh, out Mesh overlapsMeshResult, TextLog textLog, CancellationToken cancel, IProgress<double> progress)`

Gets the intersections of this mesh with itself.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value. If negative, the positive value will be used. WARNING! Good tolerance values are in the magnitude of 10^-7, or RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon*10.
- `perforations` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline[]) — The array of perforations.
- `overlapsPolylines` (System.Boolean) — If true, the next argument is computed.
- `overlapsPolylinesResult` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline[]) — The array of overlaps, in terms of polylines.
- `overlapsMesh` (System.Boolean) — If true, the next argument is computed.
- `overlapsMeshResult` (Rhino.Geometry.Mesh) — A mesh that represents the overlaps in terms of surfaces.
- `textLog` (Rhino.FileIO.TextLog) — A report of the outcome.
- `cancel` (System.Threading.CancellationToken) — A cancellation token.
- `progress` (System.IProgress<Double>) — A progress reporter.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — An array of intersection and overlaps polylines.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_GetSelfIntersections.htm)

#### `public MeshUnsafeLock GetUnsafeLock(bool writable)`

Allows to obtain unsafe pointers to the underlying unmanaged data structures of the mesh.

**Remarks:** The lock implements the IDisposable interface, and one call of its Dispose() or ReleaseUnsafeLock(MeshUnsafeLock) will update the data structure as required. This can be achieved with a using statement (Using in Vb.Net).

**Parameters:**
- `writable` (System.Boolean) — true if user will need to write onto the structure. false otherwise.

**Returns:** `MeshUnsafeLock` — A lock that needs to be released.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_GetUnsafeLock.htm)

#### `public string GetUserString(string key)`

Gets user string from this geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `key` (System.String) — id used to retrieve the string.

**Returns:** `String` — string associated with the key if successful. null if no key was found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetUserString.htm)

#### `public NameValueCollection GetUserStrings()`

Gets a copy of all (user key string, user value string) pairs attached to this geometry.

**Returns:** `NameValueCollection` — A new collection.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetUserStrings.htm)

#### `public bool HealNakedEdges(double distance)`

Attempts to "heal" naked edges in a mesh based on a given distance. First attempts to move vertexes to neighboring vertexes that are within that distance away. Then it finds edges that have a closest point to the vertex within the distance and splits the edge. When it finds one it splits the edge and makes two new edges using that point.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — Distance to not exceed when modifying the mesh.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_HealNakedEdges.htm)

#### `public bool IsManifold()`

Returns true if every mesh "edge" has at most two faces.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the mesh is manifold, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_IsManifold.htm)

#### `public bool IsManifold(bool topologicalTest, out bool isOriented, out bool hasBoundary)`

Gets a value indicating whether or not the mesh is manifold. A manifold mesh does not have any edge that borders more than two faces.

**Parameters:**
- `topologicalTest` (System.Boolean) — If true, the query treats coincident vertices as the same.
- `isOriented` (System.Boolean) — isOriented will be set to true if the mesh is a manifold and adjacent faces have compatible face normals.
- `hasBoundary` (System.Boolean) — hasBoundary will be set to true if the mesh is a manifold and there is at least one "edge" with no more than one adjacent face.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if every mesh "edge" has at most two adjacent faces.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_IsManifold_1.htm)

#### `public bool IsPointInside(Point3d point, double tolerance, bool strictlyIn)`

Determines if a point is inside a solid mesh.

**Remarks:** The caller is responsible for making certain the mesh is valid, closed, and 2-manifold before calling this function. If the mesh is not, the behavior is undetermined.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — 3d point to test.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — (>=0) 3d distance tolerance used for ray-mesh intersection and determining strict inclusion. This is expected to be a tiny value.
- `strictlyIn` (System.Boolean) — If strictlyIn is true, then point must be inside mesh by at least tolerance in order for this function to return true. If strictlyIn is false, then this function will return true if point is inside or the distance from point to a mesh face is <= tolerance.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if point is inside the solid mesh, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_IsPointInside.htm)

#### `public bool IsValidWithLog(out string log)`

Determines if an object is valid. Also provides a report on errors if this object happens not to be valid.

**Parameters:**
- `log` (System.String) — A textual log. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this object is valid; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_IsValidWithLog.htm)

#### `public bool MakeDeformable()`

If possible, converts the object into a form that can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, an non-uniform scaling.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — false if object cannot be converted to a deformable object. true if object was already deformable or was converted into a deformable object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_MakeDeformable.htm)

#### `public bool MatchEdges(double distance, bool rachet)`

Moves face edges of an open mesh to meet adjacent face edges. The method will first try to match vertices, and then then it will try to split edges to make the edges match.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — The distance tolerance. Use larger tolerances only if you select specific edges to close.
- `rachet` (System.Boolean) — If true, matching the mesh takes place in four passes starting at a tolerance that is smaller than your specified tolerance and working up to the specified tolerance with successive passes. This matches small edges first and works up to larger edges. If false, then a single pass is made.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True of edges were matched, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_MatchEdges.htm)

#### `public uint MemoryEstimate()`

Computes an estimate of the number of bytes that this object is using in memory.

**Returns:** `UInt32` — An estimated memory footprint.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_MemoryEstimate.htm)

#### `public bool MergeAllCoplanarFaces(double tolerance)`

Merges adjacent coplanar faces into single faces.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for determining when edges are adjacent. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAbsoluteTolerance property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if faces were merged, false if no faces were merged.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_MergeAllCoplanarFaces.htm)

#### `public bool MergeAllCoplanarFaces(double tolerance, double angleTolerance)`

Merges adjacent coplanar faces into single faces.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance for determining when edges are adjacent. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAbsoluteTolerance property.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — Angle tolerance, in radians, for determining when faces are parallel. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAngleToleranceRadians property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if faces were merged, false if no faces were merged.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_MergeAllCoplanarFaces_1.htm)

#### `protected override void NonConstOperation()`

Clear local cache on non constant calls

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_NonConstOperation.htm)

#### `public Vector3d NormalAt(int faceIndex, double t0, double t1, double t2, double t3)`

Evaluate a mesh normal at a set of barycentric coordinates. Barycentric coordinates must be assigned in accordance with the rules as defined by MeshPoint.T.

**Parameters:**
- `faceIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of triangle or quad to evaluate.
- `t0` (System.Double) — First barycentric coordinate.
- `t1` (System.Double) — Second barycentric coordinate.
- `t2` (System.Double) — Third barycentric coordinate.
- `t3` (System.Double) — Fourth barycentric coordinate. If the face is a triangle, this coordinate will be ignored.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A Normal vector to the mesh or Vector3d.Unset if the faceIndex is not valid or if the barycentric coordinates could not be evaluated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_NormalAt_1.htm)

#### `public Vector3d NormalAt(MeshPoint meshPoint)`

Evaluate a mesh normal at a set of barycentric coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `meshPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshPoint) — MeshPoint instance containing a valid Face Index and Barycentric coordinates.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A Normal vector to the mesh or Vector3d.Unset if the faceIndex is not valid or if the barycentric coordinates could not be evaluated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_NormalAt.htm)

#### `public Mesh Offset(double distance)`

Makes a new mesh with vertices offset a distance in the opposite direction of the existing vertex normals. Same as Mesh.Offset(distance, false)

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — A distance value to use for offsetting.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh on success, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Offset.htm)

#### `public Mesh Offset(double distance, bool solidify)`

Makes a new mesh with vertices offset a distance in the opposite direction of the existing vertex normals. Optionally, based on the value of solidify, adds the input mesh and a ribbon of faces along any naked edges. If solidify is false it acts exactly as the Offset(distance) function.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — A distance value.
- `solidify` (System.Boolean) — true if the mesh should be solidified.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh on success, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Offset_1.htm)

#### `public Mesh Offset(double distance, bool solidify, Vector3d direction)`

Makes a new mesh with vertices offset a distance along the direction parameter. Optionally, based on the value of solidify, adds the input mesh and a ribbon of faces along any naked edges. If solidify is false it acts exactly as the Offset(distance) function.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — A distance value.
- `solidify` (System.Boolean) — true if the mesh should be solidified.
- `direction` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of offset for all vertices.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh on success, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Offset_2.htm)

#### `public Mesh Offset(double distance, bool solidify, Vector3d direction, out List<int> wallFacesOut)`

Makes a new mesh with vertices offset a distance along the direction parameter. Optionally, based on the value of solidify, adds the input mesh and a ribbon of faces along any naked edges. If solidify is false it acts exactly as the Offset(distance) function. Returns list of wall faces, i.e. the faces that connect original and offset mesh when solidified.

**Parameters:**
- `distance` (System.Double) — A distance value.
- `solidify` (System.Boolean) — true if the mesh should be solidified.
- `direction` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of offset for all vertices.
- `wallFacesOut` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Int32>) — Returns list of wall faces.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh on success, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Offset_3.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve PullCurve(Curve curve, double tolerance)`

Gets a polyline approximation of the input curve and then moves its control points to the closest point on the mesh. Then it "connects the points" over edges so that a polyline on the mesh is formed.

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — A curve to pull.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — A polyline curve, or null if none could be constructed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_PullCurve.htm)

**Returns:** `Point3d[]` — An array of points. This can be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_PullPointsToMesh.htm)

#### `public void RebuildNormals()`

Removes mesh normals and reconstructs the face and vertex normals based on the orientation of the faces.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_RebuildNormals.htm)

#### `public bool Reduce(int desiredPolygonCount, bool allowDistortion, int accuracy, bool normalizeSize)`

Reduce polygon count

**Parameters:**
- `desiredPolygonCount` (System.Int32) — desired or target number of faces
- `allowDistortion` (System.Boolean) — If true mesh appearance is not changed even if the target polygon count is not reached
- `accuracy` (System.Int32) — Integer from 1 to 10 telling how accurate reduction algorithm to use. Greater number gives more accurate results
- `normalizeSize` (System.Boolean) — If true mesh is fitted to an axis aligned unit cube until reduction is complete

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if mesh is successfully reduced and false if mesh could not be reduced for some reason.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Reduce_2.htm)

#### `public bool Reduce(int desiredPolygonCount, bool allowDistortion, int accuracy, bool normalizeSize, bool threaded)`

Reduce polygon count

**Parameters:**
- `desiredPolygonCount` (System.Int32) — desired or target number of faces
- `allowDistortion` (System.Boolean) — If true mesh appearance is not changed even if the target polygon count is not reached
- `accuracy` (System.Int32) — Integer from 1 to 10 telling how accurate reduction algorithm to use. Greater number gives more accurate results
- `normalizeSize` (System.Boolean) — If true mesh is fitted to an axis aligned unit cube until reduction is complete
- `threaded` (System.Boolean) — If True then will run computation inside a worker thread and ignore any provided CancellationTokens and ProgressReporters. If False then will run on main thread.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if mesh is successfully reduced and false if mesh could not be reduced for some reason.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Reduce_3.htm)

#### `public static bool RequireIterativeCleanup(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes, double tolerance)`

Analyzes some meshes, and determines if a pass of CreateFromIterativeCleanup would change the array. All available cleanup steps are used. Currently available cleanup steps are:- mending of single precision coincidence even though double precision vertices differ.- union of nearly identical vertices, irrespectively of their origin.- removal of t-joints along edges.

**Remarks:** See AlsoReferenceMesh ClassRhino.Geometry NamespaceMesh.CreateFromIterativeCleanup(IEnumerable<Mesh>, Double)

**Parameters:**
- `meshes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Mesh>) — A list, and array or any enumerable of meshes.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A 3d distance. This is usually a value of about 10e-7 magnitude.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if meshes would be changed, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_RequireIterativeCleanup.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)`

Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Angle of rotation in radians.
- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of the axis of rotation.
- `rotationCenter` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully rotated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Rotate.htm)

#### `public bool Scale(double scaleFactor)`

Scales the object by the specified factor. The scale is centered at the origin.

**Parameters:**
- `scaleFactor` (System.Double) — The uniform scaling factor.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully scaled.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Scale.htm)

#### `public void SetTextureCoordinates(TextureMapping tm, Transform xf, bool lazy)`

Set texture coordinates using given mapping and applying given transform. Set lazy to false to generate texture coordinates right away.

**Parameters:**
- `tm` (Rhino.Render.TextureMapping) — Texture mapping
- `xf` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transform to apply to the texture mapping
- `lazy` (System.Boolean) — Whether to generate lazily (true) or right away (false)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_SetTextureCoordinates.htm)

#### `public void SetTextureCoordinates(TextureMapping tm, Transform xf, bool lazy, bool seamCheck)`

Set texture coordinates using given mapping and applying given transform. Set lazy to false to generate texture coordinates right away.

**Parameters:**
- `tm` (Rhino.Render.TextureMapping) — Texture mapping
- `xf` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transform to apply to the texture mapping
- `lazy` (System.Boolean) — Whether to generate lazily (true) or right away (false)
- `seamCheck` (System.Boolean) — If true then some mesh edges might be unwelded to better represent UV discontinuities in the texture mapping. This only happens for the following mappings: Box, Sphere, Cylinder

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_SetTextureCoordinates_1.htm)

#### `public bool Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem)`

Smooths a mesh by averaging the positions of mesh vertices in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much vertices move towards the average of the neighboring vertices.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices along naked edges will not be modified.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Smooth_1.htm)

#### `public bool Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem, Plane plane)`

Smooths a mesh by averaging the positions of mesh vertices in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much vertices move towards the average of the neighboring vertices.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices along naked edges will not be modified.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — If SmoothingCoordinateSystem.CPlane specified, then the construction plane.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Smooth_2.htm)

#### `public bool Smooth(IEnumerable<int> vertexIndices, double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem, Plane plane)`

Smooths part of a mesh by averaging the positions of mesh vertices in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `vertexIndices` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Int32>) — The mesh vertex indices that specify the part of the mesh to smooth.
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much vertices move towards the average of the neighboring vertices.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true vertices along naked edges will not be modified.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — If SmoothingCoordinateSystem.CPlane specified, then the construction plane.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Smooth.htm)

#### `public int SolidOrientation()`

Determines orientation of a "solid" mesh.

**Returns:** `Int32` — +1 = mesh is solid with outward facing normals.-1 = mesh is solid with inward facing normals.0 = mesh is not solid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_SolidOrientation.htm)

#### `public Mesh[] Split(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes)`

Split a mesh with a collection of meshes. Suggestion: upgrade to overload with tolerance. Does not split at coplanar intersections.

**Parameters:**
- `meshes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Mesh>) — Meshes to split with.

**Returns:** `Mesh[]` — An array of mesh segments representing the split result.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Split_2.htm)

#### `public Mesh[] Split(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes, double tolerance, bool splitAtCoplanar, bool createNgons, TextLog textLog, CancellationToken cancel, IProgress<double> progress)`

Split a mesh with a collection of meshes.

**Parameters:**
- `meshes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Mesh>) — Meshes to split with.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A value for intersection tolerance. WARNING! Correct values are typically in the (10e-8 - 10e-4) range.An option is to use the document tolerance diminished by a few orders or magnitude.
- `splitAtCoplanar` (System.Boolean) — If false, coplanar areas will not be separated.
- `createNgons` (System.Boolean) — If true, creates ngons along the split ridge.
- `textLog` (Rhino.FileIO.TextLog) — A text log to write onto.
- `cancel` (System.Threading.CancellationToken) — A cancellation token.
- `progress` (System.IProgress<Double>) — A progress reporter item. This can be null.

**Returns:** `Mesh[]` — An array of mesh parts representing the split result, or null: when no mesh intersected, or if a cancel stopped the computation.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Split_4.htm)

#### `public Mesh[] Split(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes, double tolerance, bool splitAtCoplanar, TextLog textLog, CancellationToken cancel, IProgress<double> progress)`

Split a mesh with a collection of meshes.

**Parameters:**
- `meshes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Mesh>) — Meshes to split with.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A value for intersection tolerance. WARNING! Correct values are typically in the (10e-8 - 10e-4) range.An option is to use the document tolerance diminished by a few orders or magnitude.
- `splitAtCoplanar` (System.Boolean) — If false, coplanar areas will not be separated.
- `textLog` (Rhino.FileIO.TextLog) — A text log to write onto.
- `cancel` (System.Threading.CancellationToken) — A cancellation token.
- `progress` (System.IProgress<Double>) — A progress reporter item. This can be null.

**Returns:** `Mesh[]` — An array of mesh parts representing the split result, or null: when no mesh intersected, or if a cancel stopped the computation.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Split_3.htm)

#### `public Mesh[] Split(Mesh mesh)`

Split a mesh with another mesh. Suggestion: upgrade to overload with tolerance.

**Parameters:**
- `mesh` (Rhino.Geometry.Mesh) — Mesh to split with.

**Returns:** `Mesh[]` — An array of mesh segments representing the split result.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Split.htm)

#### `public Mesh[] Split(Plane plane)`

Split a mesh by an infinite plane.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The splitting plane.

**Returns:** `Mesh[]` — A new mesh array with the split result. This can be null if no result was found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Split_1.htm)

#### `public Mesh[] SplitDisjointPieces()`

Splits up the mesh into its unconnected pieces.

**Returns:** `Mesh[]` — An array containing all the disjoint pieces that make up this Mesh.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_SplitDisjointPieces.htm)

#### `public Mesh[] SplitWithProjectedPolylines(IEnumerable<PolylineCurve> curves, double tolerance)`

Splits a mesh by adding edges in correspondence with input polylines, and divides the mesh at partitioned areas. Polyline segments that are measured not to be on the mesh will be ignored.

**Parameters:**
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<PolylineCurve>) — An array, a list or any enumerable of polyline curves.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value.

**Returns:** `Mesh[]` — An array of meshes, or null if no change would happen.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_SplitWithProjectedPolylines.htm)

#### `public Mesh[] SplitWithProjectedPolylines(IEnumerable<PolylineCurve> curves, double tolerance, TextLog textLog, CancellationToken cancel, IProgress<double> progress)`

Splits a mesh by adding edges in correspondence with input polylines, and divides the mesh at partitioned areas. Polyline segments that are measured not to be on the mesh will be ignored.

**Parameters:**
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<PolylineCurve>) — An array, a list or any enumerable of polyline curves.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value.
- `textLog` (Rhino.FileIO.TextLog) — A text log, or null.
- `cancel` (System.Threading.CancellationToken) — A cancellation token to stop the computation at a given point.
- `progress` (System.IProgress<Double>) — A progress reporter to inform the user about progress. The reported value is indicative.

**Returns:** `Mesh[]` — An array of meshes, or null if no change would happen.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_SplitWithProjectedPolylines_1.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

#### `public bool Translate(double x, double y, double z)`

Translates the object along the specified vector.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — The X component.
- `y` (System.Double) — The Y component.
- `z` (System.Double) — The Z component.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully translated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Translate_1.htm)

#### `public bool Translate(Vector3d translationVector)`

Translates the object along the specified vector.

**Parameters:**
- `translationVector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A moving vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully translated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Translate.htm)

#### `public int UnifyNormals()`

Attempts to fix inconsistencies in the directions of mesh faces in a mesh. This function does not modify mesh vertex normals, it rearranges the mesh face winding and face normals to make them all consistent. Note, you may want to call Mesh.Normals.ComputeNormals() to recompute vertex normals after calling this functions.

**Returns:** `Int32` — number of faces that were modified.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_UnifyNormals.htm)

#### `public int UnifyNormals(bool countOnly)`

Attempts to fix inconsistencies in the directions of mesh faces in a mesh. This function does not modify mesh vertex normals, it rearranges the mesh face winding and face normals to make them all consistent. Note, you may want to call Mesh.Normals.ComputeNormals() to recompute vertex normals after calling this functions.

**Parameters:**
- `countOnly` (System.Boolean) — If true, then only the number of faces that would be modified is determined.

**Returns:** `Int32` — If countOnly=false, the number of faces that were modified. If countOnly=true, the number of faces that would be modified.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_UnifyNormals_1.htm)

#### `public void Unweld(double angleToleranceRadians, bool modifyNormals)`

Makes sure that faces sharing an edge and having a difference of normal greater than or equal to angleToleranceRadians have unique vertexes along that edge, adding vertices if necessary.

**Parameters:**
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Angle at which to make unique vertices.
- `modifyNormals` (System.Boolean) — Determines whether new vertex normals will have the same vertex normal as the original (false) or vertex normals made from the corresponding face normals (true)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Unweld.htm)

#### `public bool UnweldEdge(IEnumerable<int> edgeIndices, bool modifyNormals)`

Adds creases to a smooth mesh by creating coincident vertices along selected edges.

**Parameters:**
- `edgeIndices` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Int32>) — An array of mesh topology edge indices.
- `modifyNormals` (System.Boolean) — If true, the vertex normals on each side of the edge take the same value as the face to which they belong, giving the mesh a hard edge look. If false, each of the vertex normals on either side of the edge is assigned the same value as the original normal that the pair is replacing, keeping a smooth look.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_UnweldEdge.htm)

#### `public bool UnweldVertices(IEnumerable<int> topologyVertexIndices, bool modifyNormals)`

Ensures that faces sharing a common topological vertex have unique indices into the MeshVertexList collection.

**Parameters:**
- `topologyVertexIndices` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Int32>) — Topological vertex indices, from the MeshTopologyVertexList collection, to be unwelded. Use TopologyVertexIndex(Int32) to convert from vertex indices to topological vertex indices.
- `modifyNormals` (System.Boolean) — If true, the new vertex normals will be calculated from the face normal.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_UnweldVertices.htm)

#### `public double Volume()`

Compute volume of the mesh.

**Returns:** `Double` — Volume of the mesh.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Volume.htm)

#### `public void Weld(double angleToleranceRadians)`

Makes sure that faces sharing an edge and having a difference of normal greater than or equal to angleToleranceRadians share vertexes along that edge, vertex normals are averaged.

**Parameters:**
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Angle at which to weld vertices.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_Weld.htm)

#### `public Mesh WithDisplacement(MeshDisplacementInfo displacement)`

Constructs new mesh from the current one, with displacement applied to it.

**Parameters:**
- `displacement` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshDisplacementInfo) — Information on mesh displacement.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh with shutlining.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_WithDisplacement.htm)

#### `public Mesh WithEdgeSoftening(double softeningRadius, bool chamfer, bool faceted, bool force, double angleThreshold)`

Constructs new mesh from the current one, with edge softening applied to it.

**Parameters:**
- `softeningRadius` (System.Double) — The softening radius.
- `chamfer` (System.Boolean) — Specifies whether to chamfer the edges.
- `faceted` (System.Boolean) — Specifies whether the edges are faceted.
- `force` (System.Boolean) — Specifies whether to soften edges despite too large a radius.
- `angleThreshold` (System.Double) — Threshold angle (in degrees) which controls whether an edge is softened or not. The angle refers to the angles between the adjacent faces of an edge.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh with soft edges.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_WithEdgeSoftening.htm)

#### `public Mesh WithShutLining(bool faceted, double tolerance, IEnumerable<ShutLiningCurveInfo> curves)`

Constructs new mesh from the current one, with shut lining applied to it.

**Parameters:**
- `faceted` (System.Boolean) — Specifies whether the shutline is faceted.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance of the shutline.
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<ShutLiningCurveInfo>) — A collection of curve arguments.

**Returns:** `Mesh` — A new mesh with shutlining. Null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Mesh_WithShutLining.htm)

### Properties
- `ComponentStates` (MeshVertexStatusList, get) — Gets access to the vertex hidden/visibility collection in this mesh. This is a runtime property and it is not saved in the 3dm file.
- `DisjointMeshCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of disjoint (topologically unconnected) pieces in this mesh.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `FaceNormals` (MeshFaceNormalList, get) — Gets access to the face normal collection in this mesh.
- `Faces` (MeshFaceList, get) — Gets access to the mesh face list.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasCachedTextureCoordinates` (Boolean, get) — Will return true if SetCachedTextureCoordinates has been called; otherwise will return false.
- `HasPrincipalCurvatures` (Boolean, get) — HasPrincipalCurvatures
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsClosed` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if every mesh "edge" has two or more faces.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsOriented` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the mesh is manifold and every pair of faces that share an "edge" have compatible orientations.
- `IsSolid` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the mesh is solid. A "solid" is a closed oriented manifold.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `Ngons` (MeshNgonList, get) — Gets access to the mesh ngon list. Ngons represent groups of Faces (triangles + quads).Faces are used to tessellate an Ngon internally.When a triangle or quad is referenced in the Ngon list, it is no longer visualized and conceived as a single entity, but takes part of the Ngon.If you need to get access to both Ngons and the faces that are not referenced by Ngons, that is, all polygons that are visible in the mesh, then use the GetNgonAndFacesEnumerable() helper method.
- `Normals` (MeshVertexNormalList, get) — Gets access to the vertex normal collection in this mesh.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `PartitionCount` (Int32, get) — Number of partition information chunks stored on this mesh based on the last call to CreatePartitions
- `TextureCoordinates` (MeshTextureCoordinateList, get) — Gets access to the vertex texture coordinate collection in this mesh.
- `TopologyEdges` (MeshTopologyEdgeList, get) — Gets the MeshTopologyEdgeList object associated with this mesh. This object stores edge connectivity.
- `TopologyVertices` (MeshTopologyVertexList, get) — Gets the MeshTopologyVertexList object associated with this mesh. This object stores vertex connectivity and the indices of vertices that were unified while computing the edge topology.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.
- `VertexColors` (MeshVertexColorList, get) — Gets access to the (optional) vertex color collection in this mesh.
- `Vertices` (MeshVertexList, get) — Gets access to the vertices set of this mesh.

## MeshCheckParameters (struct)

Output of a mesh checking operations.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshCheckParameters.htm)

### Methods
#### `public static MeshCheckParameters Defaults()`

Gets the default mesh check parameters.

**Returns:** `MeshCheckParameters` — The default mesh check parameters.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshCheckParameters_Defaults.htm)

### Properties
- `CheckForBadNormals` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for vertex normals that are not unit vectors, are zero length or vary greatly from the general direction of the face normals.
- `CheckForDegenerateFaces` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for triangles and quads that have zero area and quads where one sub triangle has zero area.
- `CheckForDisjointMeshes` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for faces in the mesh that are totally isolated from other faces in the mesh. (i.e. an island of faces, or many islands, that have a boundary of naked edges)
- `CheckForDuplicateFaces` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for faces that have vertexes (not necessarily vertex indexes) that are identical.
- `CheckForExtremelyShortEdges` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for topological edges where the distance between the endpoints is less than ExtremelyShortEdgeDistance.
- `CheckForInvalidNgons` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for ngons that have invalid information.
- `CheckForNakedEdges` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for topological edges in the mesh topology where the face count is 1. Note, this check is also made if CheckForDuplicateFaces is true but the output is not shown if CheckForNakedEdges is false.
- `CheckForNonManifoldEdges` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for topological edges in the mesh topology where the face count is greater than 2. Note, this check is also made if CheckForDuplicateFaces is true but the output is not shown if CheckForNonManifoldEdges is false.
- `CheckForRandomFaceNormals` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for adjacent faces that have the same edge orientation. This typically means that clockwise/counterclockwise orientation, and face normal are not in sync.
- `CheckForSelfIntersection` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for self intersections.
- `CheckForUnusedVertices` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, then look for vertices that are not referenced by any face in the mesh.
- `DegenerateFaceCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total degenerate face count for all meshes.
- `DisjointMeshCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total count of disjoint pieces for all meshes.
- `DuplicateFaceCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total count of duplicate faces for all meshes.
- `ExtremelyShortEdgeCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total number of extremely short edges for all meshes.
- `InvalidNgonCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total number of invalid ngons for all meshes.
- `NakedEdgeCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total number of naked edges for all meshes.
- `NonManifoldEdgeCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total number of non-manifold edges for all meshes.
- `NonUnitVectorNormalCount` (Int32, get) — Get the total count of normals that are not unit vectors for all meshes.
- `RandomFaceNormalCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total count of random face normals for all meshes.
- `SelfIntersectingPairsCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total count of self intersections for all meshes.
- `UnusedVertexCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total count of unused vertices for all meshes.
- `VertexFaceNormalsDifferCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total count of vertex normals that differ greatly from face normals for all meshes.
- `ZeroLengthNormalCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total count of zero length normals for all meshes.

## MeshDisplacementInfo (class)

Contains mesh displacement information.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshDisplacementInfo.htm)

### Constructors
- `public MeshDisplacementInfo(RenderTexture texture, TextureMapping mapping)` — Constructs a displacement information instance with default values. Users of this class should not rely on default values to stay constant across service releases.

### Properties
- `Black` (Double, get/set) — Value considered lowest point in the displacement.
- `BlackMove` (Double, get/set) — The amount of displacement for the black color in the texture.
- `ChannelNumber` (Int32, get/set) — Mapping channel number for the displacement mapping.
- `FaceLimit` (Int32, get/set) — Runs a mesh reduction as a post process o simplify the result of displacement to meet the specified number of faces.
- `FairingAmount` (Int32, get/set) — Straightens rough feature edges. The value specifies the number of passes.
- `InstanceTransform` (Transform, get/set) — Instance transformation of the mesh.
- `Mapping` (TextureMapping, get) — The texture mapping of the mesh.
- `MappingTransform` (Transform, get/set) — Texture mapping transform.
- `MemoryLimit` (Int32, get/set) — Specifies how much memory can be allocated for use by the displacement mesh. Value in megabytes.
- `PostWeldAngle` (Double, get/set) — Specifies the maximum angle between face normals of adjacent faces that will get welded together.
- `RefineSensitivity` (Double, get/set) — Specifies how sensitive the divider for contrasts is on the displacement texture.Specify 1 to split all mesh edges on each refine step.Specify 0.99 to make even slight contrasts on the displacement texture cause edges to be split.Specifying 0.01 only splits edges where heavy contrast exists.
- `RefineStepCount` (Int32, get/set) — Specifies the number of refinement passes.
- `SweepPitch` (Double, get/set) — Specifies how densely the object is initially subdivided. The lower the value, the higher the resolution of the displaced mesh.
- `Texture` (RenderTexture, get) — The texture used as displacement.
- `White` (Double, get/set) — Value considered highest point of the displacement texture.
- `WhiteMove` (Double, get/set) — The amount of displacement for the white color in the texture.

## MeshExtruderFaceDirectionMode (enum)

Defines how mesh extruder decides orients faces

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshExtruderFaceDirectionMode.htm)

### Values
- `Keep` = `0` — Maintain all face directions
- `OrientClosedFrontOut` = `1` — Orient closed meshes front face out

## MeshExtruderParameterMode (enum)

Parameter mode for creating texture coordinates and surface parameters

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshExtruderParameterMode.htm)

### Values
- `CoverWalls` = `0` — Stretch parameters slightly on extruded faces to cover walls
- `KeepAndStretch` = `1` — Keep parameters on extruded faces and stretch on walls

## MeshFace (struct)

Represents the values of the four indices of a mesh face quad. If the third and fourth values are the same, this face represents a triangle.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace.htm)

### Constructors
- `public MeshFace(int a, int b, int c)` — Constructs a new triangular Mesh face.
- `public MeshFace(int a, int b, int c, int d)` — Constructs a new quadrangular Mesh face.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(MeshFace other)`

Compares this MeshFace with another MeshFace and returns a value of 1, 0, or -1, referring to dictionary order. Index evaluation priority is first A, then B, then C, then D.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshFace) — The other MeshFace to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this < other. Priority is for index of corner A first, then B, then C, then D.+1: otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_CompareTo.htm)

#### `public static bool operator ==(MeshFace a, MeshFace b)`

Determines whether two MeshFace structures have equal values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshFace) — The first MeshFace.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshFace) — The second MeshFace.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the indices of the two points are exactly equal; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_op_Equality.htm)

#### `public bool Equals(MeshFace other)`

Returns a value indicating whether the other mesh face has precisely the same value as the current one.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshFace) — The other mesh face for comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the other face is, also orderly, equal to the present one; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_Equals.htm)

#### `public override bool Equals(Object obj)`

Returns a value indicating whether the other object obj has precisely the same value as the current one.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — Any object the represents the other mesh face for comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if obj is a mesh face that, also orderly, equals to the present one; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_Equals_1.htm)

#### `public MeshFace Flip()`

Reverses the orientation of the face by swapping corners. The first corner is always maintained.

**Returns:** `MeshFace` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.MeshFace.Flip"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_Flip.htm)

#### `public override int GetHashCode()`

Returns a runtime-stable hash code for the current mesh face. You are not allowed to rely on persistence of this hash code in serialization, but for each version of RhinoCommon, this hash code will be the same for each mesh face.

**Returns:** `Int32` — A non-unique integer that represents this mesh face.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_GetHashCode.htm)

Determines whether two MeshFace structures have different values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshFace) — The first MeshFace.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshFace) — The second MeshFace.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the indices of the two points are in any way different; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_op_Inequality.htm)

#### `public bool IsValid()`

Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is considered to be valid. Note that even valid mesh faces could potentially be invalid in the context of a specific Mesh, if one or more of the corner indices exceeds the number of vertices on the mesh. If you want to perform a complete validity check, use IsValid(int) instead.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.MeshFace.IsValid"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_IsValid.htm)

#### `public bool IsValid(int vertexCount)`

Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is considered to be valid. Unlike the simple IsValid function, this function takes upper bound indices into account.

**Parameters:**
- `vertexCount` (System.Int32) — Number of vertices in the mesh that this face is a part of.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the face is considered valid, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_IsValid_2.htm)

#### `public bool IsValid(Point3d[] points)`

Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is considered to be valid. Unlike the simple IsValid function, this function takes actual point locations into account.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d[]) — Array of vertices with which to validate the face.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the face is considered valid, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_IsValid_1.htm)

#### `public bool IsValidEx(ref Point3d[] points)`

Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is considered to be valid. Unlike the simple IsValid function, this function takes actual point locations into account.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d[]) — Array of vertices with which to validate the face.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the face is considered valid, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_IsValidEx.htm)

#### `public bool Repair(Point3d[] points)`

Attempts to repair this mesh face by taking both face indexes and actual vertex locations into account.

**Remarks:** This function assumes the face is invalid and attempts to repair unconditionally. It is only worthwhile to call Repair on faces where IsValid returns false.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d[]) — Array of vertices with which to consider when repairing the face.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the face was repaired, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_Repair.htm)

#### `public bool RepairEx(ref Point3d[] points)`

Attempts to repair this mesh face by taking both face indexes and actual vertex locations into account.

**Remarks:** This function assumes the face is invalid and attempts to repair unconditionally. It is only worthwhile to call Repair on faces where IsValid returns false. This function is the analog of UnsafeNativeMethods.ON_MeshFace_Repair done completely here to avoid copying the point array.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d[]) — Array of vertices with which to consider when repairing the face.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the face was repaired, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_RepairEx.htm)

#### `public void Set(int a, int b, int c)`

Sets all the corners for this face as a triangle.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (System.Int32) — Index of first corner.
- `b` (System.Int32) — Index of second corner.
- `c` (System.Int32) — Index of third corner.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_Set.htm)

#### `public void Set(int a, int b, int c, int d)`

Sets all the corners for this face as a quad.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (System.Int32) — Index of first corner.
- `b` (System.Int32) — Index of second corner.
- `c` (System.Int32) — Index of third corner.
- `d` (System.Int32) — Index of fourth corner.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_Set_1.htm)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Returns a string representation for this MeshFace. This is to provide a meaningful visualization of this structure and is subject to change in newer releases.

**Returns:** `String` — The string representation.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshFace_ToString.htm)

### Properties
- `A` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the first corner index of the mesh face.
- `B` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the second corner index of the mesh face.
- `C` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the third corner index of the mesh face.
- `D` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the fourth corner index of the mesh face. If D equals C, the mesh face is considered to be a triangle rather than a quad.
- `IsQuad` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is a quad. A mesh face is considered to be a triangle when C does not equal D, thus it is possible for an Invalid mesh face to also be a quad.
- `IsTriangle` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is a triangle. A mesh face is considered to be a triangle when C equals D, thus it is possible for an Invalid mesh face to also be a triangle.
- `Item` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the vertex index associated with an entry in this face.
- `Unset` (MeshFace, get) — Gets an Unset MeshFace. Unset faces have Int32.MinValue for all corner indices.

## MeshNgon (class)

Represents a mesh ngon. When retrieved from the Ngon property of a mesh, this contains faces that have edge valence strictly higher than 4.When retrieving Ngons from GetNgonAndFacesEnumerable(), this might contain also triangles and quads.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshNgon.htm)

### Methods
#### `public uint[] BoundaryVertexIndexList()`

Get the outer boundary mesh vertex list of the ngon. Vertices are sorted counterclockwise with respect to the direction of the face, although the degree by which vertex normals will respect this might vary.

**Returns:** `UInt32[]` — A list of mesh vertex indexes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshNgon_BoundaryVertexIndexList.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(MeshNgon otherNgon)`

Compares this MeshNgon with otherNgon and returns a value of 1, 0, or -1. Priority is for vertex count, then face count, then vertex index list values, then face index list values.

**Parameters:**
- `otherNgon` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshNgon) — The other MeshNgon to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to otherNgon-1: if this < otherNgon. +1: if this > otherNgon.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshNgon_CompareTo.htm)

#### `public static MeshNgon Create(IList<int> meshVertexIndexList, IList<int> meshFaceIndexList)`

Constructs an ngon from lists of mesh vertex and face indexes.

**Parameters:**
- `meshVertexIndexList` (System.Collections.Generic.IList<Int32>) — A list of mesh vertex indexes that define the outer boundary of the ngon. The mesh vertex indexes must be in the correct order.
- `meshFaceIndexList` (System.Collections.Generic.IList<Int32>) — A list of mesh face indexes that define the interior of the ngon. The mesh face indexes may be in any order.

**Returns:** `MeshNgon` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.MeshNgon.Create(System.Collections.Generic.IList{System.Int32},System.Collections.Generic.IList{System.Int32})"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshNgon_Create.htm)

#### `public static bool operator ==(MeshNgon a, MeshNgon b)`

Determines whether two MeshNgon structures have equal values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshNgon) — The first MeshNgon.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshNgon) — The second MeshNgon.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the vertex and face index lists are identical; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshNgon_op_Equality.htm)

#### `public bool Equals(MeshNgon otherNgon)`

Determines if this ngon and otherNgon are identical.

**Parameters:**
- `otherNgon` (Rhino.Geometry.MeshNgon) — The other ngon for comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if otherNgon is identical to this ngon; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshNgon_Equals.htm)

#### `public override bool Equals(Object otherObj)`

Determines if otherObj is a MeshNgon and is identical to this ngon.

**Parameters:**
- `otherObj` (System.Object) — Any object the represents the other mesh face for comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if otherObj is a MeshNgon and is identical to this ngon; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshNgon_Equals_1.htm)

#### `public uint[] FaceIndexList()`

Get the ngon's mesh face index list.

**Returns:** `UInt32[]` — A list of mesh face indexes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshNgon_FaceIndexList.htm)

#### `public override int GetHashCode()`

Returns a runtime-stable hash code for the current mesh ngon. You are not allowed to rely on persistence of this hash code in serialization, but for each instance of the application, this hash code will be the same for ngons with identical vertex and face lists.

**Returns:** `Int32` — A non-unique integer that represents this mesh ngon.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshNgon_GetHashCode.htm)

Determines whether two MeshNgon structures have different values.

Set the ngon vertex and face index lists.

Returns a string representation for this MeshNgon. This is to provide a meaningful visualization of this structure and is subject to change in newer releases.

- `Empty` (MeshNgon, get) — Gets an empty MeshNgon.
- `FaceCount` (Int32, get) — Get the number of faces in this ngon.
- `Item` (Int32, get) — Get a mesh vertex index from the ngon's vertex index list.

- `StartFaceIndex` (Int32, get) — Start of subinterval of parent mesh face array

## MeshPipeCapStyle (enum)

Enumerates methods to end a mesh pipe.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshPipeCapStyle.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — Capping is skipped. The resulting object will be simpler.
- `Flat` = `1` — A flat surface will cap the pipe.
- `Box` = `2` — A simple construction will cap the pipe.
- `Dome` = `3` — A meridians-and-parallels hemisphere construction will cap the pipe.

## MeshPoint (class)

Represents a point that is found on a mesh.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshPoint.htm)

### Methods
#### `public bool GetTriangle(out int a, out int b, out int c)`

Gets the mesh face indices of the triangle where the intersection is on the face takes into consideration the way the quad was split during the intersection.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (System.Int32) — [Missing <param name="a"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.MeshPoint.GetTriangle(System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)"]
- `b` (System.Int32) — [Missing <param name="b"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.MeshPoint.GetTriangle(System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)"]
- `c` (System.Int32) — [Missing <param name="c"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.MeshPoint.GetTriangle(System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)"]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.MeshPoint.GetTriangle(System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshPoint_GetTriangle.htm)

### Properties
- `ComponentIndex` (ComponentIndex, get) — Gets the component index of the intersecting element in the mesh.
- `EdgeIndex` (Int32, get) — When set, EdgeIndex is an index of an edge in the mesh's edge list.
- `EdgeParameter` (Double, get) — Edge parameter when found.
- `FaceIndex` (Int32, get) — FaceIndex is an index of a face in mesh.Faces. When ComponentIndex refers to a vertex, any face that uses the vertex may appear as FaceIndex. When ComponenctIndex refers to an Edge or EdgeIndex is set, then any face that uses that edge may appear as FaceIndex.
- `Mesh` (Mesh, get) — The mesh that is related to this point.
- `Point` (Point3d, get) — Gets the location (position) of this point.
- `T` (Double[], get) — Barycentric quad coordinates for the point on the mesh face mesh.Faces[FaceIndex]. If the face is a triangle disregard T[3] (it should be set to 0.0). If the face is a quad and is split between vertexes 0 and 2, then T[3] will be 0.0 when point is on the triangle defined by vi[0], vi[1], vi[2], and T[1] will be 0.0 when point is on the triangle defined by vi[0], vi[2], vi[3]. If the face is a quad and is split between vertexes 1 and 3, then T[2] will be 0.0 when point is on the triangle defined by vi[0], vi[1], vi[3], and m_t[0] will be 0.0 when point is on the triangle defined by vi[1], vi[2], vi[3].
- `Triangle` (Char, get) — Face triangle where the intersection takes place: 0 is unsetA is 0,1,2B is 0,2,3C is 0,1,3D is 1,2,3

## MeshUnsafeLock (class)

Permits access to the underlying mesh raw data structures in an unsafe way.

**Remarks:** This lock object needs to be disposed before using the Mesh in other calculations and this can be achieved with the using keyword (Using in VB.Net).

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshUnsafeLock.htm)

### Methods
#### `public MeshFace* FacesArray(out int length)`

Retrieves a pointer to the raw faces array, which uses 4 integers for each face. MeshFace.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Int32) — The length of the array. This value is returned by reference (out in C#).

**Returns:** `MeshFace*` — The beginning of the vertex array. Item 0 is the first vertex, and item length-1 is the last valid one.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshUnsafeLock_FacesArray.htm)

#### `public Vector3f* NormalVector3fArray(out int length)`

Retrieves a pointer to the raw mesh vertex normal array, which uses vectors defined with single precision floating point numbers, or throws an exception if none is available.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Int32) — The length of the array. This value is returned by reference (out in C#).

**Returns:** `Vector3f*` — The beginning of the vertex array. Item 0 is the first vertex, and item length-1 is the last valid one.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshUnsafeLock_NormalVector3fArray.htm)

#### `public void Release()`

Releases the lock and updates the underlying unmanaged data structures.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshUnsafeLock_Release.htm)

#### `public Point3d* VertexPoint3dArray(out int length)`

Retrieves a pointer to the raw mesh vertex array, which uses coordinates defined with double precision floating point numbers, or throws an exception if none is available.

**Remarks:** When running this method for writing and if the mesh has also a single precision array, the array is synchronized if the pointer to the single precision array was not requested. Otherwise, it is up to the caller to ensure that the mesh vertices are kept synchronized, by setting both arrays to the required values.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Int32) — The length of the array. This value is returned by reference (out in C#).

**Returns:** `Point3d*` — The beginning of the vertex array. Item 0 is the first vertex, and item length-1 is the last valid one. If no array is available, null is returned.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshUnsafeLock_VertexPoint3dArray.htm)

#### `public Point3f* VertexPoint3fArray(out int length)`

Retrieves a pointer to the raw mesh vertex array, which uses coordinates defined with single precision floating point numbers, or null if none is available.

**Remarks:** When running this method for writing, it is up to the caller to ensure that the mesh has no double precision array that needs to be synchronized. Otherwise, also the double precision array must be synchronized.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Int32) — The length of the array. This value is returned by reference (out in C#). 0 is returned when there is no single precision array.

**Returns:** `Point3f*` — The beginning of the vertex array. Item 0 is the first vertex, and item length-1 is the last valid one. If no array is available, null is returned.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_MeshUnsafeLock_VertexPoint3fArray.htm)

## MeshingParameterStyle (enum)

Type of Mesh Parameters used by the RhinoDoc for meshing objects

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_MeshingParameterStyle.htm)
