- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Point2d` — A new point that is coordinate-wise summed with the vector.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2d_Add_1.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(Point2d other)`

Compares this Point2d with another Point2d. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — The other Point2d to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y+1: otherwise.

#### `public static bool operator >(Point2d a, Point2d b)`

Determines whether the first specified Point2d comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second point. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — First point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — Second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2d_op_GreaterThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >=(Point2d a, Point2d b)`

Determines whether the first specified Point2d comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second point, or it is equal to it. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — First point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — Second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y >= b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2d_op_GreaterThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static bool operator !=(Point2d a, Point2d b)`

Determines whether two Point2d have different values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the two points differ in any coordinate; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2d_op_Inequality.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <(Point2d a, Point2d b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second point. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — First point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — Second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2d_op_LessThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <=(Point2d a, Point2d b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second point, or it is equal to it. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — First point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — Second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y <= b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2d_op_LessThanOrEqual.htm)

Constructs the string representation for the current point.

**Returns:** `String` — The point representation in the form X,Y.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2d_ToString.htm)

#### `public string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider formatProvider)`

Formats the value of the current instance using the specified format.

**Parameters:**
- `format` (System.String) — The format to use. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to use the default format defined for the type of the IFormattable implementation.
- `formatProvider` (System.IFormatProvider) — The provider to use to format the value. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to obtain the numeric format information from the current locale setting of the operating system.

**Returns:** `String` — The value of the current instance in the specified format.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2d_ToString_1.htm)

#### `public void Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the present point in place. The transformation matrix acts on the left of the point. i.e., result = transformation*point

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2d_Transform.htm)

### Properties
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — If any coordinate of a point is UnsetValue, then the point is not valid.
- `Item` (Double, get/set) — Accesses the coordinates of this point.
- `MaximumCoordinate` (Double, get) — Gets the largest valid coordinate, or RhinoMath.UnsetValue if no coordinate is valid.
- `MinimumCoordinate` (Double, get) — Gets the smallest (both positive and negative) valid coordinate, or RhinoMath.UnsetValue if no coordinate is valid.
- `Origin` (Point2d, get) — Gets a point at 0,0.
- `Unset` (Point2d, get) — Gets a point at RhinoMath.UnsetValue,RhinoMath.UnsetValue.
- `X` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X (first) coordinate of the point.
- `Y` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y (second) coordinate of the point.

## Point2f (struct)

Represents the two coordinates of a point in two-dimensional space, using Single-precision floating point numbers.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Point2f.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Point2f(double x, double y)` — Initializes a new two-dimensional point from two double-precision floating point numbers as coordinates. Coordinates will be internally converted to floating point numbers. This might cause precision loss.
- `public Point2f(float x, float y)` — Initializes a new two-dimensional point from two components.

### Methods
#### `public static Vector2f operator +(Point2f a, Point2f b)`

Sums two Point2fs.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2f) — First point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2f) — Second point.

**Returns:** `Vector2f` — An added Vector2f.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2f_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(Point2f other)`

Compares this Point2f with another Point2f. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2f) — The other Point2f to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y+1: otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2f_CompareTo.htm)

#### `public double DistanceTo(Point2f other)`

Computes the distance between two points.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2f) — Other point for distance measurement.

**Returns:** `Double` — The length of the line between this and the other point; or 0 if any of the points is not valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2f_DistanceTo.htm)

#### `public double DistanceToSquared(Point2f other)`

Computes the squared distance between two points.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2f) — Other point for distance measurement.

**Returns:** `Double` — The squared length of the line between this and the other point; or 0 if any of the points is not valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2f_DistanceToSquared.htm)

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2f) — First point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2f) — Second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y <= b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2f_op_LessThanOrEqual.htm)

Multiplies a Point2f by a scalar.

- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2f) — First point.
- `b` (System.Single) — Scalar.

**Returns:** `Point2f` — A multiplied Point2f.

#### `public string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider formatProvider)`

Formats the value of the current instance using the specified format.

**Parameters:**
- `format` (System.String) — The format to use. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to use the default format defined for the type of the IFormattable implementation.
- `formatProvider` (System.IFormatProvider) — The provider to use to format the value. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to obtain the numeric format information from the current locale setting of the operating system.

**Returns:** `String` — The value of the current instance in the specified format.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point2f_ToString_1.htm)

### Properties
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether this point is considered valid.
- `Item` (Single, get/set) — Accesses the coordinates of this point.
- `Unset` (Point2f, get) — Gets the standard unset point.
- `X` (Single, get/set) — Gets or sets the X (first) component of the vector.
- `Y` (Single, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y (second) component of the vector.

## Point3d (struct)

Represents the three coordinates of a point in three-dimensional space, using Double-precision floating point values.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Point3d(Point3d point)` — Initializes a new point by copying coordinates from another point.
- `public Point3d(Point3f point)` — Initializes a new point by copying coordinates from a single-precision point.
- `public Point3d(Point4d point)` — Initializes a new point by copying coordinates from a four-dimensional point. The first three coordinates are divided by the last one. If the W (fourth) dimension of the input point is zero, then it will be just discarded.
- `public Point3d(Vector3d vector)` — Initializes a new point by copying coordinates from the components of a vector.
- `public Point3d(double x, double y, double z)` — Initializes a new point by defining the X, Y and Z coordinates.

#### `public static bool ArePointsCoplanar(IEnumerable<Point3d> points, double tolerance)`

Determines whether a set of points is coplanar within a given tolerance.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — A list, an array or any enumerable of Point3d.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value. A default might be RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if points are on the same plane; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_ArePointsCoplanar.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(Point3d other)`

Compares this Point3d with another Point3d. Component evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The other Point3d to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y == other.Y and this.Z < other.Z+1: otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_CompareTo.htm)

#### `public static Point3d[] CullDuplicates(IEnumerable<Point3d> points, double tolerance)`

Removes duplicates in the supplied set of points.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — A list, an array or any enumerable of Point3d.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The minimum distance between points. Points that fall within this tolerance will be discarded. .

**Returns:** `Point3d[]` — An array of points without duplicates; or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_CullDuplicates.htm)

Computes the distance between two points.

- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Other point for distance measurement.

**Returns:** `Double` — The length of the line between this and the other point; or 0 if any of the points is not valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_DistanceTo.htm)

Computes the square of the distance between two points. This method is usually largely faster than DistanceTo().

#### `public static bool operator >(Point3d a, Point3d b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second point. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z is larger than b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_op_GreaterThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >=(Point3d a, Point3d b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second point, or it is equal to it. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z >= b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_op_GreaterThanOrEqual.htm)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_op_Implicit.htm)

Interpolate between two points.

**Parameters:**
- `pA` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — First point.
- `pB` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Second point.
- `t` (System.Double) — Interpolation parameter. If t=0 then this point is set to pA. If t=1 then this point is set to pB. Values of t in between 0.0 and 1.0 result in points between pA and pB.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_Interpolate.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <(Point3d a, Point3d b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second point. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z is smaller than b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_op_LessThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <=(Point3d a, Point3d b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second point, or it is equal to it. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z <= b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_op_LessThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static Point3d[] SortAndCullPointList(IEnumerable<Point3d> points, double minimumDistance)`

Orders a set of points so they will be connected in a "reasonable polyline" order. Also, removes points from the list if their common distance exceeds a specified threshold.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — A list, an array or any enumerable of Point3d.
- `minimumDistance` (System.Double) — Minimum allowed distance among a pair of points. If points are closer than this, only one of them will be kept.

**Returns:** `Point3d[]` — The new array of sorted and culled points.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_SortAndCullPointList.htm)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_Transform.htm)

#### `public static bool TryParse(string input, out Point3d result)`

Converts the string representation of a point to the equivalent Point3d structure.

**Parameters:**
- `input` (System.String) — The point to convert.
- `result` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The structure that will contain the parsed value.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_TryParse.htm)

#### `public static Point3d operator -(Point3d point)`

Computes the additive inverse of all coordinates in the point, and returns the new point.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — A point value that, when summed with the point input, yields the Origin.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3d_op_UnaryNegation.htm)

### Properties
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Each coordinate of the point must pass the IsValidDouble(Double) test.
- `Item` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets an indexed coordinate of this point.
- `MaximumCoordinate` (Double, get) — Gets the largest (both positive and negative) valid coordinate in this point, or RhinoMath.UnsetValue if no coordinate is valid.
- `MinimumCoordinate` (Double, get) — Gets the smallest (both positive and negative) coordinate value in this point.
- `Origin` (Point3d, get) — Gets the value of a point at location 0,0,0.
- `Unset` (Point3d, get) — Gets the value of a point at location RhinoMath.UnsetValue,RhinoMath.UnsetValue,RhinoMath.UnsetValue.
- `X` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X (first) coordinate of this point.
- `Y` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y (second) coordinate of this point.
- `Z` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Z (third) coordinate of this point.

## Point3dGrid (class)

Represents a rectangular grid of 3D points.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Point3dGrid.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Point3dGrid()` — Initializes a rectangular grid of points, with no points in it.
- `public Point3dGrid(int rows, int columns)` — Initializes a rectangular grid of points with a given number of columns and rows.
- `protected Point3dGrid(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Protected constructor for internal use.

### Methods
#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

#### `protected void ConstructConstObject(Object parentObject, int subobjectIndex)`

Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

**Parameters:**
- `parentObject` (System.Object) — The parent object.
- `subobjectIndex` (System.Int32) — The sub-object index.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_ConstructConstObject.htm)

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)`

Overridden in order to destroy local display cache information

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Dispose.htm)

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

Determines if an object is valid. Also provides a report on errors if this object happens not to be valid.

If possible, converts the object into a form that can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, an non-uniform scaling.

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)`

Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Angle of rotation in radians.
- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of the axis of rotation.
- `rotationCenter` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully rotated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Rotate.htm)

#### `public bool Scale(double scaleFactor)`

Scales the object by the specified factor. The scale is centered at the origin.

**Parameters:**
- `scaleFactor` (System.Double) — The uniform scaling factor.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully scaled.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Scale.htm)

Attach a user string (key,value combination) to this geometry.

- `value` (System.String) — string associated with key.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_SetUserString.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

Translates the object along the specified vector.

**Parameters:**
- `translationVector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A moving vector.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Translate.htm)

### Properties
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## Point3f (struct)

Represents the three coordinates of a point in three-dimensional space, using Single-precision floating point numbers.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Point3f(float x, float y, float z)` — Initializes a new two-dimensional vector from two components.

### Methods
#### `public static Point3f operator +(Point3f point1, Point3f point2)`

Adds a point to another point.

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — A point.
- `point2` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — Another point.

**Returns:** `Point3f` — A new vector that is the sum of point1 plus point2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(Point3f other)`

Compares this Point3f with another Point3f. Component evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The other Point3d to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y == other.Y and this.Z < other.Z+1: otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_CompareTo.htm)

#### `public double DistanceTo(Point3f other)`

Computes the distance between two points.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — Other point for distance measurement.

**Returns:** `Double` — The length of the line between this and the other point; or 0 if any of the points is not valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_DistanceTo.htm)

#### `public double DistanceToSquared(Point3f other)`

Computes the squared distance between two points.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — Other point for distance measurement.

**Returns:** `Double` — The squared length of the line between this and the other point; or 0 if any of the points is not valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_DistanceToSquared.htm)

#### `public bool EpsilonEquals(Point3f other, float epsilon)`

Check that all values in other are withing epsilon of the values in this

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f)
- `epsilon` (System.Single)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Point3f.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Point3f,System.Single)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_EpsilonEquals.htm)

#### `public static bool operator ==(Point3f a, Point3f b)`

Determines whether two points have equal values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the coordinates of the two points are exactly equal; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_op_Equality.htm)

#### `public override bool Equals(Object obj)`

Determines whether the specified System.Object is a Point3f and has the same values as the present point.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The specified object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if obj is Point3f and has the same coordinates as this; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_Equals_1.htm)

#### `public bool Equals(Point3f point)`

Determines whether the specified Point3f has the same values as the present point.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The specified point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if point has the same coordinates as this; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_Equals.htm)

#### `public static explicit operator Point3f (Point3d point)`

Converts a double-precision point in a single-precision point. Needs explicit casting to help retain precision.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point.

**Returns:** `Point3f` — The resulting point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_op_Explicit.htm)

#### `public static explicit operator Vector3f (Point3f point)`

Converts a single-precision point in a single-precision vector. Needs explicit casting to help retain precision.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — A point.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — The resulting point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_op_Explicit_1.htm)

#### `public override int GetHashCode()`

Computes a hash code for the present point.

**Returns:** `Int32` — A non-unique integer that represents this point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_GetHashCode.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >(Point3f a, Point3f b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second point. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z is larger than b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_op_GreaterThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >=(Point3f a, Point3f b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second point, or it is equal to it. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z >= b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_op_GreaterThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static bool operator !=(Point3f a, Point3f b)`

Determines whether two points have different values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the two points differ in any coordinate; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_op_Inequality.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <(Point3f a, Point3f b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second point. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z is smaller than b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_op_LessThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <=(Point3f a, Point3f b)`

Determines whether the first specified point comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second point, or it is equal to it. Coordinates evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The first point.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z <= b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_op_LessThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static Point3f operator *(Point3f point, float value)`

Multiplies a point by a factor.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — A point.
- `value` (System.Single) — A value.

- `value` (System.Single) — A value.
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — A point.

**Returns:** `Point3f` — A new vector that is the multiplication of point by value.

#### `public static Vector3f Subtract(Point3f point1, Point3f point2)`

Subtracts a point from another point. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — A point.
- `point2` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — Another point.

Subtracts a point from another point.

- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — A point.
- `point2` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — Another point.

#### `public override string ToString()`

Constructs the string representation for the current point.

**Returns:** `String` — The point representation in the form X,Y,Z.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_ToString.htm)

Formats the value of the current instance using the specified format.

**Parameters:**
- `format` (System.String) — The format to use. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to use the default format defined for the type of the IFormattable implementation.
- `formatProvider` (System.IFormatProvider) — The provider to use to format the value. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to obtain the numeric format information from the current locale setting of the operating system.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_ToString_1.htm)

#### `public void Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the present point in place. The transformation matrix acts on the left of the point. i.e., result = transformation*point

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point3f_Transform.htm)

### Properties
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Each coordinate of the point must pass the IsValidSingle(Single) test.
- `Origin` (Point3f, get) — Gets the value of a point at location 0,0,0.
- `Unset` (Point3f, get) — Gets the value of a point at location RhinoMath.UnsetValue,RhinoMath.UnsetValue,RhinoMath.UnsetValue.
- `X` (Single, get/set) — Gets or sets the X (first) component of the vector.
- `Y` (Single, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y (second) component of the vector.
- `Z` (Single, get/set) — Gets or sets the Z (third) component of the vector.

## Point4d (struct)

Represents the four coordinates of a point in four-dimensional space. The W (fourth) dimension is often considered the weight of the point as seen in 3D space.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Point4d.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Point4d(Point3d point)` — Initializes a new instance of the Point4d class from the coordinates of a point.
- `public Point4d(Point4d point)` — Initializes a new point by copying coordinates from another point.
- `public Point4d(double x, double y, double z, double w)` — Initializes a new instance of the Point4d class based on coordinates.

### Methods
#### `public static Point4d Add(Point4d point1, Point4d point2)`

Sums two Point4d together. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the + operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point4d) — First point.
- `point2` (Rhino.Geometry.Point4d) — Second point.

**Returns:** `Point4d` — A new point that results from the weighted addition of point1 and point2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point4d_Add.htm)

#### `public static Point4d operator +(Point4d point1, Point4d point2)`

Sums two Point4d together.

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point4d) — First point.
- `point2` (Rhino.Geometry.Point4d) — Second point.

**Returns:** `Point4d` — A new point that results from the weighted addition of point1 and point2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Point4d_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public static double operator *(Point4d point1, Point4d point2)`

Multiplies two Point4d together, returning the dot (internal) product of the two. This is not the cross product.

- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Point4d) — The first point.

## PointCloud (class)

Represents a collection of coordinates with optional normal vectors and colors.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_PointCloud.htm)

### Constructors
- `public PointCloud()` — Initializes a new instance of the PointCloud class that is empty.
- `public PointCloud(IEnumerable<Point3d> points)` — Initializes a new instance of the PointCloud class, copying the content from a set of points.
- `public PointCloud(PointCloud other)` — Initializes a new instance of the PointCloud class, copying (Merge) the content of another point cloud.
- `protected PointCloud(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Binds with the Rhino default serializer to support object persistence.

### Methods
#### `public void Add(Point3d point)`

Append a new point to the end of the list.

- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to append.

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

#### `public PointCloudUnsafeLock GetUnsafeLock(bool writable)`

Allows the developer to obtain unsafe pointers to the underlying unmanaged data structures of the PointCloud.

**Remarks:** The lock implements the IDisposable interface, and one call of its Dispose() or ReleaseUnsafeLock(PointCloudUnsafeLock) will update the data structure as required. This can be achieved with a using statement (Using in Vb.Net).

**Parameters:**
- `writable` (System.Boolean) — true if user will need to write onto the structure. false otherwise.

**Returns:** `PointCloudUnsafeLock` — A lock that needs to be released.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PointCloud_GetUnsafeLock.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

### Properties
- `ContainsColors` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the points in this point cloud have colors assigned to them.
- `ContainsHiddenFlags` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the points in this point cloud have hidden flags assigned to them.
- `ContainsNormals` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the points in this point cloud have normals assigned to them.
- `ContainsPointValues` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the points in this point cloud have extra values assigned to them. Extra values can be used to store a user-defined values, such as intensity.
- `Count` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of points in this point cloud.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `HiddenPointCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of points that have their Hidden flag set.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `Item` (PointCloudItem, get) — Gets the item at the given index.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## PointCloudItem (class)

Represents a single item in a point cloud. A PointCloud item always has a location, but it has an optional normal vector and color.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_PointCloudItem.htm)

### Properties
- `Color` (Color, get/set) — Gets or sets the color of this point cloud item. If this point cloud item does not have a color, System.Drawing.Color.Black is returned.
- `Hidden` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the hidden flag of this point cloud item. If this point cloud item does not have a hidden flag, false is returned.
- `Index` (Int32, get) — Gets the index of this point cloud item.
- `Location` (Point3d, get/set) — Gets or sets the location of this point cloud item.
- `Normal` (Vector3d, get/set) — Gets or sets the normal vector for this point cloud item. If this point cloud item does not have a normal vector, Vector3d.Unset is returned.
- `PointValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or set the extra value of this point cloud item. The extra value can be used to store a user-defined value, such as intensity. If this point cloud item does not have an extra value, RhinoMath.UnsetValue is returned.
- `X` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X component of this point cloud item location.
- `Y` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y component of this point cloud item location.
- `Z` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Z component of this point cloud item location.

## PointCloudUnsafeLock (class)

Permits access to the underlying PointCloud raw data structures in an unsafe way.

**Remarks:** This lock object needs to be disposed before using the PointCloud in other calculations and this can be achieved with the using keyword (Using in VB.Net).

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_PointCloudUnsafeLock.htm)

### Methods
#### `public int* ColorArray(out int length)`

Retrieves a pointer to the raw array of colors, which are represented as 32-bit integers in ARGB format, or null if none is available.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Int32) — The length of the array. This value is returned by reference (out in C#). 0 is returned when there is no single precision array.

**Returns:** `Int32*` — The beginning of the color array. Item 0 is the first vertex, and item length-1 is the last valid one. If no array is available, null is returned.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PointCloudUnsafeLock_ColorArray.htm)

#### `public Vector3d* NormalArray(out int length)`

Retrieves a pointer to the raw array of point normals, or null if none is available.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Int32) — The length of the array. This value is returned by reference (out in C#). 0 is returned when there is no single precision array.

**Returns:** `Vector3d*` — The beginning of the vector array. Item 0 is the first vertex, and item length-1 is the last valid one. If no array is available, null is returned.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PointCloudUnsafeLock_NormalArray.htm)

#### `public Point3d* PointArray(out int length)`

Retrieves a pointer to the raw point array, which uses coordinates defined with double precision floating point numbers, or null if none is available.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Int32) — The length of the array. This value is returned by reference (out in C#). 0 is returned when there is no single precision array.

**Returns:** `Point3d*` — The beginning of the point array. Item 0 is the first vertex, and item length-1 is the last valid one. If no array is available, null is returned.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PointCloudUnsafeLock_PointArray.htm)

#### `public void Release()`

Releases the lock and updates the underlying unmanaged data structures.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PointCloudUnsafeLock_Release.htm)

#### `public double* ValueArray(out int length)`

Retrieves a pointer to the raw array of point values, or null if none is available.

**Parameters:**
- `length` (System.Int32) — The length of the array. This value is returned by reference (out in C#). 0 is returned when there is no single precision array.

**Returns:** `Double*` — The beginning of the value array. Item 0 is the first vertex, and item length-1 is the last valid one. If no array is available, null is returned.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PointCloudUnsafeLock_ValueArray.htm)

## PointContainment (enum)

Defines enumerated values for closed curve/point spatial relationships.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_PointContainment.htm)

### Values
- `Unset` = `0` — Relation is meaningless.
- `Inside` = `1` — Point is on the interior of the region implied by the closed curve.
- `Outside` = `2` — Point is on the exterior of the region implied by the closed curve.
- `Coincident` = `3` — Point is coincident with the curve and therefor neither inside not outside.

## PointFaceRelation (enum)

Enumerates the possible point/BrepFace spatial relationships.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_PointFaceRelation.htm)

### Values
- `Exterior` = `0` — Point is on the exterior (the trimmed part) of the face.
- `Interior` = `1` — Point is on the interior (the existing part) of the face.
- `Boundary` = `2` — Point is in limbo.

## PolyCurve (class)

Represents a curve that is the result of joining several (possibly different) types of curves.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_PolyCurve.htm)

### Constructors
- `public PolyCurve()` — Initializes a new, empty polycurve.
- `protected PolyCurve(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Protected constructor for internal use.

### Methods
#### `public bool Append(Arc arc)`

Appends and matches the start of the arc to the end of polycurve. This function will fail if the polycurve is closed or if SegmentCount > 0 and the arc is closed.

**Parameters:**
- `arc` (Rhino.Geometry.Arc) — Arc segment to append.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PolyCurve_Append.htm)

#### `public bool Append(Curve curve)`

Appends and matches the start of the curve to the end of polycurve. This function will fail if the PolyCurve is closed or if SegmentCount > 0 and the new segment is closed.

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Segment to append.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PolyCurve_Append_1.htm)

#### `public bool Append(Line line)`

Appends and matches the start of the line to the end of polycurve. This function will fail if the polycurve is closed.

**Parameters:**
- `line` (Rhino.Geometry.Line) — Line segment to append.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PolyCurve_Append_2.htm)

#### `public bool AppendSegment(Curve curve)`

Appends the curve to the polycurve without changing the new segment's geometry. This function will fail if the PolyCurve is closed or if SegmentCount > 0 and the new segment is closed.

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — Segment to append.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PolyCurve_AppendSegment.htm)

#### `public bool ChangeClosedCurveSeam(double t)`

If this curve is closed, then modify it so that the start/end point is at curve parameter t.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Curve parameter of new start/end point. The returned curves domain will start at t.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ChangeClosedCurveSeam.htm)

#### `public bool ChangeDimension(int desiredDimension)`

Changes the dimension of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `desiredDimension` (System.Int32) — The desired dimension.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the curve's dimension was already desiredDimension or if the curve's dimension was successfully changed to desiredDimension; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ChangeDimension.htm)

#### `public Curve CleanUp()`

Removes any nesting of polycurves. If this polycurve has just a single segment, the segment is returned. If, after nest removal, there are adjacent segments which are polylines, they are combined into a single polyline. The new curve may have a different domain from this polycurve. If the start and end segments of a closed input are polylines, the result may have a different seam location since the start and end segments will be combined.

**Returns:** `Curve` — A new curve that is not necessarily a polycurve if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PolyCurve_CleanUp.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation()`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve in the world XY plane. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve with respect to world XY plane.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation(Plane plane)`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve in a given plane. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The plane in which to solve the orientation.

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve in the given plane.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation_1.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation(Transform xform)`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to map the curve to the world XY plane.

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve in the world XY-plane.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation_2.htm)

#### `public CurveOrientation ClosedCurveOrientation(Vector3d upDirection)`

Determines the orientation (counterclockwise or clockwise) of a closed, planar curve in a given plane. Only works with simple (no self intersections) closed, planar curves.

**Parameters:**
- `upDirection` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector that is considered "up".

**Returns:** `CurveOrientation` — The orientation of this curve with respect to a defined up direction.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosedCurveOrientation_3.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double t)`

Finds parameter of the point on a curve that is closest to testPoint. If the maximumDistance parameter is > 0, then only points whose distance to the given point is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to search from.
- `t` (System.Double) — Parameter of local closest point.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double t, double maximumDistance)`

Finds the parameter of the point on a curve that is closest to testPoint. If the maximumDistance parameter is > 0, then only points whose distance to the given point is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to project.
- `t` (System.Double) — parameter of local closest point returned here.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — The maximum allowed distance. Past this distance, the search is given up and false is returned.Use 0 to turn off this parameter.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoint_1.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoints(Curve otherCurve, out Point3d pointOnThisCurve, out Point3d pointOnOtherCurve)`

Gets closest points between this and another curves.

**Parameters:**
- `otherCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The other curve.
- `pointOnThisCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on this curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `pointOnOtherCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on other curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoints.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoints(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, out Point3d pointOnCurve, out Point3d pointOnObject, out int whichGeometry)`

Finds the object (and the closest point in that object) that is closest to this curve. Breps, surfaces, curves and point clouds are examples of objects that can be passed to this function.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of geometry to search.
- `pointOnCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `pointOnObject` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `whichGeometry` (System.Int32) — The index of the geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false if no object was found or selected.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoints_1.htm)

#### `public bool ClosestPoints(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, out Point3d pointOnCurve, out Point3d pointOnObject, out int whichGeometry, double maximumDistance)`

Finds the object (and the closest point in that object) that is closest to this curve. Breps, surfaces, curves and point clouds are examples of objects that can be passed to this function.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of geometry to search.
- `pointOnCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on curve. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `pointOnObject` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The point on geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `whichGeometry` (System.Int32) — The index of the geometry. This out parameter is assigned during this call.
- `maximumDistance` (System.Double) — Maximum allowable distance. Past this distance, the research is given up and false is returned.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false if no object was found or selected.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ClosestPoints_2.htm)

#### `public bool CombineShortSegments(double tolerance)`

Looks for segments that are shorter than tolerance that can be combined. For NURBS of degree greater than 1, spans are combined by removing knots. Similarly for NURBS segments of polycurves. Otherwise, RemoveShortSegments() is called. Does not change the domain, but it will change the relative parameterization.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="tolerance"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Curve.CombineShortSegments(System.Double)"]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if short segments were combined or removed. False otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_CombineShortSegments.htm)

#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

- `subobjectIndex` (System.Int32) — The sub-object index.

#### `[ObsoleteAttribute("Use version that takes a tolerance")] public PointContainment Contains(Point3d testPoint)`

Computes the relationship between a point and a closed curve region. This curve must be closed or the return value will be Unset. Both curve and point are projected to the World XY plane.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Relationship between point and curve region.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Contains.htm)

#### `[ObsoleteAttribute("Use version that takes a tolerance")] public PointContainment Contains(Point3d testPoint, Plane plane)`

Computes the relationship between a point and a closed curve region. This curve must be closed or the return value will be Unset.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Plane in which to compare point and region.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Relationship between point and curve region.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Contains_1.htm)

#### `public PointContainment Contains(Point3d testPoint, Plane plane, double tolerance)`

Computes the relationship between a point and a closed curve region. This curve must be closed or the return value will be Unset.

- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Plane in which to compare point and region.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance to use during comparison.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Relationship between point and curve region.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Contains_2.htm)

Evaluate the derivatives at the specified curve parameter.

Evaluate the derivatives at the specified curve parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — Curve parameter to evaluate.
- `derivativeCount` (System.Int32) — Number of derivatives to evaluate, must be at least 0.
- `side` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveEvaluationSide) — Side of parameter to evaluate. If the parameter is at a kink, it makes a big difference whether the evaluation is from below or above.

**Returns:** `Vector3d[]` — An array of vectors that represents all the derivatives starting at zero.

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Dispose.htm)

- `contourEnd` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — The end of the contouring line.

**Returns:** `Point3d[]` — An array of points; or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DivideAsContour.htm)

#### `public override GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Duplicates this polycurve. When not overridden in a derived class, this calls DuplicatePolyCurve().

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An exact duplicate of this curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PolyCurve_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public Curve DuplicateCurve()`

Constructs an exact duplicate of this curve.

**Returns:** `Curve` — An exact copy of this curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DuplicateCurve.htm)

#### `public PolyCurve DuplicatePolyCurve()`

Duplicates this polycurve. This is the same as Duplicate().

**Returns:** `PolyCurve` — An exact duplicate of this curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PolyCurve_DuplicatePolyCurve.htm)

#### `public Curve[] DuplicateSegments()`

Duplicates curve segments. Explodes polylines, polycurves and G1 discontinuous NURBS curves. Single segment curves, such as lines, arcs, unkinked NURBS curves, are duplicated.

**Returns:** `Curve[]` — An array of all the segments that make up this curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_DuplicateSegments.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `public Curve[] Explode()`

Explodes this PolyCurve into a list of Curve segments. This will not explode nested polycurves. Call RemoveNesting() first if you need all individual segments.

**Returns:** `Curve[]` — An array of polycurve segments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_PolyCurve_Explode.htm)

Extends a curve until it intersects a collection of objects.

- `style` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveExtensionStyle) — The style or type of extension to use.
- `endPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A new end point.

**Returns:** `Curve` — New extended curve result on success, null on failure.

Extends a curve by a specific length.

- `length` (System.Double) — Length to add to the curve end.
- `style` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveExtensionStyle) — Extension style.

**Returns:** `Curve` — A curve with extended ends or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Extend_2.htm)

#### `public Curve Extend(double t0, double t1)`

Where possible, analytically extends curve to include the given domain. This will not work on closed curves. The original curve will be identical to the restriction of the resulting curve to the original curve domain.

**Parameters:**
- `t0` (System.Double) — Start of extension domain, if the start is not inside the Domain of this curve, an attempt will be made to extend the curve.
- `t1` (System.Double) — End of extension domain, if the end is not inside the Domain of this curve, an attempt will be made to extend the curve.

**Returns:** `Curve` — Extended curve on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Extend_4.htm)

#### `public Curve Extend(Interval domain)`

Where possible, analytically extends curve to include the given domain. This will not work on closed curves. The original curve will be identical to the restriction of the resulting curve to the original curve domain.

**Parameters:**
- `domain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — Extension domain.

**Returns:** `Curve` — Extended curve on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Extend_3.htm)

Extends a curve on a surface.

- `side` (Rhino.Geometry.CurveEnd) — The end of the curve to extend.
- `surface` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — Surface that contains the curve.

#### `public double[] ExtremeParameters(Vector3d direction)`

Returns the parameter values of all local extrema. Parameter values are in increasing order so consecutive extrema define an interval on which each component of the curve is monotone. Note, non-periodic curves always return the end points.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The direction in which to perform the calculation.

**Returns:** `Double[]` — The parameter values of all local extrema.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ExtremeParameters.htm)

#### `public Curve Fair(double distanceTolerance, double angleTolerance, int clampStart, int clampEnd, int iterations)`

Fairs a curve object. Fair works best on degree 3 (cubic) curves. Attempts to remove large curvature variations while limiting the geometry changes to be no more than the specified tolerance.

**Parameters:**
- `distanceTolerance` (System.Double) — Maximum allowed distance the faired curve is allowed to deviate from the input.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — (in radians) kinks with angles <= angleTolerance are smoothed out 0.05 is a good default.
- `clampStart` (System.Int32) — The number of (control vertices-1) to preserve at start. 0 = preserve start point1 = preserve start point and 1st derivative2 = preserve start point, 1st and 2nd derivative
- `clampEnd` (System.Int32) — Same as clampStart.
- `iterations` (System.Int32) — The number of iterations to use in adjusting the curve.

**Returns:** `Curve` — Returns new faired Curve on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Fair.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public Curve Fit(int degree, double fitTolerance, double angleTolerance)`

Fits a new curve through an existing curve.

**Parameters:**
- `degree` (System.Int32) — The degree of the returned Curve. Must be bigger than 1.
- `fitTolerance` (System.Double) — The fitting tolerance. If fitTolerance is RhinoMath.UnsetValue or <=0.0, the document absolute tolerance is used.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — The kink smoothing tolerance in radians. If angleTolerance is 0.0, all kinks are smoothedIf angleTolerance is >0.0, kinks smaller than angleTolerance are smoothedIf angleTolerance is RhinoMath.UnsetValue or <0.0, the document angle tolerance is used for the kink smoothing

**Returns:** `Curve` — Returns a new fitted Curve if successful, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Fit.htm)
