#### `public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(double tolerance, double angleTolerance, double minimumLength, double maximumLength)`

Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The tolerance. This is the maximum deviation from line midpoints to the curve. When in doubt, use the document's model space absolute tolerance.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — The angle tolerance in radians. This is the maximum deviation of the line directions. When in doubt, use the document's model space angle tolerance.
- `minimumLength` (System.Double) — The minimum segment length.
- `maximumLength` (System.Double) — The maximum segment length.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — PolyCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToPolyline.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(int mainSegmentCount, int subSegmentCount, double maxAngleRadians, double maxChordLengthRatio, double maxAspectRatio, double tolerance, double minEdgeLength, double maxEdgeLength, bool keepStartPoint)`

Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `mainSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — If mainSegmentCount <= 0, then both subSegmentCount and mainSegmentCount are ignored. If mainSegmentCount > 0, then subSegmentCount must be >= 1. In this case the NURBS will be broken into mainSegmentCount equally spaced chords. If needed, each of these chords can be split into as many subSegmentCount sub-parts if the subdivision is necessary for the mesh to meet the other meshing constraints. In particular, if subSegmentCount = 0, then the curve is broken into mainSegmentCount pieces and no further testing is performed.
- `subSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — An amount of subsegments.
- `maxAngleRadians` (System.Double) — ( 0 to pi ) Maximum angle (in radians) between unit tangents at adjacent vertices.
- `maxChordLengthRatio` (System.Double) — Maximum permitted value of (distance chord midpoint to curve) / (length of chord).
- `maxAspectRatio` (System.Double) — If maxAspectRatio < 1.0, the parameter is ignored. If 1 <= maxAspectRatio < sqrt(2), it is treated as if maxAspectRatio = sqrt(2). This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of (length of longest chord) / (length of shortest chord).
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — If tolerance = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of the distance from the curve to the polyline.
- `minEdgeLength` (System.Double) — The minimum permitted edge length.
- `maxEdgeLength` (System.Double) — If maxEdgeLength = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted edge length.
- `keepStartPoint` (System.Boolean) — If true the starting point of the curve is added to the polyline. If false the starting point of the curve is not added to the polyline.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — PolylineCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToPolyline_1.htm)

#### `public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(int mainSegmentCount, int subSegmentCount, double maxAngleRadians, double maxChordLengthRatio, double maxAspectRatio, double tolerance, double minEdgeLength, double maxEdgeLength, bool keepStartPoint, Interval curveDomain)`

Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

**Parameters:**
- `mainSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — If mainSegmentCount <= 0, then both subSegmentCount and mainSegmentCount are ignored. If mainSegmentCount > 0, then subSegmentCount must be >= 1. In this case the NURBS will be broken into mainSegmentCount equally spaced chords. If needed, each of these chords can be split into as many subSegmentCount sub-parts if the subdivision is necessary for the mesh to meet the other meshing constraints. In particular, if subSegmentCount = 0, then the curve is broken into mainSegmentCount pieces and no further testing is performed.
- `subSegmentCount` (System.Int32) — An amount of subsegments.
- `maxAngleRadians` (System.Double) — ( 0 to pi ) Maximum angle (in radians) between unit tangents at adjacent vertices.
- `maxChordLengthRatio` (System.Double) — Maximum permitted value of (distance chord midpoint to curve) / (length of chord).
- `maxAspectRatio` (System.Double) — If maxAspectRatio < 1.0, the parameter is ignored. If 1 <= maxAspectRatio < sqrt(2), it is treated as if maxAspectRatio = sqrt(2). This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of (length of longest chord) / (length of shortest chord).
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — If tolerance = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of the distance from the curve to the polyline.
- `minEdgeLength` (System.Double) — The minimum permitted edge length.
- `maxEdgeLength` (System.Double) — If maxEdgeLength = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted edge length.
- `keepStartPoint` (System.Boolean) — If true the starting point of the curve is added to the polyline. If false the starting point of the curve is not added to the polyline.
- `curveDomain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — This sub-domain of the NURBS curve is approximated.

**Returns:** `PolylineCurve` — PolylineCurve on success, null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_ToPolyline_2.htm)

#### `public double TorsionAt(double t)`

Evaluate the torsion of a curve at a parmeter. Sometimes also called the "second curvature", torsion is the rate of change of a curve's osculating plane.

**Remarks:** See Barrett O'Neill, Elementary Differential Geometry, page 69.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — The evaluation parameter.

**Returns:** `Double` — The torsion if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TorsionAt.htm)

**Returns:** `Curve` — Trimmed portion of this curve is successful, null on failure.

#### `public Curve Trim(Interval domain)`

Removes portions of the curve outside the specified interval.

**Parameters:**
- `domain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — Trimming interval. Portions of the curve before curve(domain[0]) and after curve(domain[1]) are removed.

**Returns:** `Curve` — Trimmed curve if successful, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_Trim_1.htm)

Test a curve for planarity and return the plane.

#### `public bool TryGetPolyline(out Polyline polyline)`

Several types of Curve can have the form of a polyline including a degree 1 NurbsCurve, a PolylineCurve, and a PolyCurve all of whose segments are some form of polyline. IsPolyline tests a curve to see if it can be represented as a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline) — If true is returned, then the polyline form is returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this curve can be represented as a polyline; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TryGetPolyline.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetPolyline(out Polyline polyline, out double[] parameters)`

Several types of Curve can have the form of a polyline including a degree 1 NurbsCurve, a PolylineCurve, and a PolyCurve all of whose segments are some form of polyline. IsPolyline tests a curve to see if it can be represented as a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (Rhino.Geometry.Polyline) — If true is returned, then the polyline form is returned here.
- `parameters` (System.Double[]) — if true is returned, then the parameters of the polyline points are returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if this curve can be represented as a polyline; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Curve_TryGetPolyline_1.htm)

### Properties
- `Degree` (Int32, get) — Gets the maximum algebraic degree of any span or a good estimate if curve spans are not algebraic.
- `Dimension` (Int32, get) — Gets the dimension of the object. The dimension is typically three. For parameter space trimming curves the dimension is two. In rare cases the dimension can be one or greater than three.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `Domain` (Interval, get/set) — Gets or sets the domain of the curve.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsClosed` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is a closed curve.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsPeriodic` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is considered to be Periodic.
- `IsSubDFriendly` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the curve is a cubic, non-rational, uniform NURBS curve that is either periodic or has natural end conditions. Othewise, false is returned.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `PointAtEnd` (Point3d, get) — Evaluates point at the end of the curve.
- `PointAtStart` (Point3d, get) — Evaluates point at the start of the curve.
- `PointCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of points in this polyline.
- `SpanCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of non-empty smooth (c-infinity) spans in the curve.
- `TangentAtEnd` (Vector3d, get) — Evaluate unit tangent vector at the end of the curve.
- `TangentAtStart` (Vector3d, get) — Evaluates the unit tangent vector at the start of the curve.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## PreserveEnd (enum)

Preserves, or maintains, the shape of the opposite the one being edited.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_PreserveEnd.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — No constraint.
- `Position` = `1` — Location only.
- `Tangency` = `2` — Position and curve direction.
- `Curvature` = `3` — Position, direction, and radius of curvature.

## QuadRemeshParameters (class)

Parameters for QuadRemesh method

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_QuadRemeshParameters.htm)

### Constructors
- `public QuadRemeshParameters()` — Initializes a new instance of the QuadRemeshParameters class

### Properties
- `AdaptiveQuadCount` (Boolean, get/set) — Respect the original Target Quad Count value as much as possible. True returns more quads than TargetQuadCount depending on amount of high-curvature areas. Note: this value is ignored when TargetEdgeLength > 0
- `AdaptiveSize` (Double, get/set) — Larger values results in for quad sizes that adjust to match input curvature. Smaller values results in more uniform quad sizes at the risk of less feature preservation. Range [0 - 100] Note: this value is ignored when TargetEdgeLength > 0
- `DetectHardEdges` (Boolean, get/set) — When enabled the hard edges in models will be retained.
- `GuideCurveInfluence` (Int32, get/set) — 0 = Approximate 1 = Interpolate Edge Ring 2 = Interpolate Edge Loop This value is ignored if Guide Curves are not supplied
- `PreserveMeshArrayEdgesMode` (Int32, get/set) — 0=off, 1=On(Smart), 2=On(Strict) : Mesh array's created from Breps will have their brep face edge boundaries retained. Smart - Small or insignificant input faces are ignored. Strict - All input faces are factored in re-meshed result.
- `SymmetryAxis` (QuadRemeshSymmetryAxis, get/set) — Symmetry axis to use for symmetric re-meshing
- `TargetEdgeLength` (Double, get/set) — When TargetEdgeLength is greater than 0 TargetQuadCount will be re-computed with the target goal of an approximated edge length based on the area of the input mesh e.g TargetQuadCount = Input mesh Area * sqr(TargetEdgeLength) AdaptiveSize as well as AdaptiveQuadCount will also be ignored;
- `TargetQuadCount` (Int32, get/set) — The number of quads to try to achieve in the final re-meshed object Note: This value is overridden if TargetEdgeLength > 0

## QuadRemeshSymmetryAxis (enum)

Symmetrical meshing axis

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_QuadRemeshSymmetryAxis.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — No symmetric re-meshing
- `X` = `1` — Symmetry across X axis
- `Y` = `2` — Symmetry across Y axis
- `Z` = `4` — Symmetry across Z axis

## Quaternion (struct)

Represents the four coefficient values in a quaternion. The first value a is the real part, while the rest multiplies i, j and k, that are imaginary.quaternion = a + bi + cj + dk

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Quaternion(double a, double b, double c, double d)` — Initializes a new quaternion with the provided coefficients.

### Methods
#### `public static Quaternion operator +(Quaternion a, Quaternion b)`

Adds two quaternions. This sums each quaternion coefficient with its correspondent and returns a new result quaternion.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — A quaternion.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — Another quaternion.

**Returns:** `Quaternion` — A new quaternion.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public static Quaternion CrossProduct(Quaternion p, Quaternion q)`

Computes the vector cross product of p and q = (0,x,y,z), where (x,y,z) = CrossProduct(p.Vector,q.Vector).This is not the same as the quaternion product p*q.

**Parameters:**
- `p` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — A quaternion.
- `q` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — Another quaternion.

**Returns:** `Quaternion` — A new quaternion.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_CrossProduct.htm)

#### `public static double Distance(Quaternion p, Quaternion q)`

Returns the distance or norm of the difference between two quaternions.

**Parameters:**
- `p` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — A quaternion.
- `q` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — Another quaternion.

**Returns:** `Double` — (p - q).Length()

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_Distance.htm)

#### `public double DistanceTo(Quaternion q)`

Computes the distance or norm of the difference between this and another quaternion.

**Parameters:**
- `q` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — Another quaternion.

**Returns:** `Double` — (this - q).Length.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_DistanceTo.htm)

#### `public static Quaternion operator /(Quaternion q, double y)`

Divides all quaternion coefficients by a factor and returns a new quaternion with the result.

**Parameters:**
- `q` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — A quaternion.
- `y` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Quaternion` — A new quaternion.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_op_Division.htm)

#### `public bool EpsilonEquals(Quaternion other, double epsilon)`

Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — [Missing <param name="other"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion,System.Double)"]

Gets a non-unique but repeatable hashing code for this quaternion.

#### `public bool GetRotation(out double angle, out Vector3d axis)`

Returns the rotation defined by the quaternion.

**Remarks:** If the quaternion is not unitized, the rotation of its unitized form is returned.

**Parameters:**
- `angle` (System.Double) — An angle in radians.
- `axis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — unit axis of rotation of 0 if (b,c,d) is the zero vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_GetRotation_2.htm)

#### `public bool GetRotation(out Plane plane)`

Returns the frame created by applying the quaternion's rotation to the canonical world frame (1,0,0),(0,1,0),(0,0,1).

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — A plane. This out value will be assigned during this call.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_GetRotation.htm)

#### `public bool GetRotation(out Transform xform)`

Returns a transformation matrix that performs the rotation defined by the quaternion. The transformation returned by this method has the property that xform * V = q.Rotate(V). If the quaternion is not unitized, the rotation of its unitized form is returned.

**Remarks:** Do not confuse the result of this method the transformation matrix returned by MatrixForm().

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful, false otherise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_GetRotation_1.htm)

#### `public static bool operator !=(Quaternion a, Quaternion b)`

Determines whether two quaternions have different values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — A quaternion.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — Another quaternion.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the quaternions differ in any coefficient; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_op_Inequality.htm)

#### `public bool Invert()`

Modifies this quaternion to become (a/L2, -b/L2, -c/L2, -d/L2),where L2 = length squared = (a*a + b*b + c*c + d*d).This is the multiplicative inverse, i.e., (a,b,c,d)*(a/L2, -b/L2, -c/L2, -d/L2) = (1,0,0,0).

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful. false if the quaternion is zero and cannot be inverted.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_Invert.htm)

#### `public Transform MatrixForm()`

Returns 4x4 real valued matrix form of the quaternion a b c d -b a -d c -c d a -b -d -c b a which has the same arithmetic properties as the quaternion.

**Remarks:** Do not confuse this with the rotation defined by the quaternion. This function will only be interesting to math nerds and is not useful in rendering or animation applications.

**Returns:** `Transform` — A transform value.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_MatrixForm.htm)

#### `public static Quaternion operator *(Quaternion q, double x)`

Multiplies (scales) all quaternion coefficients by a factor and returns a new quaternion with the result.

**Parameters:**
- `q` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — A quaternion.
- `x` (System.Double) — A number.

- `q` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — A quaternion.

Multiplies a quaternion with another one. Quaternion multiplication (Hamilton product) is not commutative.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — The first term.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — The second term.

**Returns:** `Quaternion` — A new quaternion.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_op_Multiply.htm)

Multiplies (scales) all quaternion coefficients by a factor and returns a new quaternion with the result.

**Parameters:**
- `q` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — A quaternion.
- `x` (System.Single) — A number.

**Returns:** `Quaternion` — A new quaternion.

#### `public static Quaternion Product(Quaternion p, Quaternion q)`

The quaternion product of p and q. This is the same value as p*q.

**Parameters:**
- `p` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — The first transform.
- `q` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — The second transform.

**Returns:** `Quaternion` — A transform value.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_Product.htm)

#### `public Vector3d Rotate(Vector3d v)`

Rotates a 3d vector. This operation is also called conjugation, because the result is the same as (q.Conjugate()*(0,x,y,x)*q/q.LengthSquared).Vector.

**Remarks:** If you need to rotate more than a dozen or so vectors, it will be more efficient to calculate the rotation matrix once and use it repeatedly.

**Parameters:**
- `v` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The vector to be rotated.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — R*v, where R is the rotation defined by the unit quaternion. This is mathematically the same as the values (Inverse(q)*(0,x,y,z)*q).Vector and (q.Conjugate()*(0,x,y,x)*q/q.LengthSquared).Vector.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_Rotate.htm)

#### `public static Quaternion Rotation(double angle, Vector3d axisOfRotation)`

Returns the unit quaternion cos(angle/2), sin(angle/2)*x, sin(angle/2)*y, sin(angle/2)*z where (x,y,z) is the unit vector parallel to axis. This is the unit quaternion that represents the rotation of angle about axis.

**Parameters:**
- `angle` (System.Double) — An angle in radians.
- `axisOfRotation` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Quaternion` — A new quaternion.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_Rotation_1.htm)

#### `public static Quaternion Rotation(Plane plane0, Plane plane1)`

Returns the unit quaternion that represents the rotation that maps plane0.xaxis to plane1.xaxis, plane0.yaxis to plane1.yaxis, and plane0.zaxis to plane1.zaxis.

**Remarks:** The plane origins are ignored

**Parameters:**
- `plane0` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The first plane.
- `plane1` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The second plane.

**Returns:** `Quaternion` — A quaternion value.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_Rotation.htm)

#### `public void Set(double a, double b, double c, double d)`

Sets all coefficients of the quaternion.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="a"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion.Set(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]
- `b` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="b"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion.Set(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]
- `c` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="c"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion.Set(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]
- `d` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="d"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion.Set(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_Set.htm)

#### `public void SetRotation(double angle, Vector3d axisOfRotation)`

Sets the quaternion to cos(angle/2), sin(angle/2)*x, sin(angle/2)*y, sin(angle/2)*z where (x,y,z) is the unit vector parallel to axis. This is the unit quaternion that represents the rotation of angle about axis.

**Parameters:**
- `angle` (System.Double) — in radians.
- `axisOfRotation` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The direction of the axis of rotation.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_SetRotation_1.htm)

#### `public void SetRotation(Plane plane0, Plane plane1)`

Sets the quaternion to the unit quaternion which rotates plane0.xaxis to plane1.xaxis, plane0.yaxis to plane1.yaxis, and plane0.zaxis to plane1.zaxis.

**Remarks:** The plane origins are ignored

**Parameters:**
- `plane0` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The "from" rotation plane. Origin point is ignored.
- `plane1` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — The "to" rotation plane. Origin point is ignored.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_SetRotation.htm)

#### `public static Quaternion operator -(Quaternion a, Quaternion b)`

Subtracts a quaternion from another one. This computes the difference of each quaternion coefficient with its correspondent and returns a new result quaternion.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — A quaternion.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Quaternion) — Another quaternion.

**Returns:** `Quaternion` — A new quaternion.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_op_Subtraction.htm)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Returns a string representation of this Quaternion.

**Returns:** `String` — A textual representation.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_ToString.htm)

#### `public bool Unitize()`

Scales the quaternion's coordinates so that a*a + b*b + c*c + d*d = 1.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful. false if the quaternion is zero and cannot be unitized.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Quaternion_Unitize.htm)

### Properties
- `A` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the real part of the quaternion.
- `B` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the first imaginary coefficient of the quaternion.
- `C` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the second imaginary coefficient of the quaternion.
- `Conjugate` (Quaternion, get) — Gets a new quaternion that is the conjugate of this quaternion. This is (a,-b,-c,-d)
- `D` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the third imaginary coefficient of the quaternion.
- `I` (Quaternion, get) — Returns the (0,1,0,0) quaternion.
- `Identity` (Quaternion, get) — Returns the (1,0,0,0) quaternion.
- `Inverse` (Quaternion, get) — Computes a new inverted quaternion, (a/L2, -b/L2, -c/L2, -d/L2),where L2 = length squared = (a*a + b*b + c*c + d*d). This is the multiplicative inverse, i.e., (a,b,c,d)*(a/L2, -b/L2, -c/L2, -d/L2) = (1,0,0,0). If this is the zero quaternion, then the zero quaternion is returned.
- `IsScalar` (Boolean, get) — true if b, c, and d are all zero.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Determines if the four coefficients are valid numbers within RhinoCommon. See IsValidDouble(Double).
- `IsVector` (Boolean, get) — true if a = 0 and at least one of b, c, or d is not zero.
- `IsZero` (Boolean, get) — true if a, b, c, and d are all zero.
- `J` (Quaternion, get) — Returns the (0,0,1,0) quaternion.
- `K` (Quaternion, get) — Returns the (0,0,0,1) quaternion.
- `Length` (Double, get) — Returns the length or norm of the quaternion.
- `LengthSquared` (Double, get) — Gets the result of (a^2 + b^2 + c^2 + d^2).
- `Scalar` (Double, get) — The real (scalar) part of the quaternion This is A.
- `Vector` (Vector3d, get) — The imaginary part of the quaternion (B,C,D)
- `Zero` (Quaternion, get) — Returns the default quaternion, where all coefficients are 0.

## RTree (class)

Represents a spatial search structure based on implementations of the R-tree algorithm by Toni Gutman.

**Remarks:** The opennurbs rtree code is a modified version of the free and unrestricted R-tree implementation obtained from http://www.superliminal.com/sources/sources.htm .

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_RTree.htm)

### Constructors
- `public RTree()` — Initializes a new, empty instance of the tree.

### Methods

Removes all elements.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RTree_Clear.htm)

Constructs a new tree with an element for each point cloud point.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — Points.

**Returns:** `RTree` — A new tree, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RTree_CreateFromPointArray.htm)

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RTree_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RTree_Dispose_1.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RTree_Finalize.htm)

#### `public static IEnumerable<int[]> Point3dClosestPoints(IEnumerable<Point3d> hayPoints, IEnumerable<Point3d> needlePts, double limitDistance)`

Finds the point in a list of 3D points that is closest to a test point.

**Parameters:**
- `hayPoints` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — A series of points.
- `needlePts` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — Points to search for.
- `limitDistance` (System.Double) — The maximum allowed distance.

**Returns:** `IEnumerable<Int32[]>` — An enumerable of arrays of indices; each array contains the indices for each of the needlePts.

#### `public bool Search(BoundingBox box, EventHandler<RTreeEventArgs> callback)`

Searches for items in a bounding box. The bounding box can be singular and contain exactly one single point.

- `callback` (System.EventHandler<RTreeEventArgs>) — An event handler to be raised when items are found.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if entire tree was searched. It is possible no results were found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RTree_Search.htm)

- `box` (Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox) — A bounding box.
- `callback` (System.EventHandler<RTreeEventArgs>) — An event handler to be raised when items are found.
- `tag` (System.Object) — State to be passed inside the RTreeEventArgs Tag property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if entire tree was searched. It is possible no results were found.

#### `public static bool SearchOverlaps(RTree treeA, RTree treeB, double tolerance, EventHandler<RTreeEventArgs> callback)`

Searches two R-trees for all pairs elements whose bounding boxes overlap.

**Parameters:**
- `treeA` (Rhino.Geometry.RTree) — A first tree.
- `treeB` (Rhino.Geometry.RTree) — A second tree.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — If the distance between a pair of bounding boxes is less than tolerance, then callback is called.
- `callback` (System.EventHandler<RTreeEventArgs>) — A callback event handler.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if entire tree was searched. It is possible no results were found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RTree_SearchOverlaps.htm)

### Properties
- `Count` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of items in this tree.

## RTreeEventArgs (class)

Represents event data that is passed when an item that meets certain criteria is found and the passed RTree event is raised.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_RTreeEventArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `Cancel` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value that determines if the search should be conducted farther.
- `Id` (Int32, get) — Gets the identifier of the found item.
- `IdB` (Int32, get) — If search is using two r-trees, IdB is element b in the search.
- `IdBPtr` (IntPtr, get) — If search is using two r-trees, IdB is the element b pointer in the search.
- `IdPtr` (IntPtr, get) — Gets the identifier pointer of the found item.
- `SearchBoundingBox` (BoundingBox, get/set) — Bounding box bounds used during a search. You may modify the box in a search callback to help reduce the bounds to search.
- `SearchSphere` (Sphere, get/set) — Sphere bounds used during a search. You can modify the sphere in a search callback to help reduce the bounds to search.
- `Tag` (Object, get/set) — Gets or sets an arbitrary object that can be attached to this event args. This object will "stick" through a single search and can represent user-defined state.

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public override BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to bounding box after calculation. The geometry is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_AnnotationBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)`

**Parameters:**

- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Direction of the axis of rotation.
- `rotationCenter` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point on the axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully rotated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Rotate.htm)

#### `public bool SetOverrideDimStyle(DimensionStyle OverrideStyle)`

Set a style including overrides for this annotation object. The DimensionStyle OverrideStyle must have the override fields marked as overridden and must have it's Id set to nil. Use DimensinoStyle.SetFieldOverride(Field field) and related functions to manage override settings. To override a field, the field value must be set and the field must be marked as an override. The DimensionStyle passed in here must not be in the dimstyle table

**Parameters:**
- `OverrideStyle` (Rhino.DocObjects.DimensionStyle) — [Missing <param name="OverrideStyle"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.AnnotationBase.SetOverrideDimStyle(Rhino.DocObjects.DimensionStyle)"]

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.

- `LengthResolution` (Int32, get/set) — (Inherited from Dimension.)
- `LengthRoundoff` (Double, get/set) — (Inherited from Dimension.)
- `MaskColor` (Color, get/set) — Color to use for drawing a text mask when it is enabled. If the mask is enabled and MaskColor is System.Drawing.Color.Transparent, then the viewport's color will be used for the MaskColor
- `MaskColorSource` (DimensionStyle.MaskType, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the mask background color is from the background or from a color
- `MaskEnabled` (Boolean, get/set) — Determines whether or not to draw a Text Mask
- `MaskFrame` (DimensionStyle.MaskFrame, get/set) — Gets or sets whether to draw a frame around a text mask
- `MaskOffset` (Double, get/set) — Offset for the border around text of the rectangle used to draw the mask. This value multiplied by TextHeight is the offset on each side of the tight rectangle around the text characters to the mask rectangle. The default value is 0.1.
- `MaskUsesViewportColor` (Boolean, get/set) — If true, the viewport's color is used for the mask color. If false, the color defined by MaskColor is used

## RailType (enum)

Rail types used for creating filleted Brep edges

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_RailType.htm)

### Values
- `DistanceFromEdge` = `0` — The distance from the edge curves determines the intersection.
- `RollingBall` = `1` — The radius of a rolling ball determines the intersection.
- `DistanceBetweenRails` = `2` — The distance between the edge rails determines the intersection.

## Rectangle3d (struct)

Represents the values of a plane and two intervals that form an oriented rectangle in three dimensions.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Rectangle3d(Plane plane, Interval width, Interval height)` — Initializes a new rectangle from dimensions.
- `public Rectangle3d(Plane plane, Point3d cornerA, Point3d cornerB)` — Initializes a new rectangle from a base plane and two corner points.
- `public Rectangle3d(Plane plane, double width, double height)` — Initializes a new rectangle from width and height.

### Methods
#### `public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d point)`

Gets the point on the rectangle that is closest to a test-point.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to project.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on or in the rectangle closest to the test point or Point3d.Unset on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d point, bool includeInterior)`

Gets the point on the rectangle that is closest to a test-point.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to project.
- `includeInterior` (System.Boolean) — If false, the point is projected onto the boundary edge only, otherwise the interior of the rectangle is also taken into consideration.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point on the rectangle closest to the test point or Point3d.Unset on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d_ClosestPoint_1.htm)

#### `public PointContainment Contains(double x, double y)`

Determines if two plane parameters are included in this rectangle.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Parameter along base plane X direction.
- `y` (System.Double) — Parameter along base plane Y direction.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Parameter Rectangle relationship.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d_Contains_1.htm)

#### `public PointContainment Contains(Point3d pt)`

Determines if a point is included in this rectangle.

**Parameters:**
- `pt` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to test. The point will be projected onto the Rectangle plane before inclusion is determined.

**Returns:** `PointContainment` — Point Rectangle relationship.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d_Contains.htm)

#### `public Point3d Corner(int index)`

Gets the corner at the given index.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — Index of corner, valid values are: 0 = lower left (min-x, min-y)1 = lower right (max-x, min-y)2 = upper right (max-x, max-y)3 = upper left (min-x, max-y)

**Returns:** `Point3d` — The point at the given corner index.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d_Corner.htm)

#### `public static Rectangle3d CreateFromPolyline(IEnumerable<Point3d> polyline)`

Attempts to create a rectangle from a polyline. This method only works well for polylines that already closely resemble rectangles. If the polyline contains more than four vertices, the least significant ones will be ignored. If the polylines is non-orthogonal, the discrepancies will be averaged away. This method should not be used as a Rectangle fitter.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — Polyline to parse.

**Returns:** `Rectangle3d` — A rectangle that is shaped similarly to the polyline or Rectangle3d.Unset if the polyline does not represent a rectangle.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d_CreateFromPolyline.htm)

#### `public static Rectangle3d CreateFromPolyline(IEnumerable<Point3d> polyline, out double deviation, out double angleDeviation)`

Attempts to create a rectangle from a polyline. This method only works well for polylines that already closely resemble rectangles. If the polyline contains more than four vertices, the least significant ones will be ignored. If the polylines is non-orthogonal, the discrepancies will be averaged away. This method should not be used as a Rectangle fitter.

**Parameters:**
- `polyline` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — Polyline to parse.
- `deviation` (System.Double) — On success, the deviation will contain the largest deviation between the polyline and the rectangle.
- `angleDeviation` (System.Double) — On success, the angleDeviation will contain the largest deviation (in radians) between the polyline edges and the rectangle edges.

**Returns:** `Rectangle3d` — A rectangle that is shaped similarly to the polyline or Rectangle3d.Unset if the polyline does not represent a rectangle.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d_CreateFromPolyline_1.htm)

#### `public bool EpsilonEquals(Rectangle3d other, double epsilon)`

Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Rectangle3d) — [Missing <param name="other"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Rectangle3d.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Rectangle3d,System.Double)"]
- `epsilon` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="epsilon"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Rectangle3d.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Rectangle3d,System.Double)"]

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Rectangle3d.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Rectangle3d,System.Double)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d_EpsilonEquals.htm)

Ensures the X and Y dimensions are increasing or singleton intervals.

Gets a point along the rectangle boundary.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Rectangle3d_RecenterPlane_1.htm)

- `origin` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — New origin for plane.

Constructs a nurbs curve representation of this rectangle.

### Properties
- `Area` (Double, get) — Gets the unsigned Area of the rectangle.
- `BoundingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the world aligned bounding box for this rectangle.
- `Center` (Point3d, get) — Gets the point in the center of the rectangle.
- `Circumference` (Double, get) — Gets the circumference of the rectangle.
- `Height` (Double, get) — Gets the signed height of the rectangle. If the Y dimension is decreasing, the height will be negative.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this is a valid rectangle. A rectangle is considered to be valid when the base plane and both dimensions are valid.
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — Gets or sets the base plane of the rectangle.
- `Unset` (Rectangle3d, get) — Gets a rectangle with Unset components.
- `Width` (Double, get) — Gets the signed width of the rectangle. If the X dimension is decreasing, the width will be negative.
- `X` (Interval, get/set) — Gets or sets the dimensions of the rectangle along the base plane X-Axis (i.e. the width).
- `Y` (Interval, get/set) — Gets or sets the dimensions of the rectangle along the base plane Y-Axis (i.e. the height).

## ReduceMeshParameters (class)

Parameters for Reduce method

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_ReduceMeshParameters.htm)

### Constructors
- `public ReduceMeshParameters()` — Constructs a polygon reduction parameter object with default values. Users of this class should not rely on default values to stay constant across service releases.

### Properties
- `Accuracy` (Int32, get/set) — Integer from 1 to 10 telling how accurate reduction algorithm to use. Greater number gives more accurate results
- `AllowDistortion` (Boolean, get/set) — If true mesh appearance is not changed even if the target polygon count is not reached
- `CancelToken` (CancellationToken, get/set) — 
- `DesiredPolygonCount` (Int32, get/set) — Desired or target number of faces
- `Error` (String, get) — 
- `FaceTags` (Int32[], get/set) — 
- `LockedComponents` (ComponentIndex[], get/set) — List of topological mesh vertices and mesh vertices that will not be moved or deleted in reduction process. Each mesh vertex will lock the corresponding topological mesh vertex. In other words it is not possible to have a locked and non-locked mesh vertex at the same location.
- `NormalizeMeshSize` (Boolean, get/set) — If true mesh is fitted to an axis aligned unit cube until reduction is complete
- `ProgressReporter` (IProgress<Double>, get/set) — 

## RegionContainment (enum)

Defines enumerated values for closed curve/closed curve relationships.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_RegionContainment.htm)

### Values
- `Disjoint` = `0` — There is no common area between the two regions.
- `MutualIntersection` = `1` — The two curves intersect. There is therefore no full containment relationship either way.
- `AInsideB` = `2` — Region bounded by curveA (first curve) is inside of curveB (second curve).
- `BInsideA` = `3` — Region bounded by curveB (second curve) is inside of curveA (first curve).

## RevSurface (class)

Represents a surface of revolution. Revolutions can be incomplete (they can form arcs).

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_RevSurface.htm)

### Constructors
- `protected RevSurface(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)` — Protected constructor for internal use.

### Methods
#### `public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double u, out double v)`

Input the parameters of the point on the surface that is closest to testPoint.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point to test against.
- `u` (System.Double) — U parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.
- `v` (System.Double) — V parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public IsoStatus ClosestSide(double u, double v)`

Gets the side that is closest, in terms of 3D-distance, to a U and V parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `u` (System.Double) — A u parameter.
- `v` (System.Double) — A v parameter.

**Returns:** `IsoStatus` — A side.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ClosestSide.htm)

#### `public ComponentIndex ComponentIndex()`

If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

**Returns:** `ComponentIndex` — This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_ComponentIndex.htm)

Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

**Parameters:**
- `parentObject` (System.Object) — The parent object.
- `subobjectIndex` (System.Int32) — The sub-object index.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_ConstructConstObject.htm)

#### `public static RevSurface Create(Curve revoluteCurve, Line axisOfRevolution)`

Constructs a new surface of revolution from a generatrix curve and an axis.

**Parameters:**
- `revoluteCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — A generatrix.
- `axisOfRevolution` (Rhino.Geometry.Line) — An axis.

**Returns:** `RevSurface` — A new surface of revolution, or null if any of the inputs is invalid or on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RevSurface_Create.htm)

#### `public static RevSurface Create(Curve revoluteCurve, Line axisOfRevolution, double startAngleRadians, double endAngleRadians)`

Constructs a new surface of revolution from a generatrix curve and an axis. This overload accepts a slice start and end angles.

**Parameters:**
- `revoluteCurve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — A generatrix.
- `axisOfRevolution` (Rhino.Geometry.Line) — An axis.
- `startAngleRadians` (System.Double) — An angle in radians for the start.
- `endAngleRadians` (System.Double) — An angle in radians for the end.

**Returns:** `RevSurface` — A new surface of revolution, or null if any of the inputs is invalid or on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RevSurface_Create_1.htm)

#### `public static RevSurface Create(Line revoluteLine, Line axisOfRevolution)`

Constructs a new surface of revolution from a generatrix line and an axis. If the operation succeeds, results can be (truncated) cones, cylinders and circular hyperboloids.

**Parameters:**
- `revoluteLine` (Rhino.Geometry.Line) — A generatrix.
- `axisOfRevolution` (Rhino.Geometry.Line) — An axis.

**Returns:** `RevSurface` — A new surface of revolution, or null if any of the inputs is invalid or on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_RevSurface_Create_2.htm)

Constructs a new surface of revolution from a generatrix line and an axis. This overload accepts a slice start and end angles.Results can be (truncated) cones, cylinders and circular hyperboloids, or can fail.

**Parameters:**
- `revoluteLine` (Rhino.Geometry.Line) — A generatrix.
- `axisOfRevolution` (Rhino.Geometry.Line) — An axis.
- `startAngleRadians` (System.Double) — An angle in radians for the start.
- `endAngleRadians` (System.Double) — An angle in radians for the end.

**Returns:** `RevSurface` — A new surface of revolution, or null if any of the inputs is invalid or on error.

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `public bool Evaluate(double u, double v, int numberDerivatives, out Point3d point, out Vector3d[] derivatives)`

Evaluates a surface mathematically.

**Remarks:** The partial derivatives will be in the order {Su, Sv} for numberDerivatives = 1, {Su, Sv, Suu, Suv, Svv} for 2, {Su, Sv, Suu, Suv, Svv, Suuu, Suuv, Suvv, Svvv} for 3, and similar for higher counts.

**Parameters:**
- `u` (System.Double) — A U parameter.
- `v` (System.Double) — A V parameter.
- `numberDerivatives` (System.Int32) — The number of derivatives.
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point. This out parameter will be assigned during this call.
- `derivatives` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d[]) — A vector array. This out parameter will be assigned during this call. This can be null.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the operation succeeded; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Evaluate.htm)

#### `public bool Extend(int direction, Interval interval)`

Analytically extends the surface to include the interval.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — If 0, Surface.Domain(0) will include the interval. (the first surface parameter). If 1, Surface.Domain(1) will include the interval. (the second surface parameter).
- `interval` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — If the interval is not included in surface domain, the surface will be extended so that its domain includes the interval. Note, this method will fail if the surface is closed in the specified direction.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Extend_1.htm)

#### `public Surface Extend(IsoStatus edge, double extensionLength, bool smooth)`

Extends an untrimmed surface along one edge.

**Parameters:**
- `edge` (Rhino.Geometry.IsoStatus) — Edge to extend. Must be North, South, East, or West.
- `extensionLength` (System.Double) — distance to extend.
- `smooth` (System.Boolean) — true for smooth (C-infinity) extension. false for a C1- ruled extension.

**Returns:** `Surface` — New extended surface on success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Extend.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

#### `public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(int direction, Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, out double t)`

Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — If 0, then "u" parameter is checked. If 1, then the "v" parameter is checked.
- `continuityType` (Rhino.Geometry.Continuity) — The desired continuity.
- `t0` (System.Double) — Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity and step through the discontinuities.
- `t1` (System.Double) — (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 is will be ignored unless c is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.
- `t` (System.Double) — if a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): TRUE if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): TRUE if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetNextDiscontinuity_1.htm)

#### `public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(int direction, Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, double cosAngleTolerance, double curvatureTolerance, out double t)`

Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — If 0, then "u" parameter is checked. If 1, then the "v" parameter is checked.
- `continuityType` (Rhino.Geometry.Continuity) — The desired continuity.
- `t0` (System.Double) — Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity and step through the discontinuities.
- `t1` (System.Double) — (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 is will be ignored unless c is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.
- `cosAngleTolerance` (System.Double) — default = cos(1 degree) Used only when continuityType is G1_continuous or G2_continuous. If the cosine of the angle between two tangent vectors is <= cos_angle_tolerance, then a G1 discontinuity is reported.
- `curvatureTolerance` (System.Double) — (default = ON_SQRT_EPSILON) Used only when continuityType is G2_continuous. If K0 and K1 are curvatures evaluated from above and below and |K0 - K1| > curvature_tolerance, then a curvature discontinuity is reported.
- `t` (System.Double) — if a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): TRUE if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): TRUE if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetNextDiscontinuity.htm)

- `nurbsT` (System.Double) — T on the NURBS form.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_GetNurbsFormParameterFromSurfaceParameter.htm)

#### `public int HasNurbsForm()`

Is there a NURBS surface representation of this surface.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 unable to create NURBS representation with desired accuracy. 1 success - NURBS parameterization matches the surface's 2 success - NURBS point locus matches the surface's and the domain of the NURBS surface is correct. However, This surface's parameterization and the NURBS surface parameterization may not match. This situation happens when getting NURBS representations of surfaces that have a transcendental parameterization like spheres, cylinders, and cones.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_HasNurbsForm.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurface(IEnumerable<Point3d> points, double tolerance)`

Constructs an interpolated curve on a surface, using 3D points.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — A list, an array or any enumerable set of points.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A tolerance value.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A new NURBS curve, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_InterpolatedCurveOnSurface.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV(IEnumerable<Point2d> points, double tolerance)`

Returns a curve that interpolates points on a surface. The interpolant lies on the surface.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — List of at least two UV parameter locations on the surface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used for the fit of the push-up curve. Generally, the resulting interpolating curve will be within tolerance of the surface.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A new NURBS curve if successful, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV.htm)

#### `public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV(IEnumerable<Point2d> points, double tolerance, bool closed, int closedSurfaceHandling)`

Returns a curve that interpolates points on a surface. The interpolant lies on the surface.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — List of at least two UV parameter locations on the surface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance used for the fit of the push-up curve. Generally, the resulting interpolating curve will be within tolerance of the surface.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — If false, the interpolating curve is not closed. If true, the interpolating curve is closed, and the last point and first point should generally not be equal.
- `closedSurfaceHandling` (System.Int32) — If 0, all points must be in the rectangular domain of the surface. If the surface is closed in some direction, then this routine will interpret each point and place it at an appropriate location in the covering space. This is the simplest option and should give good results. If 1, then more options for more control of handling curves going across seams are available. If the surface is closed in some direction, then the points are taken as points in the covering space. Example, if srf.IsClosed(0)=true and srf.IsClosed(1)=false and srf.Domain(0)=srf.Domain(1)=Interval(0,1) then if closedSurfaceHandling=1 a point(u, v) in points can have any value for the u coordinate, but must have 0<=v<=1. In particular, if points = { (0.0,0.5), (2.0,0.5) } then the interpolating curve will wrap around the surface two times in the closed direction before ending at start of the curve. If closed=true the last point should equal the first point plus an integer multiple of the period on a closed direction.

**Returns:** `NurbsCurve` — A new NURBS curve if successful, or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV_1.htm)

#### `public int IsAtSeam(double u, double v)`

Tests if a surface parameter value is at a seam.

**Parameters:**
- `u` (System.Double) — Surface u parameter to test.
- `v` (System.Double) — Surface v parameter to test.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if not a seam, 1 if u == Domain(0)[i] and srf(u, v) == srf(Domain(0)[1-i], v) 2 if v == Domain(1)[i] and srf(u, v) == srf(u, Domain(1)[1-i]) 3 if 1 and 2 are true.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_IsAtSeam.htm)

#### `public bool IsAtSingularity(double u, double v, bool exact)`

Tests if a surface parameter value is at a singularity.

**Parameters:**
- `u` (System.Double) — Surface u parameter to test.
- `v` (System.Double) — Surface v parameter to test.
- `exact` (System.Boolean) — If true, test if (u,v) is exactly at a singularity. If false, test if close enough to cause numerical problems.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if surface is singular at (s,t)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_IsAtSingularity.htm)

Gets a value indicating if the surface is closed in a direction.

- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 = U, 1 = V.

#### `public Curve IsoCurve(int direction, double constantParameter)`

Gets isoparametric curve.

**Remarks:** In this function "direction" indicates which direction the resulting curve runs. 0: horizontal, 1: vertical In the other Surface functions that take a "direction" argument, "direction" indicates if "constantParameter" is a "u" or "v" parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 first parameter varies and second parameter is constant e.g., point on IsoCurve(0,c) at t is srf(t,c) This is a horizontal line from left to right 1 first parameter is constant and second parameter varies e.g., point on IsoCurve(1,c) at t is srf(c,t This is a vertical line from bottom to top.
- `constantParameter` (System.Double) — The parameter that was constant on the original surface.

**Returns:** `Curve` — An isoparametric curve or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_IsoCurve.htm)

#### `public bool LocalClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, double seedU, double seedV, out double u, out double v)`

Find parameters of the point on a surface that is locally closest to the testPoint. The search for a local close point starts at seed parameters.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point to test against.
- `seedU` (System.Double) — The seed parameter in the U direction.
- `seedV` (System.Double) — The seed parameter in the V direction.
- `u` (System.Double) — U parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.
- `v` (System.Double) — V parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the search is successful, false if the search fails.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_LocalClosestPoint.htm)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — false if object cannot be converted to a deformable object. true if object was already deformable or was converted into a deformable object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_MakeDeformable.htm)

#### `public Curve Pullback(Curve curve3d, double tolerance)`

Pulls a 3d curve back to the surface's parameter space.

- `curve3d` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The curve to pull.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — the maximum acceptable 3d distance between from surface(curve_2d(t)) to the locus of points on the surface that are closest to curve_3d.

**Returns:** `Curve` — 2d curve.

Pulls a 3d curve back to the surface's parameter space.

**Parameters:**
- `curve3d` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — A curve.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — the maximum acceptable 3d distance between from surface(curve_2d(t)) to the locus of points on the surface that are closest to curve_3d.
- `curve3dSubdomain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — A sub-domain of the curve to sample.

**Returns:** `Curve` — 2d curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Pullback_1.htm)

#### `public Curve Pushup(Curve curve2d, double tolerance)`

Computes a 3d curve that is the composite of a 2d curve and the surface map.

**Parameters:**
- `curve2d` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — a 2d curve whose image is in the surface's domain.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — the maximum acceptable distance from the returned 3d curve to the image of curve_2d on the surface.

**Returns:** `Curve` — 3d curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Pushup.htm)

#### `public Curve Pushup(Curve curve2d, double tolerance, Interval curve2dSubdomain)`

Computes a 3d curve that is the composite of a 2d curve and the surface map.

**Parameters:**
- `curve2d` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — a 2d curve whose image is in the surface's domain.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — the maximum acceptable distance from the returned 3d curve to the image of curve_2d on the surface.
- `curve2dSubdomain` (Rhino.Geometry.Interval) — The curve interval (a sub-domain of the original curve) to use.

**Returns:** `Curve` — 3d curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Pushup_1.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface Rebuild(int uDegree, int vDegree, int uPointCount, int vPointCount)`

Rebuilds an existing surface to a given degree and point count.

**Parameters:**
- `uDegree` (System.Int32) — the output surface u degree.
- `vDegree` (System.Int32) — the output surface u degree.
- `uPointCount` (System.Int32) — The number of points in the output surface u direction. Must be bigger than uDegree (maximum value is 1000)
- `vPointCount` (System.Int32) — The number of points in the output surface v direction. Must be bigger than vDegree (maximum value is 1000)

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — new rebuilt surface on success. null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Rebuild.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface RebuildOneDirection(int direction, int pointCount, LoftType loftType, double refitTolerance)`

Rebuilds an existing surface with a new surface to a given point count in either the u or v directions independently.

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — The direction (0 = U, 1 = V).
- `pointCount` (System.Int32) — The number of points in the output surface in the "direction" direction.
- `loftType` (Rhino.Geometry.LoftType) — The loft type
- `refitTolerance` (System.Double) — The refit tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — new rebuilt surface on success. null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_RebuildOneDirection.htm)

Reverses parameterization Domain changes from [a,b] to [-b,-a]

- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 for first parameter's domain, 1 for second parameter's domain.

**Returns:** `Surface` — a new reversed surface on success.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Reverse.htm)

Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully scaled.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Scale.htm)
