#### `public Curve ShortPath(Point2d start, Point2d end, double tolerance)`

Constructs a geodesic between 2 points, used by ShortPath command in Rhino.

**Parameters:**
- `start` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — start point of curve in parameter space. Points must be distinct in the domain of the surface.
- `end` (Rhino.Geometry.Point2d) — end point of curve in parameter space. Points must be distinct in the domain of the surface.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance used in fitting discrete solution.

**Returns:** `Curve` — a geodesic curve on the surface on success. null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ShortPath.htm)

#### `public Surface Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem)`

Smooths a surface by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the surface edges don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The smoothed surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Smooth.htm)

#### `public Surface Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem, Plane plane)`

Smooths a surface by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

**Parameters:**
- `smoothFactor` (System.Double) — The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.
- `bXSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in X axis direction.
- `bYSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Y axis direction.
- `bZSmooth` (System.Boolean) — When true control points move in Z axis direction.
- `bFixBoundaries` (System.Boolean) — When true the surface edges don't move.
- `coordinateSystem` (Rhino.Geometry.SmoothingCoordinateSystem) — The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — If SmoothingCoordinateSystem.CPlane specified, then the construction plane.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The smoothed surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Smooth_1.htm)

Gets number of smooth nonempty spans in the parameter direction.

#### `public Surface[] Split(int direction, double parameter)`

Splits (divides) the surface into two parts at the specified parameter

**Parameters:**
- `direction` (System.Int32) — 0 = The surface is split vertically. The "west" side is returned as the first surface in the array and the "east" side is returned as the second surface in the array. 1 = The surface is split horizontally. The "south" side is returned as the first surface in the array and the "north" side is returned as the second surface in the array
- `parameter` (System.Double) — value of constant parameter in interval returned by Domain(direction)

**Returns:** `Surface[]` — Array of two surfaces on success

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_Split.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface ToNurbsSurface()`

Gets a NURBS surface representation of this surface. Default tolerance of 0.0 is used.

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — NurbsSurface on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ToNurbsSurface.htm)

#### `public NurbsSurface ToNurbsSurface(double tolerance, out int accuracy)`

Gets a NURBS surface representation of this surface.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when creating NURBS representation.
- `accuracy` (System.Int32) — 0 = unable to create NURBS representation with desired accuracy. 1 = success - returned NURBS parameterization matches the surface's to the desired accuracy. 2 = success - returned NURBS point locus matches the surface's to the desired accuracy and the domain of the NURBS surface is correct. However, this surface's parameterization and the NURBS surface parameterization may not match to the desired accuracy. This situation happens when getting NURBS representations of surfaces that have a transcendental parameterization like spheres, cylinders, and cones.

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — NurbsSurface on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_ToNurbsSurface_1.htm)

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to geometry.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if geometry successfully transformed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Transform.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCone(out Cone cone)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cone within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance and return the cone.

**Parameters:**
- `cone` (Rhino.Geometry.Cone) — On success, the cone parameters are filled in.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCone.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCone(out Cone cone, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cone and returns the cone.

**Parameters:**
- `cone` (Rhino.Geometry.Cone) — On success, the cone parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCone_1.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cylinder within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance and return the cylinder.

**Remarks:** If successful, an infinite cylinder is returned. When a surface has a cylindrical shape, even if its ends are not circles on the cylinder, it returns the axis and radius. You can detect infinite cylinders using Cylinder.IsFinite.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCylinder.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cylinder and return the infinite cylinder.

**Remarks:** If successful, an infinite cylinder is returned. When a surface has a cylindrical shape, even if its ends are not circles on the cylinder, it returns the axis and radius. You can detect infinite cylinders using Cylinder.IsFinite.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetCylinder_1.htm)

#### `public bool TryGetFiniteCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder, double tolerance)`

Tests a surface with the assumption that it might be a right circular cylinder and returns this geometry.

**Remarks:** If successful, a finite cylinder is returned.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — tolerance to use when checking.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetFiniteCylinder.htm)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_TryGetPlane_1.htm)

#### `public Surface VariableOffset(double uMinvMin, double uMinvMax, double uMaxvMin, double uMaxvMax, double tolerance)`

Copies a surface so that all locations at the corners of the copied surface are specified distances from the original surface.

**Parameters:**
- `uMinvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMinvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Max.
- `uMaxvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMaxvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Max.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The offset tolerance.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The offset surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_VariableOffset_1.htm)

#### `public Surface VariableOffset(double uMinvMin, double uMinvMax, double uMaxvMin, double uMaxvMax, IEnumerable<Point2d> interiorParameters, IEnumerable<double> interiorDistances, double tolerance)`

Copies a surface so that all locations at the corners, and from specified interior locations, of the copied surface are specified distances from the original surface.

**Parameters:**
- `uMinvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMinvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Max.
- `uMaxvMin` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Min.
- `uMaxvMax` (System.Double) — Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Max.
- `interiorParameters` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point2d>) — An array of interior UV parameters to offset from.
- `interiorDistances` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Double>) — >An array of offset distances at the interior UV parameters.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The offset tolerance.

**Returns:** `Surface` — The offset surface if successful, null otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Surface_VariableOffset.htm)

### Properties
- `Angle` (Interval, get) — Gets the start and end angles of revolution in radians. The interval angle must be increasing and satisfy RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance < Angle.Length<= 2.0 * System.Math.PI.
- `Axis` (Line, get) — Gets the axis of revolution.
- `Curve` (Curve, get) — Gets the revolute curve.
- `Disposed` (Boolean, get) — Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.
- `HasBrepForm` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object
- `HasUserData` (Boolean, get) — Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.
- `IsDeformable` (Boolean, get) — true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.
- `IsDocumentControlled` (Boolean, get) — If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.
- `IsSolid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a values indicating whether a surface is solid.
- `IsSubDFriendly` (Boolean, get) — Returns true if the surface is a non-rational, uniform, natural or periodic, cubic NURBS surface. Otherwise, false is returend.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Tests an object to see if it is valid.
- `ObjectType` (ObjectType, get) — Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.
- `UserData` (UserDataList, get) — List of custom information that is attached to this class.
- `UserDictionary` (ArchivableDictionary, get) — Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.
- `UserStringCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the amount of user strings.

## ShutLiningCurveInfo (class)

Contains shutlining curve information. This is used in WithShutLining(Boolean, Double, IEnumerable<ShutLiningCurveInfo>).

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_ShutLiningCurveInfo.htm)

### Constructors
- `public ShutLiningCurveInfo(Curve curve, double radius, int profile, bool pull, bool isBump, IEnumerable<Interval> curveIntervals = null, bool enabled = true)` — Creates a new instance of the ShutLining curve information class.

### Properties
- `Curve` (Curve, get) — The profile curve.
- `CurveIntervals` (ReadOnlyCollection<Interval>, get) — The parts of the curve to use as profiles.
- `Enabled` (Boolean, get) — If true, this curve is active.
- `IsBump` (Boolean, get) — True if profile constitutes a bump. See Rhino's Help for more information.
- `Profile` (Int32, get) — >The profile type.
- `Pull` (Boolean, get) — True if the curve should be pulled.
- `Radius` (Double, get) — The profile radius.

## Silhouette (class)

Information about silhouette curves that are generated from geometry (surfaces, brep faces, meshes)

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Silhouette.htm)

### Methods
#### `public static Silhouette[] Compute(GeometryBase geometry, SilhouetteType silhouetteType, Point3d perspectiveCameraLocation, double tolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Compute silhouettes of a shape for a perspective projection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase) — Geometry whose silhouettes need to be computed. Can be Brep, BrepFace, Mesh, or Extrusion.
- `silhouetteType` (Rhino.Geometry.SilhouetteType) — Types of silhouette to compute.
- `perspectiveCameraLocation` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Location of perspective camera.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance to use for determining projecting relationships. Surfaces and curves that are closer than tolerance, may be treated as projecting. When in doubt use RhinoDoc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Angular tolerance to use for determining projecting relationships. A surface normal N that satisfies N o cameraDirection < Sin(angleToleranceRadians) may be considered projecting. When in doubt use RhinoDoc.ModelAngleToleranceRadians.

**Returns:** `Silhouette[]` — Array of silhouette curves.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Silhouette_Compute_2.htm)

#### `public static Silhouette[] Compute(GeometryBase geometry, SilhouetteType silhouetteType, Point3d perspectiveCameraLocation, double tolerance, double angleToleranceRadians, IEnumerable<Plane> clippingPlanes, CancellationToken cancelToken)`

Compute silhouettes of a shape for a perspective projection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase) — Geometry whose silhouettes need to be computed. Can be Brep, BrepFace, Mesh, or Extrusion.
- `silhouetteType` (Rhino.Geometry.SilhouetteType) — Types of silhouette to compute.
- `perspectiveCameraLocation` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Location of perspective camera.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance to use for determining projecting relationships. Surfaces and curves that are closer than tolerance, may be treated as projecting. When in doubt use RhinoDoc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Angular tolerance to use for determining projecting relationships. A surface normal N that satisfies N o cameraDirection < Sin(angleToleranceRadians) may be considered projecting. When in doubt use RhinoDoc.ModelAngleToleranceRadians.
- `clippingPlanes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Plane>) — Optional collection of clipping planes.
- `cancelToken` (System.Threading.CancellationToken) — Computation cancellation token.

**Returns:** `Silhouette[]` — Array of silhouette curves.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Silhouette_Compute_3.htm)

#### `public static Silhouette[] ComputeDraftCurve(GeometryBase geometry, double draftAngle, Vector3d pullDirection, double tolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)`

Computes draft curve silhouettes of a shape.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase) — Geometry whose silhouettes need to be computed. Can be Brep, BrepFace, Mesh, or Extrusion.
- `draftAngle` (System.Double) — The draft angle in radians. Draft angle can be a positive or negative value.
- `pullDirection` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — 3d direction for the mold to be pulled in, directed away from the object.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance to use for determining projecting relationships. Surfaces and curves that are closer than tolerance, may be treated as projecting. When in doubt use RhinoDoc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Angular tolerance to use for determining projecting relationships. A surface normal N that satisfies N o cameraDirection < Sin(angleToleranceRadians) may be considered projecting. When in doubt use RhinoDoc.ModelAngleToleranceRadians.

**Returns:** `Silhouette[]` — Array of silhouette curves.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Silhouette_ComputeDraftCurve.htm)

#### `public static Silhouette[] ComputeDraftCurve(GeometryBase geometry, double draftAngle, Vector3d pullDirection, double tolerance, double angleToleranceRadians, CancellationToken cancelToken)`

Computes draft curve silhouettes of a shape.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase) — Geometry whose silhouettes need to be computed. Can be Brep, BrepFace, Mesh, or Extrusion.
- `draftAngle` (System.Double) — The draft angle in radians. Draft angle can be a positive or negative value.
- `pullDirection` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — 3d direction for the mold to be pulled in, directed away from the object.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — Tolerance to use for determining projecting relationships. Surfaces and curves that are closer than tolerance, may be treated as projecting. When in doubt use RhinoDoc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance.
- `angleToleranceRadians` (System.Double) — Angular tolerance to use for determining projecting relationships. A surface normal N that satisfies N o cameraDirection < Sin(angleToleranceRadians) may be considered projecting. When in doubt use RhinoDoc.ModelAngleToleranceRadians.
- `cancelToken` (System.Threading.CancellationToken) — Computation cancellation token.

**Returns:** `Silhouette[]` — Array of silhouette curves.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Silhouette_ComputeDraftCurve_1.htm)

### Properties
- `Curve` (Curve, get) — 3D curve representing the shape of the silhouette.
- `GeometryComponentIndex` (ComponentIndex, get) — Gets the component index corresponding with this silhouette curve. This field is only set when the entire silhouette curve is part of some geometry component.
- `SilhouetteType` (SilhouetteType, get) — Gets the type of this silhouette curve.

## SilhouetteType (enum)

Enumerates the different types of silhouettes and their origins.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_SilhouetteType.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — None.
- `Projecting` = `1` — Boundary of a region that projects to a curve. The view direction is tangent to the surface over the entire region.
- `TangentProjects` = `2` — Tangent silhouette curve where the curve projects to a point (within tolerance). In this case side_fill[i] is meaningless so it's left unset.
- `Tangent` = `4` — Tangent silhouette curve. The view direction is tangent to the surface along the curve.
- `Crease` = `8` — Crease on geometry that is a silhouette.
- `Boundary` = `16` — Boundary of geometry is always a silhouette.
- `NonSilhouetteCrease` = `256` — A non-silhouette crease, meaning both sides are visible.
- `NonSilhouetteTangent` = `512` — A non-silhouette tangent edge that is not a crease.
- `NonSilhouetteSeam` = `1024` — A non-silhouette surface seam.
- `SectionCut` = `4096` — Intersection with a clipping plane.
- `MiscellaneousFeature` = `8192` — Miscellaneous curve feature.
- `DraftCurve` = `32768` — Draft curve is a curve of constant draft angle.

## SmoothingCoordinateSystem (enum)

The direction of the smoothing used by Curve, Surface, and Mesh Smooth.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_SmoothingCoordinateSystem.htm)

### Values
- `World` = `0` — World coordinates
- `CPlane` = `1` — Construction plane coordinates
- `Object` = `2` — Object u, v, and n coordinates

Apply the space morph to a plane.

#### `public abstract Point3d MorphPoint(Point3d point)`

Morphs an Euclidean point. This method is abstract.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point that will be morphed by this function.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — Resulting morphed point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SpaceMorph_MorphPoint.htm)

### Properties
- `PreserveStructure` (Boolean, get/set) — true if the morph should be done in a way that preserves the structure of the geometry. In particular, for NURBS objects, true means that only the control points are moved. The PreserveStructure value does not affect the way meshes and points are morphed. The default is false.
- `QuickPreview` (Boolean, get/set) — true if the morph should be done as quickly as possible because the result is being used for some type of dynamic preview. If QuickPreview is true, the tolerance may be ignored. The QuickPreview value does not affect the way meshes and points are morphed. The default is false.
- `Tolerance` (Double, get/set) — The desired accuracy of the morph. This value is primarily used for deforming surfaces and breps. The default is 0.0 and any value <= 0.0 is ignored by morphing functions. The Tolerance value does not affect the way meshes and points are morphed.

## Sphere (struct)

Represents the plane and radius values of a sphere.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Sphere(Plane equatorialPlane, double radius)` — Initializes a new sphere given the plane of the equator circle and radius.
- `public Sphere(Point3d center, double radius)` — Initializes a new sphere given center point and radius.

### Methods
#### `public bool ClosestParameter(Point3d testPoint, out double longitudeRadians, out double latitudeRadians)`

Finds the angle parameters on this sphere that are closest to a test point.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to project onto the sphere.
- `longitudeRadians` (System.Double) — The longitudinal angle (in radians; 0.0 to 2pi) where the sphere approaches testPoint best.
- `latitudeRadians` (System.Double) — The latitudinal angle (in radians; -0.5pi to +0.5pi) where the sphere approaches testPoint best.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure. This function will fail if the point it coincident with the sphere center.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_ClosestParameter.htm)

#### `public Point3d ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint)`

Returns point on sphere that is closest to given point.

**Parameters:**
- `testPoint` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — Point to project onto Sphere.

**Returns:** `Point3d` — Point on sphere surface closest to testPoint.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_ClosestPoint.htm)

#### `public bool EpsilonEquals(Sphere other, double epsilon)`

Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Sphere) — [Missing <param name="other"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Sphere.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Sphere,System.Double)"]

#### `public static Sphere FitSphereToPoints(IEnumerable<Point3d> points)`

Attempts to fit a sphere to a collection of points.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — Points to fit. The collection must contain at least two points.

**Returns:** `Sphere` — The Sphere that best approximates the points or Sphere.Unset on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_FitSphereToPoints.htm)

#### `public Circle LatitudeDegrees(double degrees)`

Computes the parallel at a specific latitude angle. The angle is specified in degrees.

**Parameters:**
- `degrees` (System.Double) — An angle in degrees for the meridian.

**Returns:** `Circle` — A circle.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_LatitudeDegrees.htm)

#### `public Circle LatitudeRadians(double radians)`

Computes the parallel at a specific latitude angle. The angle is specified in radians.

**Parameters:**
- `radians` (System.Double) — An angle in radians for the parallel.

**Returns:** `Circle` — A circle.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_LatitudeRadians.htm)

#### `public Circle LongitudeDegrees(double degrees)`

Computes the meridian at a specific longitude angle. The angle is specified in degrees.

**Parameters:**
- `degrees` (System.Double) — An angle in degrees.

**Returns:** `Circle` — A circle.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_LongitudeDegrees.htm)

#### `public Circle LongitudeRadians(double radians)`

Computes the meridian at a specific longitude angle. The angle is specified in radians.

**Parameters:**
- `radians` (System.Double) — An angle in radians.

**Returns:** `Circle` — A circle.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_LongitudeRadians.htm)

#### `public Vector3d NormalAt(double longitudeRadians, double latitudeRadians)`

Computes the normal at a specific angular location on the sphere.

**Parameters:**
- `longitudeRadians` (System.Double) — A number within the interval [0, 2pi].
- `latitudeRadians` (System.Double) — A number within the interval [-pi/2, pi/2].

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A vector.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_NormalAt.htm)

#### `public Point3d PointAt(double longitudeRadians, double latitudeRadians)`

Evaluates the sphere at specific longitude and latitude angles.

**Parameters:**
- `longitudeRadians` (System.Double) — A number within the interval [0, 2pi].
- `latitudeRadians` (System.Double) — A number within the interval [-pi/2,pi/2].

**Returns:** `Point3d` — A point value.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_PointAt.htm)

- `cosAngle` (System.Double) — cod(angle)
- `axisOfRotation` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false on failure.

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d axisOfRotation)`

Rotates the sphere about the center point.

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Angle of rotation (in radians)
- `axisOfRotation` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Rotation axis.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_Rotate.htm)

Rotates this sphere about a point and an axis.

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Rotation angle (in Radians)
- `axisOfRotation` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false on failure.

Converts this sphere to its NurbsSurface representation. This is synonymous with calling NurbsSurface.CreateFromSphere().

**Returns:** `NurbsSurface` — A nurbs surface representation of this sphere or null.

Converts this Sphere to a RevSurface representation. This is synonymous with calling RevSurface.CreateFromSphere().

**Returns:** `RevSurface` — A surface of revolution representation of this sphere or null.

#### `public bool Transform(Transform xform)`

Transforms this sphere. Note that non-similarity preserving transformations cannot be applied to a sphere as that would result in an ellipsoid.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation matrix to apply.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_Transform.htm)

#### `public bool Translate(Vector3d delta)`

Moves this sphere along a motion vector.

**Parameters:**
- `delta` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Motion vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success; false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Sphere_Translate.htm)

### Properties
- `BoundingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the world aligned bounding box for this Sphere. If the Sphere is Invalid, an empty box is returned.
- `Center` (Point3d, get/set) — Gets or sets the center point of the sphere.
- `Diameter` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the diameter for this sphere.
- `EquatorialPlane` (Plane, get/set) — Gets or sets the Equatorial plane for this sphere.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value that indicates whether the sphere is valid.
- `NorthPole` (Point3d, get) — Gets the point at the North Pole of the sphere. This is the parameterization singularity that can be obtained, at V value +Math.Pi/2.
- `Radius` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Radius for this sphere.
- `SouthPole` (Point3d, get) — Gets the point at the South Pole of the sphere. This is the parameterization singularity that can be obtained, at V value -Math.Pi/2.
- `Unset` (Sphere, get) — Gets a sphere with invalid members.

## SquishDeformation (enum)

The types of deformation allowed in the squish process

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_SquishDeformation.htm)

### Values
- `Free` = `0` — No preference for compression or stretching.
- `StretchMostly` = `1` — Strong bias for expansion when the pattern is deformed into the 3-D shape.
- `StretchOnly` = `2` — Absolutely no compression when the pattern is deformed into the 3-D shape.
- `CompressMostly` = `3` — Strong bias for compression when the pattern is deformed into the 3-D shape.
- `CompressOnly` = `4` — Absolutely no expansion when the pattern is deformed into the 3-D shape.
- `Custom` = `5` — The Custom option lets you set the parameters used by the custom deformations. There are four parameters you can set: BndStretch, BndCompress, InteriorStretch, InteriorCompress The default value for these parameters is 1 and they can be set to any positive number. A larger value reduces the amount of the specified deformation compared to what happens when all four parameters are equal. For example, if you want to severely limit interior expansion, you could do something like: BndStretch = 1 BndCompress = 1 InteriorStretch = 1 InteriorCompress = 100 If you want to preserve boundary lengths you could use the settings: BndStretch = 10 BndCompress = 10 InteriorStretch = 1 InteriorCompress = 1

## SquishFlatteningAlgorithm (enum)

Represents the type of flattening to use

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_SquishFlatteningAlgorithm.htm)

### Values
- `Geometric` = `0` — geometric flattening algoritm: (scale independent)the "spring" constant is proportional to 1/L^2 and the result is independent of scale.
- `PhysicalStress` = `1` — physical stress flattening algorithm: (scale dependent) the "spring" constant is proportional to 1/L.

## SquishParameters (class)

Parameters used by the 'Squish' command flattening algorithm

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_SquishParameters.htm)

### Constructors
- `public SquishParameters()` — Initializes a new instance with default values. Initial values are same as Default.

### Methods
#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SquishParameters_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing)`

For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SquishParameters_Dispose_1.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SquishParameters_Finalize.htm)

#### `public bool GetSpringConstants(out double boundaryBias, out double deformationBias)`

See if the spring constants could have been set with SetSpringConstants().

**Parameters:**
- `boundaryBias` (System.Double) — boundary_bias: 0.0 to 1.0 0.0: boundary and interior treated the same 1.0: strongest bias to preserving boundary lengths at the expense of interior distortion.
- `deformationBias` (System.Double) — deformation_bias: -1.0 to 1.0 -1.0: strongest bias in favor of compression. 0.0: no preference between compression and stretching 1.0: strongest bias in favor of stretching

**Returns:** `Boolean` — If the spring constants have values that could be set by calling SetSpringConstants(), then boundary_bias and deformation_bias are set to those values and this function returns true. Otherwise, boundaryBias and deformationBias are set to 0.0 and false is returned.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SquishParameters_GetSpringConstants.htm)

#### `public void SetDeformation(SquishDeformation deformation, bool bPreserveBoundary, double boundaryStretchConstant, double boundaryCompressConstant, double interiorStretchConstant, double interiorCompressConstant)`

Sets the squish deformation characteristics

**Parameters:**
- `deformation` (Rhino.Geometry.SquishDeformation) — [Missing <param name="deformation"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SquishParameters.SetDeformation(Rhino.Geometry.SquishDeformation,System.Boolean,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]
- `bPreserveBoundary` (System.Boolean) — [Missing <param name="bPreserveBoundary"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SquishParameters.SetDeformation(Rhino.Geometry.SquishDeformation,System.Boolean,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]
- `boundaryStretchConstant` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="boundaryStretchConstant"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SquishParameters.SetDeformation(Rhino.Geometry.SquishDeformation,System.Boolean,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]
- `boundaryCompressConstant` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="boundaryCompressConstant"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SquishParameters.SetDeformation(Rhino.Geometry.SquishDeformation,System.Boolean,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]
- `interiorStretchConstant` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="interiorStretchConstant"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SquishParameters.SetDeformation(Rhino.Geometry.SquishDeformation,System.Boolean,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]
- `interiorCompressConstant` (System.Double) — [Missing <param name="interiorCompressConstant"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SquishParameters.SetDeformation(Rhino.Geometry.SquishDeformation,System.Boolean,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SquishParameters_SetDeformation.htm)

#### `public void SetSpringConstants(double boundaryBias, double deformationBias)`

A simplified interface for setting the Stretch and Compression spring constants

**Parameters:**
- `boundaryBias` (System.Double) — boundary_bias: 0.0 to 1.0 0.0: boundary and interior treated the same 1.0: strongest bias to preserving boundary lengths at the expense of interior distortion.
- `deformationBias` (System.Double) — deformation_bias: -1.0 to 1.0 -1.0: strongest bias in favor of compression. 0.0: no preference between compression and stretching 1.0: strongest bias in favor of stretching

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SquishParameters_SetSpringConstants.htm)

### Properties
- `AbsoluteLimit` (Double, get/set) — If -1.0 <= AnsoluteLimit < 0.0, then then an absolute compression limit is applied so that (2d length)/(3d length) >= abs(AbsoluteLimit). In particular, Absolute = -1.0, then no compression is permitted(2d length) >= (3d length). If 0.0 < m_absolute_limit <= 1.0 then then an absolute stretching limit is applied so that (2d length)/(3d length) <= 1/abs(AbsoluteLimit). Examples: AbsoluteLimit 1.0: no stretching, (2d length) <= 1.0*(3d length) 0.5: cap on stretching, 0.5*(2d length) <= (3d length) -0.5: cap on compression, (2d length) >= 0.5*(3d length) -1.0: no compression, (2d length) >= 1.0*(3d length)
- `Algorithm` (SquishFlatteningAlgorithm, get/set) — The flattening agorithm to use: Geometric: (scale independent)the "spring" constant is proportional to 1/L^2 and the result is independent of scale. PhysicalStress: (scale dependent) the "spring" constant is proportional to 1/L.
- `BoundaryCompressConstant` (Double, get/set) — Spring constant for compressed boundary edges times the rest length
- `BoundaryStretchConstant` (Double, get/set) — Spring constant for stretched boundary edges
- `Default` (SquishParameters, get) — Gets the default Squishing parameters
- `InteriorCompressConstant` (Double, get/set) — Spring constant for compressed interior edges times the rest length
- `InteriorStretchConstant` (Double, get/set) — Spring constant for stretched boundary edges times the rest length
- `PreserveTopology` (Boolean, get/set) — the mesh has coincident vertices and PreserveTopology is true, then the flattening is based on the mesh's topology and coincident vertices will remain coincident. Otherwise coincident vertices are free to move apart.
- `SaveMapping` (Boolean, get/set) — If SaveMapping is true, then ON_SquishMesh() will save extra information on the squished mesh so 3d points and curves near the input mesh can be mapped to the squished mesh and 2d points and curves on the squished mesh can be mapped back to the 3d mesh.

#### `public Brep SquishSurface(SquishParameters sp, Surface surface, IEnumerable<GeometryBase> marks, List<GeometryBase> squished_marks_out)`

Squish the surface into a flat brep

**Parameters:**
- `sp` (Rhino.Geometry.SquishParameters) — The parameters for the squish operation
- `surface` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — The surface to be squished
- `marks` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — Point, PountCloud, TextDot, and Curve objects to squish. Can be null.
- `squished_marks_out` (System.Collections.Generic.List<GeometryBase>) — A list of the squished marks, with null entires for marks that fail to squish. Can be null.

**Returns:** `Brep` — A brep representing the flattened surface

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Squisher_SquishSurface_1.htm)

#### `public static SubD CreateFromCylinder(Cylinder cylinder, uint circumferenceFaceCount, uint heightFaceCount, SubDEndCapStyle endCapStyle, SubDEdgeTag endCapEdgeTag, SubDComponentLocation radiusLocation)`

Creates a SubD form of a cylinder.

**Parameters:**
- `cylinder` (Rhino.Geometry.Cylinder) — The defining cylinder.
- `circumferenceFaceCount` (System.UInt32) — Number of faces around the cylinder.
- `heightFaceCount` (System.UInt32) — Number of faces in the top-to-bottom direction.
- `endCapStyle` (Rhino.Geometry.SubDEndCapStyle) — The end cap style.
- `endCapEdgeTag` (Rhino.Geometry.SubDEdgeTag) — The end cap edge tag.

#### `public static SubD CreateFromLoft(IEnumerable<NurbsCurve> curves, bool closed, bool addCorners, bool addCreases, int divisions)`

Creates a SubD lofted through shape curves.

**Remarks:** Shape curves must be in the proper order and orientation and have point counts for the desired surface. Shape curves must be either all open or all closed.

**Parameters:**
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<NurbsCurve>) — An enumeration of SubD-friendly NURBS curves to loft through.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — Creates a SubD that is closed in the lofting direction. Must have three or more shape curves.
- `addCorners` (System.Boolean) — With open curves, adds creased vertices to the SubD at both ends of the first and last curves.
- `addCreases` (System.Boolean) — With kinked curves, adds creased edges to the SubD along the kinks.
- `divisions` (System.Int32) — The segment number between adjacent input curves.

**Returns:** `SubD` — A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SubD_CreateFromLoft.htm)

Create a new SubD from a mesh.

**Parameters:**
- `mesh` (Rhino.Geometry.Mesh) — The input mesh.

**Returns:** `SubD` — A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SubD_CreateFromMesh.htm)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SubD_CreateFromMesh_1.htm)

#### `public static SubD CreateFromSurface(Surface surface, SubDFromSurfaceMethods method, bool corners)`

Create a SubD that approximates the surface. If the surface is a SubD friendly NURBS surface and withCorners is true, then the SubD and input surface will have the same geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `surface` (Rhino.Geometry.Surface) — [Missing <param name="surface"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SubD.CreateFromSurface(Rhino.Geometry.Surface,Rhino.Geometry.SubDFromSurfaceMethods,System.Boolean)"]
- `method` (Rhino.Geometry.SubDFromSurfaceMethods) — Selects the method used to calculate the SubD.
- `corners` (System.Boolean) — If the surface is open, then the corner vertices with be tagged as VertexTagCorner. This makes the resulting SubD have sharp corners to match the appearance of the input surface.

**Returns:** `SubD` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SubD.CreateFromSurface(Rhino.Geometry.Surface,Rhino.Geometry.SubDFromSurfaceMethods,System.Boolean)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SubD_CreateFromSurface.htm)

#### `public static SubD CreateFromSweep(NurbsCurve rail1, IEnumerable<NurbsCurve> shapes, bool closed, bool addCorners, bool roadlikeFrame, Vector3d roadlikeNormal)`

Fits a SubD through a series of profile curves that define the SubD cross-sections and one curve that defines a SubD edge.

**Remarks:** Shape curves must be in the proper order and orientation. Shape curves must have the same point counts and rail curves must have the same point counts. Shape curves will relocated to the nearest pair of Greville points on the rails. Shape curves will be made at each pair of rail edit points where there isn't an input shape.

**Parameters:**
- `rail1` (Rhino.Geometry.NurbsCurve) — A SubD-friendly NURBS curve to sweep along.
- `shapes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<NurbsCurve>) — An enumeration of SubD-friendly NURBS curves to sweep through.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — Creates a SubD that is closed in the rail curve direction.
- `addCorners` (System.Boolean) — With open curves, adds creased vertices to the SubD at both ends of the first and last curves.
- `roadlikeFrame` (System.Boolean) — Determines how sweep frame rotations are calculated. If false (Freeform), frame are propogated based on a refrence direction taken from the rail curve curvature direction. If true (Roadlike), frame rotations are calculated based on a vector supplied in "roadlikeNormal" and the world coordinate system.
- `roadlikeNormal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — If roadlikeFrame = true, provide 3D vector used to calculate the frame rotations for sweep shapes. If roadlikeFrame = false, then pass Unset.

**Returns:** `SubD` — A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SubD_CreateFromSweep_1.htm)

#### `public static SubD CreateFromSweep(NurbsCurve rail1, NurbsCurve rail2, IEnumerable<NurbsCurve> shapes, bool closed, bool addCorners)`

Fits a SubD through a series of profile curves that define the SubD cross-sections and two curves that defines SubD edges.

**Remarks:** Shape curves must be in the proper order and orientation. Shape curves must have the same point counts and rail curves must have the same point counts. Shape curves will relocated to the nearest pair of Greville points on the rails. Shape curves will be made at each pair of rail edit points where there isn't an input shape.

**Parameters:**
- `rail1` (Rhino.Geometry.NurbsCurve) — The first SubD-friendly NURBS curve to sweep along.
- `rail2` (Rhino.Geometry.NurbsCurve) — The second SubD-friendly NURBS curve to sweep along.
- `shapes` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<NurbsCurve>) — An enumeration of SubD-friendly NURBS curves to sweep through.
- `closed` (System.Boolean) — Creates a SubD that is closed in the rail curve direction.
- `addCorners` (System.Boolean) — With open curves, adds creased vertices to the SubD at both ends of the first and last curves.

**Returns:** `SubD` — A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SubD_CreateFromSweep.htm)

#### `public void Dispose()`

Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)`

Deletes the underlying native pointer during a Dispose call or GC collection

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_SubD_Dispose.htm)

#### `public virtual GeometryBase Duplicate()`

Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()`

Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

**Returns:** `GeometryBase` — An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_DuplicateShallow.htm)

#### `public void EnsurePrivateCopy()`

If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_EnsurePrivateCopy.htm)

#### `protected override void Finalize()`

Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Runtime_CommonObject_Finalize.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(bool accurate)`

Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `accurate` (System.Boolean) — If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_3.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox.htm)

#### `public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Orientation plane for BoundingBox.
- `worldBox` (Rhino.Geometry.Box) — Aligned box in World coordinates.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_1.htm)

#### `public virtual BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Transform xform)`

Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `xform` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

**Returns:** `BoundingBox` — The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_GeometryBase_GetBoundingBox_2.htm)

#### `public static SubD[] JoinSubDs(IEnumerable<SubD> subdsToJoin, double tolerance, bool joinedEdgesAreCreases)`

Joins an enumeration of SubDs to form as few as possible resulting SubDs. There may be more than one SubD in the result array.

**Remarks:** All of the input SubDs are copied and added to the result array in one form or another.

- `subdsToJoin` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<SubD>) — An enumeration of SubDs to join.
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — The join tolerance.
- `joinedEdgesAreCreases` (System.Boolean) — If true, merged boundary edges will be creases. If false, merged boundary edges will be smooth.
