### Properties
- `A` (Point3d, get) — Gets the first triangle corner.
- `AB` (Line, get) — Gets the triangle edge connecting the A and B corners.
- `AltitudeA` (Line, get) — Gets the altitude line starting at corner A.
- `AltitudeB` (Line, get) — Gets the altitude line starting at corner B.
- `AltitudeC` (Line, get) — Gets the altitude line starting at corner C.
- `AngleA` (Double, get) — Gets the angle at the A corner.
- `AngleB` (Double, get) — Gets the angle at the B corner.
- `AngleC` (Double, get) — Gets the angle at the C corner.
- `Area` (Double, get) — Gets the area inside this triangle.
- `AreaCenter` (Point3d, get) — Gets the triangle area centroid.
- `B` (Point3d, get) — Gets the second triangle corner.
- `BC` (Line, get) — Gets the triangle edge connecting the B and C corners.
- `BoundingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the bounding box of this triangle.
- `C` (Point3d, get) — Gets the third triangle corner.
- `CA` (Line, get) — Gets the triangle edge connecting the C and A corners.
- `Circumcenter` (Point3d, get) — Gets the triangle circumcenter.
- `Circumcircle` (Circle, get) — Gets the circumcircle of this triangle.
- `MedianA` (Line, get) — Gets the median line starting at corner A.
- `MedianB` (Line, get) — Gets the median line starting at corner B.
- `MedianC` (Line, get) — Gets the median line starting at corner C.
- `Orthocenter` (Point3d, get) — Gets the triangle orthocenter.
- `Perimeter` (Double, get) — Gets the perimeter of this triangle. This is the sum of the lenghts of all edges.
- `PerpendicularAB` (Line, get) — Gets the perpendicular bisector for edge AB.
- `PerpendicularBC` (Line, get) — Gets the perpendicular bisector for edge BC.
- `PerpendicularCA` (Line, get) — Gets the perpendicular bisector for edge CA.

## Unroller (class)

Represents the operation of unrolling a single surface.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Unroller(Brep brep)` — Initializes a new instance of the Unroller class with a brep.
- `public Unroller(Surface surface)` — Initializes a new instance of the Unroller class with surface.

### Methods
#### `public void AddFollowingGeometry(Curve curve)`

Adds a curve that should be unrolled along with the surface/brep.

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — The curve.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_AddFollowingGeometry.htm)

#### `public void AddFollowingGeometry(IEnumerable<Curve> curves)`

Adds curves that should be unrolled along with the surface/brep.

**Parameters:**
- `curves` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Curve>) — An array, a list or any enumerable set of curves.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_AddFollowingGeometry_5.htm)

#### `public void AddFollowingGeometry(IEnumerable<Point3d> points)`

Adds points that should be unrolled along with the surface/brep.

**Parameters:**
- `points` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — An array, a list or any enumerable set of points.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_AddFollowingGeometry_6.htm)

#### `public void AddFollowingGeometry(IEnumerable<Point3d> dotLocations, IEnumerable<string> dotText)`

Adds text dots that should be unrolled along with the surface/brep.

**Parameters:**
- `dotLocations` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Point3d>) — An array, a list, or any enumerable set of dot locations.
- `dotText` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<String>) — An array, a list, or any enumerable set of dot strings.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_AddFollowingGeometry_7.htm)

#### `public void AddFollowingGeometry(IEnumerable<TextDot> dots)`

Adds text dots that should be unrolled along with the surface/brep.

**Parameters:**
- `dots` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<TextDot>) — An array, a list or any enumerable set of text dots.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_AddFollowingGeometry_8.htm)

#### `public void AddFollowingGeometry(Point point)`

Adds a point that should be unrolled along with the surface/brep.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point) — A point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_AddFollowingGeometry_1.htm)

#### `public void AddFollowingGeometry(Point3d point)`

Adds a point that should be unrolled along with the surface/brep.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_AddFollowingGeometry_2.htm)

#### `public void AddFollowingGeometry(Point3d dotLocation, string dotText)`

Adds a text dot that should be unrolled along with the surface/brep.

**Parameters:**
- `dotLocation` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d) — A dot point.
- `dotText` (System.String) — A dot text.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_AddFollowingGeometry_3.htm)

#### `public void AddFollowingGeometry(TextDot dot)`

Adds a text dot that should be unrolled along with the surface/brep.

**Parameters:**
- `dot` (Rhino.Geometry.TextDot) — A text dot.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_AddFollowingGeometry_4.htm)

#### `public int FollowingGeometryIndex(Curve curve)`

Given an unrolled curve, returns the index of the source, or following curve.

**Parameters:**
- `curve` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve) — An unrolled curve returned by Unroller.PerformUnroll.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the curve added by Unroller.AddFollowingGeometry if successful, otherwise -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_FollowingGeometryIndex.htm)

#### `public int FollowingGeometryIndex(TextDot dot)`

Given an unrolled text dot, returns the index of the source, or following text dot.

**Parameters:**
- `dot` (Rhino.Geometry.TextDot) — An unrolled text dot returned by Unroller.PerformUnroll.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the text dot added by Unroller.AddFollowingGeometry if successful, otherwise -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_FollowingGeometryIndex_1.htm)

#### `public Brep[] PerformUnroll(out Curve[] unrolledCurves, out Point3d[] unrolledPoints, out TextDot[] unrolledDots)`

Executes unrolling operations.

**Parameters:**
- `unrolledCurves` (Rhino.Geometry.Curve[]) — An array of unrolled curves is assigned during the call in this out parameter.
- `unrolledPoints` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3d[]) — An array of unrolled points is assigned during the call in this out parameter.
- `unrolledDots` (Rhino.Geometry.TextDot[]) — An array of unrolled text dots is assigned during the call in this out parameter.

**Returns:** `Brep[]` — An array of breps. This array can be empty.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_PerformUnroll.htm)

#### `public int PerformUnroll(List<Brep> flatbreps)`

Executes unrolling operations.

**Parameters:**
- `flatbreps` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Brep>) — List of breps containing flattened results.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of breps in result

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Unroller_PerformUnroll_1.htm)

### Properties
- `AbsoluteTolerance` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the absolute tolerance for the unrolling operation. Absolute tolerance is used in the evaluation of new entities, such as intersections, re-projections and splits.In the current implementation, absolute tolerance is used in tessellating rails, fitting curves and pulling back trims.
- `ExplodeOutput` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value determining whether geometry should be exploded.
- `ExplodeSpacing` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets a value determining whether spacing should be exploded.
- `RelativeTolerance` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the relative tolerance for the unrolling operation. Relative tolerance is used in the evaluation of intrinsic properties, such as computations "along" the surface or brep.In the current implementation, relative tolerance is used to decide if a surface is flat enough to try to unroll. That helps ease the scale dependency. The surface has to be linear in one direction within (length * RelativeTolerance) to be considered linear for that purpose. Otherwise smash will ignore that tolerance and unroll anything.

## Vector2d (struct)

Represents the two components of a vector in two-dimensional space, using Double-precision floating point numbers.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Vector2d(double x, double y)` — Initializes a new instance of the vector based on two, X and Y, components.

### Methods
#### `public static Vector2d Add(Vector2d vector1, Vector2d vector2)`

Sums up two vectors. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the + operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that results from the component-wise addition of the two vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Add.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d operator +(Vector2d vector1, Vector2d vector2)`

Sums up two vectors.

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that results from the component-wise addition of the two vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(Vector2d other)`

Compares this Vector2d with another Vector2d. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — The other Vector2d to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y+1: otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_CompareTo.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d Divide(Vector2d vector, double t)`

Divides a Vector2d by a number, having the effect of shrinking it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the / operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Divide.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d operator /(Vector2d vector, double t)`

Divides a Vector2d by a number, having the effect of shrinking it.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_Division.htm)

#### `public bool EpsilonEquals(Vector2d other, double epsilon)`

Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d)
- `epsilon` (System.Double)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d,System.Double)"]

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_EpsilonEquals.htm)

#### `public static bool operator ==(Vector2d a, Vector2d b)`

Determines whether two vectors have equal values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if components of the two vectors are pairwise equal; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_Equality.htm)

#### `public override bool Equals(Object obj)`

Determines whether the specified System.Object is a Vector2d and has the same value as the present vector.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The specified object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if obj is Vector2d and has the same components as this; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Equals_1.htm)

#### `public bool Equals(Vector2d vector)`

Determines whether the specified vector has the same value as the present vector.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — The specified vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if vector has the same components as this; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Equals.htm)

#### `public override int GetHashCode()`

Provides a hashing value for the present vector.

**Returns:** `Int32` — A non-unique number based on vector components.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_GetHashCode.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >(Vector2d a, Vector2d b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_GreaterThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >=(Vector2d a, Vector2d b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y >= b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_GreaterThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static bool operator !=(Vector2d a, Vector2d b)`

Determines whether two vectors have different values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if any component of the two vectors is pairwise different; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_Inequality.htm)

#### `public bool IsTiny()`

Uses RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance for IsTiny calculation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if vector is very small, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_IsTiny.htm)

#### `public bool IsTiny(double tolerance)`

Determines whether a vector is very short.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A nonzero value used as the coordinate zero tolerance. .

**Returns:** `Boolean` — (Math.Abs(X) <= tiny_tol) AND (Math.Abs(Y) <= tiny_tol) AND (Math.Abs(Z) <= tiny_tol).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_IsTiny_1.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <(Vector2d a, Vector2d b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_LessThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <=(Vector2d a, Vector2d b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y <= b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_LessThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d Multiply(double t, Vector2d vector)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Multiply_2.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d operator *(double t, Vector2d vector)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_Multiply_2.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d Multiply(Vector2d vector, double t)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Multiply_1.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d operator *(Vector2d vector, double t)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_Multiply_1.htm)

#### `public static double Multiply(Vector2d vector1, Vector2d vector2)`

Multiplies two vectors together, returning the dot product (or inner product). (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Double` — A value that results from the evaluation of v1.X*v2.X + v1.Y*v2.Y. This value equals v1.Length * v2.Length * cos(alpha), where alpha is the angle between vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Multiply.htm)

#### `public static double operator *(Vector2d vector1, Vector2d vector2)`

Multiplies two vectors together, returning the dot product (or inner product).

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Double` — A value that results from the evaluation of v1.X*v2.X + v1.Y*v2.Y. This value equals v1.Length * v2.Length * cos(alpha), where alpha is the angle between vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_Multiply.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d Negate(Vector2d vector)`

Computes the reversed vector. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - unary operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector to negate.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Negate.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians)`

Rotates this vector.

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Angle of rotation (in radians).

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Rotate.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d Subtract(Vector2d vector1, Vector2d vector2)`

Subtracts the second vector from the first one. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that results from the component-wise difference of vector1 - vector2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Subtract.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d operator -(Vector2d vector1, Vector2d vector2)`

Subtracts the second vector from the first one.

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector that results from the component-wise difference of vector1 - vector2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_Subtraction.htm)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Constructs a string representation of the current vector.

**Returns:** `String` — A string in the form X,Y.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_ToString.htm)

#### `public string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider formatProvider)`

Formats the value of the current instance using the specified format.

**Parameters:**
- `format` (System.String) — The format to use. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to use the default format defined for the type of the IFormattable implementation.
- `formatProvider` (System.IFormatProvider) — The provider to use to format the value. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to obtain the numeric format information from the current locale setting of the operating system.

**Returns:** `String` — The value of the current instance in the specified format.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_ToString_1.htm)

#### `public static Vector2d operator -(Vector2d vector)`

Computes the opposite vector.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2d) — A vector to negate.

**Returns:** `Vector2d` — A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_op_UnaryNegation.htm)

#### `public bool Unitize()`

Unitizes the vector in place. A unit vector has length 1 unit. An invalid or zero length vector cannot be unitized.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success or false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2d_Unitize.htm)

### Properties
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether this vector is valid. A valid vector must be formed of valid component values for x, y and z.
- `Length` (Double, get) — Computes the length (or magnitude, or size) of this vector. This is an application of Pythagoras' theorem.
- `SquareLength` (Double, get) — Returns the square of the length of the vector.
- `Unset` (Vector2d, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components set as RhinoMath.UnsetValue,RhinoMath.UnsetValue.
- `X` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X (first) component of this vector.
- `Y` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y (second) component of this vector.
- `Zero` (Vector2d, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components 0,0.

## Vector2f (struct)

Represents the two components of a vector in two-dimensional space, using Single-precision floating point numbers.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Vector2f(float x, float y)` — Creates an instance.

### Methods
#### `public static Vector2f operator +(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)`

Computes the sum between two vectors.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector2f` — The difference.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(Vector2f other)`

Compares this Vector2f with another Vector2f. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — The other Vector2f to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y+1: otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_CompareTo.htm)

Check that all values in other are withing epsilon of the values in this

- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f)

#### `public static bool operator ==(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)`

Determines whether two vectors have equal values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the components of the two vectors are exactly equal; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_op_Equality.htm)

Determines whether the specified System.Object is a Vector2f and has the same values as the present vector.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The specified object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if obj is Vector2f and has the same coordinates as this; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_Equals_1.htm)

#### `public bool Equals(Vector2f vector)`

Determines whether the specified vector has the same values as the present vector.

- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — The specified vector.

Computes a hash number that represents the current vector.

**Returns:** `Int32` — A hash code that is not unique for each vector.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_GetHashCode.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_op_GreaterThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >=(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y >= b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_op_GreaterThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static bool operator !=(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)`

Determines whether two vectors have different values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the two vectors differ in any component; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_op_Inequality.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_op_LessThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <=(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y <= b.Y; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_op_LessThanOrEqual.htm)

Multiplies two Vector2f together, returning the dot (internal) product of the two. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — The first point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector2f_op_Multiply.htm)

Computes the difference between two vectors.

- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f) — First vector.

## Vector3d (struct)

Represents the three components of a vector in three-dimensional space, using Double-precision floating point numbers.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Vector3d(Point3d point)` — Initializes a new instance of a vector, copying the three components from the three coordinates of a point.
- `public Vector3d(Vector3d vector)` — Initializes a new instance of a vector, copying the three components from a vector.
- `public Vector3d(Vector3f vector)` — Initializes a new instance of a vector, copying the three components from a single-precision vector.
- `public Vector3d(double x, double y, double z)` — Initializes a new instance of a vector, using its three components.

### Methods
#### `public static Vector3d Add(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)`

Sums up two vectors. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the + operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that results from the component-wise addition of the two vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Add.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d operator +(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)`

Sums up two vectors.

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that results from the component-wise addition of the two vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public static bool AreOrthogonal(Vector3d x, Vector3d y, Vector3d z)`

Test whether three vectors describe an orthogonal axis system. All vectors must be mutually perpendicular this to be the case.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — X axis vector.
- `y` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Y axis vector.
- `z` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Z axis vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all vectors are non-zero and mutually perpendicular.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_AreOrthogonal.htm)

#### `public static bool AreOrthonormal(Vector3d x, Vector3d y, Vector3d z)`

Test whether three vectors describe an orthogonal, unit axis system. All vectors must be mutually perpendicular and have unit length for this to be the case.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — X axis vector.
- `y` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Y axis vector.
- `z` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Z axis vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all vectors are non-zero and mutually perpendicular.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_AreOrthonormal.htm)

#### `public static bool AreRighthanded(Vector3d x, Vector3d y, Vector3d z)`

Test whether three vectors describe a right-handed, orthogonal, unit axis system. The vectors must be orthonormal and follow the right-hand ordering; index-finger=x, middle-finger=y, thumb=z.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — X axis vector.
- `y` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Y axis vector.
- `z` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Z axis vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all vectors are non-zero and mutually perpendicular.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_AreRighthanded.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(Vector3d other)`

Compares this Vector3d with another Vector3d. Component evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The other Vector3d to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y == other.Y and this.Z < other.Z+1: otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_CompareTo.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d CrossProduct(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)`

Computes the cross product (or vector product, or exterior product) of two vectors. This operation is not commutative.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that is perpendicular to both a and b, has Length == a.Length * b.Length * sin(theta) where theta is the angle between a and b.The resulting vector is oriented according to the right hand rule.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_CrossProduct.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d Divide(Vector3d vector, double t)`

Divides a Vector3d by a number, having the effect of shrinking it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the / operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Divide.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d operator /(Vector3d vector, double t)`

Divides a Vector3d by a number, having the effect of shrinking it.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_Division.htm)

**Returns:** `Int32` — A non-unique number that represents the components of this vector.

#### `public static bool operator >(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The second vector.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_GreaterThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >=(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z >= b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_GreaterThanOrEqual.htm)

Converts a single-precision (float) vector in a double-precision vector, without needing casting.

- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A single-precision vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — The same vector, expressed using double-precision values.

Determines whether two vectors have different values.

- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Another vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if any coordinate pair is different; false otherwise.

#### `public int IsParallelTo(Vector3d other)`

Determines whether this vector is parallel to another vector, within one degree (within Pi / 180).

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Vector to use for comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Parallel indicator: +1 = both vectors are parallel 0 = vectors are not parallel, or at least one of the vectors is zero-1 = vectors are anti-parallel.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_IsParallelTo.htm)

#### `public int IsParallelTo(Vector3d other, double angleTolerance)`

Determines whether this vector is parallel to another vector, within a provided tolerance.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Vector to use for comparison.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — Angle tolerance (in radians).

**Returns:** `Int32` — Parallel indicator: +1 = both vectors are parallel.0 = vectors are not parallel or at least one of the vectors is zero.-1 = vectors are anti-parallel.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_IsParallelTo_1.htm)

#### `public bool IsPerpendicularTo(Vector3d other)`

Test to see whether this vector is perpendicular to within one degree of another one.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Vector to compare to.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if both vectors are perpendicular, false if otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_IsPerpendicularTo.htm)

#### `public bool IsPerpendicularTo(Vector3d other, double angleTolerance)`

Determines whether this vector is perpendicular to another vector, within a provided angle tolerance.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Vector to use for comparison.
- `angleTolerance` (System.Double) — Angle tolerance (in radians).

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if vectors form Pi-radians (90-degree) angles with each other; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_IsPerpendicularTo_1.htm)

#### `public bool IsTiny()`

Uses RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance for IsTiny calculation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if vector is very small, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_IsTiny.htm)

#### `public bool IsTiny(double tolerance)`

Determines whether a vector is very short.

**Parameters:**
- `tolerance` (System.Double) — A nonzero value used as the coordinate zero tolerance. .

**Returns:** `Boolean` — (Math.Abs(X) <= tiny_tol) AND (Math.Abs(Y) <= tiny_tol) AND (Math.Abs(Z) <= tiny_tol).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_IsTiny_1.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z is smaller than b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_LessThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <=(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z <= b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_LessThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d Multiply(double t, Vector3d vector)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Multiply_2.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d operator *(double t, Vector3d vector)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_Multiply_2.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d Multiply(Vector3d vector, double t)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Multiply_1.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d operator *(Vector3d vector, double t)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_Multiply_1.htm)

#### `public static double Multiply(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)`

Multiplies two vectors together, returning the dot product (or inner product). This differs from the cross product. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Double` — A value that results from the evaluation of v1.X*v2.X + v1.Y*v2.Y + v1.Z*v2.Z. This value equals v1.Length * v2.Length * cos(alpha), where alpha is the angle between vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Multiply.htm)

#### `public static double operator *(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)`

Multiplies two vectors together, returning the dot product (or inner product). This differs from the cross product.

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Double` — A value that results from the evaluation of v1.X*v2.X + v1.Y*v2.Y + v1.Z*v2.Z. This value equals v1.Length * v2.Length * cos(alpha), where alpha is the angle between vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_Multiply.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d Negate(Vector3d vector)`

Computes the reversed vector. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - unary operator otherwise)

**Remarks:** Similar to Reverse(), but static for CLR compliance.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector to negate.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Negate.htm)

#### `public bool PerpendicularTo(Vector3d other)`

Sets this vector to be perpendicular to another vector. Result is not unitized.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Vector to use as guide.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false if input vector is zero or invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_PerpendicularTo.htm)

#### `public bool Reverse()`

Reverses this vector in place (reverses the direction). If this vector is Invalid, no changes will occur and false will be returned.

**Remarks:** Similar to Negate, that is only provided for CLR language compliance.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success or false if the vector is invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Reverse.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis)`

Rotates this vector around a given axis.

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Angle of rotation (in radians).
- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Rotate.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d Subtract(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)`

Subtracts the second vector from the first one. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that results from the component-wise difference of vector1 - vector2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Subtract.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d operator -(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)`

Subtracts the second vector from the first one.

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector that results from the component-wise difference of vector1 - vector2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_Subtraction.htm)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Returns the string representation of the current vector, in the form X,Y,Z.

**Returns:** `String` — A string with the current location of the point.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_ToString.htm)

#### `public string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider formatProvider)`

Formats the value of the current instance using the specified format.

**Parameters:**
- `format` (System.String) — The format to use. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to use the default format defined for the type of the IFormattable implementation.
- `formatProvider` (System.IFormatProvider) — The provider to use to format the value. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to obtain the numeric format information from the current locale setting of the operating system.

**Returns:** `String` — The value of the current instance in the specified format.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_ToString_1.htm)

#### `public void Transform(Transform transformation)`

Transforms the vector in place. The transformation matrix acts on the left of the vector; i.e.,result = transformation*vector

**Parameters:**
- `transformation` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation matrix to apply.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Transform.htm)

#### `public static Vector3d operator -(Vector3d vector)`

Computes the opposite vector.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector to negate.

**Returns:** `Vector3d` — A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_op_UnaryNegation.htm)

#### `public bool Unitize()`

Unitizes the vector in place. A unit vector has length 1 unit. An invalid or zero length vector cannot be unitized.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success or false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_Unitize.htm)

#### `public static double VectorAngle(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)`

Compute the angle between two vectors. This operation is commutative.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — First vector for angle.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Second vector for angle.

**Returns:** `Double` — If the input is valid, the angle (in radians) between a and b; RhinoMath.UnsetValue otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_VectorAngle.htm)

#### `public static double VectorAngle(Vector3d a, Vector3d b, Plane plane)`

Computes the angle on a plane between two vectors.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Second vector.
- `plane` (Rhino.Geometry.Plane) — Two-dimensional plane on which to perform the angle measurement.

**Returns:** `Double` — On success, the angle (in radians) between a and b as projected onto the plane; RhinoMath.UnsetValue on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_VectorAngle_1.htm)

#### `public static double VectorAngle(Vector3d v1, Vector3d v2, Vector3d vNormal)`

Computes the angle of v1, v2 with a normal vector.

**Parameters:**
- `v1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — First vector.
- `v2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Second vector.
- `vNormal` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Normal vector.

**Returns:** `Double` — On success, the angle (in radians) between a and b with respect of normal vector; RhinoMath.UnsetValue on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3d_VectorAngle_2.htm)

### Properties
- `IsUnitVector` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this is a unit vector. A unit vector has length 1.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether this vector is valid. A valid vector must be formed of valid component values for x, y and z.
- `IsZero` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the X, Y, and Z values are all equal to 0.0.
- `Item` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets a vector component at the given index.
- `Length` (Double, get) — Computes the length (or magnitude, or size) of this vector. This is an application of Pythagoras' theorem. If this vector is invalid, its length is considered 0.
- `MaximumCoordinate` (Double, get) — Gets the largest (both positive and negative) component value in this vector.
- `MinimumCoordinate` (Double, get) — Gets the smallest (both positive and negative) component value in this vector.
- `SquareLength` (Double, get) — Computes the squared length (or magnitude, or size) of this vector. This is an application of Pythagoras' theorem. While the Length property checks for input validity, this property does not. You should check validity in advance, if this vector can be invalid.
- `Unset` (Vector3d, get) — Gets the value of the vector with each component set to RhinoMath.UnsetValue.
- `X` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X (first) component of the vector.
- `XAxis` (Vector3d, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components 1,0,0.
- `Y` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y (second) component of the vector.
- `YAxis` (Vector3d, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components 0,1,0.
- `Z` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Z (third) component of the vector.
- `ZAxis` (Vector3d, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components 0,0,1.
- `Zero` (Vector3d, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components 0,0,0.

## Vector3f (struct)

Represents the three components of a vector in three-dimensional space, using Single-precision floating point numbers.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f.htm)

### Constructors
- `public Vector3f(float x, float y, float z)` — Constructs a new vector from 3 single precision numbers.

### Methods
#### `public static Point3f Add(Point3f point, Vector3f vector)`

Sums up a point and a vector, and returns a new point. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the + operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — A point.
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Point3f` — A new point that results from the addition of point and vector.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Add.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f Add(Vector3f vector1, Vector3f vector2)`

Sums up two vectors. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the + operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that results from the component-wise addition of the two vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Add_1.htm)

#### `public static Point3f operator +(Point3f point, Vector3f vector)`

Sums up a point and a vector, and returns a new point.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Rhino.Geometry.Point3f) — A point.
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Point3f` — A new point that results from the addition of point and vector.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_Addition.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f operator +(Vector3f vector1, Vector3f vector2)`

Sums up two vectors.

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that results from the component-wise addition of the two vectors.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_Addition_1.htm)

#### `public int CompareTo(Vector3f other)`

Compares this Vector3f with another Vector3f. Component evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The other Vector3f to use in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y == other.Y and this.Z < other.Z+1: otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_CompareTo.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f CrossProduct(Vector3f a, Vector3f b)`

Computes the cross product (or vector product, or exterior product) of two vectors. This operation is not commutative.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — First vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — Second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that is perpendicular to both a and b, has Length == a.Length * b.Length andwith a result that is oriented following the right hand rule.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_CrossProduct.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f Divide(Vector3f vector, double t)`

Divides a Vector3f by a number, having the effect of shrinking it, t times. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the / operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Divide.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f Divide(Vector3f vector, float t)`

Divides a Vector3f by a number, having the effect of shrinking it, t times. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the / operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Single) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Divide_1.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f operator /(Vector3f vector, double t)`

Divides a Vector3f by a number, having the effect of shrinking it, t times.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Double) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

Divides a Vector3f by a number, having the effect of shrinking it, t times.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.
- `t` (System.Single) — A number.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

Check that all values in other are withing epsilon of the values in this

- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f)

Determines whether the specified System.Object is a Vector3f and has the same values as the present vector.

- `obj` (System.Object) — The specified object.

Converts a double-precision vector in a single-precision vector. Needs explicit casting to help retain precision.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — The resulting vector.

Computes a hash number that represents the current vector.

**Returns:** `Int32` — A hash code that is not unique for each vector.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_GetHashCode.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >(Vector3f a, Vector3f b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z is larger than b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_GreaterThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator >=(Vector3f a, Vector3f b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z >= b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_GreaterThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static bool operator !=(Vector3f a, Vector3f b)`

Determines whether two vectors have different values.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if the two vectors differ in any component; false otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_Inequality.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <(Vector3f a, Vector3f b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z is smaller than b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_LessThan.htm)

#### `public static bool operator <=(Vector3f a, Vector3f b)`

Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

**Parameters:**
- `a` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The first vector.
- `b` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The second vector.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z <= b.Z; otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_LessThanOrEqual.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f Multiply(float t, Vector3f vector)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Single) — A number.
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Multiply_2.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f operator *(float t, Vector3f vector)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it.

**Parameters:**
- `t` (System.Single) — A number.
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_Multiply_2.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f Multiply(Vector3f vector, float t)`

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.

Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it.

- `t` (System.Single) — A number.

#### `public static double Multiply(Vector3f point1, Vector3f point2)`

Multiplies two Vector3f together, returning the dot (internal) product of the two. This is not the cross product. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The first point.
- `point2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Double` — A value that results from the coordinate-wise multiplication of point1 and point2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Multiply.htm)

#### `public static double operator *(Vector3f point1, Vector3f point2)`

Multiplies two Vector3f together, returning the dot (internal) product of the two. This is not the cross product.

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The first point.
- `point2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — The second point.

**Returns:** `Double` — A value that results from the coordinate-wise multiplication of point1 and point2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_Multiply.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f Negate(Vector3f vector)`

Computes the reversed vector. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - unary operator otherwise)

**Remarks:** This is similar to Reverse(), but is static for CLR compliance, and with default name.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector to negate.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Negate.htm)

#### `public bool PerpendicularTo(Vector3f other)`

Sets this vector to be perpendicular to another vector. Result is not unitized.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — Vector to use as guide.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false if input vector is zero or invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_PerpendicularTo.htm)

#### `public bool Reverse()`

Reverses this vector in place (reverses the direction). If this vector contains RhinoMath.UnsetValue, the reverse will also be invalid and false will be returned.

**Remarks:** Similar to Negate, that is only provided for CLR language compliance.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success or false if the vector is invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Reverse.htm)

#### `public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3f rotationAxis)`

Rotates this vector around a given axis.

**Parameters:**
- `angleRadians` (System.Double) — Angle of rotation (in radians).
- `rotationAxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — Axis of rotation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Rotate.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f Subtract(Vector3f vector1, Vector3f vector2)`

Subtracts the second vector from the first one. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - operator otherwise)

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that results from the component-wise difference of vector1 - vector2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Subtract.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f operator -(Vector3f vector1, Vector3f vector2)`

Subtracts the second vector from the first one.

**Parameters:**
- `vector1` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector.
- `vector2` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A second vector.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector that results from the component-wise difference of vector1 - vector2.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_Subtraction.htm)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Constructs the string representation of the current vector.

**Returns:** `String` — The vector representation in the form X,Y,Z.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_ToString.htm)

#### `public string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider formatProvider)`

Formats the value of the current instance using the specified format.

**Parameters:**
- `format` (System.String) — The format to use. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to use the default format defined for the type of the IFormattable implementation.
- `formatProvider` (System.IFormatProvider) — The provider to use to format the value. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to obtain the numeric format information from the current locale setting of the operating system.

**Returns:** `String` — The value of the current instance in the specified format.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_ToString_1.htm)

#### `public void Transform(Transform transformation)`

Transforms the vector in place. The transformation matrix acts on the left of the vector; i.e.,result = transformation*vector

**Parameters:**
- `transformation` (Rhino.Geometry.Transform) — Transformation matrix to apply.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Transform.htm)

#### `public static Vector3f operator -(Vector3f vector)`

Computes the opposite vector.

**Parameters:**
- `vector` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3f) — A vector to negate.

**Returns:** `Vector3f` — A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_op_UnaryNegation.htm)

#### `public bool Unitize()`

Unitizes the vector in place. A unit vector has length 1 unit. An invalid or zero length vector cannot be unitized.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true on success or false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_Vector3f_Unitize.htm)

### Properties
- `IsUnitVector` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this is a unit vector. A unit vector has length 1.
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Returns an indication regarding the validity of this vector.
- `IsZero` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the X, Y, and Z values are all equal to 0.0.
- `Length` (Single, get) — Computes the length (or magnitude, or size) of this vector. This is an application of Pythagoras' theorem. If this vector is invalid, its length is considered 0.
- `SquareLength` (Single, get) — Returns the square length of the vector. This property does not check for the validity of the inputs.
- `Unset` (Vector3f, get) — Gets the value of the vector with each component set to RhinoMath.UnsetValue.
- `X` (Single, get/set) — Gets or sets the X (first) component of this vector.
- `XAxis` (Vector3f, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components 1,0,0.
- `Y` (Single, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y (second) component of this vector.
- `YAxis` (Vector3f, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components 0,1,0.
- `Z` (Single, get/set) — Gets or sets the Z (third) component of this vector.
- `ZAxis` (Vector3f, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components 0,0,1.
- `Zero` (Vector3f, get) — Gets the value of the vector with components 0,0,0.

## VolumeMassProperties (class)

Contains static initialization methods and allows access to the computed metrics of volume, volume centroid and volume moments in in solid meshes, in solid surfaces and in solid (closed) boundary representations.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/T_Rhino_Geometry_VolumeMassProperties.htm)

### Methods
#### `public bool CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMoments(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the moments matrix with repect to centroid coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_VolumeMassProperties_CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMoments.htm)

#### `public bool CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia(out double x, out Vector3d xaxis, out double y, out Vector3d yaxis, out double z, out Vector3d zaxis)`

Calculates the principal moments of inertia and principal axes with repect to centroid coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `xaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for x.
- `y` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `yaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for y.
- `z` (System.Double) — Principal moment.
- `zaxis` (Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d) — Principal axis for z.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if successful.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_VolumeMassProperties_CentroidCoordinatesPrincipalMomentsOfInertia.htm)

#### `public static VolumeMassProperties Compute(Brep brep)`

Compute the VolumeMassProperties for a single Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — Brep to measure.

**Returns:** `VolumeMassProperties` — The VolumeMassProperties for the given Brep or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_VolumeMassProperties_Compute.htm)

#### `public static VolumeMassProperties Compute(Brep brep, bool volume, bool firstMoments, bool secondMoments, bool productMoments)`

Compute the VolumeMassProperties for a single Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — Brep to measure.
- `volume` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume.
- `firstMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume first moments, volume, and volume centroid.
- `secondMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume second moments.
- `productMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume product moments.

**Returns:** `VolumeMassProperties` — The VolumeMassProperties for the given Brep or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_VolumeMassProperties_Compute_1.htm)

#### `public static VolumeMassProperties Compute(Brep brep, bool volume, bool firstMoments, bool secondMoments, bool productMoments, double relativeTolerance, double absoluteTolerance)`

Compute the VolumeMassProperties for a single Brep.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Rhino.Geometry.Brep) — Brep to measure.
- `volume` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume.
- `firstMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume first moments, volume, and volume centroid.
- `secondMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume second moments.
- `productMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume product moments.
- `relativeTolerance` (System.Double) — The relative tolerance used for the calculation. In overloads of this function where tolerances are not specified, 1.0e-6 is used.
- `absoluteTolerance` (System.Double) — The absolute tolerancwe used for the calculation. In overloads of this function where tolerances are not specified, 1.0e-6 is used.

**Returns:** `VolumeMassProperties` — The VolumeMassProperties for the given Brep or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_VolumeMassProperties_Compute_2.htm)

#### `public static VolumeMassProperties Compute(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry)`

Computes the VolumeMassProperties for a collection of geometric objects. At present only Breps, Surfaces, and Meshes are supported.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — Objects to include in the volume computation.

**Returns:** `VolumeMassProperties` — The VolumeMassProperties for the entire collection or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_VolumeMassProperties_Compute_7.htm)

#### `public static VolumeMassProperties Compute(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, bool volume, bool firstMoments, bool secondMoments, bool productMoments)`

Computes the VolumeMassProperties for a collection of geometric objects. At present only Breps, Surfaces, Meshes and Planar Closed Curves are supported.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<GeometryBase>) — Objects to include in the volume computation.
- `volume` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume.
- `firstMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume first moments, volume, and volume centroid.
- `secondMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume second moments.
- `productMoments` (System.Boolean) — true to calculate volume product moments.

**Returns:** `VolumeMassProperties` — The VolumeMassProperties for the entire collection or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/rhinocommon-api-docs/7/html/M_Rhino_Geometry_VolumeMassProperties_Compute_8.htm)
